[2026-05-17] update | Silent backup output pruning

  • Pruned 118 silent cron output files from 90d5cf918b5e (Princess Storybook GitHub backup), keeping the most recent 24 outputs for short-term auditability while removing redundant no-op history.
  • Reduced total cron output files from 168 to 50 and trimmed stored cron output size without touching secrets, runtime DBs, or source code.

[2026-05-17] update | Background audit model-routing trial

  • Set weekly continuity memory audit (a42c23ac4083) and weekly Sapho lean-system audit (292f4883ad21) to openai-codex / gpt-5.4-mini.
  • Added an open loop to review whether running these important background audits on 5.4-mini creates quality issues, shallow recommendations, bad pruning, or operating-system drift after the next 1–2 runs.

[2026-05-17] update | Self-improving skill loop inspected

  • Inspected whether Sapho's recurring-output feedback loop is becoming durable skill/template improvements rather than relying only on memory.
  • Finding: the loop works in-turn; recent corrections produced patches to governing skills, reusable templates, cron prompts, and wiki pages. Weakness: it is not yet fully autonomous and can create reference-file/template bloat.
  • Closed the original open loop in concepts/open-loops.md and carried the lean-governance question into the planned Sapho-system bloat review. Added jenffry-continuity/references/self-improving-skill-loop-audit-2026-05-17.md.

[2026-05-17] update | Dedicated gym review refreshed

  • Ran a Hevy source-of-truth gym review for the first week of the Sapho 5-day routine: 4 workouts, 61 working sets, average 48 minutes; classified as a strong week because Push/Pull/Lower plus one posture/cardio day were covered.
  • Updated concepts/physical-health.md with the weekly review, key lift anchors, routine coverage, and next-week target.

[2026-05-17] update | Independent finance review refreshed

  • Refreshed SimpleFIN data for the independent finance review: 15 accounts and 556 transactions; transaction data currently runs through 2026-05-14.
  • Updated concepts/finances.md with May month-to-date and 2026-05-08 through 2026-05-14 weekly budget status, remaining monthly usable amount after TD reserve, and next-week pacing guardrail.

[2026-05-17] update | Progress tracking dashboard and 1–5 review scale

  • Created concepts/progress-tracking-system.md to define lightweight daily/weekly/monthly tracking, using a 1–5 scale by default instead of green/yellow/red.
  • Created concepts/weekly-progress-dashboard.md as the living weekly page where daily signals can be logged and summarized into trends for weekly and monthly reviews.
  • Updated review templates, governing skill, cron prompts, current plan, decision log, and index so daily questions feed weekly/monthly reviews and both daily/weekly plans enforce one big task plus one quick win per day.

[2026-05-17] update | Daily templates aligned to cascade

  • Updated the daily morning template to mirror the monthly/weekly structure at daily altitude: yesterday review and lessons, weekly/monthly goal alignment, plan today, capacity/mental-health/gym/finance signals, system/hygiene, tracking/calibration.
  • Updated the daily night closeout template to review today, check weekly/monthly alignment, then prepare tomorrow with protected moves and a reset/sleep guardrail.
  • Updated the daily morning and night cron prompts to follow this cascade.

[2026-05-17] update | Weekly weekend/social reservoir requirement

  • Updated the weekly review weekend/social section so Sapho brings three realistic weekend/fun suggestions plus the current ideas reservoir from concepts/leisure.md.
  • Updated weekend-social-planning so previously shared ideas are preserved in the wiki reservoir and resurfaced at the right weekly planning moment.
  • Updated the Sunday weekly review cron prompt to use the reservoir, optionally search for fresh local/event ideas, recommend one option, and let Jenffry pick one to plan.

[2026-05-17] update | Monthly and weekly gym/finance template calibration

  • Updated the monthly template so past-month review explicitly includes numeric gym and finance reviews: Hevy attendance/performance/progression/stalls and SimpleFIN/budget actuals against monthly budget and weekly pacing.
  • Updated monthly planning so the month-by-week plan includes finance and gym goals: monthly budget target, weekly budget/pacing number, gym attendance target, minimum successful week/month, and progression focus.
  • Updated the weekly template to mimic the monthly review at weekly execution altitude: past-week review/lessons with gym and finance actuals, monthly-goal alignment, then concrete day-by-day planning with goal links.
  • Updated the Sunday weekly review cron prompt and monthly cron prompt to follow this structure.

[2026-05-17] update | Monthly review template reordered

  • Updated the monthly strategic review template from Jenffry's feedback: start with past-month review and lessons, then long-term bearing, then plan the month by week.
  • Combined the old progress/drift and domain review sections into the first section so the month starts with what happened, what went right/wrong, what improved/worsened, and lessons for next month.
  • Monthly planning now explicitly creates weekly goals that stem from long-term goals and inform future weekly reviews.
  • Updated the monthly cron prompt and check-in cadence reference to match this order.

[2026-05-17] update | Friday mental-health check-in and audio watch item

  • Created recurring Friday 5:30 PM ET dedicated mental-health check-in cron job 7cb679d0225b with TTS/audio attachment enabled.
  • Added the Friday mental-health template to the recurring review templates reference.
  • Kept the daily morning/night mental-health signals in place.
  • Noted one missed audio attachment as a watch item only; no active TTS investigation unless the issue recurs.

[2026-05-17] update | Review templates, audio parity, mental-health check-in, and SimpleFIN fix

  • Created reusable review templates at ticktick-task-management/references/review-templates-2026-05-17.md for monthly strategic review, weekly review, daily morning check-in, and daily night closeout/mental-health reset.
  • Updated the check-in cadence calibration reference so recurring user-facing reviews use the templates, attach audio notes matching written text exactly, and include a light mental-health capacity/reset check.
  • Added an open loop to verify whether Hermes Agent's self-improving skills loop is capturing useful notes and turning review feedback into skill/template patches.
  • Fixed /opt/data/scripts/simplefin_import.py to request SimpleFIN /accounts with version=2, pending=1, and a start-date window. Verified the default import now returns 15 accounts and 556 transactions; SimpleFIN returns a non-fatal 90-day cap warning.

[2026-05-17] update | Google Workspace check-in signal restored

  • During the Sunday weekly review job, reran Google Workspace auth with the known dependency prefix and verified AUTHENTICATED.
  • Verified Gmail unread search and Calendar listing for the coming week work again.
  • Updated concepts/open-loops.md: no Google re-auth is needed unless the failure recurs; future failures should first retry with PYTHONPATH=/opt/data/google-python-deps before assuming OAuth revocation.

[2026-05-17] update | Check-in cadence recalibrated

  • Updated Sapho's soft check-in cron cadence from Jenffry's voice feedback.
  • Daily morning check-in remains 5:30 AM ET but now runs Monday-Saturday only; Sunday 5:30 AM ET is a combined weekly review, Sunday morning check-in, and weekend/social plan.
  • Paused the standalone Tuesday weekend/social planning job to avoid duplicate planning messages.
  • Moved the relationship/fatherhood clarity prompt to Saturday 10:00 AM ET as a default weekend reflection window.
  • Rewrote the weekly and monthly review cron prompts to be more goal-oriented and planning-smart rather than generic audits.
  • Added weekly and monthly gym progress review using Hevy as source of truth.
  • Added weekly and monthly finance check-in/planning using SimpleFIN/budget signals where available.
  • Changed continuity memory audit to background/local delivery only, so it updates continuity without interrupting Jenffry.
  • Added self-improvement questions, tracking questions, and right-sized past progress review to user-facing check-ins/reviews.
  • Added Jenffry's follow-up calibration: monthly reviews must include a long-term planning session; weekly reviews must plan every day of the week including the weekend, use goal-oriented suggestions and pushback, and avoid overstacking days/weeks with tasks.
  • Added second weekly-review calibration: use clearer top-level sections; make Gym review and Financial review distinct major sections; ignore normal 9–5 Princess UX/UI work in personal weekly planning; use one big important task plus one quick win per day as the default load rule; schedule chosen weekend/social activity as a concrete TickTick item.
  • Updated concepts/decision-log.md, concepts/open-loops.md, and the relevant check-in/weekend/health/finance planning skills.

[2026-05-17] update | Google Workspace morning-check-in signal reopened

  • During the 5:30 AM morning check-in, Google Workspace auth returned TOKEN_REVOKED / invalid_grant, so Gmail and Calendar could not be inspected.
  • Reopened the email/calendar morning-check-in open loop in concepts/open-loops.md with the next action to re-run OAuth setup and verify Gmail search plus Calendar listing.

[2026-05-17] backup | Sapho system backup regularized

  • Checked cron and confirmed Sapho previously had only one-off local/GitHub-safe backups from 2026-05-14, while Princess Storybook had a recurring 30-minute backup.
  • Added /opt/data/scripts/sapho_system_backup.sh.
  • Manually verified it creates a local full restore archive plus a GitHub-safe sanitized backup, then pushes the safe backup to private repo jenffry90-arch/jenffry-sapho-system-backup.
  • Scheduled no-agent daily cron job ff09fc1ab416 at 0 8 * * * so backups do not consume model credits.
  • Local full restore archives stay server-only because they may contain secrets/tokens.

[2026-05-17] fix | Gateway restart/compaction continuity guard

  • Investigated Jenffry's report that context is often lost after compaction, gateway restart, or model/session switch, especially the last user message.
  • Confirmed compaction code already anchors the latest user message into the protected tail (_ensure_last_user_message_in_tail) and existing compression-boundary tests pass.
  • Found a separate restart-resume weakness: during an in-flight gateway restart, the synthetic auto-resume event had empty text, and the user's just-received message could be missing from the JSONL transcript because normal transcript persistence happened only after the agent turn completed.
  • Patched /opt/hermes/gateway/run.py to checkpoint non-internal inbound user text to JSONL before the agent starts, de-duplicate that checkpoint during normal post-turn persistence, and change empty synthetic auto-resume wording to continue the latest unanswered user request from history.
  • Added regression coverage in tests/gateway/test_restart_resume_pending.py; verified 80 passed across restart-resume and compression persistence/boundary tests.

[2026-05-17] verify | DeepSeek V4 Pro configured for fallback and auxiliary tasks

  • Jenffry clarified the intended setup: keep direct DeepSeek API for compression/session compacting, compare direct DeepSeek V4 Pro pricing against OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro, and if headline prices match use OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro as primary fallback.
  • Verified current pricing sources: direct DeepSeek V4 Pro shows $0.435/M cache-miss input and $0.87/M output during the 75% discount window; OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro shows the same headline $0.435/M input and $0.87/M output.
  • Verified /opt/data/config.yaml: primary remains openai-codex + gpt-5.5; fallback_providers is openrouter + deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro; auxiliary compression, session-search, and title-generation are direct deepseek + deepseek-v4-pro.
  • Verified live calls: OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro returned OPENROUTER_DEEPSEEK_V4_PRO_OK; direct DeepSeek V4 Pro returned DIRECT_DEEPSEEK_V4_PRO_OK.
  • Verified gateway is running and started after the configured state was present.

[2026-05-16] verify | OpenRouter fallback configured and live-tested

  • Jenffry asked whether OpenRouter fallback was done after sending the API key.
  • Verified OPENROUTER_API_KEY is present in /opt/data/.env without exposing the value.
  • Verified /opt/data/config.yaml has fallback_providers configured for OpenRouter with openai/gpt-4o-mini.
  • Ran a tiny live OpenRouter test through Hermes CLI; response returned exactly OPENROUTER_TEST_OK.
  • Gateway is running after the key/config were present, so Telegram sessions should have the fallback available. Closed the OpenRouter fallback open loop.

[2026-05-16] update | Sapho operating-system checkup refreshed

  • Jenffry asked for a full system checkup from the strategic layer above code: how Sapho helps, where the support system is strong, and what should improve.
  • Reviewed continuity wiki pages, scheduled jobs, and TickTick Today/Overdue signals.
  • Updated concepts/open-loops.md with the refreshed assessment: architecture is strong but not fully self-correcting; main gaps are dated TickTick overload, stale dashboard language relative to active Storybook work, check-in calibration, OpenRouter fallback blocked on API key, and the need for periodic Sapho usefulness audits.

[2026-05-16] update | Princess Storybook tokenization rule saved as decision

  • Jenffry emphasized that future components must keep the tokenized layer alive.
  • Updated plans/princess-storybook-scratch-build.md with a standing rule: new components must define/extend semantic tokens, component CSS must consume those tokens, exact source values belong in semantic tokens, Storybook display surfaces continue using --pc-viz-* / --pc-card-*, and tokenization is part of done.
  • Added a decision-log entry: 2026-05-16 — Tokenization is a standing rule for Princess Storybook components.

[2026-05-16] update | Semantic Storybook/component tokens added and OpenRouter fallback scoped

  • Jenffry requested full tokenization so token changes propagate across the design system, plus OpenRouter fallbacks.
  • Added src/styles/semantic-tokens.css and imported it after generated foundation tokens. It now centralizes Storybook visualization, card, Button, Form field, Alert, and Evidence/source-confidence semantic tokens.
  • Refactored Button, FormField, Alert, and Evidence CSS to consume semantic tokens instead of scattering source values/hardcoded colors through component rules. Exact source-backed values still exist, but now live as semantic token values.
  • Verified npm run build and npm run build-storybook; committed and pushed 7a86baa (Add semantic component tokens).
  • Inspected Hermes/OpenRouter setup without exposing secrets. No OPENROUTER_API_KEY or OpenRouter credential exists yet, so OpenRouter fallback activation is blocked on Jenffry providing a key; recorded this in open loops.

[2026-05-16] update | Storybook visualization now uses Princess spacing tokens

  • Jenffry identified a broader visual-system issue: many Storybook display boxes felt like 1px wrappers, and the Storybook visualization should use the Princess design system itself to display foundations/components.
  • Added a dedicated Storybook visualization layer in src/index.css with token-based --pc-viz-* variables for page padding, card padding, preview padding, gaps, and mild 8px display radius.
  • Strengthened shared display cards/panels across Foundations and Components so cards, previews, text/detail areas, and list containers use Princess spacing tokens instead of cramped ad-hoc spacing.
  • Verified npm run build and npm run build-storybook; committed and pushed 8dd07bf (Polish Storybook visualization spacing).

[2026-05-16] update | Components overview and layout polish added

  • Jenffry requested a Components / Overview page and identified visual polish issues: insufficient inner padding, bullets escaping containers, and overly large rounded corners outside the Rounded corner foundation examples.
  • Added src/components/Overview.stories.tsx and placed Components / Overview first in the Components storySort order.
  • Added global Storybook layout polish in src/index.css: safer list padding, container/card padding, bullets inside container bounds, and broad 8px radius normalization for cards/frames/placeholders while preserving explicit source/component shapes where needed.
  • Verified npm run build and npm run build-storybook; verified static Storybook index has Components / Overview plus existing Buttons & links Documentation/content stories. Backup cron committed/pushed this as 893b6d0 (Backup Princess Storybook work: 2026-05-16 17:08:06 UTC).

[2026-05-16] update | Local Princess font files installed

  • Jenffry uploaded Archive.zip containing DomaineDisplay.ttc and ProximaNova.ttc.
  • Safely inspected the ZIP: 2 entries, 0 unsafe paths, both valid font collections.
  • Split the .ttc collections into local .ttf files under public/fonts/, then removed the original collections from the repo working tree to keep browser mapping simpler.
  • Added local-only @font-face rules for DomaineDisplay weights 400/500/700 and proxima-nova weights 400/500/600/700 in src/index.css.
  • Updated public/fonts/README.md with installed file names, weights, and redistribution caution.

[2026-05-16] fix | External font URLs removed

  • Jenffry preferred actual local font files and did not want Storybook relying on Princess.com or Typekit font URLs.
  • Removed the runtime https://use.typekit.net/mvj6ajr.css import and the Princess.com DomaineDisplay font URL references from src/index.css.
  • Kept the corrected CSS family names in generated tokens (DomaineDisplay and proxima-nova) so local font files can map cleanly when supplied.
  • Added public/fonts/README.md documenting the expected local font files/weights and the rule not to commit unlicensed commercial font files without confirmation.
  • Verified npm run build and npm run build-storybook; confirmed no Princess.com/Typekit font URL references remain in source or static Storybook output; committed and pushed 0893481 (Remove external font URLs).

[2026-05-16] fix | Typography web font rendering corrected

  • Jenffry flagged that Storybook was not using the right typographies.
  • Root cause: the token export names the fonts Domaine Display and Proxima Nova, but the Princess web CSS exposes the real browser font-family names as DomaineDisplay and proxima-nova. Storybook was falling back instead of rendering the source fonts.
  • Updated scripts/generate-tokens.mjs so generated typography variables use the real Princess web family names while preserving Figma-name fallbacks.
  • Added the Princess Typekit CSS import for proxima-nova and @font-face definitions for DomaineDisplay regular and bold from the public Princess font assets.
  • Verified the Typekit CSS and Domaine font URLs return HTTP 200; reran npm run tokens:build, npm run build, and npm run build-storybook; verified the Typography story exists in the local Storybook index; committed and pushed 520292c (Correct Princess typography font loading).

  • Reworked Foundations / Typography from a type-size token starter into sectioned source-backed documentation with visual samples and style metadata.
  • Verified npm run tokens:build, npm run build, and npm run build-storybook; verified the Typography story exists in the local Storybook index; backed up by cron commit 9b10778 (Backup Princess Storybook work: 2026-05-16 10:38:02 UTC).

[2026-05-15] update | Princess Storybook itinerary day-breakdown pattern added

[2026-05-15] update | Princess Storybook scratch v0 created

  • Removed old Storybook Design System clone locally and removed its obsolete backup script/job.

[2026-05-15] decision | Princess Storybook build-from-scratch

  • Jenffry decided to build a custom Princess Storybook from scratch rather than clone-and-prune Storybook Design System.
  • Rationale: Storybook Design System felt too small; React USWDS and Carbon are valuable references but too externally opinionated to own directly.
  • Direction: use Storybook Design System, React USWDS, and Carbon as references/north stars; build Princess-specific foundations/components/patterns intentionally.
  • Cleanup: stopped the old clone's Storybook server and temporary Cloudflare tunnel; paused old clone backup job f1ea41a336d3.

[2026-05-14] update | Princess Storybook clone-and-prune started

  • Jenffry chose Storybook Design System as the base for a clone-and-prune strategy.
  • Cloned storybookjs/design-system into /opt/data/projects/princess-storybook-design-system and created branch princess-clone-and-prune-spike.
  • Installed dependencies, verified static Storybook build, started dev server at http://localhost:6006/, and added PRINCESS_CLONE_AND_PRUNE.md as the local operating note.
  • Recorded the setup details, warnings, and next prune step in concepts/princess-ux-work.md.

[2026-05-14] update | Princess Storybook scaffold comparison

  • Compared three possible Storybook scaffold paths for the Princess design-system pilot: Storybook Design System, React USWDS, and a minimal Storybook + tokens starter.
  • Working recommendation: use a clean minimal React + Vite + Storybook project as the actual base, while borrowing structure/testing ideas from Storybook Design System and accessibility/documentation rigor from React USWDS.
  • Recorded the rationale in concepts/princess-ux-work.md: this maximizes control, avoids false completeness, and fits incomplete Princess design foundations.

[2026-05-14] update | Princess Storybook design-system project research

  • Updated concepts/princess-ux-work.md with Storybook design-system project progress.
  • Captured the research direction: use Storybook as a coded/design-system reference for Princess, starting with a small pilot rather than a full system.
  • Recorded the feasibility assessment that an existing open-source Storybook/design system can be valuable as scaffolding, but should not be blindly reskinned into a false-complete Princess system.

  • Created system/context-compiler.md as the home of record for task-specific context loading.

  • Updated index.md, system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, system/source-of-truth-registry.md, and system/sapho-operating-system.md so context loading has a canonical home and roadmap status.

[2026-05-20] create | Autonomy policy and Level 4 boundaries added

  • Created system/autonomy-policy.md as the home of record for Sapho action-permission levels.
  • Defined Levels 0–4 with Level 4 requiring explicit approval for destructive, external, money, credential/privacy, legal/medical/immigration, broad automation, and high-blast-radius changes.
  • Updated index.md, system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, system/source-of-truth-registry.md, system/sapho-operating-system.md, and system/communication-taxonomy.md to wire the policy into Sapho's operating layers.

[2026-05-20] create | Autonomy Level 4 eval examples added

  • Created /opt/data/evals/sapho/autonomy-levels-v1.jsonl with 16 seed scenarios covering Levels 0–4, especially Level 4 boundaries around destructive data actions, live restore, external sends, public publishing, money, credentials/privacy, legal/medical/immigration, broad automation, and high-blast-radius model/tool changes.
  • Updated /opt/data/evals/sapho/README.md, system/autonomy-policy.md, system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, and system/source-of-truth-registry.md.

[2026-05-21] update | Daily signal logged

2026-05-21 — Dashboard weekly/monthly goal source rule

  • Updated the dashboard generator to render those weekly/monthly goal cards inside Goals and main tasks.

2026-05-21 — Dashboard finance cockpit daily refresh

2026-05-24 — Weekly goal clarified by Jenffry

2026-05-25 — Dashboard Today/Goals cleanup

  • Removed the Today tab kicker, monthly TickTick task card, goal-source helper text, and Personal signals/Open-loop load tracking block from the public dashboard.
  • Kept weekly/monthly goal sentences and progress bars while reducing the Today tab to the useful dated-task view.

2026-05-25 — Dashboard Goals & Tasks tab merge

  • Moved the Goals section into the former Today landing tab, renamed that tab to Goals & Tasks, and removed the separate duplicate Goals & Tasks tab.
  • Updated the Health goal row to summarize dashboard-backed progress with Mental health and Physical health bars derived from the Health tab.
  • Updated the Finance goal row to summarize dashboard-backed progress with guardrail/savings/utilization bars derived from the Finance tab; other non-dashboard goal areas remain on their provisional single-bar display.

2026-05-25 — Dashboard Goals section polish

  • Updated Goals rows to use full-width Health/Finance-style progress bars with spacing between stacked bars.
  • Replaced vague goal subtitles with short one-sentence goal summaries for each life area.
  • Simplified Finance in Goals to one Budget streak bar: progress is months under budget out of a 6-month target, using available SimpleFIN history.
  • Made the Goals and main tasks card full-width on desktop.
  • Recorded the future SMART-goal tracking rule: weekly/monthly goals should eventually be backed by TickTick/habit/dashboard source data rather than hardcoded percentages.

2026-05-25 — Dashboard life-area tabs added

  • Added dashboard tabs for Relationships, Career, Leisure, Life Admin, and Sapho System.
  • Kept Goals & Tasks, Health, Finance, and Wiki as existing surfaces.
  • New life-area tabs use lean wiki-backed summaries and source cards so richer dashboards can be added later without recreating the generic removed Life Map block.
  • Sapho System tab now surfaces operating-layer summaries from Sapho operating system, current strategy, open loops, decision log, and dashboard pages.
  • Updated system/dashboard.md so dashboard documentation matches the live tab structure.

2026-05-25 — Mental-health aggregate dashboard bars

  • Added two aggregate groups directly under the Mental health title in the Health tab: Overall mental stability and Mental health stabilizers.
  • Overall mental stability uses 7-day and 30-day averages from Stop checking, Mood, Anxiety control, and Energy score habits.
  • Mental health stabilizers uses 7-day and 30-day averaged progress from the remaining mental-health supports: score habits become score percent, completion habits become expected-completion percent, and the group averages those percents.
  • Kept detailed individual mental-health rows below the two aggregate groups.

[2026-05-25] decision | Social moved to Relationships

  • Moved Social from Leisure to Relationships in the continuity wiki: areas/relationships/social.md is now the home for friendships, invitations, social rebuilding, D&D/tabletop leads when socially framed, meetups, and conversation confidence.
  • Updated Leisure to focus on hobbies, outings, play, restoration, and future excitement rather than social rebuilding.
  • Updated dashboard generation so the Relationships tab includes Social and the Leisure goal wording no longer treats social as a leisure signal.
  • Moved obvious social TickTick items from Leisure to Relationships: Explore beginner-friendly D&D options near Miami and Delve back into pick-up.
  • Patched relevant Sapho skills so future reviews and weekend planning route social/friendship ideas to Relationships/Social and hobby/fun ideas to Leisure.

2026-06-23 — Sapho storage and weekly Steward repair

  • Repaired the weekly Continuity Steward failure mode: the 2026-06-21 cron error was a script timeout after 120s, while the nested Steward run itself completed successfully. Set cron.script_timeout_seconds to 900 so no-agent Steward script jobs have enough time to finish.
  • Manually reran /opt/data/scripts/continuity_steward.py improve; it exited 0 and wrote logs/continuity-steward/20260623T205941Z-weekly-improvement.log.
  • Hardened daily pre-backup cleanup to prune transient Gradle/NPM/audio caches in addition to bounded Sapho backup generations.
  • Ran cleanup and improved /opt/data free space from about 702 MB / 99% used to about 5.8 GB / 84% used.
  • Verified backup archives with gzip -t /opt/data/backups/sapho-*.tar.gz and checked script syntax for the modified cleanup and Steward wrappers.

Career

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Career splits into Work and Business; AI-agent work can be work leverage, hobby, or business possibility depending context.
  • Current priority: Princess UX/work quality first, business-development possibility second, with the long-term business direction kept visible.
  • Active signals: senior UX growth, Princess UX direction, Blaze Creative Agency, AI leverage.
  • Open loops: clarify business direction without overloading mental health or work execution.
  • Next concrete move: use work for job/practice context and business for venture experiments.

Career is the parent area for work, job craft, professional leverage, business experiments, and future earning power.

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Build career and business power toward owning a business capable of making $1M/year, without destabilizing health or family.
  • 12-month proof: Princess UX work shows stronger senior-level execution and 2–3 visible proof assets; AI/workflow experiments produce real leverage; business direction is tested through small evidence, not fantasy comparison.
  • Current monthly target: Do strong Princess UX work first; keep AI/business experiments bounded.
  • This week's commitment: Focus on the highest-stakes Princess UX task.
  • Daily rule / next move: One concrete work/proof move per workday; no abstract career planning unless it turns into a visible artifact or decision.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: work owns job craft/execution context; princess-ux owns Princess-specific continuity; business owns parked/active business experiments; Jira remains official work execution when available.

Long-term direction

I have my own business and it's making 1 million dollars a year.

Subpages

  • work
  • business

Context

Career has two active tracks:

  1. Work / Princess UX: lead execution domain right now. Success means stronger UX quality, reliability, influence, proof assets, and AI leverage used on real work.
  2. Business: option-value domain. Success means small experiments and proof assets that reveal whether Blaze, AI-agent work, or another business path deserves real commitment.

AI-agent work can be hobby, work leverage, or business-development possibility depending on context. Do not force it into one bucket prematurely.

Reduction rule

Career work should reduce to concrete evidence:

  • a shipped or documented work artifact
  • a reusable workflow or template
  • a real AI/work leverage experiment
  • a small business test
  • a decision to park, cut, or continue

Everything else is context or backlog, not a live goal.

Subpages

  • work — job craft, Princess execution, senior UX growth.
  • business — business experiments and option value.
  • princess-ux — Princess-specific continuity and project context.

Preserved source

Business contains Blaze Creative Agency, business-development ideas, and earning-leverage experiments. Career source material is preserved on career.

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Build or revive a business path with real income potential, eventually supporting the $1M/year ambition.
  • 12-month proof: At least one small business/AI/workflow experiment creates evidence about demand, capability, offer shape, or bandwidth fit.
  • Current monthly target: Keep business as option value unless a small experiment is chosen; do not let it compete with health, family, or Princess execution.
  • This week's commitment: No standing business work unless it produces a concrete proof asset or directly helps current work.
  • Daily rule / next move: If an idea appears, capture it lightly; only schedule it when there is a clear experiment.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: This page owns business experiments; career owns the broader work/business direction; TickTick gets only chosen concrete experiments.

Work

Work contains the day-job/professional execution side of Career.

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Become a stronger senior UX/product designer whose work is clearer, faster, better communicated, and more trusted.
  • 12-month proof: 2–3 strong Princess/professional proof projects or case studies exist, and work execution feels less chaotic.
  • Current monthly target: Keep Princess UX work organized around clarify, design, review, handoff, and systemize buckets without duplicating Jira.
  • This week's commitment: Protect the current highest-stakes Princess UX task and classify active work into real next actions, blocked items, or Jira-only items.
  • Daily rule / next move: Do one concrete work execution block; avoid turning work organization itself into the day's main project unless it unblocks execution.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: Jira owns official work status; TickTick owns personal dated work blocks; this page and princess-ux own synthesis and context.

Subpages

  • princess-ux
  • princess-storybook

Career source material is preserved on career.

Context

Work is the execution side of Career. The active purpose is not to mirror Jira. It is to help Jenffry choose the right work block, clarify ambiguity, prepare handoff, and turn completed work into senior-UX proof.

Operating buckets

Use these buckets for Princess/work tasks:

  1. Clarify: stakeholder question, requirement, acceptance rule, technical constraint.
  2. Design: Figma/prototype/UX output.
  3. Review: compare implementation/stage/prototype against expected UX.
  4. Handoff: document states, edge cases, requirements, or Jira-ready notes.
  5. Systemize: convert repeated work into a template, AI workflow, component pattern, or design-system asset.

Reduction rule

Do not keep a long active work inventory here. Keep only:

  • current goal spine
  • operating buckets
  • durable context that reduces future clarification cost
  • links to Princess-specific pages

Official status belongs in Jira when available; personal dated execution belongs in TickTick.

Subpages

  • princess-ux
  • princess-storybook

Princess Storybook

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active work project page.
  • Current priority: scratch build; references only, not base.
  • Active signals: reusable foundations/components/source-backed patterns; local fonts, not Princess/Typekit runtime URLs.
  • Open loops: continue Storybook build from saved plan.
  • Next concrete move: preserve Storybook-specific decisions here.

Migrated source: plans/princess-storybook-scratch-build.md

Princess Storybook Scratch Build Plan

Decision

Build a custom Princess Storybook from scratch. Use Storybook Design System, React USWDS, Carbon, and Material Design as references/north stars, not as source bases.

Why

  • Storybook Design System is too small to be the base.
  • React USWDS is useful for accessibility/form discipline but too government-specific.
  • Carbon is useful for enterprise completeness but too IBM/product-system-specific.
  • Material Design is useful for interaction principles, motion, accessibility, responsive layout, density, and component behavior patterns, but too Google/Android-specific to own as the Princess design language.
  • Princess needs clean reusable foundations/components first; specific work-project concepts belong outside the design-system Storybook unless later generalized into approved, reusable patterns.

North-star references

  1. Storybook Design System
  • lightweight docs/story organization
  • package structure
  • Chromatic/testing pattern
  1. React USWDS
  • accessibility discipline
  • form components
  • validation states
  • API/prop documentation
  1. Carbon
  • enterprise completeness
  • state/variant depth
  • component coverage model
  • mature design-system documentation
  1. Material Design
  • interaction principles and component behavior patterns
  • motion and transition guidance
  • accessibility and responsive layout guidance
  • density, spacing, navigation, and form-behavior references
  • use as reference only; do not import Google/Android visual identity into Princess

Build strategy

Phase 0: Repo setup

  • Create a fresh repo/project, likely /opt/data/projects/princess-storybook.
  • Use React + Vite + Storybook.
  • Add TypeScript.
  • Add CSS variables for tokens first.
  • Add Style Dictionary later only if token complexity warrants it.
  • Create private GitHub repo and backup automation for the new base.

Phase 1: Foundations

Create docs/stories for:

  • color
  • typography
  • spacing
  • radius
  • elevation/shadow
  • breakpoints/responsive behavior
  • evidence labels: confirmed, inferred, proposed, placeholder

Phase 2: Core components

Build first components:

  1. Button
  2. Input/Form Field
  3. Alert/Notification

Each component must include:

  • default state
  • hover/focus/active/disabled where relevant
  • error/loading where relevant
  • accessibility notes
  • usage guidance
  • evidence label
  • Figma/source reference when available

Phase 3: Source-backed reusable patterns

Do not use the design system as a home for specific work projects. Project-specific explorations, Jira/workstream synthesis, and decision-question surfaces belong in the wiki, Figma exploration files, or separate prototypes.

Only add patterns to Storybook when they are generalized into reusable design-system guidance and can be labeled with source confidence.

Phase 4: Preview/sharing

Short-term:

  • local Storybook dev server
  • temporary tunnel only when needed

Better longer-term:

  • Chromatic for Storybook review/versioning, or
  • Cloudflare Pages / Vercel / Netlify static Storybook, with access control if needed

Phase 5: Backup

  • Private GitHub repo as canonical backup.
  • Script-only scheduled backup job, no AI/model cost.
  • Do not backup node_modules, build outputs, secrets, or generated caches.

Definition of useful v0

The first useful version exists when Jenffry can open a preview and see:

  • foundations page
  • Button component
  • Input/Form Field component
  • Alert/Notification component
  • no project-specific workstream patterns
  • clear labels showing what is Princess source/refined vs inferred/proposed/temporary scaffold
  • enough quality that the artifact helps design thinking rather than becoming admin soup

2026-05-15 backup/credit-interruption check

Follow-up after Jenffry saw a credit-limit interruption and a failed cron backup alert.

Findings:

  • Project files were intact at /opt/data/projects/princess-storybook.
  • Git state was clean: local main matched origin/main at commit d8880418ab1f.
  • GitHub private repo jenffry90-arch/princess-storybook was reachable and synced.
  • npm run build succeeded.
  • npm run build-storybook succeeded.
  • The failed backup alert was caused by script path mismatch: cron resolves relative scripts under /opt/data/scripts/, but the script had been written only to /opt/data/home/scripts/.
  • Fix applied: copied backup script to /opt/data/scripts/princess_storybook_git_backup.sh, verified it targets /opt/data/projects/princess-storybook, and updated cron job 90d5cf918b5e to continue using princess_storybook_git_backup.sh.
  • Manual backup script run completed with no changes needed, and authenticated remote check confirmed local and GitHub main both at d8880418ab1f.

Status: no work appears lost from the interruption; backup path issue is fixed. The cron job's last error field may continue to show the old failure until the scheduler records the next successful run.

2026-05-15 source-confidence labels clarified

New displayed labels:

  • Princess source — directly verified from Princess Figma, production UI, documentation, or approved product decision.
  • Princess refined — based on real Princess source, then cleaned up for Storybook reuse/documentation.
  • Work inference — built from observed Princess work patterns or travel-commerce logic, not yet official.
  • UX proposal — Sapho/UX recommendation for discussion, not current Princess behavior.
  • Temporary scaffold — structural stand-in until real Princess evidence is added.

2026-05-15 Figma MCP configured

Configured Hermes native MCP server figma using the existing local Figma token without storing the token value in config.

Configuration approach:

  • Config path: /opt/data/config.yaml
  • MCP server name: figma
  • Server package: figma-developer-mcp via npx
  • Auth source: reads /opt/data/.env, maps FIGMA_TOKEN to FIGMA_API_KEY inside the MCP subprocess
  • Secret handling: token value is not written to config, wiki, GitHub, or project files
  • Sampling disabled for this MCP server

Verification:

  • hermes mcp list shows figma enabled.
  • hermes mcp test figma connects successfully and discovers 2 tools: get_figma_data and download_figma_images.

Operational note: gateway/current sessions need restart or /reload-mcp/new session before Figma MCP tools appear in Sapho's live tool list.

2026-05-16 Typography web font rendering correction

Jenffry flagged that Storybook was not using the right typographies.

Root cause:

  • The Figma token/PDF language uses the human-readable names Domaine Display and Proxima Nova.
  • The actual Princess web CSS exposes those fonts to the browser as DomaineDisplay and proxima-nova.
  • Storybook had the correct source style values, but the browser font-family names were wrong, so it could fall back to Georgia/system sans.

Completed:

  • Updated scripts/generate-tokens.mjs so --pc-font-family-display generates as "DomaineDisplay", "Domaine Display", Georgia, "Times New Roman", serif.
  • Updated scripts/generate-tokens.mjs so --pc-font-family-sans generates as "proxima-nova", "Proxima Nova", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, ....
  • Added @import url('https://use.typekit.net/mvj6ajr.css') for Princess proxima-nova loading.
  • Added @font-face rules for DomaineDisplay regular and bold using the public Princess font asset URLs.
  • Ran npm run tokens:build so src/styles/tokens.css reflects the corrected generated family names.
  • Verified Typekit CSS and Domaine font asset URLs return HTTP 200.
  • Verified npm run build and npm run build-storybook both pass.
  • Verified Foundations/Typography exists in the local Storybook index.
  • Committed and pushed to GitHub at 520292c with message Correct Princess typography font loading.

Jenffry clarified the padding issue was broader: the Storybook display layer itself should use the Princess design system to present foundations/components, rather than looking like thin 1px wrappers around content.

Completed:

  • Added dedicated Storybook visualization variables in src/index.css: --pc-viz-page-padding, --pc-viz-card-padding, --pc-viz-card-gap, --pc-viz-card-radius, and --pc-viz-preview-padding.
  • Reworked the shared display layer so foundation/component cards, source cards, preview panels, text/detail bodies, and list containers use Princess spacing tokens consistently.

2026-05-16 Semantic component tokens added

Jenffry asked whether Storybook was tokenized so a token change would reflect across the design system, then asked Sapho to make that true.

Completed:

  • Added src/styles/semantic-tokens.css and imported it after generated Figma foundation tokens in src/index.css.
  • Created semantic token groups for:
  • Storybook visualization: --pc-viz-*
  • shared display cards: --pc-card-*
  • buttons: --pc-button-*
  • form fields: --pc-field-*
  • alerts/notifications: --pc-alert-*
  • evidence/source-confidence UI: --pc-evidence-*
  • Refactored Button.css, FormField.css, Alert.css, and Evidence.css to consume semantic tokens instead of scattering source values through component CSS.
  • Kept exact source-backed values where needed, but promoted them into semantic tokens so future changes happen from one place.

Standing rule for all future components:

  1. New components must define semantic component tokens before or alongside visual CSS.
  2. Component CSS should consume semantic tokens, not raw colors/spacing/radius/elevation values, except for temporary scaffolds that are explicitly labeled and converted before source-backed promotion.
  3. Exact Figma/source values are allowed, but they belong in the semantic token layer, not scattered through component rules.
  4. Reusable Storybook display surfaces must keep using --pc-viz-* / --pc-card-* tokens so the Storybook itself remains a design-system surface.
  5. During component review, tokenization is part of done: if changing a token would not update the component globally, the component is not fully integrated.
  • Verified npm run build and npm run build-storybook pass.
  • Committed and pushed 7a86baa with message Add semantic component tokens.
Princess UX
areas/career/work/princess-ux.md
degree 18

Status: Active work project context. Current priority: keep Princess UX/UI context available without mixing Jira with personal TickTick clutter. Active signals: UX/UI work clusters, Princess planning, AI/workflow support. Open loops: preserve Princess-specific

CareerCurrent StrategyOpen Loops

Princess UX

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active work project context.
  • Current priority: keep Princess UX/UI context available without mixing Jira with personal TickTick clutter.
  • Active signals: UX/UI work clusters, Princess planning, AI/workflow support.
  • Open loops: preserve Princess-specific context and avoid duplicate task systems.
  • Next concrete move: use this page for Princess UX continuity.

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Use Princess UX work to become trusted with more senior ambiguity and produce stronger design decisions, systems, prototypes, handoffs, and proof assets.
  • 12-month proof: 2–3 shipped or documented Princess projects/case studies demonstrate work quality, execution, influence, visible proof, and AI/work leverage.
  • Current monthly target: Keep current Princess work legible enough to execute: clarify before designing, review committed items, finish small batches, and consolidate official status in Jira.
  • This week's commitment: Focus on the highest-stakes active Princess task; use AI/workflow only when it improves that real task.
  • Daily rule / next move: Pick one Princess item and classify it as clarify, design, review, handoff, or systemize before working.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: Jira/Figma own official project artifacts; TickTick owns personal execution blocks; this page owns durable context, questions, and synthesis.

Active context rule

Use this page as a durable context/evidence file, not as a live task list. The live goal is the Goal Spine above. When this page gets too long, preserve older project detail under raw/reference and keep only current questions, decisions, and reusable patterns here.

Migrated source: concepts/princess-ux-work.md

Princess UX/UI Work

This page is the planning/context layer for Jenffry's Princess UX/UI work. Jira should remain the work execution/source-of-truth board once organized; TickTick should hold only concrete dated personal execution blocks; Sapho should help with triage, clarification, synthesis, and handoff preparation.

Current purpose

Help Jenffry keep Princess UX/UI work organized enough to execute calmly, communicate clearly, and use the work for senior UX growth rather than just task survival.

12-month senior UX success buckets

Jenffry endorsed five buckets as the Princess UX/career goal frame:

  • Work quality: cleaner flows, better systems thinking, stronger prototypes, better edge cases, stronger design decisions.
  • Execution: faster, more reliable, better at finishing, handoff, and ambiguity reduction.
  • Influence: more trust from PMs/devs/stakeholders and more leadership in conversations.
  • Portfolio / visible proof: 2–3 strong shipped or documented projects/case studies.
  • AI/work leverage: AI used for research, synthesis, design review, Jira clarity, and documentation without creating admin soup.

Current Princess work clusters from TickTick

Immediate / this week

  • Payment / booking failure handling — write team clarification questions before design starts. Focus: failure matrix, Manage Booking handoff, confirmation modal behavior, partial failures, and no-charge booking-creation failure.
  • FCC Balance / pricing transparency — get Sweeta context and capture Jenffry's info dump. Core issue: logged-in guest with FCC saw unclear price/balance/deposit behavior; engineering says FCC deduction was in Polar PMTS response but visible only via header dropdown.
  • Flexpay stage review — compare implementation against expected UX; capture bugs, gaps, and dev feedback.
  • Nearest Airport review — review at the 4 PM meeting and log feedback/follow-up actions afterward.
  • Confirmation Page updates — remove phone number, check whether removing cancellation link is possible, and surface Manage Booking link for all three booking scenarios.
  • Enriched Ports — revise icon direction and create shareable prototype for Jim.
  • Flexpay mobile summary — build mobile summary pages and add them to ready-for-dev file.
  • Flexpay guest info for other markets — complete before mid-May.

Organization / system work

  • Organize Jira — reflect all active Princess projects in Jira and make it the execution/source-of-truth board.
  • Mobile right rail behavior — define or confirm UX interaction spec: expansion, extent, open/close actions, and scroll behavior.

Career leverage / AI workflow

  • UX + AI workflow — gather practical ideas, turn them into repeatable workflows, test one on a real Princess task, and capture what worked.
  • Storybook/component states — define one Princess component's useful states.
  • Figma foundations / token extraction — audit whether color, type, spacing, radius, and component variables are ready for Figma → tokens → React prototype pipeline.
  • Research → design implications → prototype requirements template — create a reusable transformation template.
  • Princess-specific AI design guidelines — define rules for AI-assisted Princess design/prototype changes.

Operating structure

Use three layers:

  1. Jira — official work board and project status once organized.
  2. TickTick 💼Career list — Jenffry's personal dated execution blocks for work, Princess UX/UI, and business/career tasks.
  3. Sapho/wiki — context, prioritization, question lists, synthesis, templates, and recurring review signal.

2026-05-18 taxonomy update: the TickTick project with ID 69ffb6278f0818deaa8b8917 is now 💼Career. Princess UX/UI remains a work subtrack inside Career rather than its own top-level life-area list.

2026-05-09 update: Created dedicated TickTick list 💼Career (69ffb6278f0818deaa8b8917) and moved 20 active Princess-related tasks into it from 💼Career, including the former Princess-column items and Princess-specific UX + AI workflow items.

Current triage pattern

For each Princess item, Sapho should classify it as one of:

  • Clarify — missing decision, stakeholder question, technical constraint, or acceptance rule.
  • Design — actual Figma/prototype/UX output needed.
  • Review — compare stage/prototype/implementation against expected UX.
  • Handoff — document requirements, states, edge cases, or Jira-ready notes.
  • Systemize — convert repeated work into template, AI workflow, component state model, or design-system asset.

First recommended work sequence

  1. Clarify before designing: Payment booking failure + FCC Balance need questions/context first.
  2. Review committed items: Flexpay stage + Nearest Airport reviews should generate concrete Jira follow-ups.
  3. Finish small Confirmation Page batch: phone number, cancellation link feasibility, Manage Booking link.
  4. Consolidate in Jira: after the immediate clusters are understood, make Jira reflect the real project map.
  5. Use one real Princess task to test UX + AI workflow: do not keep AI workflow abstract.

Storybook design-system project progress

2026-05-14 research direction: Jenffry wants to explore creating a Storybook-based Princess Cruises design system because the current design-side system is incomplete. The goal is not merely a pretty component catalog; the useful version would become a higher-level coded/design-system reference that improves Princess work, senior UX leverage, AI-assisted design workflows, documentation, and handoff quality.

Recommended pilot scope

Start with a small Storybook pilot rather than the whole system:

  1. Minimal foundations: color, typography, spacing, radius.
  2. First components: Button, Input/Form Field, Alert/Notification.
  3. Then one real Princess pattern: Payment/booking failure alert + form state, mobile right rail, or payment summary.
  4. Include Figma references, states/variants, accessibility notes, and usage guidance.

Reusing an existing Storybook/design system

Initial feasibility assessment: reusing an existing open-source Storybook/design-system repo as a scaffold is possible and may be valuable, but only as a structural accelerator, not as a visual/UX source of truth. Candidate reference/scaffold sources researched include Storybook's own design-system repo, IBM Carbon, and React USWDS. These show mature patterns for component docs, tokens, Storybook stories, testing, and package structure.

Best use of an existing system:

  • copy/learn the repository structure, documentation pattern, story organization, testing setup, and token pipeline;
  • replace visual identity with Princess tokens and CSS values;
  • adapt only generic components whose behavior fits Princess needs;
  • treat complex Princess booking/payment patterns as custom work, not copied components.

Main caution: simply skinning a full external component library can create false completeness. It may look complete while preserving alien behavior, naming, accessibility assumptions, spacing logic, and interaction models. The project should only use an existing Storybook as scaffolding if it improves speed without masking missing Princess product decisions.

2026-05-14 scaffold comparison: three practical options

Comparison criteria for a Princess Storybook pilot: speed to useful prototype, fit for incomplete design foundations, ability to express Princess tokens/CSS, low false-completeness risk, accessibility/documentation quality, maintainability for a non-engineer-led UX effort, and usefulness as senior UX/career proof.

Option 1: Storybook Design System repo

Source: storybookjs/design-system.

Strengths:

  • Strong Storybook-native example from the Storybook/Chromatic ecosystem.
  • Includes real component stories, docs, package/distribution pattern, Emotion styling, React components, CI, and Chromatic usage.
  • Leaner than large enterprise systems, so easier to study as a scaffold.
  • MIT license and mature enough to be a usable reference.

Risks:

  • Visual/interaction choices are Storybook-branded, not Princess-branded.
  • It is not a generic starter; it is a real product design system for Storybook web properties.
  • Uses assumptions that may not match Princess implementation stack or Figma structure.

Best Princess use:

  • Use as structural reference: Storybook config, component/story organization, docs style, package setup, Chromatic/testing pattern.
  • Do not use as the actual Princess component source.

Assessment: best reference scaffold if the goal is a serious but still manageable coded design-system prototype.

Option 2: React USWDS

Source: trussworks/react-uswds.

Strengths:

  • Active React + TypeScript component library based on USWDS 3.0.
  • Good example of turning an external design standard into reusable React components with Storybook documentation.
  • Strong accessibility and government/public-service design-system influence.
  • Useful model for component APIs, prop documentation, variants, and standards alignment.

Risks:

  • Its design language is federal/government, not cruise/travel/ecommerce.
  • It depends on USWDS CSS/version alignment; this is an extra inherited constraint Princess does not need.
  • Reskinning it would likely preserve alien naming, markup, and behavior assumptions.

Best Princess use:

  • Use as reference for accessibility, component coverage, docs discipline, and how to implement a design standard in React.
  • Do not use as the base repo unless the goal is only to study structure.

Assessment: best reference for accessibility/documentation rigor, but weaker as a direct scaffold.

Option 3: Minimal Storybook + tokens starter

Source pattern: create a fresh React + Vite + Storybook project, then add a token pipeline and selected addons/tools. Relevant references include official Storybook React Vite docs, Style Dictionary for token builds, and small starter/addon repos.

Strengths:

  • Lowest false-completeness risk because every component added must be intentional.
  • Best fit for incomplete Princess design foundations: we can represent known, inferred, and proposed tokens separately.
  • Easier to align with Princess-specific components instead of inherited generic library assumptions.
  • Cleanest path for a personal/internal/portfolio pilot.

Risks:

  • Slower initial setup than cloning a mature repo.
  • Requires deciding folder conventions, token naming, docs structure, and first components.
  • Some starter repos are inactive or too small, so this should be assembled from current official tooling rather than copied wholesale.

Best Princess use:

  • Start fresh with React + Vite + Storybook.
  • Add Style Dictionary or plain CSS variables for tokens.
  • Copy only proven documentation/story patterns from Storybook Design System, React USWDS, and Carbon.
  • Build only the first 3 foundations/components plus one real Princess pattern.

Assessment: best actual starting method for Princess because it maximizes control and minimizes inherited design debt.

Working recommendation

Use Option 3 as the actual build path, with Option 1 and Option 2 as references:

  1. Create a clean minimal Storybook + React + Vite project.
  2. Add a lightweight token layer: CSS variables first, Style Dictionary later if useful.
  3. Borrow structure/docs/testing ideas from Storybook Design System.
  4. Borrow accessibility and component-state rigor from React USWDS.
  5. Keep Carbon as a reference for mature enterprise completeness, not as a base.
  6. Build a pilot around Button, Form Field/Input, Alert, and one real Princess booking/payment pattern.

Decision status: revised after Jenffry's speed concern. A clone-and-prune strategy is acceptable if treated as an audit-and-replacement workflow, not as a reskin. The test is whether replacing/correcting/deleting inherited pieces is faster than clean-building while still preserving Princess-specific judgment.

2026-05-14 clone-and-prune start

Jenffry chose to proceed with the Storybook Design System repo using a clone-and-prune strategy.

Local setup completed:

  • Cloned https://github.com/storybookjs/design-system.git into /opt/data/projects/princess-storybook-design-system.
  • Created working branch princess-clone-and-prune-spike.
  • Installed dependencies using npx --yes yarn@1.22.22 install --frozen-lockfile because global yarn is not installed and corepack enable cannot write symlinks to /usr/bin in this environment.
  • Verified static Storybook build with npx yarn@1.22.22 build-storybook; output generated at /opt/data/projects/princess-storybook-design-system/storybook-static.
  • Started local dev server at http://localhost:6006/ and verified HTTP 200 response.
  • Added local working note: /opt/data/projects/princess-storybook-design-system/PRINCESS_CLONE_AND_PRUNE.md.

Initial technical observations:

  • Repo uses Storybook 7.6.6, React 17, Emotion/Storybook theming, Webpack 5, Chromatic, and 46 story files.
  • Package license is stated as MIT in package.json; no separate LICENSE file was present in the clone, so attribution/license handling should be added before any publishable or portfolio-facing output.
  • Build warnings are mainly large-bundle warnings and outdated Browserslist data; not blockers for the pilot.

Clone-and-prune operating rule:

  • For inherited components, assign: keep, modify, replace, delete, or defer.
  • For Princess components/patterns, label evidence as: confirmed, adapted, inferred, proposed, or placeholder.

Next practical step: final decision made after reviewing Storybook Design System and considering the alternatives. Jenffry chose to build a custom Princess Storybook from scratch, using Storybook Design System, React USWDS, and Carbon as references/north stars rather than bases.

Decision rationale:

  • Storybook Design System is too small to serve as a useful base.
  • React USWDS and Carbon are useful references but carry too much external product/design philosophy to own directly.
  • The custom build should borrow the best patterns while keeping Princess product judgment, booking/payment patterns, naming, and token structure intentional.

Immediate implementation direction:

  1. Create a fresh Princess Storybook project instead of pruning the clone.
  2. Use React + Vite + Storybook as the likely base.
  3. Start with CSS variables/design tokens, then add Style Dictionary only if token complexity justifies it.
  4. Build the pilot around foundations, Button, Input/Form Field, Alert/Notification, and one Princess booking/payment failure pattern.
  5. Keep the three systems as active references:
  • Storybook Design System: docs/story structure and lightweight package organization.
  • React USWDS: accessibility, form rigor, and component API discipline.
  • Carbon: enterprise-level completeness and component/state coverage.

Operational cleanup completed:

  • Stopped the temporary Cloudflare public tunnel for the old clone.
  • Stopped the old Storybook dev server.
  • Paused the old clone backup job Princess Storybook GitHub backup (f1ea41a336d3) so it does not continue backing up the wrong base.

Access/backup update:

  • VPS public IP checked as 204.168.200.125.
  • Dev server process is running via npx yarn@1.22.22 storybook -- --host 0.0.0.0 at http://localhost:6006/ on the VPS.
  • Recommended safe viewing method from Jenffry's own computer is SSH local port forwarding rather than exposing Storybook publicly: ssh -L 6006:localhost:6006 <vps-user>@204.168.200.125, then open http://localhost:6006 locally.
  • Installed cloudflared locally at /opt/data/home/.local/bin/cloudflared and created a temporary public Cloudflare quick tunnel for sharing: https://asbestos-mil-fog-junior.trycloudflare.com; verified HTTP 200 response. This URL is public/unauthenticated and temporary; use only for low-risk sharing until a named/authenticated tunnel or static preview deployment is configured.
  • Created private GitHub backup repo: jenffry90-arch/princess-storybook-design-system.
  • Renamed original Storybook remote to upstream; set private repo as origin.
  • Pushed branch princess-clone-and-prune-spike with commit Start Princess clone-and-prune spike.
  • Created scheduled backup job Princess Storybook GitHub backup (f1ea41a336d3) every 30 minutes using /opt/data/home/scripts/princess_storybook_git_backup.sh; it stays silent when there are no changes and pushes committed changes when present.

Open Storybook questions

  • Is this a personal prototype, internal Princess work asset, or portfolio/career proof?
  • What stack should it use: React + Vite/Next, plain CSS variables, Tailwind, or the company's actual implementation stack?
  • Can Jenffry access the relevant Princess Figma foundations and component libraries?
  • Should the first build be scaffolded from an existing open-source design system or started clean with selected patterns copied manually?
  • Which first Princess component/pattern will prove value fastest?

Enriched Ports + Port Count Enhancement context

2026-05-15 synthesis from Jenffry: the Enriched Ports work and Port Count Enhancement work overlap and were combined.

Original Enriched Ports problem

The initial goal was to highlight itineraries that include scenic cruising.

During review, the team discovered that not all scenic cruising is the same:

  1. Scenic cruising / viewing experience
  • The ship passes by or through a meaningful location.
  • The guest experience is primarily looking/viewing.
  • No distinct onboard activity is attached.
  • This appears to be the majority of scenic cruising cases.
  1. Enriched cruising
  • A scenic cruising moment that has an activity attached to the viewing experience.
  • The activity makes it more than passive viewing.
  • This became the working definition for “enriched cruising.”

Design implication: scenic cruising was split into two categories because guest value and expectation differ:

  • scenic cruising = viewing-only experience;
  • enriched cruising = viewing experience plus attached activity.

Planned surfaces:

  • Search results/search card.
  • Itinerary page/itinerary timeline.

Port Count Enhancement problem

The goal is to portray ports/days in a way that better matches how guests think about an itinerary.

Key shift: stop describing only “ports”; instead describe the trip in day categories that add up or at least explain the itinerary more intuitively.

Working counting concepts:

  • Total itinerary days — the full length of the itinerary.
  • Destination days — count days with destinations, including embarkation/departure port and disembarkation/arrival port.
  • Sea days — ordinary cruising-at-sea days.
  • Scenic cruising days — viewing-focused scenic cruising days.
  • Enriched cruising days — scenic cruising days with an attached activity.
  • Land days — cruise-tour land portion days, counted separately from cruise days when relevant.

Embark/disembark counting rule:

  • If embarkation and disembarkation ports are different, count both as destination days.
  • If they are the same round-trip port, count them as one destination because they are the same port.

Key unresolved counting issue

Round trips create a math/communication problem:

  • Destination days + sea days + scenic/enriched cruising days may not equal total itinerary days if the same round-trip embark/disembark port is counted once rather than twice.
  • This needs either a counting rule, label, or explanatory copy so the numbers do not feel inconsistent or wrong.

Possible framing to explore:

  • Separate “unique destinations” from “destination days.”
  • Or count embarkation/disembarkation as itinerary days but show the same port once in the destination list.
  • Or use “ports visited” for unique places and “itinerary day breakdown” for day math.

Current design task

Organize the combined task around three questions:

  1. Taxonomy: What are the categories? Destination day, sea day, scenic cruising day, enriched cruising day, land day.
  2. Counting logic: What exactly gets counted in each category, especially embark/disembark and round trips?
  3. Display logic: How should search cards and itinerary pages show the value without confusing the guest?

Open questions for Jenffry

  • For Enriched Ports / Port Count Enhancement: should “destination days” mean unique destinations, calendar days with destination activity, or both as separate labels?
  • For round trips, should the same embark/disembark port be counted once for unique destination count but twice in the day breakdown?
  • Should scenic cruising and enriched cruising be shown as separate day categories, or should enriched cruising appear as a badge/detail inside scenic cruising?
  • What is the official Jira structure today: epics, tickets, loose board, or something else?
  • Which Princess projects are politically/highest-stakes this week?
  • Who are the key stakeholders for each active cluster: Jim, Sweeta, Washington/Guilherdes, engineers, PMs?
  • What does “organized” mean for Princess work: cleaner Jira, weekly priority map, Figma file cleanup, meeting prep, handoff templates, or all of these?
  • What recurring cadence would help without adding admin soup: Monday planning, end-of-day work capture, Friday work review, or only on-demand?

Finances

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Cash-flow is the active finance front; investing remains visible but secondary.
  • Current priority: prevent budget drift and credit-card interest while building toward the long-term direction: save for the future, support family, and afford the desired lifestyle.
  • Active signals: one-income household, weekly operating number, debt/loan obligations, emergency fund/401k context.
  • Open loops: verify forward assumptions and keep finance review short.
  • Next concrete move: use cash-flow for operational budget context and investing for longer-term direction.

Finances is split between operational cash-flow and longer-term investing.

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Regular income supports family, future saving, and Jenffry's desired lifestyle without credit-card-interest pressure.
  • 12-month proof: The household can run on ordinary income with about $400/month comfortable excess, clean card payoff behavior, and no dependence on tax refunds or one-off cash.
  • Current monthly target: Keep June inside budget and pay cards correctly.
  • This week's commitment: Stay within weekly spending pace and catch overages early.
  • Daily rule / next move: Avoid optional spending unless it has been deliberately chosen inside the weekly guardrail.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: SimpleFIN/dashboard cockpit owns live spending; this page owns finance goal and budget interpretation; cash-flow holds operational budget details; investing stays secondary.

Long-term direction

I am able with my income to save money for the future, support my family and provide myself the lifestyle that I want.

June 2026 budget draft

  • Assumption: June paychecks follow the current biweekly cadence and land around 2026-06-10 and 2026-06-24.
  • Expected net pay: about $2,883 each, or about $5,765.95 total for June.
  • Starting liquid cash at 2026-05-31: checking $4,246.96 + savings $922.99 = $5,169.95.
  • Current credit-card balances after 2026-06-01 refresh: Amazon $1,349.65, Quicksilver 0736 $731.04, Quicksilver 1208 $404.09, Savor $806.80, Chase $362.96; total $3,654.54.
  • Known statement cycles supplied by Jenffry: Savor 2026-04-03 to 2026-05-02; Quicksilver 0736 2026-04-03 to 2026-05-02; Amazon next closing date 2026-06-05.
  • Inferred missing cycles/dates for June planning: Savor and Quicksilver 0736 likely close next on 2026-06-02 and are due around 2026-06-27; Quicksilver 1208 likely closes around 2026-05-17 and is due around 2026-06-11; Chase likely closes around 2026-06-01 and is due around 2026-06-26; Amazon's 2026-06-05 close likely supports a July due date, while the June due amount comes from the prior close around 2026-05-05.
  • Estimated June credit-card due portion using the supplied/observed cycle dates: Amazon $1,052.57, Quicksilver 0736 $731.04, Quicksilver 1208 $216.09, Savor $806.80, Chase $362.96; estimated total $3,169.46.
  • Tight discretionary caps for June: shopping/household $100, leisure $75, dining/social $125.
  • Keep travel and misc at $0 unless something truly unavoidable comes up.
  • Card note: this is still an inference from transactions/balances, not statement data. Exact June card dues and statement closing dates require the actual statements or card portal due/current-due fields.

Migrated source: concepts/finances.md

Finances

This page tracks Jenffry's finance and household-budget context.

Current financial context

  • Household is on one income.
  • Current regular income is $2,882.97 biweekly net.
  • Money is tight because fixed debt/loan obligations consume most regular income.
  • Budget planning must exclude tax refunds, interest, large deposits, reimbursements, transfers, and other one-off income.
  • April 2026 looked temporarily better because of IRS tax refund/credit entries, but those are not normal income.
  • Credit card interest/fees appeared in April and May; the budget should prevent interest from restarting.
  • Emergency/long-term savings context from 2026-05-11: Jenffry reported about $36k emergency fund and over $500k in 401k. The issue is monthly comfort/cashflow, not total net-worth panic.

Baseline income assumption

Use regular take-home pay only for everyday planning.

  • Biweekly net income: $2,882.97
  • Annualized net income: $74,957.22
  • Monthly average: $6,246.44
  • Weekly average: $1,441.49
  • Do not build everyday budget around tax refunds, reimbursements, transfers, interest, cash deposits, or one-off income.

Observed current-paycheck pattern in 2026 includes Princess Cruise deposits around the stated biweekly income. April 2026 IRS TREAS 310 entries of $8,501 and $1,495 are treated as one-off income and excluded from the budget baseline.

Structural constraint

Debt/loan payments dominate the current budget.

Current assumed debt/loan obligations:

  • JPMorgan Chase: $3,496.12/month
  • TD Auto Finance reserve: $466.86/month
  • Honda: likely paid off or at least uncertain after the large $12,259.70 payment on 2026-04-07; confirm whether any $298.31 Honda payment still continues.

JPMorgan + TD equals $3,962.98/month, leaving about $2,283.46/month for all non-debt household spending.

Realistic monthly budget — income-based

This is the working budget from the 2026-05-14 transaction review, with the dashboard budget adjusted on 2026-05-25 to make leisure more realistic while keeping the one-income constraint intact.

  • JPMorgan loan/debt: $3,496.12
  • TD Auto Finance reserve: $466.86

  • Leisure: $100.00
  • Personal care: $50.00
  • Health / wellness / medical: $75.00

  • Misc / buffer: $0.00

Total budgeted: $6,246.42 against $6,246.44 monthly average income.

Mentat assessment: this budget is realistic because it respects the actual one-income constraint, but it is not comfortable. It has no meaningful buffer unless a debt payment disappears, income increases, or categories are cut further.

Weekly operating budget equivalent

JPMorgan loan/debt, TD Auto Finance reserve, and Bills & utilities are monthly obligations/reserves, not weekly spending categories. The weekly dashboard view is for controllable operating-spending pace only.

  • Fees / interest: $0.00
  • Misc / buffer: $0.00

Weekly operating budget: about $375.94/week after monthly JPMorgan, TD, and Bills & utilities obligations/reserves.

May 2026 status through 2026-05-14

From the 2026-05-17 SimpleFIN refresh. Live transaction data currently runs through 2026-05-14, so spending after that date may not yet be reflected.

  • Actual May budget spending so far: $5,085.04
  • Non-debt May spending so far: $1,588.92
  • Remaining against monthly average income: $1,161.40
  • If reserving TD Auto Finance for later in May: real usable remaining amount is about $694.54
  • Practical remaining daily pace after TD reserve: about $40.86/day for the remaining 17 days after 2026-05-14, or about $285.99 for a 7-day stretch.

Category status:

  • JPMorgan loan/debt: $3,496.12 spent; $0 remaining
  • TD Auto Finance reserve: $0 spent; $466.86 should stay reserved
  • Bills & utilities: $368.15 spent; $281.85 remaining
  • Groceries: $270.25 spent; $379.75 remaining
  • Auto: gas/tolls/parking: $192.26 spent; $157.74 remaining
  • Dining & social: $42.50 spent; $107.50 remaining
  • Shopping / household: $188.92 spent; over by $13.92
  • Leisure: $124.15 spent; over by $74.15
  • Software / subscriptions: $65.83 spent; over by $15.83
  • Travel: $209.80 spent; over by $209.80
  • Fees / interest: $97.07 spent; over by $97.07

Week of 2026-05-08 through 2026-05-14:

  • Week spending: $488.05
  • Non-debt weekly budget: about $525.54
  • Remaining against weekly non-debt budget: about $37.49
  • Problem areas: leisure, auto, shopping, and software were over their weekly caps; bills are tracked monthly, while groceries and dining/social were controlled.

Budget rules

  1. Treat $525/week as the practical non-debt operating budget.
  2. For the rest of May 2026, treat about $720 as usable after reserving TD Auto Finance.
  3. No more travel spending in May unless unavoidable.
  4. No more leisure spending in May if avoidable unless it is intentional and inside the updated $100/month leisure cap.
  5. Shopping only for true household necessities until the month resets.
  6. Fees/interest are a leak to fix, not a normal category.
  7. Dining/social is allowed but modest; lunch with friends is acceptable if kept around $15–20.
  8. If Honda still continues as a monthly payment, revise the budget immediately because there is currently no room for it.
  9. Extra/irregular income order: emergency buffer, planned annual expenses, debt/large obligations, then fun.
  10. Do not restart automatic vault/savings transfers unless the month is already cash-positive.

12-month finance direction

Jenffry wants to move closer to financial freedom, but the current issue is monthly comfort and cashflow. The desired 12-month marker remains a comfortable budget with about $400/month excess after ordinary expenses, including room for enjoyment money.

Current 12-month success markers:

  • Emergency fund remains intact and not used for ordinary lifestyle leakage.
  • Credit-card interest does not restart.
  • Monthly budget works on regular income, not tax refunds, reimbursements, transfers, or one-off income.
  • There is roughly $400/month excess after normal expenses.
  • The budget includes some enjoyment money rather than being almost entirely survival money.
  • Weekly finance review, spending caps, monthly budget reset, and eventual automatic savings all support the system.

Transaction API integration

As of 2026-05-14, SimpleFIN Bridge is connected for read-only transaction access.

  • Secret storage: SIMPLEFIN_ACCESS_URL in /opt/data/.env.
  • Local importer: /opt/data/scripts/simplefin_import.py.
  • Current output folder: /opt/data/finance/simplefin/.
  • 2026-05-17 refresh succeeded: 15 accounts and 556 transactions returned; SimpleFIN may include a non-fatal range-cap warning.
  • Use the importer before finance reviews to refresh accounts.csv and transactions.csv.
  • Continuation task created in TickTick 💰Finances: Continue SimpleFIN transaction API setup, due 2026-05-18 at 9:00 AM America/New_York.

Security rule: do not save or print the SimpleFIN access URL in the wiki, memory, or final replies. Treat it like a financial-data credential even though access is read-only.

Forward assumptions to verify

Previous assumptions from earlier finance cleanup:

  • Claude subscription cancelled.
  • DoorDash DashPass cancelled.
  • Rocket Money cancelled.
  • Spotify cancelled.
  • Honda payment no longer active.
  • Prime is annual, not monthly.

The most important unresolved verification is whether Honda truly stopped after the April 2026 payoff-sized payment.
