Mental HealthPhysical HealthSapho Operating SystemCurrent Strategy

Health

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Mental health is the leading life priority; physical health is now a completed support pipeline in normal review/calibration mode.
  • Current priority: dashboard Health cockpit is split into Mental health and Physical health.
  • Active signals: sleep, capacity, rumination/observer-mode, future-excitement/presence, Hevy workout rhythm, pickleball/activity mentions, weight/measurements, strength anchors, medical/lab follow-up.
  • Open loops: collect better mental-health matching data without feeding overanalysis; keep physical-health measurement/strength updates in normal weekly/monthly review rather than active system-build mode.
  • Next concrete move: keep mental-health tracking light and subjective, while physical-health progress can use Hevy plus monthly measurements.

Health is the home base for body, mind, emotional capacity, gym, sleep, medical follow-up, and stabilizing practices.

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Build a stable body-and-mind system where mental health leads and physical health supports energy, confidence, and presence.
  • 12-month proof: Mental-health checking is lower and more manageable; physical-health measurements, strength, cardio, and medical follow-through are moving in the right direction.
  • Current monthly target: Keep mental-health practice simple and keep workouts, measurements, and medical follow-ups moving.
  • This week's commitment: Do the health anchors: workouts, ENT reschedule call, meal-prep refinement, and sleep support without turning tracking into admin clutter.
  • Daily rule / next move: Choose one health action that stabilizes the day; do not turn tracking into another checking loop.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: mental-health owns mental-health practice and TickTick Habit signals; physical-health owns Hevy-backed body/strength/cardio goals; the dashboard displays, not decides.

Subpages

  • mental-health
  • physical-health

Migration note

The prior health overview and routine page were merged here and into the child pages.

Migrated source: concepts/health.md

Health

This is the top-level life-area page for Health.

Subcategories

1. Mental health

Use mental-health for the mental-health goal layer: observer mode, acceptance/commitment work, therapy/Sapho support, and signs of grounded presence.

2. Physical health

Use physical-health for Hevy-backed body composition, strength, cardio, and medical-loop targets.

Use physical-health for practical routines: sleep, food, gym preparation, grooming, energy, body maintenance, and medical follow-through.

Working rule

Health is one life area with two subcategories: mental health and physical health. Physical health includes the practical body-system routines that make better weeks easier.

Hevy API data review — 2026-05-24
areas/health/hevy-api-data-review-2026-05-24.md

Review what Sapho can retrieve from Jenffry's Hevy Pro API and how to use it for dashboards, weekly reviews, monthly reviews, and gym-planning feedback without adding TickTick clutter. Docs: https://api.hevyapp.com/docs/ API version in docs: 0.0.1 Status: earl

No resolved wikilinks.

Hevy API data review — 2026-05-24

Purpose

Review what Sapho can retrieve from Jenffry's Hevy Pro API and how to use it for dashboards, weekly reviews, monthly reviews, and gym-planning feedback without adding TickTick clutter.

API source

  • Docs: https://api.hevyapp.com/docs/
  • API version in docs: 0.0.1
  • Status: early public API for Hevy Pro users; Hevy warns the API may change.
  • Auth pattern: api-key header. Do not print the key.
  • Local helper: /opt/data/scripts/hevy_api.py

Live account inventory checked

  • Workouts: 149 total, fetched across 15 pages.
  • Routines: 13 total, fetched across 2 pages.
  • Routine folders: 1 total.
  • Exercise templates: 436 total, fetched across 44 pages.
  • Body measurement entries: 3 total.
  • Workout events since 2026-01-01: available, 3 pages.

Endpoint categories available

User info

  • GET /v1/user/info
  • Data shape: user id, name, profile URL.
  • Use: identity check only. Low training value.

Workouts

  • GET /v1/workouts
  • GET /v1/workouts/count
  • GET /v1/workouts/events?since=...
  • GET /v1/workouts/{workoutId}
  • POST /v1/workouts
  • PUT /v1/workouts/{workoutId}

Observed workout fields:

  • id
  • title
  • description
  • routine_id
  • start_time
  • end_time
  • created_at
  • updated_at
  • exercises

Observed exercise-in-workout fields:

  • exercise_template_id
  • title
  • index
  • notes
  • sets
  • superset_id

Observed set fields:

  • index
  • type
  • weight_kg
  • reps
  • distance_meters
  • duration_seconds
  • rpe
  • custom_metric

Observed set metric coverage across current workout history:

  • Reps: 2,127 set values.
  • Weight: 2,026 set values.
  • Distance: 7 set values.
  • Duration: 3 set values.
  • Set types: normal 1,253; failure 443; dropset 296; warmup 142.

Best uses:

  • Weekly consistency: days trained, last workout, missed routine days.
  • Routine coverage: Push/Pull/Lower/Posture/Cardio balance.
  • Set volume by exercise and by routine.
  • Strength progression by exercise: weight, reps, estimated 1RM, best set, volume.
  • Cardio/treadmill progression when distance and duration are logged.
  • Workout duration and training density.
  • Historical trend charts.

Caution:

  • /v1/workouts pageSize max is 10.
  • Exercise titles can vary; use exercise_template_id for reliable matching.
  • Warmups should usually be excluded from strength-progress calculations.
  • Dropsets and failure sets should be tracked but not treated the same as normal working sets in every metric.

Workout events

  • GET /v1/workouts/events?since=...
  • Returns updates and deletes, newest to oldest.
  • Use: maintain a local cache without re-fetching all workouts every time.

Best use:

  • Build a local Hevy cache under /opt/data and update it incrementally before dashboards/check-ins.
  • Detect deleted or edited workouts so the dashboard does not silently drift.

Exercise history

  • GET /v1/exercise_history/{exerciseTemplateId}
  • Optional filters: start_date, end_date.

Observed history entry fields:

  • exercise_template_id
  • workout_id
  • workout_title
  • workout_start_time
  • workout_end_time
  • set_type
  • weight_kg
  • reps
  • distance_meters
  • duration_seconds
  • rpe
  • custom_metric

Observed top-used exercise histories are available when using template IDs found in actual workouts. The first generic template page can produce empty histories because those templates may not have been used.

High-value template IDs observed:

  • Triceps Pushdown: 93A552C6
  • Lateral Raise (Dumbbell): 422B08F1
  • Preacher Curl (Machine): 1E9A6B8E
  • Bicep Curl (Barbell): A5AC6449
  • Lat Pulldown (Cable): 6A6C31A5
  • Bench Press (Smith Machine): 0FBF7195
  • Iso-Lateral Row (Machine): AA1EB7D8
  • Incline Bench Press (Smith Machine): 3A6FA3D1
  • Pull Up: 1B2B1E7C
  • Bench Press (Barbell): 79D0BB3A
  • Chest Fly (Machine): 78683336
  • Pull Up (Assisted): 2C37EC5E
  • Shrug (Machine): 19A38071
  • Leg Extension (Machine): 75A4F6C4

Best uses:

  • Faster and cleaner per-exercise trend analysis than scanning all workouts.
  • Dashboard strength ladder for chosen anchor exercises.
  • Auto-detect stale exercises or exercises with enough data for trend confidence.

Routines

  • GET /v1/routines
  • GET /v1/routines/{routineId}
  • POST /v1/routines
  • PUT /v1/routines/{routineId}

Observed routine fields:

  • id
  • title
  • folder_id
  • created_at
  • updated_at
  • exercises

Observed routine exercise fields:

  • exercise_template_id
  • title
  • index
  • notes
  • rest_seconds
  • sets
  • superset_id

Current routines include:

  • Sapho Day 1 — Push + Sauna
  • Sapho Day 2 — Posture Cardio A
  • Sapho Day 3 — Pull + Sauna
  • Sapho Day 4 — Posture Cardio B
  • Sapho Day 5 — Lower + Core
  • Lower
  • Upper
  • Legs
  • Pull
  • Push
  • Rutina A
  • Rutina B
  • Rutina C

Best uses:

  • Canonical program definition.
  • Compare planned routine vs completed workouts.
  • Identify if completed workouts follow the current Sapho 5-Day plan or older routines.
  • Program audit: missing movement patterns, too much arm isolation, low lower-body frequency, missing cardio/posture days.

Caution:

  • Write actions should be verified by re-listing routines.
  • Routine creation/update is a side effect; do only when explicitly requested.

Routine folders

  • GET /v1/routine_folders
  • GET /v1/routine_folders/{folderId}
  • POST /v1/routine_folders

Observed folder fields:

  • id
  • title
  • index
  • created_at
  • updated_at

Use:

  • Mostly organization. Low analytical value.
  • Could help separate active Sapho plan from old routines.

Exercise templates

  • GET /v1/exercise_templates
  • GET /v1/exercise_templates/{exerciseTemplateId}
  • POST /v1/exercise_templates

Observed template fields:

  • id
  • title
  • type
  • equipment
  • primary_muscle_group
  • secondary_muscle_groups
  • is_custom

Best uses:

  • Muscle-group classification.
  • Equipment classification.
  • Stable exercise matching by ID.
  • Build dashboards by movement/muscle category.
  • Improve weekly reviews: not just routine titles, but actual trained areas.

Body measurements

  • GET /v1/body_measurements
  • GET /v1/body_measurements/{date}
  • POST /v1/body_measurements
  • PUT /v1/body_measurements/{date}

Observed fields:

  • date
  • weight_kg
  • neck_cm
  • shoulder_cm
  • chest_cm
  • left_bicep_cm
  • right_bicep_cm
  • left_forearm_cm
  • right_forearm_cm
  • waist
  • abdomen
  • hips
  • left_thigh
  • right_thigh
  • left_calf
  • right_calf
  • created_at
  • id

Current measurement coverage:

  • 2026-05-11: full body measurement set, except no explicit body-fat API field observed.
  • 2026-02-26: weight only.
  • 2024-07-31: weight only.

Best uses:

  • Body-composition dashboard.
  • Measurement trend lines when more entries are added.
  • Goal progress bars: current measurement on left, goal on right.
  • Weight trend.

Caution:

  • Body fat percentage was not observed as a Hevy body-measurement API field.
  • PUT /v1/body_measurements/{date} overwrites all fields; omitted fields become null. Never update measurements partially unless first fetching existing values and merging.

Current data pattern

Top historical workout titles:

  • Rutina B: 43
  • Rutina A: 39
  • Lunes y jueves: 24
  • Martes y viernes: 22
  • Rutina C: 11
  • Sapho Day 1/2/3/5 currently have 2 each.
  • Sapho Day 4 exists as a routine but had no observed completed workout in the latest fetched history at time of review.

Most frequent exercises in history:

  • Triceps Pushdown: 62 workout appearances.
  • Lateral Raise (Dumbbell): 59.
  • Preacher Curl (Machine): 58.
  • Bicep Curl (Barbell): 53.
  • Lat Pulldown (Cable): 39.
  • Bench Press (Smith Machine): 36.
  • Iso-Lateral Row (Machine): 32.
  • Incline Bench Press (Smith Machine): 31.
  • Pull Up: 29.
  • Bench Press (Barbell): 26.

Recent workouts checked:

  • 2026-05-22: Sapho Day 5 — Lower + Core.
  • 2026-05-20: Sapho Day 3 — Pull + Sauna.
  • 2026-05-19: Sapho Day 2 — Posture Cardio A.
  • 2026-05-18: Sapho Day 1 — Push + Sauna.
  • 2026-05-16: Sapho Day 5 — Lower + Core.
  • 2026-05-15: Sapho Day 3 — Pull + Sauna.
  • 2026-05-12: Sapho Day 2 — Posture Cardio A.
  • 2026-05-11: Sapho Day 1 — Push + Sauna.

Recommended Sapho usage model

Daily

Do not run a heavy Hevy review daily. Use Hevy only if Jenffry asks or if a dashboard refresh needs it.

Daily check-in should ask/track:

  • Today’s intended workout or substitution.
  • Whether workout happened.
  • Energy/sleep/mental capacity signal.

Weekly review

Use Hevy heavily.

Weekly review should include:

  • Workouts completed.
  • Last workout date.
  • Routine coverage.
  • Minimum / strong / ideal classification.
  • Missed routine day.
  • Best strength signal.
  • Next-week gym target.

Monthly review

Use Hevy for strategic health review:

  • 30-day consistency.
  • Routine balance.
  • Strength trend.
  • Body measurement changes.
  • Cardio trend.
  • Stale or overused patterns.
  • Next-month training focus.

Dashboard

Best Hevy dashboard uses:

  • 7-day / 30-day workout counts.
  • Lifting vs cardio/posture split.
  • Current Sapho routine coverage.
  • Strength ladder by anchor exercises.
  • Body measurement progress.
  • Treadmill mph conversion from distance + duration.
  • Last workout / next suggested workout.

Local cache

Build a local cache using:

  1. Initial full pull from /v1/workouts, /v1/routines, /v1/exercise_templates, /v1/body_measurements.
  2. Incremental updates from /v1/workouts/events?since=....
  3. Per-exercise histories for dashboard anchor exercises.

This avoids refetching all 149 workouts repeatedly and handles edits/deletes.

Biggest technical improvements to make next

Recommended next move: upgrade /opt/data/scripts/hevy_api.py.

Add these commands:

  1. inventory
  • Shows total workouts, routines, templates, measurements, and API coverage.
  1. cache-sync
  • Builds a local Hevy cache so we do not repeatedly pull all workouts.
  1. exercise-trends
  • Uses exercise_template_id for anchor-lift trends.
  1. weekly-review
  • Outputs the minimum / strong / ideal classification automatically.
  1. body-measurements
  • Gives clean dashboard-ready body measurement data.
  1. routine-coverage
  • Detects which Sapho plan days were hit or missed.

Decision notes

  • Hevy should remain the gym source of truth.
  • TickTick should not be used for workout details.
  • Dashboard and weekly/monthly reviews should consume Hevy data.
  • For future dashboard work, use stable Hevy exercise_template_id whenever possible; exercise title text is a fallback only.
  • Hevy API is good enough for a serious Sapho gym intelligence layer. The main limitation is not data access — it is building the right local cache and using stable exercise IDs instead of fragile title matching.
Mental Health
areas/health/mental-health.md
degree 31

Status: Active tracking system is now live through the final 15 TickTick mental-health habit trackers and the dashboard Health tab. Current priority: use the dashboard as the lightweight trend surface while keeping weekly review as the interpretation layer; av

Physical HealthRelationshipsLeisurePerception LogHealthProgress TrackingCurrent StrategyOpen Loops

Mental Health

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active tracking system is now live through the final 15 TickTick mental-health habit trackers and the dashboard Health tab.
  • Current priority: use the dashboard as the lightweight trend surface while keeping weekly review as the interpretation layer; avoid turning daily tracking into rumination.
  • Active signals: Stop checking, Healthy diet, Mood, Sleep, Anxiety control, Energy, Walk/no-phone, Self reflect, Therapy, Physical activity, Social outing, Leisure activity, Life story, Relax, and Meditate.
  • Open loops: align Leisure and Relationships goals with outward-facing life without reducing them to mental-health tools; refine the Sapho mental-health support profile over time.
  • Next concrete move: use the first weekly review after the tracker launch to check whether these 15 signals are useful, too much, or missing one important signal.

Mental health is the lead stabilizing domain. Physical routines matter here when they support emotional capacity, morning energy, and future excitement, but detailed routine tracking belongs in physical-health. Leisure and relationships are separate life areas, but parts of them intentionally serve mental health by turning attention outward, restoring identity, and creating future excitement.

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Live present, grounded, and outward-facing enough that fear/checking no longer organizes the day.
  • 12-month proof: A stable response to checking exists, acceptance practice happens most weeks, and outward-focus hobbies/social/family actions are part of normal life.
  • Current monthly target: By 2026-06-30, use the first SMART practice loop: ignore + redirect in the day, acceptance/embrace meditation target, and at least 4 outward-focus actions.
  • This week's commitment: Use the 15 TickTick mental-health habits only as a lightweight signal baseline; identify what helps versus what becomes noise.
  • Daily rule / next move: Check in once, then move outward into a person, task, body action, hobby, or family moment.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: TickTick Habit Records own raw daily scores/notes; this page owns the mental-health goal; weekly/monthly reviews interpret trends.

Mental-health SMART goal cascade

Long-term goal

Over the long term, I want to live as someone present, grounded, and committed to the actual life I have, with enough acceptance, outward attention, identity, and future excitement that fear no longer organizes my life.

Success looks like:

  • Fear, anxiety, intrusive thoughts, and checking can arise without becoming the center of the day.
  • My main operating procedure is: ignore checking, redirect attention, accept the feeling, and embrace that I can survive it.
  • I can feel anxiety, grief, fear, or intrusive thoughts without treating them as proof that my life is broken.
  • I am present in real activities: conversations, family, pickleball, hobbies, social settings, film/cinema, and work.
  • My identity feels repaired through action and social mirroring: people see my personality, interests, humor, taste, and presence in real life.
  • The broader life is good even if I do not feel fully “fixed.” Acceptance is part of success, not a consolation prize.

12-month goal

By 2027-05-24, I will feel more like myself again and stronger because of this chapter, as shown by a stable daily response to fear/checking, regular acceptance practice, active outward-focus hobbies/social life, and better presence in conversations, family, pickleball, and leisure.

12-month proof points:

  • I use the core strategy during normal days: ignore, redirect, accept, embrace.
  • Meditation or equivalent acceptance/emotional exposure practice happens most weeks, with a target of 20 minutes, 5 days per week when capacity allows.
  • I protect at least 1 outward-focus action per week through Leisure, Relationships, family, hobbies, or social life.
  • Intrusive thoughts and fear are handled as internal events, not commands or predictions.
  • I have at least one active identity/outward-focus channel — for example film club, cinema-related activity, RC racing, pickleball, windsurfing, social outings, or another hobby — that gets real attention outside my inner world.
  • I practice acceptance: even if anxiety returns or never fully disappears, life can still be good and livable.

6-month goal

By 2026-11-24, I will have a reliable mental-health operating loop: daily anti-checking response, 5-day acceptance/embrace practice target, weekly outward-focus action, and cross-links to Leisure and Relationships goals that support identity repair and future excitement.

6-month proof points:

  • The Sapho mental-health support profile exists: fear as the core mechanism, helpful responses, unhelpful responses, boundaries, and preferred language during spirals.
  • The weekly review includes a compact mental-health review: habit-note trend, strain/load if voluntarily given or inferable, observer-mode/checking trend, acceptance practice follow-through, outward-focus action, and one adjustment. Daily outputs should not ask for sleep/capacity scores.
  • A short anti-checking / observer-mode reset script exists and has been practiced.
  • Leisure goals define outward-focus hobbies, including whether film club belongs in the current cycle.
  • Relationships/social goals define intentional social exposure and identity repair without making relationships serve only mental health.
  • Tracking is useful but not obsessive: checkmarks and weekly review are preferred; 1–5 scores are supporting signals, not a self-worth grade.

Current monthly goal — through 2026-06-30

By 2026-06-30, I will establish the first SMART mental-health practice loop: use “ignore + redirect” as my daytime operating procedure, complete acceptance/embrace meditation on 20 days, and complete at least 4 outward-focus actions connected to hobbies, social life, family, or leisure.

Monthly proof points:

  • Daytime operating procedure: when checking/anxiety appears, use “I’m fine, I don’t need to check,” then redirect attention to a task, person, or hobby.
  • Acceptance/embrace practice: meditate or sit with the feeling for 20 minutes, 5 days per week, with 3 sessions/week as the minimum acceptable floor, aiming for 20 sessions by June 30.
  • Acceptance content: during practice, include both:
  • “I don’t have the life I wanted, but I have a very good life,” then name 3 things I am grateful for.
  • “If the bad feeling comes back, that is okay. I can deal with it. I will be fine. It may return or never fully go away, and I can still live.”
  • Outward-focus actions: complete 4 total outward-focus actions this month, using any Leisure/Relationships/Family source. No specific activity target is required. Examples: film club exploration, RC racing, pickleball, windsurfing step, movie/cinema activity, social outing, family outing, gym as energy builder.
  • Identity/social repair: capture at least 1 social or relationship action that lets personality be seen in real life, while leaving the detailed social/relationship goals to relationships and leisure.
  • Tracking: use the final 15 TickTick habit trackers as the lightweight data layer. Score habits track 7-day and 30-day averages; completion habits track 7-day and 30-day completion rates. Weekly review interprets the pattern; daily notes are evidence, not a demand for daily analysis.

Current weekly goal

This week, I will use the final mental-health tracker set to build a clean baseline: score the six daily signals, complete the daily grounding/reflection habits when useful, and protect at least one social/leisure/physical/therapy/life-story action.

Weekly proof:

  • Daily score signals: Stop checking, Healthy diet, Mood, Sleep, Anxiety control, and Energy have enough entries to produce a useful 7-day average.
  • Daily completion signals: Walk/no-phone and Self reflect are used as behavior checkmarks, not as mood autopsies.
  • Weekly completion signals: Therapy, Physical activity, Social outing, Leisure activity, and Life story each get one useful entry when they actually happen or are intentionally practiced.
  • Practice frequency: Relax reaches at least 3/week; Meditate aims for 5/week.
  • Weekly review asks: which signal predicted a better day, which habit was noise, and what should repeat next week?

Daily move

Today, I will complete the lightweight TickTick mental-health check-ins once, then use the data to move outward rather than keep checking myself.

Daily practice menu:

  1. Ignore checking: “I’m fine. I don’t need to check.”
  2. Redirect attention: put focus into a task, person, hobby, gym, family moment, or concrete next action.
  3. Accept: sit with the feeling without trying to escape it; name 3 things I am grateful for in the actual life I have.
  4. Embrace: invite the feared feeling to come back and do its worst; remind myself I can deal with it and still be fine.

Observer-mode reset script

Use this when checking, fear, observer mode, or intrusive thoughts start pulling attention inward. Keep it short; the goal is to exit the loop, not analyze the loop.

  1. Name it once: “This is fear/checking. I do not need to solve it right now.”
  2. Ignore the checking demand: “I’m fine. I don’t need to check.”
  3. Redirect outward: choose one visible next object of attention — task, person, family moment, work step, gym movement, hobby, movie, or physical environment.
  4. If the feeling stays: “It can stay. I can carry it and still live this moment.”
  5. Micro-action: do one concrete 2-minute outward action before reassessing anything.

Boundaries:

  • Do not repeat the script more than twice in a row; after that, move the body or change the environment.
  • Do not use the reset as a new way to check whether anxiety is gone.
  • Save deeper acceptance/embrace work for the 20-minute practice window.

Core problem targets

The current SMART targets cover these mental-health problem areas:

  1. Anxiety / feeling unsafe internally.
  2. Observer mode / constant checking.
  3. Intrusive thoughts.
  4. Low future excitement.
  5. Identity repair / feeling like yourself again.
  6. Acceptance / not resisting how life feels.
  7. Sleep / morning energy.
  8. Presence in conversations, family, pickleball, and hobbies.
  9. Social isolation / not enough people contact.

Fear is the core mechanism behind much of the pattern. The operating goal is not to eliminate fear on command; it is to stop fear from becoming the authority over attention, identity, and the future.

Core strategy

Daytime operating procedure

Use during normal life when checking, anxiety, observer mode, or intrusive thoughts appear:

  1. Ignore: “I’m fine. I don’t need to check.”
  2. Redirect: turn attention toward the outside world — task, person, hobby, family, work, gym, movie, activity, or next concrete action.

This is the main procedure throughout the day. It is deliberately simple because complicated analysis can become more checking.

Deliberate acceptance / embrace practice

Use in a bounded practice window, not all day:

  1. Accept: feel what is present instead of escaping, avoiding, distracting, or proving it away.
  2. Reframe actual life: “I don’t have the life I wanted, but I have a very good life.” Name 3 things I am grateful for.
  3. Embrace fear: the bad feeling may return; it may never fully go away; I can deal with it and still be fine.
  4. Re-enter life: end the practice by doing one concrete outward-facing action.

Target: 20 minutes per day, 5 days per week when capacity allows.

Current distress patterns

Anxiety, low future-excitement, intrusive thoughts

Jenffry identified a possible root pattern: when his mental health starts to feel worst, the source is usually anxiety. This appears linked with a lack of excitement about the future and the presence of intrusive thoughts.

Working interpretation, not diagnosis:

  • Anxiety may be the upstream driver behind many spikes.
  • Low future-excitement may make anxiety feel more trapped or totalizing.
  • Intrusive thoughts are part of the distress pattern and should be noticed without treating them as commands, predictions, or proof.
  • Useful response is likely not deeper rumination, but grounding plus recommitment to a concrete future-building action.

Observer mode / constant checking

Jenffry described a distressing pattern he calls “observer mode,” possibly dissociation-like. He later identified a more specific mechanism: because of the weed-related trauma plus underlying depression, anxiety, and stress, he may be constantly checking whether he is okay. That checking itself becomes a major source of distress and pulls him out of living in the moment.

The pattern includes:

  • looking into life from an outside observer perspective
  • zooming in on his own feelings and internal state
  • overthinking and over-analyzing how he feels
  • drawing broad, sweeping, all-encompassing conclusions from moment-to-moment feelings
  • becoming too aware of minute details of his own life

Desired movement:

  • more in the moment
  • more grounded
  • less stopped by overthinking
  • less dissociation-like distance from his life
  • less analysis of the fact that he is analyzing

Global life-story framing

Jenffry identified that repeated global wording such as “my life is over” may have deepened the distress pattern. The useful correction is not to deny painful specifics, but to stop letting one painful chapter define the whole life story.

Working interpretation, not diagnosis:

  • The mind can treat repeated all-encompassing language as evidence that the whole future is closed.
  • A better frame is specific and bounded: this chapter was painful, unwanted, and complicated; it does not prove that the whole life is ruined.
  • Writing Jenffry's life story may serve as a global reframe: preserve truth, but make room for future chapters, fatherhood, health, relationship clarity, ambition, and excitement.

Acceptance and non-resistance

Acceptance is not giving up. It is the protection against the “what if I do all this and it does not work?” loop.

Useful acceptance frame:

  • Healing work matters.
  • Also, life must be livable and good even if the feeling does not disappear on schedule.
  • Resisting the possibility of anxiety returning can restart the same fear/checking loop.
  • Acceptance means: this is life, this is what is here, and it can still be good.

Cross-links to other life areas

Leisure / future excitement

The future-excitement and hobbies portion of mental health will be filled through leisure. Candidate outward-focus channels include film club, RC racing, pickleball, windsurfing, movies/cinema writing, social outings, family outings, and gym as an energy builder.

Film club belongs primarily in Leisure, but it is especially relevant to mental health because it combines social exposure, identity repair, hobby focus, and outward attention.

Relationships / social identity repair

The social and identity-repair portion of mental health will connect to relationships and leisure. The mental-health use is intentional: relationships and social exposure can help Jenffry feel seen as a person again. But those areas should also stand on their own beyond mental health.

Tracking method

Preferred default: TickTick habit check-ins + weekly review, not daily deep scoring or notes analysis. The dashboard is the trend surface; Sapho's weekly/monthly reviews are the interpretation layer.

Current TickTick source-of-truth habits:

  • Daily 1–5 + note:
  • Stop checking
  • Healthy diet
  • Mood
  • Sleep
  • Anxiety — Option B anxiety control, 1 poor control to 5 strong control.
  • Energy
  • Daily note:
  • Walk, no phone
  • Self reflect
  • Weekly note, at least once/week:
  • Therapy
  • Physical activity
  • Social outing
  • Leisure activity
  • Life story
  • Practice frequency + note:
  • Relax — at least 3 times/week.
  • Meditate — at least 5 times/week.

TickTick API note: the public dashboard uses the final 15 habit trackers plus TickTick Habit Log emoji scores/notes. Raw Habit Log note content is written to /opt/data/wiki/private/health/mood-tracking-notes.md and is intentionally included in the public dashboard publisher.

Support implications for Sapho

Helpful Sapho stance:

  • recognize fear as the core pattern when it appears
  • distinguish facts, feelings, interpretations, and sweeping conclusions
  • avoid feeding circular self-monitoring
  • help Jenffry return to the daytime operating procedure: ignore checking and redirect attention
  • reserve acceptance/embrace work for bounded practice windows unless Jenffry explicitly wants to process
  • use acceptance-and-commitment framing: “this can be hard and still survivable; what life do you choose to commit to today?”
  • treat mental health as the top priority, while keeping family, body, work, leisure, and relationships connected to recovery

Avoid:

  • excessive interrogation of internal states when Jenffry is already in observer mode
  • overlong analysis that encourages more rumination
  • acting like therapy, diagnosis, or medical authority
  • pretending dashboard numbers can fully measure mental recovery
  • making safety/escalation discussion the center when Jenffry says he knows how to handle it, while still using ordinary emergency judgment if he ever reports danger or inability to stay safe

Dashboard / data rules

  • Mental-health dashboard signals should follow the final TickTick habit tracker list: Stop checking, Therapy, Physical activity, Healthy diet, Walk no phone, Social outing, Mood, Sleep, Anxiety, Energy, Relax, Leisure activity, Life story, Self reflect, and Meditate.
  • Use daily signals as trend context, not as a demand for analysis. Habit Log notes can be reviewed weekly/monthly.
  • Weekly review is the main interpretation layer: what helped, what worsened checking, what should repeat, and what should be dropped.
  • Perception dumps stay raw in perception-log unless Jenffry explicitly asks to process them.

Current StrategyOpen LoopsDashboardProgress Tracking

Physical Health

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active support system for body composition, strength, cardio capacity, sleep/energy, and medical follow-through; system build marked complete on 2026-05-24 and now in normal review/calibration mode.
  • Current priority: serve the dashboard and goals layer first: keep current body measurements, Hevy workout rhythm, strength anchors, and the numeric goal ladder clear and source-backed.
  • Active signals: Hevy 7-day/30-day workouts and sets, routine balance, weight, waist, abdomen, body-fat estimate, key lift anchors, treadmill/cardio capacity, sleep/energy, testosterone/lipids follow-up.
  • Open loops: update body measurements monthly; keep strength anchors mapped to stable Hevy exercise IDs; refine the meal-prep default; configure a health-data API/bridge only after credentials/phone-permission path is clear; avoid TickTick workout clutter.
  • Next concrete move: keep Hevy as the physical-health source of truth and review body/strength trends weekly/monthly against the numeric cascade below.

Physical health contains gym, sleep, food, body maintenance, grooming, medical follow-up, and physical capacity. Hevy is the gym and body-measurement source of truth. The dashboard displays this page; it should not invent separate physical-health goals.

Health data API exploration

2026-06-19 Google Health API option-1 assessment:

  • Google Health API is the official cloud/OAuth path replacing Fitbit Web API, not a direct cloud mirror of Android Health Connect.
  • It can likely help Sapho if Jenffry's Fitbit / Pixel Watch / Fitbit app data includes the needed activity, sleep, heart, weight, and device signals.
  • It may not expose all phone-side Health Connect aggregate data from arbitrary Android apps; Health Connect still requires an on-device Android bridge for those sources.
  • All Google Health API scopes are restricted and require OAuth consent; unverified apps can be used with manually added test users, but supporting more than 100 users requires Google's security review.
  • Local helper created: /opt/data/scripts/google_health_api.py. It supports config status, OAuth URL generation, authorization-code exchange, profile/device reads, raw list calls, and daily rollups. It currently has no credentials configured.
  • Next setup dependency: Jenffry must create/enable a Google Cloud project OAuth client for Google Health API, add himself as a test user, add required scopes, and store GOOGLE_HEALTH_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_HEALTH_CLIENT_SECRET, and later GOOGLE_HEALTH_REFRESH_TOKEN locally in /opt/data/.env without pasting secrets in chat.

Food-tracking tool preference

2026-06-26 food-tracking selection signal:

  • Jenffry wants a Pixel/Android food tracker that supports Health Connect, prioritizes fast/simple meal logging, has low cost, and avoids diet-program/meal-plan clutter.
  • Photo or natural-language meal logging is useful but secondary.
  • As-of-2026-06-26 research shortlist: try MyNetDiary first; consider Cronometer for accuracy, MacrosFirst for simple macro logging, and MacroFactor only if a polished paid experience is worth the cost.
  • Re-verify Health Connect support and current prices before subscribing; do not turn the tracker choice into a broad diet-program or meal-plan project.

Nutrition execution default

2026-07-05 meal-prep signal:

  • Jenffry accepted a narrow 5-day breakfast/lunch meal-prep default for immediate grocery execution: Greek yogurt protein bowls for breakfast and chicken/rice/vegetable bowls for lunch.
  • Design principle: high-protein, filling, easy-to-repeat meals that reduce food friction without turning nutrition into a second job.
  • Concrete grocery checklist and prep details live in TickTick under 🏋‍♂Health as “Buy groceries for 5-day breakfast/lunch meal prep”; no due date was set in the capture.
  • Treat this as a breakfast/lunch execution default, not a full-diet commitment or dinner plan.
  • 2026-07-15 planning update: Jenffry said the meal-prep plan still needs refinement. Keep the existing task as the concrete execution holder, but do not treat the current checklist as final.

Training routine direction

2026-06-27 Hevy routine signal:

  • The prior open question was whether to redesign lifting so every major muscle is trained twice per week.
  • 2026-07-15 planning update: Jenffry chose the direction. Cardio days will be pickleball, and he will move soon to a 3-days/week full-body workout. No active open loop remains on the workout-routine decision for now.
  • Implementation note: when Jenffry asks to update Hevy, verify actual current routines/exercises through the Hevy API before changing anything.

Medical follow-up status

2026-07-15 planning update:

  • Cardiology follow-up: done. Jenffry had an ultrasound and reported that everything was good.
  • Testosterone follow-up: the repeat testosterone test was better than the first one. Follow-up is already scheduled as the urology appointment.
  • ENT follow-up: Jenffry missed the appointment and needs to call to reschedule. TickTick task was updated to Call ENT to reschedule missed appointment.

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Build a lean, muscular, healthy body with lower abdominal fat, maintained/increased muscle, stronger lifts, better cardio, and medically informed hormone/lipid follow-through.
  • 12-month proof: Visible recomposition by 2027-05-22: about 208 lb, 35 in waist, 36 in abdomen, about 17% body-fat estimate, stronger key lifts, and 20 minutes at 6 mph running capacity.
  • Current monthly target: Maintain the training and measurement loop without changing targets prematurely; use medical input and 4–6 weeks of Hevy data before recalibration.
  • This week's commitment: Follow the active weekly health anchors in current-strategy; do not duplicate a fixed weekly task list here because this page owns physical-health targets, not live weekly planning.
  • Daily rule / next move: Train, recover, or schedule the next medical/body-system step; do not duplicate Hevy into TickTick.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: Hevy owns workouts/body measurements; this page owns numeric targets; weekly/monthly reviews own interpretation; the dashboard displays.

Strength tracking exercises

These are the exercises the dashboard should use in the strength tracking section. The dashboard should prefer stable Hevy exercise_template_id values and use exact titles only as fallback.

  • Bench press — 205 x 5
  • Shoulder press dumbbell — 100 x 10
  • Triceps pushdown — 120 x 10
  • Pull up — 12 unassisted pull-ups across 3–5 sets
  • Bicep curl — 95 x 10
  • Leg press — 550 x 8
  • Romanian deadlift — 225 x 5
  • Running / treadmill — 6.0 mph

Recent-current strength anchors from Hevy:

  • Bench press: baseline 135 x 10; current best from Hevy uses Bench Press (Barbell); goal 205 x 5.
  • Shoulder press dumbbell: baseline 50 x 10; current best from Hevy uses Shoulder Press (Dumbbell); goal 100 x 10.
  • Triceps pushdown: baseline 60 x 10; current best from Hevy uses Triceps Pushdown; goal 120 x 10.
  • Pull up: baseline 6; current best from Hevy uses Pull Up, not Assisted Pull-up; goal 12 unassisted pull-ups across 3–5 sets.
  • Bicep curl: baseline 60 x 10; current best from Hevy uses Bicep Curl (Barbell); goal 95 x 10.
  • Leg press: baseline 360 x 10; current best from Hevy uses Leg Press (Machine); goal 550 x 8.
  • Romanian deadlift: baseline 135 x 6; current best from Hevy uses Romanian Deadlift (Barbell); goal 225 x 5.
  • Treadmill: baseline 1.8 mph; current best from Hevy uses Treadmill; goal 6.0 mph. Hevy stores this as distance in meters plus duration in seconds, and the dashboard converts it to mph.

Hevy dashboard anchor IDs:

  • Bench Press (Barbell): 79D0BB3A
  • Shoulder Press (Dumbbell): 878CD1D0
  • Triceps Pushdown: 93A552C6
  • Pull Up: 1B2B1E7C
  • Bicep Curl (Barbell): A5AC6449
  • Leg Press (Machine): C7973E0E

  • Treadmill: 243710DE

Practice layer

  • Weekly: review Hevy 7-day workout count, routine balance, minimum/strong/ideal classification, and next-week gym target in the weekly review.
  • Monthly: update weight/waist/abdomen/chest/arms/thighs/calves in Hevy and review body trend against strength performance.
  • Quarterly: reassess whether the 12-month strength targets are too aggressive, too easy, or causing pain/recovery problems.
  • Daily: do not duplicate workouts into TickTick. Daily planning may name gym intention/completion/substitution only.

Data cautions

  • Some older Hevy entries appear unsuitable for target-setting, especially obvious outliers such as a Smith-machine bench entry around 1140 lb. Use recent-current anchors, not all-time raw PRs, for goal extrapolation.
  • Body-fat percentage is not directly measured in Hevy yet. Use waist/abdomen trend, photos, strength, and weight as observable proxies unless a consistent body-fat measurement method is added.
  • Dashboard physical-health goals are display outputs. This page owns the numeric targets; the direct refresh script owns mechanical Hevy/source syncing; weekly/monthly reviews own interpretation.

Preserved historical material

Older migrated notes from concepts/physical-health.md and concepts/health-routines.md were moved out of the active page because they contained stale or conflicting targets. They are preserved at /opt/data/wiki/raw/reference/physical-health-migrated-material-2026-05-24.md.

degree 30

Leisure contains hobbies, outings, play, restoration, and future excitement. The point is to protect aliveness, not turn fun into another productivity system. Social rebuilding and friendships belong under relationships / social. Status: Active restoration and

RelationshipsSocialHobbiesOutingsCurrent StrategySapho Operating System

Leisure

Leisure contains hobbies, outings, play, restoration, and future excitement. The point is to protect aliveness, not turn fun into another productivity system. Social rebuilding and friendships belong under relationships / social.

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active restoration and future-excitement domain.
  • Current priority: one intentional leisure action each week without TickTick clutter, building toward regular new exciting activities and enjoyable hobbies.
  • Active signals: Leisure activity habit completion, Leisure TickTick tasks, outing backlog, weekly/monthly leisure spending, and a cleaned movie pipeline that requires re-verification before scheduling.
  • Open loops: choose concrete logistics only when an outing or hobby step is selected.
  • Next concrete move: keep ideas here; move only chosen concrete logistics to TickTick.

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Regularly do new exciting activities and maintain hobbies Jenffry genuinely enjoys.
  • 12-month proof: Leisure feels alive and normal: planned outings, active hobbies, future excitement, and less default collapse into passive screen time; social connection remains under Relationships/Social.
  • Current monthly target: Do two intentional outings: one family outing and one solo hobby activity.
  • This week's commitment: Choose or do one intentional leisure activity.
  • Daily rule / next move: No daily leisure admin; capture ideas here and move only selected logistics to TickTick.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: This page owns leisure pipeline/bucket list; TickTick owns chosen dated logistics; Habit Log owns completion signal; dashboard scoring is display-only.

Long-term direction

I regularly do new exciting activities and have hobbies that I enjoy.

12-month direction

  • Maintain a normal monthly rhythm of 1 family outing and 1 solo hobby thing, with social outings tracked under Relationships.
  • Keep movies, TV shows, and video games as hobbies without letting them replace future-building leisure activity.
  • Play pickleball about once every 1–2 weeks.
  • Progress in windsurfing until “mastering” means achieving planing.
  • Continue exploring fishing and other activities.
  • Future excitement comes from hanging out with friends and doing new things.

SMART cascade

12-month outcome

By May 2027, leisure feels alive and normal: planned outings, hobbies that create future excitement, and less default collapse into passive screen time. Social connection is tracked under Relationships.

6-month goals

  • Complete at least 12 intentional leisure activities in 12 weeks.
  • Choose one hobby track to actively advance: windsurfing, fishing, gaming, VR, tabletop/D&D if hobby-framed, RC racing, drone flying, or another chosen interest.

Monthly goals

  • Do 2 intentional outings/month.
  • Do 1 family outing/month.
  • Do 1 solo hobby activity/month.
  • Keep leisure spending visible against the monthly Leisure and Dining & social budget categories.

Weekly goals

  • Complete the Leisure activity habit at least once/week.
  • Keep at least 1 concrete fun/hobby option scheduled or chosen for the week.
  • Move only selected logistics to TickTick; leave unchosen ideas in the backlog.

Daily rule

No daily leisure admin. If a good idea appears, capture it here or in the relevant subpage; do not create a TickTick task unless it has a concrete date, place, or next action.

Dashboard source rules

  • TickTick owns concrete execution: dated leisure tasks, chosen logistics, and near-term hobby actions.
  • TickTick Habit Log owns completion tracking for Leisure activity.
  • Finance dashboard owns spending signal through Leisure and Dining & social budget categories.
  • This wiki page and subpages own the idea backlog and long-term direction.
  • The dashboard should show signal and choices, not raw source/provenance helper text.
  • Dashboard leisure scoring uses this weighted rubric: Enjoyment 14%, Restoration 13%, Excitement 13%, Social 10%, Money 8%, Risk 7%, Novelty 8%, Logistics 6%, Meaning 13%, and Physical health 8%.
  • Money, Risk, and Logistics are friction dimensions in the score; the other seven are benefit dimensions.

Subpages

  • hobbies
  • outings

Hobbies and leisure to protect

  • Windsurfing
  • Gaming
  • Time with friends
  • Trying the VR headset
  • Developing AI agents when hobby-framed, not when it becomes work/admin
  • Fishing
  • Pickleball
  • Tabletop/D&D or other nerd-culture activities when hobby-framed rather than primarily social
  • RC racing
  • Drone flying / play

Pipeline

All leisure ideas live here. Unscored items stay at 0 until Jenffry manually scores them.

  • Finka Table & Tap — Miami restaurant / dining outing idea. Asian-fusion/Cuban/Peruvian-style food, craft cocktails, and beers on tap; useful for a leisure dinner, date-night, or social/family meal option when budget and timing fit. Address: 14690 SW 26th St, Miami, FL 33175. Phone: 305-227-8818. Source Google Maps link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/dgRkHa2TujUsg8yBA
  • Casa Sensei — Fort Lauderdale restaurant / dining outing idea. Pan-Asian and Latin fusion on Las Olas; useful for a higher-effort leisure dinner, date-night, or social outing when the drive north fits. Address: 1200 E Las Olas Blvd STE 101, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301. Phone: 954-530-4176. Source Google Maps link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/DmZUqKarVzkwZyxS7
  • Supergirl (2026) — stale June showtime lead; re-verify current availability before turning into a task. Prior source: AMC Sunset Place 24 screenshot /opt/data/cache/images/img_dbb8ceeb2903.jpg.
  • Dune: Part Three (2026) — opening Dec 18. AutoNation IMAX 3D Theater, 26.3 mi. IMAX 70MM Film, no passes, with showtimes at 7:00p and 10:00p. Source screenshot: /opt/data/cache/images/img_8c5d709a7c79.jpg.
  • Spider-Man: Brand New Day (2026) — upcoming live-action Spider-Man movie starring Tom Holland; Marvel lists the U.S. theatrical release date as July 31, 2026. Source: https://www.marvel.com/movies/spider-man-brand-new-day
  • Disclosure Day (2026) — stale June showtime lead; re-verify current availability before turning into a task. Prior source: CMX Miami Lakes 13 screenshot /opt/data/cache/images/img_3060879bf229.jpg.
  • Minions & Monsters (2026) — upcoming Minions movie / Minions 3 from Illumination; official movie site lists theatrical release as July 1, 2026. Source: https://www.minionsmovie.com/
  • Pan's Labyrinth 20th Anniversary (2026) — Guillermo del Toro's dark fantasy returns to theaters nationwide on Oct 9, 2026 in 4K, 3D, and premium HDR by Barco; Cineverse + Fathom Entertainment release. Source: https://www.fathomentertainment.com/news/guillermo-del-toros-masterpiece-pans-labyrinth-returns-in-3d-for-20th-anniversary-from-cineverse-and-fathom-entertainment-on-october-9/
  • Windsurfing: active skill track; next step is already represented in TickTick when a concrete windsurfing action is scheduled.
  • Fishing: solo restoration track; protect the waterfront/Bronco/near-car vibe and only schedule concrete outings.
  • Pickleball: recurring body-system and fun track every 1–2 weeks when calendar allows.
  • Indoor rock climbing: active indoor outing / skill track; good weather-safe physical leisure option and potential social/family-friendly activity depending on venue and energy.
  • Finishing Elden Ring and Shadow of the Erdtree DLC: protected gaming track when it restores energy rather than replacing future-building activity.
  • VR headset session: actual time using the VR headset as a distinct hobby step, not bundled with gaming.
  • RC racing: concrete RC racing session or meetup if it still has pull.
  • Drone flying / play: time actually flying or playing with the drone, if it still feels interesting.
  • D&D / tabletop one-shot: social if the goal is friendship/community; hobby-framed if the goal is play/nerd-culture restoration.
  • BLEACH: THOUSAND-YEAR BLOOD WAR - THE CALAMITY (2026): stale June screening lead; re-verify current availability before turning into a task. Prior source: Cinépolis Luxury Cinemas Coconut Grove screenshot /opt/data/cache/images/img_16b0e6087728.jpg.
  • Frost Science — Extreme Sports: Beyond Human Limits: interactive Frost Science Museum exhibit in Miami focused on extreme sports and human performance. Good potential weekend activity with wife and son: indoor/weather-safe, family-friendly, hands-on, and connected to wingsuit flying/parachute-like visuals from the ad Jenffry saw. On view through Monday September 7, 2026; included with museum admission. Pair with a simple Downtown/Brickell/Bayside meal only if energy allows. Source: https://www.frostscience.org/exhibition/extreme-sports-beyond-human-limits/
  • Play Baldur's Gate 3 with Ray: co-op gaming session; good as a shared hobby outing when both schedules line up.

Bucket list

Longer-range life/adventure ideas that should stay visible without becoming TickTick clutter. Raw source preserved at raw/reference/bucket-list-2026-06-01.md.

Adventure and awe

  • Plane confidently in windsurfing: progress until planing is reliable, then take dedicated windsurfing trips to Hood River and Bonaire.
  • Grand Canyon: South Rim, sunrise/sunset, hike below the rim, overnight stay; stretch goals include Colorado River rafting, helicopter tour, and rim-to-rim hike.
  • Southwest parks road trip: Grand Canyon, Zion, Bryce, Monument Valley, Antelope Canyon, Horseshoe Bend, and Sedona.
  • Pacific Coast Highway road trip: San Francisco, Big Sur, Monterey, Santa Barbara, Malibu, and Los Angeles.
  • Northern Lights in Alaska: plan a trip around aurora viewing.
  • Paragliding: experience flight from above.
  • Ride on a jet ski: fast water/adrenaline experience.
  • Travel on a boat: meaningful day or trip on the water.
  • Go on a cruise: multi-day travel by ship.
  • Drive a Ferrari: experience a dream performance car.

Travel and culture

  • Solo trip: one meaningful independent trip for freedom and reflection.
  • Japan: culture, food, history, tradition, and modern life.
  • Return to Cuba: reconnect with family history, people, culture, and memory.
  • Yellowstone National Park: geysers, wildlife, hot springs, and big landscapes.
  • Florence, Italy: art, architecture, food, and Renaissance history.

Growth, creativity, and self-expression

  • Best shape of my life: strength, endurance, energy, confidence, and discipline.
  • MMA classes: physical/mental challenge and practical skill.
  • Perform comedy: get on stage in front of an audience.
  • Write a short story: finish and share one complete creative piece.
  • Create a short film: bring an idea to life through filmmaking.
  • Start a film club: create a space to watch and discuss great films.

Freedom and life-building

  • Build a successful business: create something valuable and durable.
  • Buy a home: own a stable home base.
  • Achieve financial freedom: build enough financial independence to expand choice and security.

Connection and legacy

  • Have/make great friends: build a strong circle of real friendships.
  • Take Mom on a memorable trip: plan an experience she will not forget.
  • Host a memorable dinner or gathering: good food, music, conversation, and people Jenffry cares about.
  • Start an annual trip tradition: recurring adventure with friends or family.
  • See a favorite artist live: Bruno Mars or Imagine Dragons.

Solo-outing preference

A recent solo-outing preference was:

  • Sunday solo plan was just for Jenffry, not family/baby-friendly.
  • He wanted to remove the Bronco roof.
  • Preferred activity should keep him close to the parked car.
  • Fishing from a pier or waterfront parking was the preferred vibe.

Planning implication

Leisure should restore agency, not become avoidance.

Current constraint signal

2026-06-17 clarity signal: Jenffry wants to build what he can, but feels defeated because the life he wants includes gym, movies, pickleball, business work, a film club, off-roading, travel, attending a UFC fight, windsurfing, camping, and similar aliveness/adventure tracks. Marriage and parental responsibilities currently make that life feel impossible. Treat this as an agency/aliveness constraint, not merely a leisure wishlist: future planning should test which activities are still buildable in small seasonal blocks, which need family/co-parenting negotiation, and which are symbolic of the larger unlived-life pressure.

Life Admin

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Maintenance renamed to Life Admin.
  • Current priority: immigration/citizenship and other concrete admin obligations without over-fragmenting, in service of an efficient and stable life.
  • Active signals: green card / USCIS follow-up, admin obligations, home/system maintenance.
  • Open loops: citizenship/immigration path timing.
  • Next concrete move: use TickTick only for concrete admin actions.

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Keep life organized in an efficient, stable way so admin obligations do not create chronic background stress.
  • 12-month proof: Immigration/citizenship path is actively managed; key documents, appointments, home/admin obligations, and follow-ups are concrete and dated when needed.
  • Current monthly target: Protect USCIS follow-up timing and avoid broad admin clutter.
  • This week's commitment: Handle only admin actions that are due, deadline-bound, or blocking something.
  • Daily rule / next move: One admin action only when it is concrete; otherwise leave strategy in the wiki.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: This page owns admin/immigration strategy; TickTick owns dated admin tasks; open loops own unresolved decisions.

Long-term direction

My life is organized in an efficient and stable way.

Preserved migrated source note

This page absorbed the older concepts/maintenance.md material during the life-area migration. The current life-area name is Life Admin: necessary admin, home/system obligations, legal/immigration follow-through, and other concrete upkeep that should not become its own life area.

Life Admin stays plain for now: no standing subcategories unless a future stream clearly earns one. Immigration/citizenship context lives here because it is an important necessary process, but it is not a separate top-level life area.

Life Admin goal: immigration / citizenship path

Become a U.S. citizen when eligible and when the immigration path allows it.

12-month desired state

Ideally, Jenffry has his green card within 12 months. If that is not fully within his control, the controllable goal is to create clarity and take every reasonable action available on the case after 2+ years pending. The green card matters because it affects travel and job security.

Observable success for this goal has two layers:

  • Outcome layer: green card received within 12 months, if the process allows it.
  • Control layer: if the green card is still pending, Jenffry is not passively waiting. He has checked the case status, normal processing time, documents/notices, and practical escalation options, then taken the reasonable next step available.

This goal is considered good enough for the current 12-month goals pass. The remaining work is execution, not more planning.

Monthly check-ins should review this path as a standing item so it does not become passive waiting. The review should ask: is the case still pending, has status/processing time changed, are documents/notices current, is any escalation/attorney/congressional/ombudsman option now appropriate, and does TickTick need a new concrete next action?

Current active next action

Status as of 2026-05-18:

  • Jenffry received a USCIS email reply.
  • The reply means another request should be submitted in about 2 months.
  • Keep the email reply and the request timing handy so the follow-up does not drift.

Reasoning captured 2026-05-11 and updated 2026-05-18:

  • The original fresh USCIS outside-normal-processing inquiry was still the right first move.
  • USCIS has now replied, so the next step is a new request after the waiting period in the reply.
  • If a non-delay issue appears — lost documents, clear USCIS error, missed notice, stuck approved expedite, or urgent humanitarian issue — earlier Ombudsman/attorney/congressional escalation may still make sense.

Execution sequence:

  1. Preserve the USCIS reply email.
  2. Record the next-request timing from the email so the follow-up happens in about 2 months.
  3. When that window opens, submit the next USCIS request.
  4. If a new problem appears before then, reassess escalation sooner.

TickTick should hold only the concrete follow-up when its due date is clear; the broad immigration/citizenship goal stays here in the wiki.

TickTick boundary

  • The broad goal Become a US Citizen should not stay active in TickTick as a container task.
  • Concrete immigration actions can live in TickTick when they are specific, dated, and actionable.
  • Strategic context, goal framing, and long-running citizenship path context belong here.

areas/my-life-story.md

Long-term direction: Re-author the life story and personal philosophy together so painful chapters are specific and meaningful rather than globalized into “my life is over,” and so Jenffry can name what he believes, values, refuses, and chooses now. 12-month p

No resolved wikilinks.

My Life Story

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Re-author the life story and personal philosophy together so painful chapters are specific and meaningful rather than globalized into “my life is over,” and so Jenffry can name what he believes, values, refuses, and chooses now.
  • 12-month proof: A simple chapter scaffold plus a personal-philosophy scaffold contain enough raw memories, reframes, values, and principles to support identity repair, future excitement, grounded acceptance, and chosen action.
  • Current monthly target: Begin the integrated life-story + personal-philosophy project by defining the working philosophy scaffold and selecting the first story chapter/fragment; do not force a finished autobiography or manifesto.
  • This week's commitment: Hold one bounded project session to shape the scaffold or capture one fragment/principle pair only if it helps; do not turn this into homework or reassurance checking.
  • Daily rule / next move: If a fragment appears, write it as raw material; otherwise leave it alone.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: This page owns the story structure; TickTick Habit Log may provide raw fragments; mental-health reviews interpret only when useful.

Dashboard contract

The My Life Story dashboard tab is a nested structured timeline, not a journal dump.

The story may remain blank until source material is supplied. The purpose of the tab is to provide a stable storytelling architecture so raw memories can be placed without forcing a finished autobiography too early.

2026-07-20 project decision: this is now the next major Sapho/Jenffry project — integrate Jenffry's life story with a comprehensive personal philosophy in a way that is meaningful to him. The artifact should not be only memoir and should not be only abstract principles. It should connect lived chapters with chosen values, duties, standards, sources of meaning, and the kind of man/father/partner/creator Jenffry is trying to become.

Starting framework supplied by Jenffry:

  • Personality / operating style: INTJ, from Michael Caloz Cognitive Functions Test results. Top fits from the provided result link: INTJ 97, ENTJ 85, INTP 71. Raw function scores: Te 12, Ni 11, Ne 8, Ti 7, Fi 5, Si 2, Se 1, Fe 0. Use this as a self-understanding lens, not a cage or excuse.
  • Philosophical starting point: Aristotle's eudaimonic ethics / eudaimonia — the project should ask what a flourishing life looks like for Jenffry in practice, through virtue, chosen action, friendship, contemplation, duty, embodied life, and meaningful work.
  • Prior system source: the completed TickTick note System is preserved at raw/reference/ticktick-system-note-2026-07-20.md. It contributes the earlier purpose, values, filters, pillars, lifetime goals, and axioms below.
  • Jordan Peterson source themes: Jenffry says all listed Peterson themes are relevant starting material: truth, responsibility, voluntary confrontation with chaos, aiming upward, ordering one's house, meaning through worthy burden, resentment as a signal, disciplined dangerousness/capability, sacrifice for the greater thing, order/chaos balance, and fatherhood/masculine responsibility. The currently live theme is resentment as a signal: resentment should be treated as evidence of something avoided, unspoken, unfairly carried, or misaligned — not as automatic permission to blame, withdraw, or act impulsively.

Extracted starting material from the TickTick System note:

  • Purpose: climb the hierarchy of competence in business and storytelling.
  • Core values: Achievement, Freedom, Valuable.
  • Goal/task filters: achievement asks for excellence, tangible outcome, proof/validation, and competence/reputation impact; freedom asks whether the action is chosen, worth limited time, and increases autonomy; value asks whether it contributes to others and deepens transactional or intrinsic connection.
  • Pillars: The Craft / storytelling and filmmaking; Wealth & Ventures; Health & Performance; Family & Relationships.
  • Lifetime goals: become a masterful filmmaker/author; build profitable businesses/investments; maintain peak physical and mental vitality; become a stable, supportive, present patriarch with deep lifelong friendships.
  • Philosophical axioms: create order in chaos; truth through empirical proof and peer validation; human nature as self-interested but cooperation-capable; ethical action as movement away from harm and toward benefit.
  • Transmutation theme: pain, inadequacy, betrayal, loneliness, and past failure are to be converted into disciplined competence and future freedom, not resentment or revenge.

Integration caution: the note contains useful fire, ambition, and standards, but some older language is punitive and shame-driven. In the new eudaimonic version, preserve the strength and transmutation while revising away from identity claims like “I am nothing” or “coward” as final truth. The mature version should aim at flourishing, virtue, fatherhood, friendship, craft, embodied vitality, and contribution — not only domination of inadequacy.

Integrated project frame — life story × personal philosophy

Purpose:

  • Turn life-story work from a mood-autopsy into a meaning-making project.
  • Connect what happened to what Jenffry now believes, values, chooses, and refuses.
  • Create a usable philosophy that can guide decisions, not a decorative essay.
  • Preserve painful truths without letting any one chapter define the whole life.

Project target supplied by Jenffry:

  • What the project is supposed to give him: a framework for becoming the man he wants to become; a personal constitution for decision-making; clarity about who he is; a philosophy he can actually live by; and a finished written artifact he can reread.
  • Kind of man he is trying to become: a man who lives up to his potential — strong, smart, financially well, healthy, charismatic, and beloved.
  • First-resolution target man details:
  • Strong: physically powerful and emotionally resilient.
  • Smart: technically/craft intelligent, socially intelligent, and knowledgeable.
  • Financially well: money matters for freedom, status, beauty/experiences, power/options, and creative independence.
  • Healthy: health means being able to enjoy life to the fullest, have all sorts of experiences, and not be limited by health when pursuing goals.
  • Charismatic: attractive to women, respected by men, warm/socially beloved, funny/playful, and calmly commanding.
  • Beloved: loved by friends and family; having a group of people he loves, can count on, and can share life with.
  • Highest purpose: Jenffry wants to become this man so he can make the most of his life.
  • What making the most of life includes: sexual/romantic fulfillment; wealth and freedom; adventure and beauty; physical vitality; close friendships; respect/status; intellectual mastery; contribution to others; being remembered well; and inner self-respect.
  • Hierarchy of life goods:
  • Non-negotiable foundation: physical vitality, close friendships, and inner self-respect.
  • Primary ambitions: wealth and freedom, adventure and beauty, and respect/status.
  • Supporting goods: intellectual mastery, contribution to others, being remembered well, and sexual/romantic fulfillment.
  • Life he refuses: a life where he is physically weak/unhealthy, socially isolated, not proud of himself, dependent on others, and respected by no one.

Working rule:

Every major life chapter should eventually answer two questions: What did this chapter do to me? and What principle do I choose from it now?

Personal philosophy scaffold

Draft domains to define over time:

  1. Reality and truth — how Jenffry wants to face facts, uncertainty, pain, and self-deception.
  2. Self and identity — who he is beyond fear, work performance, relationship status, or a single painful chapter.
  3. Fatherhood and duty — what Nolan/fatherhood requires under any future family structure.
  4. Love, marriage, and desire — what honest love, attraction, responsibility, compassion, and boundaries mean.
  5. Work, ambition, and competence — what kind of builder/designer/businessman Jenffry wants to become.
  6. Body, discipline, and vitality — how health, strength, energy, and self-respect support the life he is authoring.
  7. Friendship, play, and beauty — why hobbies, movies, games, social life, taste, and future excitement are not optional extras.
  8. Money, freedom, and security — what financial stability is for, and what kind of freedom it should protect.
  9. Spirituality, meaning, and mortality — what makes life worth living when it is not the exact life once imagined.
  10. Action code — the short principles Jenffry can actually use when choosing what to do next.

Chapter-to-philosophy method

For each chapter or major memory, capture:

  1. Event / scene: what happened, concretely.
  2. Emotional truth: what it felt like without exaggerating it into the whole life.
  3. Story formed: what belief about self, people, love, work, safety, or the future may have formed.
  4. Cost and gift: what it damaged, delayed, taught, strengthened, or revealed.
  5. Chosen principle: what Jenffry chooses to believe or practice now because of it.
  6. Living proof: what action, habit, relationship, or decision would show that principle is real.

Source boundary: raw fragments can come from direct conversation, therapy insights, TickTick Life story Habit Log notes, processed perception/brain dumps, and explicit project sessions. Do not auto-convert every emotional note into philosophy; this project should be meaningful, not compulsive.

Whole-story structure

The whole life story should follow this narrative arc:

  1. Opening image — what the world felt like at the beginning.
  2. Want / need — what Jenffry wanted externally and needed internally.
  3. Pressure / conflict — the constraint, wound, duty, fear, or obstacle.
  4. Choice — the decision, avoidance, sacrifice, or action that changed direction.
  5. Turning point — the moment after which the old arrangement could not fully continue.
  6. Cost — what was lost, paid, delayed, or carried.
  7. Meaning — what the chapter taught, distorted, healed, or revealed.
  8. Bridge — how the chapter hands momentum to the next one.

Chapter rule

Each chapter must follow the same structure as the whole story:

  • opening image
  • want / need
  • pressure / conflict
  • choice
  • turning point
  • cost
  • meaning
  • bridge

Each individual scene or memory inside a chapter should use the same mini-arc when enough detail exists.

Chapter timeline scaffold

Chapter 1 — Origins

  • Role in whole story: Setup / ordinary world.
  • Known seed: born in Cuba.
  • Missing: early memories, family context, place, emotional tone, formative needs, first pressures.

Chapter 2 — Separation and adaptation

  • Role in whole story: Inciting disruption / crossing a threshold.
  • Missing: major move or separation events, losses, adaptations, identity shifts, who helped, what changed.

Chapter 3 — Identity under pressure

  • Role in whole story: Rising conflict / tests.
  • Missing: school, belonging, masculinity, shame/pride, social status, faith, pressure, rebellion, self-image.

Chapter 4 — Ambition, work, and competence

  • Role in whole story: Agency-building / midpoint reorientation.
  • Missing: work history, ambition, competence, money, career turns, pride, frustration, future drive.

Chapter 5 — Love, marriage, and truth

  • Role in whole story: Intimacy conflict / confrontation with reality.
  • Missing: relationship beginning, hope, attraction, disillusionment, duty, truth signals, choices.

Chapter 6 — Fatherhood

  • Role in whole story: Higher duty / new center of gravity.
  • Known seed: Nolan is central; fatherhood is not a side note.
  • Missing: birth/fatherhood moment, fears, hopes, duty, model of fatherhood, spiritual/emotional leadership.

Chapter 7 — Body, health, and energy

  • Role in whole story: Recovery arc / rebuilding capacity.
  • Missing: body story, health turning points, gym/fitness meaning, energy, confidence, discipline.

Chapter 8 — Friendship, play, and future excitement

  • Role in whole story: Renewal / future chapters stay possible.
  • Missing: friends, play, hobbies, D&D, pickleball, movies, joy, social recovery, community.

Chapter 9 — The next authored chapter

  • Role in whole story: Resolution into choice / forward design.
  • Missing: what Jenffry is choosing now, what he refuses to keep repeating, what future he is authoring.

Source rules

  • Do not force a finished narrative before raw material exists.
  • Do not let painful chapters globalize into “the whole life.”
  • Do not turn the life story into a mood autopsy or reassurance ritual.
  • TickTick Life story Habit Log notes can provide raw fragments.
  • This page owns the dashboard-facing structure for the My Life Story tab.

Relationships

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Relationship clarity, fatherhood duty, and social rebuilding are active; this area is organized by relationship type, goals, contained people, related tasks, and current phase.
  • Current priority: keep partner/marriage truth, fatherhood presence, and social rebuilding distinct while making the next honest action visible.
  • Active signals: Aubrey/marriage clarity, Nolan/fatherhood presence, friend/community rebuilding, current TickTick Relationships tasks, and the latest clarity-session synthesis.
  • Open loops: the marriage/transition truth-telling path remains unresolved; fatherhood duty must stay concrete; social rebuilding needs one small experiment at a time.
  • Next concrete move: draft the honest statement to Aubrey as a truth-testing exercise, protect Nolan-related concrete tasks, and keep social experiments low-clutter.

Relationships covers core relational health: partner/marriage, fatherhood, family, friendships, and social connection. Social rebuilding belongs here, not under Leisure.

Goal spine

  • Long-term direction: Build truthful, responsible relationships: honest partner/marriage structure, reliable fatherhood, and real adult friendship/community.
  • 12-month proof: The partner/marriage structure is honestly chosen and acted on; Nolan experiences a present father; one repeatable social channel is active.
  • Current monthly target: Get clear enough to act on the partner question while staying present as a father.
  • This week's commitment: Finish one relationship-clarity output and one Nolan/family action.
  • Daily rule / next move: Do one truthful or responsible relationship action; do not use fatherhood duty to hide partner truth, and do not use partner pain to weaken fatherhood.
  • Source-of-truth / dashboard rule: partner, fatherhood, and social own their relationship type goals; clarity-sessions preserves evidence; the dashboard shows only the three type cards.

Dashboard relationship model

The Relationships tab should be organized by type of relationship, then by the goals for that type, the people contained in that type, related execution tasks, and the current phase/signal.

Type 1 — Partner / marriage

  • Source: partner
  • Long-term direction: Be in a loving happy relationship with a beautiful woman who's life goals and values are aligned with mine.
  • Current goal: reach and act on the honest partner/marriage truth without hiding behind guilt, resentment, or infinite analysis.
  • People contained: Aubrey.
  • Current phase: truth-testing and ethical conversation/transition planning.
  • Related tasks: honest statement draft, partner/marriage clarity writing, therapist-informed exercises, and any concrete Aubrey conversation/logistics that emerge.

Type 2 — Fatherhood / family duty

  • Source: fatherhood
  • Long-term direction: Be a reliably present father who protects, provides, and instructs while serving as a spiritual and emotional leader to Nolan.
  • Current goal: protect Nolan's experience of a present, consistent, helpful father regardless of the marriage structure.
  • People contained: Nolan; Aubrey when co-parenting/logistical support affects Nolan's stability.
  • Current phase: non-negotiable duty layer; fatherhood should not be collapsed into the marriage decision.
  • Related tasks: Nolan milestones, birthday/logistics, routines, care share, school/activities, and fatherhood clarity outputs.

Type 3 — Social / friendship / community

  • Source: social
  • Long-term direction: Have close friends whom I see regularly and we have fun and do regular activities that I love.
  • Current goal: rebuild adult peer connection and being-seen-as-himself without turning social life into admin soup.
  • People contained: friends, potential friends, tabletop/D&D/community leads, film/social groups, and recurring peer contexts.
  • Current phase: low-clutter rebuilding; keep a small reservoir and choose one concrete experiment at a time.
  • Related tasks: only chosen logistics or specific outreach/events; broader ideas stay in social.

Dashboard guardrails

  • Do not confuse fatherhood duty with marriage obligation.
  • Do not offer Aubrey a fake stay-attempt if the real internal signal is dread/obligation rather than hope/relief.
  • Do not frame separation as escape from responsibility.
  • Do not let analysis replace the next truthful draft/conversation.
  • Do not turn social rebuilding into another planning database.

Phase 3 dashboard signals

The Phase 3 signal layer should stay lightweight and only answer four questions:

  • Clarity stage: what phase is the partner/marriage decision work in right now?
  • Fatherhood presence: are Nolan/family-duty standards visible and backed by concrete tasks when needed?
  • Social experiment status: is social rebuilding a small reservoir or a concrete current experiment?
  • Task concreteness: are Relationships tasks dated/actionable, or are they turning into abstract analysis?

These signals are dashboard aids, not new obligations. If they create admin clutter, simplify or remove them.

Evidence and source pages

  • partner — partner/marriage context, Aubrey-specific goals, and truth-telling constraints.
  • fatherhood — Nolan/fatherhood context, presence standards, and family-duty signals.
  • social — friendship/community rebuilding and social reservoir.
  • clarity-sessions — detailed evidence log and therapist-guided scenario work.

Relationship Clarity Sessions

No resolved wikilinks.

Relationship Clarity Sessions

This page preserves outputs from relationship/fatherhood clarity sessions so Jenffry can revisit the reasoning later without relying on chat memory. These notes are clarity evidence, not final legal/relationship decisions.

Standing clarity frame moved from migrated source

These are durable clarity prompts and context preserved from the old main Relationships page. They stay here because this page is the detailed evidence log.

Relationship/fatherhood clarity aims

Ongoing relationship/fatherhood clarity work should aim for:

  • clearer observations
  • questions
  • scenarios
  • possible futures
  • boundaries
  • regrets to avoid
  • next actions

Avoid circular analysis. For this goal area, the work itself defines the final 12-month answer: Jenffry cannot know the final family/relationship structure in advance because the clarity sessions are the practice layer that will reveal it.

Therapist exercise frame

Use the therapist-guided scenario exercise when a clarity session needs to make tradeoffs visible instead of abstract:

  • scenarios where Jenffry is being selfish while staying in the marriage
  • scenarios where Jenffry is being selfless while staying in the marriage
  • scenarios where Jenffry is being selfish while leaving the marriage / not living in it
  • scenarios where Jenffry is being selfless while leaving the marriage / not living in it

12-month desired state

Jenffry wants to achieve clarity, act on that clarity, and live with the execution in the best and most organized way possible.
