Continuity Steward Capture

Temporary staging area for continuity candidates captured from recent conversations.

This page is not canonical truth. The daily integration pass decides what enters the real wiki. Sensitive or ambiguous items remain staged for review.

Weekly hygiene note — 2026-07-05: Active capture backlog was compressed again. CS-20260704-A/B were merged and integrated as a compact nutrition execution default in areas/health/physical-health.md; TickTick remains the grocery checklist source. CS-20260701-A was processed because areas/leisure.md already has the Pan's Labyrinth pipeline item. Overlapping relationship/fatherhood capture items were compressed into conservative staged clusters without creating a final relationship, fatherhood, custody/legal, decision-log, recurring-review, or dashboard-facing goal conclusion.

Weekly hygiene note — 2026-07-12: No substantial context was deleted. CS-20260707-A was moved from captured to staged-for-review because it is sensitive relationship-trial structure material and should not remain in the generic active-capture queue. CS-20260702-A was moved from captured to deferred because no accepted workout-program change is recorded yet. Older processed entries were compressed into grouped summaries to keep this page from becoming a second archive.

Weekly hygiene note — 2026-07-19: No substantial context was deleted. Low-risk already-routed items were moved out of the generic active-capture queue: CS-20260715-C/D/E and CS-20260716-B/C are now integrated; CS-20260715-A is integrated as a task-routing rule; CS-20260715-I is discarded because the later scheduled park/pickleball item superseded it; CS-20260716-A is discarded as a passed weekly logistics item owned by TickTick/review context. Remaining sensitive or unresolved items were moved from generic captured status to staged-for-review or deferred so the active-capture queue does not become a second archive. No relationship/fatherhood conclusions, finance commitments, open-loop closures, decision-log entries, recurring-review behavior, or dashboard-facing goals were created.

Weekly hygiene note — 2026-06-28: Active capture items were compressed to reduce backlog bloat. Duplicated leisure correction items were processed because the canonical leisure page/dashboard already reflects Minions & Monsters and not Despicable Me 4. Duplicate lab screenshot items were merged into one staged medical candidate. Relationship/fatherhood items remain staged and conservative; no final relationship, fatherhood, custody, or decision-log conclusion was created.

Active captured items

CS-20260723-B — GPT-5.6 routing migration completed and live gateway verified

  • Status: captured
  • Captured at UTC: 2026-07-23T00:00:00Z
  • Source message range: 38851..38869
  • Sensitivity: low-medium — Sapho/Hermes runtime configuration, provider/model routing, backup location, and live gateway behavior; no credential values should be preserved.
  • Suggested destination: system/decision-log.md for the accepted/applied model-routing change if not already recorded; system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md only for any remaining model-routing follow-up. Mark or supersede the unresolved migration framing in CS-20260722-H rather than creating a duplicate open loop. Config, skills, and cron remain the implementation sources.
  • Confidence: high that the migration was applied and verified according to the assistant's execution report; high that it supersedes CS-20260722-H's intermediate “under investigation” state.
  • Action recommendation: During integration, verify the live config and relevant job/skill routing, then replace CS-20260722-H's pending framing with a compact completed decision record. Preserve the rollback path only if still present and useful; never store OAuth tokens or other credentials.
  • Candidate signal: Sapho reported successful live responses from gpt-5.6-sol, gpt-5.6-terra, and gpt-5.6-luna, while the unsuffixed gpt-5.6 alias remained unsupported through ChatGPT Codex authentication. Active routing was changed to Sol for main Sapho conversations and high-consequence continuity/system work, Luna for title generation, session search, and the paused lightweight relationship prompt, with Terra available for balanced everyday work. OpenRouter fallback remained disabled. The Continuity Steward was moved from GPT-5.5 to GPT-5.6 Sol, Python syntax was validated, the Hermes operating skill's obsolete 5.5/5.4 recommendation was updated, and the live Telegram gateway was reportedly already processing this conversation through Sol without restart. A pre-change config backup was retained at /opt/data/backups/config-before-gpt56-20260722T160250Z.yaml.
  • Why it may matter: This establishes the current model-quality/cost routing policy, resolves the prior migration uncertainty, and provides a rollback point if GPT-5.6 routing causes reliability or usage problems. Future system advice should not revert to the obsolete GPT-5.5/5.4 policy.
  • Proposed canonical wording: Sapho model-routing decision, 2026-07-22: GPT-5.6 migration was applied and live-tested through the OpenAI Codex provider. Use gpt-5.6-sol for main Sapho conversations and high-consequence continuity/system work; use gpt-5.6-luna for lightweight auxiliary work such as titles and session search; keep gpt-5.6-terra available as the balanced everyday option. The unsuffixed gpt-5.6 alias is not supported through the current ChatGPT Codex authentication, and OpenRouter fallback remains disabled. A pre-migration config backup exists for rollback.
  • Integration notes: Verify current config before canonicalizing because model routing can change after the transcript. Distinguish verified implementation from public model-positioning claims, which are time-sensitive. Keep exact runtime configuration in config/cron/skills and only the durable rationale/decision in the wiki. Do not store credentials. The lightweight relationship prompt is paused; its model assignment is implementation context, not evidence that the prompt should be reactivated.

CS-20260723-C — Meal-control trial shifts from batch cooking toward resilient low-prep assembly

  • Status: captured
  • Captured at UTC: 2026-07-23T00:00:00Z
  • Source message range: 38870..38878
  • Sensitivity: low-medium — nutrition, body-composition support, food spending, and time/capacity constraints; no diagnosis or medical treatment.
  • Suggested destination: areas/health/physical-health.md or the relevant nutrition owner page as a refinement to the five-day experiment in CS-20260722-I. system/current-strategy.md only if Jenffry accepts the trial as a current focus. TickTick remains the execution source for any separately requested grocery or preparation tasks.
  • Confidence: high that the low-friction breakfast/lunch direction matches Jenffry's established preferences; medium on adoption because this message records Sapho's recommendation and no visible user acceptance or trial result follows.
  • Action recommendation: During integration, merge with CS-20260722-I rather than adding a second meal-prep plan. Preserve the new 30–45 minute preparation ceiling and the low-/busy-/overloaded-week fallback structure as proposed experiment design, not as a completed habit or permanent diet decision. If the earlier 60–75 minute workflow remains staged, supersede it with the lower-friction version.
  • Candidate signal: Sapho assessed that traditional full meal prep would compete with Jenffry's scarce time and recommended a minimum-viable food-control system: assemble Greek yogurt, protein powder, frozen berries, and a measured topping for breakfast; assemble lunch from cooked chicken, microwave rice, steam-in-bag vegetables, and one or two sauces. The resilience rule was to buy back preparation time with ingredients rather than uncontrolled takeout: cook chicken and batch lunches in a low-pressure week, buy cooked chicken in a busy week, and use a known healthier restaurant or prepared-meal fallback with predictable calories in an overloaded week. The proposed test was five workdays of breakfast/lunch control with all advance preparation capped at 30–45 minutes, reviewing actual prep time, satiety, adherence, approximate lunch-cost comparison, and main friction.
  • Why it may matter: This materially improves the odds that the nutrition/finance routine survives real workweek pressure. It reframes success as predictable food control with selective convenience rather than demanding a large Sunday cooking ritual, and it supplies graceful degradation instead of an all-or-nothing failure mode.
  • Proposed canonical wording: Nutrition experiment refinement, 2026-07-22: Prefer low-prep breakfast/lunch assembly over traditional full meal prep. Run one five-workday trial with advance preparation capped at 30–45 minutes. Use a no-cook high-protein breakfast and a lunch assembled from convenient cooked protein, microwaveable carbohydrate, frozen vegetables, and simple sauces. Scale effort by capacity: batch-cook in low-pressure weeks, buy prepared components in busy weeks, and use a known calorie-predictable prepared-food fallback when overloaded. Review preparation time, satiety, adherence, approximate cost, and friction before adjusting.
  • Integration notes: This is Sapho advice, not yet an accepted permanent routine or evidence of adherence. Keep dinner flexible and avoid duplicating grocery/task details in the wiki. Do not freeze exact calories, costs, brands, or restaurant choices without user confirmation and current evidence. Deduplicate against the existing stable preference for simple breakfast/lunch meal prep.

CS-20260723-A — Late-August family workcation search shifts away from Naples toward more distinctive destinations

  • Status: captured
  • Captured at UTC: 2026-07-23T00:00:00Z
  • Source message range: 38612..38814
  • Sensitivity: medium — near-term travel with Aubrey and Nolan, remote-work constraints, discretionary household spending, and family/adult experience design; not a relationship, fatherhood, or booking decision.
  • Suggested destination: areas/leisure.md for compact active trip-planning context and the durable travel-fit preference; system/open-loops.md for the unresolved destination/lodging/budget choice. Merge with CS-20260722-J rather than creating a second travel loop. system/current-strategy.md only if Jenffry accepts the trip as a near-term priority; TickTick only after a destination or executable booking/preparation action is selected.
  • Confidence: high that Jenffry rejected Naples because it was not attractive enough and wants broader researched options; medium on the durability of the researched rankings because no replacement destination, lodging, budget, or itinerary was accepted.
  • Action recommendation: During integration, update/supersede the Naples-centered wording in CS-20260722-J. Preserve the trip as unresolved and retain only a compact shortlist if the late-August plan remains active. Treat St. Petersburg, St. Augustine, Sarasota/Siesta Key, Islamorada, Savannah, and Key West as researched options—not decisions—and recheck live prices and work suitability before any booking.
  • Candidate signal: Jenffry explicitly said Naples was not attractive enough and asked Sapho to search for more options. Research then compared historic/cultural, Gulf Coast, and Florida Keys destinations for an August 26-30, 2026 partial family workcation with Aubrey and one-year-old Nolan. Sapho's revised overall recommendation became St. Petersburg, with Sarasota/Siesta Key and St. Augustine as leading alternatives; Islamorada, Savannah, and Key West were more distinctive but generally costlier or less practical. Jenffry did not visibly accept any destination. The durable preference signal is that convenience and polish alone are insufficient: the destination should have enough visual distinction, atmosphere, culture, or beauty to feel worth organizing a trip around, while still supporting serious remote work and Nolan's needs.
  • Why it may matter: This materially changes the active trip search and prevents future advice from reverting to Naples merely because it is close and practical. It also clarifies how to rank options: distinctive atmosphere is a threshold requirement, then work reliability, infant practicality, travel burden, and budget discipline determine the best feasible choice.
  • Proposed canonical wording: Late-August 2026 family-workcation open loop: Naples has been ruled out for this trip because Jenffry does not find it attractive or distinctive enough. Continue comparing destinations that feel genuinely worth traveling to while supporting remote work and travel with Aubrey and Nolan. Current researched leaders are St. Petersburg for the strongest overall mixture, Sarasota/Siesta Key for beach beauty and easier family rhythm, and St. Augustine for historic atmosphere; these are options only, not accepted decisions. Exact destination, lodging, dates, and budget remain unresolved.
  • Integration notes: Merge with CS-20260722-J and remove its stale framing of Naples as the working recommendation. Do not infer acceptance of St. Petersburg, the August 26-30 dates, or any assistant-proposed target/ceiling. Preserve Nolan's presence as a practical childcare, room-layout, and family-experience constraint—not as evidence about Jenffry's feelings toward fatherhood or his relationship with Aubrey. The stable preference for distinctive beauty/atmosphere may already exist in persistent memory; deduplicate rather than saving it again. Travel rates, fees, availability, weather, and drive times are time-sensitive evidence and should be reverified rather than canonically frozen.

CS-20260722-J — Pre-September family partial-workcation under exploration

  • Sensitivity: medium — near-term family travel with Aubrey and Nolan, work obligations, leisure preferences, and discretionary household spending; not a relationship/fatherhood conclusion or accepted booking decision.
  • Suggested destination: areas/leisure.md for compact trip-planning context if still active; system/open-loops.md for the unresolved destination, dates, lodging, and budget decision; system/current-strategy.md only if Jenffry accepts the trip as a current near-term priority. TickTick should own bookings or other concrete actions only after selection.
  • Confidence: high on Jenffry's explicit constraints and desired trip mix; medium on durability because no destination, lodging, dates, or budget was accepted in the visible exchange.
  • Action recommendation: Preserve as a short-lived planning open loop, not a decision. Confirm whether Jenffry wants to proceed, then compare final-date lodging totals and work suitability before booking. Keep Sapho's Naples recommendation and $1,400 ceiling labeled as advice rather than Jenffry's commitment; retire the loop if the late-August window passes or Jenffry declines the trip.

He confirmed Aubrey and Nolan would come, asked Sapho to suggest a budget, and wants a mixture rather than a single-purpose trip. The initial window proposed was August 22-30, with remote work August 24-28. Sapho compared Naples, St. Augustine, and Orlando and recommended a shorter Naples stay, August 26-30, as the best balance of restoration, family time, adult atmosphere, low driving friction, and financial restraint, targeting $1,200-$1,350 all-in with a firm $1,400 ceiling. Jenffry did not visibly accept that recommendation or authorize a booking.
  • Why it may matter: The opportunity is time-bounded and intersects leisure/restoration, family experience, work reliability, and cash discipline. Future planning should remember that the trip must occur before September, include Aubrey and Nolan, support real remote work, and offer a mixed experience without assuming the proposed destination or budget is settled.

Aubrey and Nolan would come, and the desired experience is a mixture of restoration, family activities, and some adult atmosphere. No destination, lodging, dates, or budget has been accepted. Sapho's current working recommendation is a four-night Naples stay around August 26-30, with a planning target of $1,200-$1,350 all-in and a $1,400 ceiling, subject to current refundable lodging totals and workspace verification.
  • Integration notes: Distinguish Jenffry's explicit constraints from Sapho's recommendation. Do not infer that Naples, August 26-30, or $1,400 was accepted; do not book, create tasks, or make relationship/fatherhood interpretations during integration. Reverify prices, availability, cancellation terms, parking/resort fees, Wi-Fi, desk/workspace, and dates before any action because travel inventory is time-sensitive.

CS-20260722-F — hermes-next confirmed as live Sapho runtime, not safe to delete

Persistent memory may already hold the compact active-runtime fact; do not update memory from capture.

Preserve only the durable operational rule and avoid exact process IDs unless reverified.
  • Candidate signal: Sapho inspected /opt/data/hermes-next and reported it is the live Hermes Agent v0.16.0 runtime with active processes for the Telegram gateway and dashboard. Service/script pointers include /run/service/gateway-default/run, /opt/data/scripts/ensure_hermes_dashboard.sh, and /opt/data/home/.local/bin/hermes, all pointing into /opt/data/hermes-next/.venv. /opt/hermes is older at v0.15.1. Preserve Sapho's recommendation as advice, not Jenffry's decision.
  • Candidate signal: Sapho modeled the household impact of paying Mom to babysit Nolan after Aubrey returns to work. Jenffry's one-income baseline was about $2,882.97 biweekly net / about $6,246 monthly; Aubrey is expected to earn similarly but her first paycheck has not landed. Mom's rent is $1,300/month. A proposed $2,000/month babysitting salary would leave Mom about $700/month after rent and would be plausible only after Aubrey's paycheck is real. Sapho later answered that $2,000/month is fair only around 20-25 hours/week, low at 30 hours/week, and not fair for full-time 40-hour childcare; for full-time work Sapho recommended considering about $3,000/month as a family-rate balance, with scheduled-hour limits, childcare-only scope, one-month reassessment, and CPA/payroll check.
  • Why it may matter: This is an active intersection of fatherhood/Nolan care, family support, household cash flow, and fairness to Mom. Future advice should not treat the arrangement as merely “helping with rent” or assume Aubrey's income before confirmed deposits.
  • Proposed canonical wording: Childcare/family-finance open loop, 2026-07-22: Jenffry is considering paying his mother for babysitting Nolan while Aubrey returns to work. Working guardrails: do not lock in a permanent childcare salary until Aubrey's first real paycheck confirms net amount and cadence; define Mom's scheduled hours, duties, start date, payment method, and whether this replaces other childcare costs; distinguish Mom's rent/support needs from the fair value of childcare labor. Current Sapho advice was that $2,000/month is fair for roughly 20-25 hours/week but too low for full-time care; if the role is near 40 hours/week, about $3,000/month may be a more defensible family rate, subject to real cash flow and tax/payroll review.
  • Integration notes: Sensitive staging required. Do not infer Jenffry decided to pay $2,000 or $3,000, that Aubrey's income is confirmed, or that Mom accepted any arrangement. Preserve Nolan/fatherhood stability as a care constraint, not as a relationship/marriage conclusion. Avoid exact legal/tax claims beyond “check with CPA/payroll service.”

CS-20260722-I — Five-day simple meal-prep test refined for breakfast and lunch only

  • Status: captured
  • Captured at UTC: 2026-07-22T16:21:00Z
  • Source message range: 38369..38394
  • Sensitivity: low-medium — health/nutrition/body recomposition planning; no medical diagnosis or treatment advice.
  • Suggested destination: areas/health/physical-health.md or the relevant health/nutrition owner page for compact current nutrition experiment context; system/current-strategy.md only if the five-day test is accepted as an active weekly health focus. TickTick owns grocery/task execution if Jenffry separately asks. Do not update memory or create tasks from capture.
  • Confidence: high that the simplified breakfast/lunch meal-prep system is durable near-term; medium that exact macros should be canonical because they are estimates.
  • Action recommendation: During integration, preserve as a compact experiment rather than a permanent diet rule: five breakfasts and five lunches, dinner flexible, test satiety/taste/prep friction/storage/body response/waste, then adjust. Avoid expanding into dinner planning until the breakfast/lunch system proves usable.
  • Candidate signal: Jenffry asked to plan meal prep “all of it.” Sapho refined a five-day test centered on high-protein, filling, low-friction breakfast and lunch only. Breakfast is a Greek-yogurt protein bowl: about 340 g Greek yogurt, half scoop protein powder, berries, chia, and granola, estimated around 480 calories and 51 g protein. Lunch is a chicken/rice/bean/vegetable bowl: 6 oz cooked chicken, 3/4 cup cooked rice, 2 cups vegetables, 1/3 cup black beans, and sauce, estimated around 660 calories and 67 g protein. Combined breakfast/lunch estimate: about 1,140 calories and 118 g protein. Plan uses neutral chicken plus simple flavor sauces across five days, a focused grocery list, 60-75 minute prep workflow, chicken storage/freezing guardrail, dinner flexibility, optional protein snacks, and a seven-point review after five days.
  • Why it may matter: This converts the broad meal-prep intention into an executable nutrition experiment aligned with body recomposition while respecting Jenffry's preference for simple breakfast/lunch prep only and avoiding TickTick workout/food clutter.
  • Proposed canonical wording: Nutrition experiment, 2026-07-22: Run a five-day breakfast/lunch meal-prep test before expanding into dinners. Default breakfast: Greek-yogurt protein bowl with berries, chia, small granola, and optional protein powder. Default lunch: chicken/rice/bean/vegetable bowl with sauce variety. Aim for high protein and workday satiety, not a rigid full-diet overhaul. Keep dinner flexible; after five days review taste, satiety, portion size, prep friction, storage quality, body response, and waste before adjusting.
  • Integration notes: Keep macros as estimates, not medical/nutrition prescriptions. Do not canonicalize every sauce/flavor unless useful. Preserve USDA-style storage caution only compactly. If TickTick grocery tasks already exist, avoid duplicating them in the wiki.

Verify current disk state and script contents before recording exact numbers or marking any open loop resolved.
  • Candidate signal: Jenffry asked what was taking space and increasing. Sapho reported two real growth sources: /opt/data/hermes-agent/.git had grown to about 3.5 GB because it tracked many upstream branches and had 1.3 GB of Git tmp_pack_* garbage; /opt/data/state.db was about 831 MB and grows with session/message history, FTS, and trigram indexes. Sapho reported fixing the Git runaway by deleting temp packs, pinning the checkout to origin/main, pruning stale refs, running Git garbage collection, and adding cleanup handling to /opt/data/scripts/monthly_disk_cleanup.sh. Backups were described as bounded but growing with state.db. Large mostly-static directories were classified separately, with recommendation not to touch state.db yet and to consider a controlled session database retention/archive plan later.
  • Why it may matter: This explains a reliability-risk incident and prevents future disk investigations from rediscovering the same cause. It also protects continuity by flagging that session-history pruning is a strategic decision, not a casual cleanup action.
  • Proposed canonical wording: Sapho storage signal, 2026-07-22: The immediate runaway disk problem was reportedly the Hermes source checkout Git metadata/temp-pack accumulation under /opt/data/hermes-agent/.git, caused by broad upstream ref tracking and leftover tmp_pack_* files. Cleanup reportedly pinned the checkout to main, pruned stale refs/temp packs, ran Git GC, and added recurring cleanup safeguards. Remaining long-term growth source: /opt/data/state.db, the session/message SQLite database with search indexes. Do not prune session history casually; next durable decision should be a controlled session retention/archive/index plan if disk pressure returns.
  • Integration notes: Verify current filesystem/script state before canonicalizing exact GB values or saying resolved. Keep implementation details in scripts/cron and operational logs, not in broad strategy pages. Do not delete or prune state.db without explicit retention/archive approval because it affects continuity/search.

Preserve only the refined durable signal if useful: Jenffry's gym identity has shifted from needing consistency to needing balanced progression. Lower/Core is the main routine-balance repair point and pull-ups are the clearest stalled movement. Avoid a full program redesign unless Jenffry explicitly accepts one. If made current, use the proposed two-week target as a reviewable health focus, not automatic TickTick clutter.
  • Candidate signal: Jenffry asked Sapho to analyze his gym performance. Sapho reported that recent Hevy data shows strong consistency — 24 workouts in 60 days, 12 in 30 days, average 2.5-day gap, longest gap 4 days, and a Push / Pull / Lower routine. Pressing/accessory lifts and lower-body strength potential are improving, with notable increases in bench, shoulder work, curls, lateral raises, leg press, and Romanian deadlift. Upper body is leading. Lower/Core sessions are less frequent/shorter/lower-volume than Push/Pull, often around 6–12 working sets versus Push/Pull around 20–22. Pull-ups are stable at 8 reps rather than progressing, and seated row/triceps pushdown are not clearly progressing. Sapho graded overall performance B+ trending toward A if Lower/Core balance improves.
  • Why it may matter: This provides a concrete training-review frame that prevents unnecessary routine churn. The durable next step is not “get motivated” or redesign everything; it is to protect Lower/Core volume and add a simple pull-up progression rule while maintaining the consistency that is already working.
  • Proposed canonical wording: Gym performance signal, 2026-07-21/22: Jenffry's lifting routine is now consistent enough that the main issue is balanced progression rather than restarting. Recent Hevy analysis showed roughly 3 workouts/week with short gaps between sessions. Upper-body work is leading and progressing; Lower/Core shows strength potential but is under-protected because sessions are shorter and lower-volume than Push/Pull. Pull-ups are a clear stalled movement. Near-term repair: keep the current Push/Pull/Lower system, make each Lower/Core session a real 10–12+ working-set session, and add one simple pull-up progression rule.
  • Integration notes: Hevy remains the workout source of truth; verify current Hevy data before preserving exact numbers or strength estimates. Treat dumbbell/weight estimates cautiously because Hevy logging may differ by total weight vs per-hand convention. Do not turn this into fixed PR claims, a medical recommendation, a new workout program, or TickTick workout clutter. If integrated with CS-20260721-G, prefer one compact health/progress note over duplicate gym-analysis entries.

Sapho framed this as a eudaimonia-style north star: actualizing the life available as fully as possible, not mere comfort, duty, money, family, ambition, pleasure, virtue, healing, hedonism, or dominance alone.
  • Why it may matter: This gives the life story/personal philosophy project a higher-level aim above the Target Man traits and clarifies what “fullness” means for Jenffry. It also sharpens future goal-alignment checks: Sapho should not reduce his aims to comfort or obligation, but must help integrate desire, freedom, beauty, vitality, friendship, respect, mastery, contribution, legacy, and private self-respect without letting any one good destroy love, duty, health, or integrity.
  • Proposed canonical wording: Personal philosophy north-star draft, 2026-07-21: Jenffry wants to become the Target Man so he can make the most of his life. Working definition of “making the most of life”: a full human life that includes sexual/romantic fulfillment, wealth and freedom, adventure and beauty, physical vitality, close friendships, respect/status, intellectual mastery, contribution to others, being remembered well, and inner self-respect. Draft synthesis: Jenffry’s flourishing life should be embodied, romantic, competent, adventurous, socially loved, respected, intellectually alive, useful, memorable in a good way, and privately self-respecting.
  • Integration notes: Treat this as a draft philosophy signal, not a rigid identity claim or accepted complete constitution. Do not infer marriage, separation, fatherhood, sex/romance, financial, career, or relocation decisions from these goods. Preserve the sensitive distinction that fatherhood/family duty may need integration with the selected goods even though “fatherhood done well” and “deep love/family” were not selected in this message range. Avoid rewriting the omission as rejection of Nolan/fatherhood or family; stage it only as a signal that the next hierarchy questions may need careful integration. The next unresolved project question is how Jenffry ranks the selected goods into non-negotiable, primary, and supporting categories.

  • Action recommendation: During integration, verify whether areas/my-life-story.md already contains the project purpose and Target Man v1. If present, mark this item integrated/deduped rather than duplicating. If missing, integrate compactly as the project's north-star target and first-resolution Target Man section, preserving it as a working draft. Add or preserve an open loop only for the unanswered next prompt: “What is the highest purpose of becoming that man?”
  • Candidate signal: Jenffry answered the two target-first questions for the life story/personal philosophy project. He said the project is supposed to give him a framework for becoming the man he wants to become, a personal constitution for decision-making, clarity about who he is, a philosophy he can actually live by, and a finished written artifact he can reread. He described the target man as someone who lives up to his potential: strong, smart, financially well, healthy, charismatic, and beloved. In first-resolution answers, he defined strong as physically powerful and emotionally resilient; smart as technical/craft intelligent, socially intelligent, and knowledgeable; money as serving freedom, status, beauty/experiences, power/options, and creative independence; health as enabling him to enjoy life fully, have varied experiences, and not be limited in accomplishing his goals; charisma as attraction to women, respect from men, warmth/social belovedness, humor/playfulness, and calm commanding presence; and beloved as being loved by friends and family, with a group of people he loves, can count on, and can share life with.
  • Why it may matter: This gives the project a concrete north star and prevents the life-story/philosophy work from drifting into unbounded analysis. It also surfaces a durable value pattern: Jenffry wants power, status, freedom, attraction, competence, health, beauty, and excellence, but also love, family, friendship, warmth, shared life, and trust. Future philosophy work should integrate both sides rather than collapsing into either cold domination or soft comfort.
  • Proposed canonical wording: Life story/personal philosophy target draft, 2026-07-21: The project should produce a framework for becoming, a personal constitution for decision-making, clarity about who Jenffry is, a livable philosophy, and a rereadable written artifact. Target Man v1: Jenffry is trying to become a man who lives up to his potential — physically powerful and emotionally resilient; technically/craft intelligent, socially intelligent, and knowledgeable; financially well enough to have freedom, status, beauty/experiences, power/options, and creative independence; healthy enough to enjoy life fully and not be limited in accomplishing goals; charismatic in a way that is attractive to women, respected by men, warm/socially beloved, funny/playful, and calmly commanding; and beloved by friends and family he loves, can count on, and can share life with. Working synthesis: become powerful enough to be free, disciplined enough to be trustworthy, warm enough to be loved, and truthful enough not to betray himself.
  • Integration notes: Treat this as a working draft, not a rigid identity claim. Do not infer relationship, marriage, fatherhood, sex/attraction, or financial decisions from the target traits. Preserve the tension between excellence/power/freedom and love/warmth/shared life as a useful design constraint for the philosophy. Avoid hardening “beloved” into a demand that everyone admire him or “status” into the only money goal. The next unresolved project question is the highest purpose above the traits: “I want to become this man so that I can ______.”

  • Candidate signal: Jenffry said he is considering doing a test period with his wife and noted that a flat pros/cons list misses unequal weighting, specifically Nolan. Sapho reframed Nolan as a high-weight constraint and sacred duty rather than one ordinary point: any path must protect fatherhood, stability, and Nolan's emotional security, but that does not automatically require staying married. The marriage-specific truth to test remains whether there is enough attraction, pride, desire, chosen husbandhood, and aligned future for a real marriage, versus a stay-pull driven mainly by comfort, warmth, guilt relief, family image, fear, and hope. Sapho recommended that any test period be bounded, explicit, and truth-oriented — roughly 6–8 weeks, 90 days max — with measured signals around romantic desire vs reduced panic, pride/attraction/connection vs comfort, freely moving toward Aubrey vs performing effort from guilt, fatherhood protected without romantic self-deception, and discussion of the real issue rather than only household behavior.
  • Why it may matter: This sharpens the decision framework for one of Jenffry's most sensitive active life questions. It prevents future reasoning from flattening Nolan into either “just one pro” or an automatic stay-married command, while also guarding against a vague test period that temporarily soothes guilt or panic without answering the real marriage question.
  • Proposed canonical wording: Relationship/fatherhood clarity signal, 2026-07-17: Jenffry is considering a marriage test period and specifically recognized that Nolan cannot be weighted like an ordinary pros/cons item. Working frame: Nolan/fatherhood is a high-weight duty and stability constraint — any path must protect Jenffry's presence, Nolan's emotional security, and non-impulsive co-parenting/family stability — but it is not a simple veto that automatically means staying married. A test period would only be clean if it is bounded, explicit, and truth-oriented: testing whether the marriage can become genuinely chosen, romantic, desired, and aligned, not merely calmer, more tolerable, or guilt-soothing.
  • Integration notes: Preserve sensitive distinctions. Do not infer that Jenffry decided to do the test period, decided to stay, decided to separate, or accepted any specific length/criteria. Keep Nolan/fatherhood duty separate from romantic self-deception and from relief/escape impulses. Avoid wording that turns Sapho's recommendation into Jenffry's decision or Aubrey's agreement. If integrated, link/deduplicate with CS-20260707-A and keep as relationship clarity/fatherhood guardrails rather than dashboard-facing goal, TickTick task, or decision-log entry.

Sapho drafted the OFSEC loop — Open, Follow, Share, Expand, Close — plus the clean-rep standard: one clean interaction rep does not require a new friend, phone number, perfect confidence, or no awkwardness; it means opening once, asking one follow-up, sharing one small personal detail, and closing without self-attack. Jenffry chose to save the framework to the wiki, not add a TickTick task.
  • Why it may matter: This is a durable self-development priority under Relationships/Social, distinct from leisure planning and partner/fatherhood work. A concrete framework can turn vague social anxiety or loneliness pressure into practice reps, future social-channel experiments, and reviewable progress without making friendship into admin work.
  • Proposed canonical wording: Social development priority: Jenffry wants to work on his social life and social skills through a practical interaction guide. Current framework: OFSEC — Open with a low-pressure situational question/comment; Follow with one genuine follow-up; Share one small piece of himself; Expand if there is energy toward recommendations/groups/repeatability; Close lightly. Practice standard: aim for one clean rep, not a perfect interaction or instant friendship.
  • Integration notes: Treat as Relationships → Social, not Leisure only. Keep partner/fatherhood distinctions separate. Since message 37341 says the wiki was already updated at areas/relationships/social.md, integration should verify and log/deduplicate rather than duplicate content. If converted later into a skill, keep the skill action-shaped and leave social context in the wiki.

Do not create tasks, calendar items, or canonical decisions from this capture alone.

The working frame is an honest, time-boxed experiment to test whether the marriage can become mutually chosen, desired, and sustainable — not a false promise to stay, a divorce countdown, or a way to avoid pain. Candidate structure discussed: roughly 8–12 weeks, with Sapho recommending a 10-week honest repair trial; shared success criteria should include honest willingness, movement toward romantic/sexual desire or chosen partnership, Aubrey feeling genuinely wanted rather than managed, protection of Nolan/family life from pressure dynamics, and capacity for hard truth without collapse or punishment.
  • Integration notes: If integrated, include guardrails as signals rather than final rules: no fake reassurance; no promised outcome beyond effort/honesty/responsibility; no sex/affection as performance proof; no punishing honesty; no repeated separation threats during conflict; no making Aubrey responsible for fixing Jenffry's desire; no secret divorce preparation while presenting full repair; no using Nolan as an argument. Candidate cadence: start conversation to define commitments, weekly 30–60 minute check-ins, midpoint review around week 4–6, final review with known possible outcomes: continue repair, extend once with changes, transition toward separation, or stop early if harmful/coercive/false.

  • Source message range: 35326..35357
  • Sensitivity: medium — depressed emotional state, workplace trust/status concerns, performance dip, and life-direction fear.
  • Suggested destination: work/career owner page if useful; system/open-loops.md only for a bounded recovery loop.
  • Staged summary: Jenffry experienced a non-crisis but serious work-trust/performance dip state: coworkers' attitudes felt changed, performance felt lower, and this triggered loss-of-control/life-direction fear. He clarified it was not crisis-level and fixable. Treat as a bounded work execution/social-signal recovery problem, not proof of betrayal or life failure. Near-term recovery frame: identify the concrete work gap, make one non-accusatory trust-repair check-in, and execute one visible win.

CS-20260621-LIFE — Compressed staged constrained-week/selfhood/leisure calibration cluster

  • Status: staged-for-review
  • Covers prior items: CS-20260621-A and CS-20260625-A.
  • Sensitivity: medium — life-structure/focus signals that overlap with relationship/family strain.
  • Suggested destination: system/current-strategy.md during an accepted strategy refresh; possible leisure owner page only if needed.
  • Staged summary: The weekly minimum viable life stack is a temporary floor for constrained weeks, not Jenffry's desired life. When the stack feels like life on scraps, run a time-reclaim pass: fixed obligations, negotiable obligations, leaks, protected selfhood time, and one realistic first 90-minute block. When tired, low-friction group leisure at home can count as deliberate restoration/social contact rather than failure to pursue novelty, but restoration should not become default life shrinkage. If a higher-excitement outing is skipped, preserve the weekly intentional outing/novelty aim with another real candidate. In marriage ambiguity, keep casual group warmth distinct from romantic reassurance or relationship processing.

Processed items

Processed items are compressed here briefly, then may be pruned further by a later weekly wiki improvement loop when safe.

  • 2026-07-19 weekly improvement: moved already-routed health, server-storage, social-framework, overload-guardrail, and childcare-routing items out of active capture; discarded superseded/passed leisure logistics; moved Mom housing/finance/work-organization, household-season, and sensitive relationship/fatherhood candidates to staged/deferred review rather than leaving them in generic captured status.
  • 2026-07-12 weekly improvement: older processed history was compressed into grouped summaries. Active sensitive relationship/fatherhood items remain staged; no final relationship, fatherhood, custody/legal, decision-log, recurring-review, or dashboard-facing goal conclusion was created.
  • 2026-07-05 processed group: compressed relationship/fatherhood staged capture items into conservative clusters, compressed constrained-week/leisure calibration, integrated the accepted breakfast/lunch meal-prep default into areas/health/physical-health.md, and processed Pan's Labyrinth because areas/leisure.md already owns the pipeline item.
  • 2026-06-28 processed group: compressed active capture backlog, merged duplicate testosterone/lab screenshot items into CS-20260624-A, processed duplicate Minions correction candidates, and integrated compact fitness-routine and food-tracking preference signals into areas/health/physical-health.md while leaving sensitive relationship/fatherhood material staged.
  • 2026-06-21 to 2026-06-23 processed group: compressed the constrained-week planning/selfhood/time-reclaim candidates, integrated the stopped-daily-check-ins update into current-strategy.md and check-in-responses.md, reflected the accepted Jun 22–28 weekly goal and weekly-planning calibration in current-strategy.md, confirmed the Life Admin dashboard command-center preference was already reflected in system/dashboard.md, discarded the superseded weekly stack, and processed the completed Steward repair/storage item recorded in wiki/log.md.
Continuity Steward
system/continuity-steward.md
degree 3

Status: Active automatic continuity layer for Sapho's wiki; 2026-07-19 weekly hygiene moved already-routed items out of active capture, discarded superseded/passed leisure logistics, and found capture/integration cadence current. Current priority: keep routine

No resolved wikilinks.

Continuity Steward

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active automatic continuity layer for Sapho's wiki; 2026-07-19 weekly hygiene moved already-routed items out of active capture, discarded superseded/passed leisure logistics, and found capture/integration cadence current.
  • Current priority: keep routine wiki-update responsibility out of live Sapho conversation while preventing capture backlog and sensitive staged items from becoming a second archive.
  • Active signals: new Telegram conversation content, capture page backlog, daily integration results, weekly wiki-improvement findings, staged sensitive relationship/fatherhood clusters, Mom housing/finance staging, deferred work/workout/current-season candidates, scheduled-rhythm drift, dashboard-summary freshness, stale Goal Spine dates, and Steward run-state health.
  • Open loops: monitor whether the steward over-captures, misses durable context, creates wiki bloat, leaves sensitive items staged too long, lets current-strategy/Goal Spine targets expire, or repeats weekly-loop/storage failures.
  • Next concrete move: use the Sunday weekly review to refresh accepted July/weekly goals, decide whether Mom housing/finance deserves a canonical loop, and decide whether staged relationship-trial material changes the Aubrey-facing open-loop wording; keep relationship/fatherhood conclusions staged unless Jenffry explicitly accepts them.

Purpose

The Continuity Steward exists because Sapho can miss durable wiki updates while doing live reasoning, emotional support, planning, and tool work.

The Steward is not a second Sapho personality. It is an automatic continuity layer that reviews conversation history separately and owns routine wiki-continuity capture/integration.

Core contract

Continuity Steward v1 is an automatic three-layer continuity system:

  1. A frequent capture pass reviews only new Telegram conversation content since the last reviewed marker and writes candidate durable signals to a temporary capture page.
  2. A daily integration pass reviews captured candidates, deduplicates and filters them, then applies safe canonical wiki updates or stages sensitive items for review.
  3. A weekly wiki improvement loop audits the wiki itself for bloat, stale context, duplicate routing, weak source-of-truth ownership, open-loop hygiene, and strategy freshness.

Sapho is relieved of routine wiki-update responsibility. Sapho edits the wiki directly only when Jenffry explicitly asks for a specific page/file update, when the task itself is wiki-editing, or when safety/integrity requires an immediate correction.

Responsibility split

Sapho owns

  • live reasoning and advising
  • emotional and strategic synthesis
  • planning and task judgment
  • tool execution when directly requested
  • explicit wiki edits when Jenffry asks Sapho to update a specific page/file
  • wiki-editing tasks when the user request itself is to edit/review/update the wiki

Sapho should not need to maintain a background obligation to decide what to save during ordinary conversation.

Continuity Steward owns

  • automatic review of new conversation material
  • candidate capture to the temporary capture page
  • daily canonical integration
  • staged review for sensitive or ambiguous continuity items
  • wiki log entries for actual canonical changes
  • weekly wiki hygiene/improvement
  • anti-clutter filtering

Layer 1 — Capture pass

Cadence: every 3 hours.

Trigger rule: the capture script checks the session database first. If there are no new Telegram user/assistant messages since the last reviewed message marker, it exits silently and does not spend an LLM call.

Input:

  • only new Telegram user/assistant messages since the last capture marker
  • current Continuity Steward policy
  • source-of-truth routing rules

Output:

  • structured candidate items appended to system/continuity-steward-capture.md
  • no canonical wiki mutation
  • updated steward state marker only after successful capture processing

Capture asks: Could this matter later?

Layer 2 — Daily integration pass

Cadence: daily at 4:30 AM Eastern.

Input:

  • active captured items from system/continuity-steward-capture.md
  • canonical routing rules
  • relevant wiki owner pages as needed

Output:

  • safe canonical wiki updates
  • staged items left in capture page when sensitive/ambiguous
  • discarded/processed status markings
  • wiki log entry for actual canonical changes

Integration asks: Should this become canonical now, and where?

Layer 3 — Weekly wiki improvement loop

Cadence: Sunday after daily integration.

Purpose: keep the wiki useful, compressed, and source-of-truth clean rather than allowing capture/integration to create gradual bloat.

The weekly loop reviews:

  • stale or duplicate capture items
  • stale open loops
  • current-strategy freshness
  • dashboard summaries that no longer reflect owner pages
  • duplicated relationship/fatherhood analysis
  • pages becoming archives rather than decision surfaces
  • source-of-truth routing drift
  • Steward health: capture cadence, integration cadence, backlog size, repeated errors, over-capture, and under-capture

The weekly loop may auto-apply low-risk cleanup, but should stage/report major changes.

Canonical routing

Use system/source-of-truth-registry.md as the routing constitution. Key homes:

  • current focus / plan → system/current-strategy.md
  • accepted decisions → system/decision-log.md
  • unresolved questions → system/open-loops.md
  • relationship evidence / clarity work → areas/relationships/clarity-sessions.md
  • fatherhood guardrails → areas/relationships/fatherhood.md
  • partner/marriage structure → areas/relationships/partner.md
  • health patterns/goals → health area owner pages
  • finance strategy → finance owner pages
  • work/career direction → career/work/project owner pages
  • short stable preference → persistent memory candidate, usually staged unless very low-risk
  • repeatable procedure → skill candidate
  • concrete executable task → TickTick candidate, not auto-created unless explicitly allowed
  • raw brain/perception dump → raw inbox only under raw-only rules

Capture item statuses

Use these statuses in the capture page:

  • captured — new candidate waiting for daily integration
  • integrated — applied to canonical wiki
  • staged-for-review — important but needs Jenffry/Sapho review
  • discarded — duplicate, noisy, or not durable
  • deferred — may matter but needs later context
  • memory-candidate — may belong in persistent memory; do not auto-save if sensitive
  • skill-candidate — may belong in a skill/procedure
  • task-candidate — may belong in TickTick only if concrete and allowed

Sensitivity policy

The Steward must be conservative with relationship, fatherhood, and decision material.

Rules:

  • Do not infer “Jenffry decided to divorce” unless Jenffry explicitly accepted that decision.
  • Distinguish Sapho recommendation from Jenffry decision.
  • Save relationship/fatherhood material as signals or working interpretations unless final decisions were explicit.
  • Do not write blunt identity claims that harden raw emotion into false truth.
  • Example: do not save “Jenffry does not want Nolan.” Better: “Jenffry loves Nolan and accepts fatherhood duty, but part of him does not want fatherhood and needs integration rather than denial.”
  • Claims about Aubrey's motives should be framed as Jenffry's reported experience and/or Sapho working interpretation, not as court-like fact unless verified.

Auto-apply vs stage rules

Capture pass

Never mutates canonical wiki pages. It only appends candidate items to the capture page.

Daily integration may auto-apply

  • low-risk explicit-save items
  • clear open-loop additions
  • clear wiki log entries
  • small current-strategy corrections directly stated by Jenffry
  • relationship/fatherhood notes when carefully framed as signals/working interpretations rather than final decisions
  • source-of-truth maintenance that is obvious and reversible

Daily integration must stage

  • decision-log entries unless explicitly decided
  • memory updates unless short, stable, and low-risk
  • major strategy changes
  • sensitive identity claims
  • fatherhood/marriage conclusions
  • claims about Aubrey's intent/motives
  • legal/medical/financial implications
  • ambiguous destination or unclear durability

Never save automatically

  • secrets, tokens, passwords, credentials
  • momentary venting with no durable signal
  • every emotional fluctuation
  • repetitive restatements already captured
  • raw dumps outside their raw-only inbox rules
  • tasks without a concrete action/date
  • diary-like every-message summaries

Notification policy

  • Capture pass: silent unless broken.
  • Daily integration: local/quiet by default; Telegram only for repeated failures or items requiring Jenffry review.
  • Weekly improvement: local by default; short Telegram summary only if it finds a meaningful issue requiring attention.

State

The Steward state file is /opt/data/state/continuity-steward-state.json.

It tracks:

  • last capture run time
  • last reviewed message id
  • last integration run time
  • last weekly improvement run time
  • capture item counter
  • run errors

The marker prevents duplicate review and lets capture inspect only new content.

Current Strategy

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Scannable control tower; 2026-07-19 hygiene found the active weekly goal expires tonight and still needs review acceptance/evidence rather than automatic rewrite.
  • Current priority: clarity-first execution with mental health leading; the next major meaning-making project is integrating Jenffry's life story with a comprehensive personal philosophy.
  • Active signals: one big important task plus one quick win per day; overloaded workday evenings use triage, not moral-test execution; TickTick remains execution-only; life-story/philosophy work should be bounded and meaning-making, not rumination homework.
  • Open loops: keep the active weekly/monthly goals synchronized with open-loops, life-area summaries, and accepted weekly/monthly review outputs; do not let dashboard/admin work displace the July health/relationships/finance/leisure focus.
  • Next concrete move: run the 2026-07-19 weekly review, score the Jul 13–19 commitments from evidence, and then accept/cut/correct the next weekly goal.

This page is the current strategy control tower under the root operating model in sapho-operating-system. It should stay short enough to scan quickly. Detailed life-area context belongs in the area pages; unresolved items belong in open-loops; decisions belong in decision-log.

Now

Current phase

Jenffry is in a clarity-first rebuild, not a generic productivity push.

The system should create:

  • clearer decisions
  • fewer live options
  • realistic next steps
  • stabilizing routines
  • useful experiments
  • reduced fog

Priority order

  1. Health — mental health first; physical-health routines as stabilizer.
  2. Relationships — partner / marriage clarity and fatherhood presence.
  3. Finances — one-income budget stability and no credit-card-interest restart.
  4. Career — strong Princess UX execution, senior UX growth, AI/work leverage, and business experiments only when concrete.
  5. Leisure — restoration, hobbies, outings, and future excitement.
  6. Life Admin — immigration/admin/home obligations kept concrete and contained.

Current operating truth

  • TickTick is the execution layer only: concrete tasks, dates, appointments, and protected review sessions.
  • Sapho/check-ins/wiki are the strategy, planning, memory, progress, and advisor layer.
  • Hevy is the gym source of truth; weekly reviews interpret it.
  • The wiki/dashboard should make the operating system legible without becoming another task system.

Next

Current priority

  • Integrated life-story + personal-philosophy project: re-author Jenffry's life story and define a usable personal philosophy so painful chapters become specific and meaningful rather than globalized into “my life is over.”

Near-term top 3

  1. Life story + personal philosophy: build the scaffold first, then capture chapter/principle pairs in bounded sessions.
  2. Mental-health system: keep support profile, observer-mode reset, bounded processing, and future-excitement work connected to the life-story/philosophy project without feeding overanalysis.
  3. Relationships/fatherhood clarity: continue written clarity sessions without circular analysis; preserve fatherhood duty and truthfulness as philosophy inputs, not automatic conclusions.

Current concrete moves

  • Complete the overdue health baseline actions: book the testosterone/blood-pressure appointment and prepare the lab checklist.
  • Keep the current workday anchored to the payment-page Princess UX task.
  • Start the life-story/philosophy project with a bounded scaffold session: define the personal-philosophy domains and choose the first chapter/fragment to work on.
  • Use weekly/monthly reviews to turn broad goals into a few weekly commitments, not a larger TickTick inventory.
  • Continue cleaning the continuity control surfaces in this order: open-loops → this page → TickTick action firewall → cron/review overlap audit. The first three are now complete; next is the cron/review overlap audit.

Waiting / Blocked

  • Relationship/fatherhood clarity: waiting on the next written clarity session and/or scenario exercise output.
  • Mental-health support profile: active upgrade now; first-pass cascade lives in mental-health, and the life-story/philosophy project becomes the next meaning-making artifact. Remaining support-profile pieces are helpful/unhelpful responses, warning signs, and escalation boundaries.
  • Physical-health targets: system/pipeline marked complete on 2026-05-24; cardiology ultrasound follow-up is done and good per Jenffry; repeat testosterone test improved and follow-up is scheduled as the urology appointment; keep under normal weekly/monthly review with medical input/labs and Hevy data before over-calibrating or changing targets.
  • Finance stability: waiting on clean month-to-month budget data and weekly finance review signals.
  • Princess UX organization: waiting on real work-status classification: done, blocked, Jira-only, collapse, archive, or next action.
  • Immigration: USCIS replied; next request is due in about two months unless a non-delay issue appears.
  • Sapho upgrades: roadmap items wait behind open-loop cleanup, strategy compression, and action-firewall work.

Active weekly goal

Proposed from the 2026-07-19 Sunday weekly review; pending Jenffry acceptance, cuts, or correction.

  • Goal: By Sunday night, 2026-07-26, reschedule the missed ENT follow-up, protect three body sessions or two lifts plus one cardio/pickleball substitute, make one Aubrey/fatherhood container move, and run a finance brake focused on Mom housing cost reality plus no unplanned shopping/dining/leisure spend.
  • Progress: 0% — new proposed week has not started; treat this as pending until Jenffry accepts, cuts, or edits it.
  • Source: 2026-07-19 weekly review evidence: Jul 13–19 completed cardiologist follow-up, two Hevy workouts/42 working sets, three TickTick Gym completions, Weekend Pickleball, Mom housing search, Amazon return, and chair tightening; slipped signals include ENT still active, finance at $866 weekly non-debt spending versus a $376 pace, overdue Life Admin/Career/system clutter, no captured weekly-review reply, and no TickTick habit check-ins in the query window.
  • Next concrete move: Jenffry replies with accepted goals, one cut, and any strategy correction; Sapho then finalizes this section and updates affected area Goal Spines if needed.
  • Planning calibration: center the week on ENT + body rhythm + finance reality; keep relationship/fatherhood to one contained move; choose only one leisure/social anchor and park extra events.

Weekly commitments — proposed 2026-07-20 to 2026-07-26

  1. Health/medical: call ENT to reschedule the missed appointment; protect three body sessions, with minimum success as two lifts plus one cardio/pickleball/walk substitute.
  2. Relationships/fatherhood: make one Aubrey/fatherhood container move — prep, timing choice, or conversation if contained — while protecting family presence.
  3. Finances/life admin: review Mom housing cost reality and weekly finances; hold a spending brake after last week’s over-pace result.
  4. Leisure/social: choose one intentional low-cost leisure/social anchor only; park extra event options unless deliberately selected.

Active monthly goal

Proposed from the 2026-07-01 monthly long-term goals review; pending Jenffry acceptance, cuts, or correction.

  • Goal: By 2026-07-31, protect the health rhythm, cut finance drift back toward budget pace, and convert relationship/fatherhood clarity into one truthful next-step container while keeping leisure to one chosen anchor per week.
  • Progress: 0% — new July goal set from June review evidence; do not inflate progress until commitments are completed or protected in the weekly reviews.
  • Source: 2026-07-01 monthly review evidence: June delivered strong gym consistency and medical follow-through, but finances finished materially over budget and relationship/fatherhood clarity still needs an acted-on container rather than more analysis.
  • Next concrete move: Jenffry replies with accepted July targets, one cut, and any correction; Sapho then finalizes this section and weekly reviews inherit the accepted version.

SMART goal rule

Active goals should be written as one sentence each with:

  • a specific outcome
  • a measurable proof point or count
  • a clear deadline
  • one next concrete move

Keep the goal short enough to scan quickly. If a goal cannot be made SMART yet, move the missing piece to open-loops instead of padding the goal.

This week's operating rule

One big important task plus one quick win per day.

  • The big task must support a goal, key life domain, or necessary operation.
  • Quick wins are allowed, but they should not crowd out the big task.
  • When a workday evening is overloaded, treat the plan as triage rather than a moral test: protect one must-move item, one urgent/quick item if needed, and one useful support action; cap broad admin/finance reviews at a short rough check unless Jenffry explicitly chooses depth.
  • Normal 9–5 Princess UX/UI work is not personal-planning clutter unless it creates after-hours spillover or strategic risk.
  • If health, family, and work conflict, protect mental health and family first, then choose the minimum viable work/admin move.

TickTick action firewall

No TickTick task should exist just because an idea is interesting.

A task belongs in TickTick only when it has at least one of these:

  • a visible next action
  • a real date/deadline
  • an appointment or event
  • a protected review session that creates output
  • a concrete errand/admin action

Otherwise route it to:

  • open-loops for unresolved questions
  • the relevant area page for durable context
  • sapho-upgrade-roadmap for Sapho/Hermes system upgrades
  • inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md or inbox/perception-log.md for raw capture
  • current chat only if it is not durable

Review cadence

  • Daily check-ins: daily morning and night scheduled check-ins were stopped on 2026-06-23 at Jenffry's request; do not expect daily replies or treat their absence as drift unless he reinstates them.
  • Ad hoc daily planning: when Jenffry asks for day planning, choose one big task and one quick win, inspect TickTick enough to avoid overload, and update active weekly-goal progress only from completed or protected commitments.
  • Check-in responses: save Jenffry's replies to scheduled weekly/monthly or reinstated daily check-ins in check-in-responses so future reviews can use them for alignment.
  • Weekly review: review commitments, TickTick alignment, Hevy, finance, weekly progress signals, saved check-in responses, archive candidates, and plan every day of the next week.
  • Monthly review: review the whole goals-to-execution system: long-term bearing, 12-month outcomes, monthly targets, weekly commitments, TickTick alignment, and Sapho process quality.
  • Weekly continuity audit: quietly catch missed durable memory/wiki updates without interrupting unless something is critically broken.

Not the focus right now

  • Generic productivity for its own sake.
  • Endless option collecting.
  • Circular relationship/fatherhood analysis.
  • Saving every detail from every chat.
  • Turning the wiki, dashboard, or TickTick into a giant archive.
  • Building second-order Sapho upgrades before the decision surfaces are clean.
  • Treating task hygiene as sufficient when upstream goals and strategy are unclear.

Deeper pages

  • open-loops — current unresolved loops and pending decisions.
  • decision-log — actual decisions and their implications.
  • sapho-operating-system — root doctrine for Jenffry ↔ World ↔ Sapho, goal loops, inner/outer signals, sensors/actuators, and anti-drift rules.
  • dashboard — dashboard feed contract and progress-tracking surface.
  • health, mental-health, physical-health — health strategy, mental-health support, and physical-health routines.
  • relationships — partner/marriage, fatherhood, family, friendships, and social connection.
  • finances — budget and investing context.
  • career, princess-ux — work, senior UX growth, AI leverage, and business direction.
  • leisure — hobbies, outings, restoration, and future excitement.
  • life-admin — immigration, admin, and home obligations.

Preserved source

The previous merged strategy page contained detailed migrated material from current-plan, focus-map, goals-cascade, and life-rebuild. It was preserved before compression at:

  • raw/reference/current-strategy-pre-compression-2026-05-20.md

Future cleanup can progressively distribute that preserved detail into the relevant area pages if useful. Do not re-expand this page unless the top control tower becomes insufficient for real planning.

Sapho Operating SystemProgress TrackingPerception LogBrain Dump SessionsStrategic OrientationCurrent StrategyHealthRelationships

Dashboard

Root operating model: sapho-operating-system. The dashboard is a cockpit for goal movement and signal quality; it displays source-owned truth and must not become a second wiki or independent task system.

Dashboard summary

  • Status: GitHub Pages dashboard opens as Sapho's Dashboard organized into tabs for Life Admin, Health, Finance, Relationships, Career, Leisure, My Life Story, Sapho System, and Wiki. The Hedonic Calculator is embedded in the Leisure tab.
  • Current priority: keep Life Admin as the default action-oriented command center while giving each major life area, decision aid, and Sapho System its own surface.
  • Active signals: current strategy, editable Life Admin weekly-goal priorities and Goal Spine commitments, active weekly/monthly goal sentences and progress bars, Morning Command Card draft/acceptance surface, live TickTick dated tasks with dashboard-only score controls, Goal Spines, SimpleFIN finance cockpit, Health cockpit, Hevy/pickleball/activity rhythm, Relationships cards, Leisure pipeline, My Life Story scaffold, Sapho System summaries, active cron inventory, skill inventory, SOUL/memory/wiki/system artifact inventory, wiki graph, and expandable page cards.
  • Open loops: active weekly/monthly goal percentages are provisional until check-ins define cleaner scoring; physical-health bars should keep being refined from source data and medical input; task score saving has a local SQLite API/watchdog but still needs a reachable public HTTPS API URL before phone/computer cross-device saving is fully live from GitHub Pages.
  • Next concrete move: weekly and monthly reviews must set active goal sentence and progress percentage in current-strategy.md; the dashboard displays them without becoming the source of truth.

Dashboard feed contract

The dashboard reads from real wiki pages, not duplicate dashboard-only pages. Each dashboard-fed page should maintain a ## Dashboard summary section.

Dashboard-fed pages should use this shape:

## Dashboard summary

- Status:
- Current priority:
- Active signals:
- Open loops:
- Next concrete move:

Rule: the dashboard should be fed by sections inside real pages, not by a duplicate dashboard-only mini-wiki.

Implication:

  • Progress signals belong in progress-tracking.
  • Perception-pattern output belongs in perception-log.
  • Brain-dump review state belongs in brain-dump-sessions.
  • Life-area status belongs in the relevant area page.
  • Dashboard implementation notes and feed rules belong here.

Dashboard-fed pages currently include:

  • strategic-orientation
  • current-strategy
  • health
  • relationships
  • finances
  • career
  • leisure
  • my-life-story
  • life-admin
  • open-loops
  • decision-log
  • progress-tracking
  • perception-log
  • brain-dump-sessions
  • log

Architecture

  • Public URL: https://jenffry90-arch.github.io/sapho-workspace-dashboard/.
  • Fallback URL: https://sapho-workspace-dashboard.netlify.app/.
  • Local generator script: /opt/data/scripts/publish_sapho_dashboard.py.
  • Dashboard-only task score backend: Cloudflare Workers + D1 is the preferred cross-device path for dashboard task/leisure score state; local SQLite API /opt/data/scripts/sapho_task_score_api.py remains a fallback/watchdog on 127.0.0.1:8787.
  • Source-of-truth rule: wiki pages own goals/state, direct source-refresh scripts own mechanical API pulls, the dashboard generator renders/displays, the task-score SQLite database owns dashboard-only task scores, and review jobs interpret trends.
  • Direct source refresh: cron job Daily dashboard source refresh (b7a5b1d24d5c) runs at 09:00, 15:00, and 21:00 UTC (0 9,15,21 * * *) with no_agent=true and local-only delivery. It refreshes dashboard-facing source summaries, syncs the physical-health source page from Hevy body measurements, refreshes the finance cockpit from SimpleFIN, publishes the dashboard to GitHub Pages, and verifies local/live GitHub markers. Reliability note: on 2026-07-05 the publisher was patched to git fetch origin main before git push --force-with-lease so generated-dashboard publishes do not fail with stale remote-tracking info after another publish advances origin/main.
  • Standalone publisher: cron job Publish Sapho dashboard (a8af467ed943) is redundant now that the direct source-refresh job publishes and verifies the dashboard; keep it paused unless there is a deliberate reason to publish before API-backed refresh.
  • Keep this as a map layer, not a task manager.

What the dashboard renders

The current generator scans the live wiki and renders:

  • tabs: Life Admin, Health, Finance, Relationships, Career, Leisure, My Life Story, Sapho System, and Wiki
  • Life Admin tab as the default command center with compact active weekly/monthly goals, editable weekly-goal priority/order controls and Goal Spine commitment rewrites, the Morning Command Card, and dated TickTick task windows with dashboard-only 1–5 score controls. Do not reintroduce the old Strategic top 3 mini-card or other dense strategy/admin helper cards on the landing surface.
  • Health tab with split Mental health and Physical health cockpit panels
  • Finance tab with SimpleFIN budget/cashflow/category cockpit data
  • Relationships tab as a custom, reduced cockpit showing only the three relationship type cards — Partner, Fatherhood, and Social
  • Career and Leisure tabs with lean life-area summaries sourced from the wiki, Leisure cockpit signals, Pipeline, Bucket list, and embedded Hedonic Calculator
  • My Life Story tab as a nested structured timeline scaffold
  • Sapho System tab with architecture cockpit, parts-and-connections diagram, active cron jobs, main active skills, SOUL/memory/wiki/system artifacts, backups, and integrations
  • Wiki tab with wikilink graph, search/filter controls, full page library, expandable page cards, excerpts, and rendered markdown

Non-goals

  • No TickTick write actions.
  • No secrets or private credentials in the generated dashboard repo.
  • No authentication layer in the current public GitHub Pages setup beyond the external task-score API if configured.
  • No backup/archive mirror in the main graph yet.
  • No dashboard-only duplicate status widgets for areas that already have real wiki pages.

Related pages
