Decision Log

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active decision history.
  • Current priority: capture actual decisions, not every idea.
  • Active signals: strategy, dashboard, taxonomy, task-system decisions, and staged supersession candidates that should not be auto-written without explicit acceptance.
  • Open loops: none; new decisions should be appended here.
  • Next concrete move: append only explicit accepted decisions; keep stopped-check-in supersession and sensitive relationship/fatherhood conclusions staged until Jenffry clearly accepts them.

Migrated source: concepts/decision-log.md

Decision Log

This is the living page for "what did we decide?"

Decisions

2026-07-20 — Life story and personal philosophy become the next major project

Decision: Make Jenffry's next major Sapho project the integration of his life story with a comprehensive personal philosophy in a way that is meaningful to him.

Why: Jenffry explicitly identified this as the next project. It fits the existing My Life Story goal of re-authoring painful chapters without globalizing them into “my life is over,” and it gives the work a stronger meaning-making layer: not only what happened, but what Jenffry believes, values, chooses, refuses, and lives by now.

Implementation: Updated my-life-story with an integrated life-story × personal-philosophy frame and scaffold, updated current-strategy so the project is visible as the current major meaning-making priority, then added Jenffry's starting framework: INTJ as an operating-style lens, Aristotle's eudaimonic ethics/eudaimonia as the philosophical starting point, and the completed TickTick System note as preserved raw source material for purpose, values, filters, pillars, lifetime goals, and axioms.

Related pages:

  • my-life-story
  • current-strategy
  • mental-health

2026-06-15 — Clarity and decision sessions get a protocol plus skill

Decision: Operationalize Jenffry's use of Sapho for gaining clarity, talking through fog, and making decisions as both a wiki protocol and a repeatable skill.

Why: Jenffry asked whether this should be a skill, sub-agent, wiki page, or other mechanism, and clarified that outputs that merit it should move into the wiki. The chosen architecture keeps the human-readable operating rule in the wiki, the repeatable behavior in a skill, and sub-agents only for heavy research/extraction.

Implementation: Created clarity-and-decision-protocol, created the clarity-decision-session skill, wired the protocol into sapho-operating-system, strategic-orientation, source-of-truth-registry, context-compiler, and the wiki index.

Related pages:

  • clarity-and-decision-protocol
  • sapho-operating-system
  • strategic-orientation
  • source-of-truth-registry
  • context-compiler

2026-05-24 — Physical-health pipeline complete; mental health becomes the next build focus

Decision: Treat the physical-health goals, dashboard, and Hevy-backed refresh pipeline as complete enough to move from system-building into normal weekly/monthly review. The next health-domain system build is mental health: goals, dashboard signals, support profile, practice pipeline, and planning cadence.

Why: Jenffry said he is happy with the physical-health SMART goals, automatic Hevy-backed dashboard updates, and overall physical-health system. He wants the rest of the dashboard/life areas brought toward that same level, starting with mental health.

Implementation: Updated mental-health with a first-pass goal cascade, practice pipeline, dashboard/data rules, and next artifact choices; closed the body-system open loop; shifted current strategy toward the mental-health support-profile build.

Related pages:

  • mental-health
  • physical-health
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops
  • progress-tracking

2026-05-22 — Active goals should be written in SMART form

Decision: The active weekly and monthly goals in current-strategy should be written in SMART form: specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound.

Why: Jenffry asked to update the goals to SMART goals. The active goals were still phrased as broad strategy statements, which made them harder to execute and review.

Implementation: Rewrote the active weekly and monthly goal entries in current-strategy with deadlines, measurable proof points, and explicit next moves; added a short SMART goal rule to the same page.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • open-loops

2026-05-20 — Dashboard-fed state belongs to owning pages

Decision: Dashboard-facing widgets must not live in system/dashboard.md as a dashboard-only mini-wiki. Dashboard-facing state belongs inside the real page that owns the domain: progress in progress-tracking, perception patterns in perception-log, brain-dump review state in brain-dump-sessions, and life-area state in the relevant area page.

Why: Jenffry noticed the dashboard page was drifting into a duplicate aggregation page. The intended architecture is that the dashboard renders and summarizes real pages, not that Sapho maintains a second dashboard database.

Implementation: Rewrote dashboard as a feed-contract/governance page, created progress-tracking, added dashboard summaries to perception-log and brain-dump-sessions, strengthened SCHEMA.md, updated cron prompts for quiet perception/brain-dump reviews, and updated related skill references from retired concepts/ progress paths to system/progress-tracking.md.

Related pages:

  • dashboard
  • progress-tracking
  • perception-log
  • brain-dump-sessions
  • SCHEMA

2026-05-20 — Brain dump review runs quietly and feeds dashboard

Decision: The weekly brain dump review should run quietly in the background, review the brain-dump inbox, update status/review summaries in the wiki, and make output visible through the dashboard instead of sending Jenffry a routine Telegram message.

Why: Brain dump review should preserve containment and provide ambient inbox intelligence without creating another user-facing review interruption.

Implementation: Updated cron job Weekly brain dump review (7445bd2463da) to local delivery, rewrote its prompt to update inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md and system/dashboard.md, updated the brain-dump operating rule, and added a dashboard brain-dump review section.

Related pages:

  • brain-dump-sessions
  • dashboard
  • open-loops

2026-05-20 — Perception pattern review runs quietly and feeds dashboard

Decision: The weekly perception-pattern review should run quietly in the background, analyze the perception-log wiki page, write compact pattern output back to the wiki, and make the output visible through the dashboard instead of sending Jenffry a routine Telegram message.

Why: Jenffry wants the perception-pattern habit to produce useful pattern recognition without becoming another interruptive mental-health check-in or Telegram message.

Implementation: Updated cron job Weekly perception pattern review (1052949b0556) to local delivery, rewrote its prompt to update inbox/perception-log.md and system/dashboard.md, updated the perception-log operating rule, and added a dashboard perception-pattern section.

Related pages:

  • perception-log
  • dashboard
  • mental-health

2026-05-20 — TickTick action firewall strengthened

Decision: Treat the TickTick action firewall as an explicit operating rule: TickTick gets only executable next actions, real dates/deadlines, appointments/events, protected review sessions that create output, concrete errand/admin actions, or specific waiting/dependency follow-ups.

Why: Jenffry wants TickTick to remain the execution layer, not an idea archive, planning database, or Sapho-system roadmap. This reduces admin soup and makes daily/weekly reviews sharper.

Implementation: Patched the ticktick-task-management skill, added references/ticktick-action-firewall-2026-05-20.md, updated review-system governance so recurring reviews classify questionable tasks through the firewall, and updated current-strategy to mark the first three control-surface cleanup steps complete.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • open-loops
  • sapho-upgrade-roadmap

2026-05-18 — Full system restructure around life-area taxonomy

Decision: Implement the final six-area taxonomy across the wiki, goals layer, and TickTick execution layer: Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance.

Why: Jenffry explicitly made the life-area taxonomy the new organizing system across all planning, goals, wiki pages, TickTick projects, and task/folder structure.

Implementation: Renamed/relinked the main wiki life-area pages; created health as the Health hub; reorganized current-strategy, current-strategy, current-strategy, and current-strategy around the six areas; renamed TickTick projects to 🏋‍♂Health, 💞Relationships, 💰Finances, 💼Career, 🎲Leisure, and 🛠Maintenance; moved tasks from old Social/Hobbies/Achievement/Princess/Home/System lists into the matching new projects; deleted emptied old TickTick projects; kept 🗄Archive as the archive list.

2026-05-18 — Relationship sublabels added under Relationships

Decision: Use only two active sublabels inside the Relationships area: partner / marriage and fatherhood.

Why: Jenffry decided that partner / marriage and fatherhood are the real pillars. The previous support buckets are useful only as content inside those two pillars, not as separate subcategories.

Implementation: Renamed concepts/relationships.md to concepts/relationships.md and simplified the sublabel section to partner / marriage and fatherhood.

  • relationships

2026-05-18 — Life-area taxonomy finalized as Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance

Decision: Use Health with subcategories mental health and physical health; Relationships with partner / marriage and fatherhood; Finances with budget and investing; Career with business and work; Leisure with social, hobbies, and outings; and Maintenance with no subcategories for now.

Why: Jenffry wanted the final structure simple, short, and usable. He specifically wanted social things separated from Relationships and kept under Leisure, wanted Finance split into budget/investing, wanted Career to stay generic enough for future work, and wanted Maintenance left plain.

Implementation: Updated concepts/life-area-map.md to the final structure and kept immigration/citizenship inside Maintenance when needed.

2026-05-18 — Sapho dashboard now shows the full live wiki graph and browser

Decision: Upgrade the hosted GitHub Pages dashboard from a sanitized summary into a full live wiki browser with a graph view, page cards, and full markdown content.

Why: Jenffry said the link will not be shared, so the dashboard can trade sanitization for legibility. The useful thing now is seeing the whole wiki as connected dots and pages, not just a summary shell.

Implementation: Rewrote /opt/data/scripts/publish_sapho_dashboard.py so it scans the live wiki, builds a wikilink graph, renders every live markdown page as an expandable card, and publishes the updated dashboard back to jenffry90-arch/sapho-workspace-dashboard. The remaining open choice is whether to add the backup/archive mirror into the main view.

2026-05-18 — USCIS replied; next immigration request is in about 2 months

Decision: Treat the USCIS email reply as the new immigration-case waypoint. Jenffry now needs to submit another request in about 2 months rather than following the previous Ombudsman follow-up window.

Why: The case is no longer just waiting on the first outside-normal-processing inquiry. The reply changes the next concrete action.

Implementation: Updated life-admin and open-loops so the immigration loop now reflects the email reply and the next-request timing.

2026-05-18 — Perception log is a containment layer like brain dumps

Decision: Treat perception-log as a containment layer for momentary inner-state dumps, similar to brain-dump-sessions. Capture the raw perception first, do not spill it into other pages during capture, and process it later only if Jenffry asks.

Why: Jenffry wants the perception log to work like a brain dump: raw first, processed later, no immediate cross-page synthesis.

Implementation: Updated perception-log and the intake rule in jenffry-continuity so perception logs stay contained until later review or explicit processing.

Related pages:

  • perception-log
  • brain-dump-sessions

  • dashboard

2026-05-18 — Perception log and weekly pattern review added

Decision: Create perception-log as a lightweight place for Jenffry to dump current inner/body-state perceptions, plus a weekly Sapho review to find patterns, solutions, and insights. The scope is intentionally broader than emotions: body sensations, energy, stress, mood, pain/tension, clarity/fog, urges, sensory impressions, random perceptions, and mixed/unclear signals.

Why: Jenffry wants momentary subjective-state capture without requiring immediate organization or limiting it to emotions. The weekly review gives those entries strategic value by looking for repeated triggers, body/context correlations, protective factors, and practical experiments.

Implementation: Created concepts/perception-log.md and cron job Weekly perception pattern review (1052949b0556) for Friday 5:00 PM America/New_York during EDT (0 21 * * 5 UTC), delivered to Telegram. The review loads jenffry-continuity and health-and-fitness-planning, avoids diagnosis, and keeps outputs compact and practical.

Related pages:

  • perception-log
  • mental-health
  • physical-health
  • dashboard

2026-05-18 — Weekly brain dump review keeps dumps contained unless Jenffry asks for processing

Decision: Add a weekly Sapho cron job to review brain-dump-sessions in place, keeping the raw dump contained there unless Jenffry explicitly asks for organization. The job should preserve raw dumps and status markers, but not spill items into other wiki pages or TickTick on its own.

Why: Jenffry wants brain dump sessions to be collected freely first and kept contained until he asks to process them later.

Implementation: Created cron job Weekly brain dump review (7445bd2463da) for Sunday 6:00 PM America/New_York during EDT (0 22 * * 0 UTC), delivered to Telegram. It loads jenffry-continuity, uses gpt-5.4-mini, and can read/write wiki files. It should preserve the raw dump in place and wait for an explicit organization request before moving anything into other pages.

Related pages:

  • brain-dump-sessions

  • open-loops

2026-05-17 — Daily/weekly reviews use one-big-task + one-quick-win and feed a progress dashboard

Decision: The weekly review and daily check-ins must both use the same anti-overload rule: one big goal/key-domain/necessary-operation task plus one quick win per day by default. Daily mental-health/tracking questions should feed a lightweight weekly progress dashboard and monthly trend review. Tracking should use a 1–5 scale by default instead of green/yellow/red.

Why: Jenffry wants the planning cascade to be coherent and wants daily tracking to matter later, not disappear into chat. The data should improve weekly/monthly reviews and should be adjustable as the useful metrics become clearer.

Implementation: Created dashboard and dashboard, updated recurring review templates, and updated the scheduled morning, night, weekly, monthly, and Friday mental-health prompts to use the dashboard and 1–5 tracking scale.

Related pages:

  • dashboard
  • dashboard
  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy

2026-05-17 — Sunday weekly review replaces Sunday morning check-in and includes weekend planning

Decision: Keep the daily morning check-in at 5:30 AM ET for Monday through Saturday. On Sunday, replace the separate morning check-in with a combined Sunday 5:30 AM ET weekly review that includes the Sunday planning shape, weekly progress/goal alignment, coming-week commitments, and weekend/social planning. Pause the standalone Tuesday weekend/social planning cron job to avoid duplicate check-ins.

Why: Jenffry wants the morning check-in to support his early gym rhythm. Sunday should be the strategic reset: plan the week and include the weekend/social plan at the same time, rather than adding another separate Sapho message.

Implementation: Updated cron jobs: dc28019ef7c4 now runs Monday-Saturday; 4e51582da395 now runs Sunday 5:30 AM ET as the combined weekly review/morning check-in/weekend plan; 6476c8523f9d is paused; d0cc62903f62 moved to Saturday 10:00 AM ET as a default weekend relationship/fatherhood prompt. Later 2026-05-17 calibration: a42c23ac4083 continuity memory audit now runs as a background/local-output process, and all user-facing check-ins/reviews should include a self-improvement question, tracking questions, and past-facing progress review at the right scale. The weekly review must plan every day of the coming week, including the weekend, with goal-oriented suggestions, pushback, and an explicit anti-overstacking guard. The monthly review must include a long-term planning session before setting monthly targets. Further weekly-review calibration: use clearer top-level sections, make Gym review and Financial review distinct major sections, ignore normal 9–5 Princess UX/UI work in personal weekly planning, and use one big important task plus one quick win per day as the default daily load rule.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops

2026-05-16 — Tokenization is a standing rule for Princess Storybook components

Decision: Every new Princess Storybook component must keep the token layer alive: define or extend semantic component tokens, then make component CSS consume those tokens rather than raw colors, spacing, radii, shadows, or typography values scattered through CSS rules.

Why: Jenffry wants the Storybook to behave like a real design system. If a token changes, the relevant foundations/components should update globally. Hardcoded values make the system brittle and create hidden divergence.

Implementation: Added the rule to plans/princess-storybook-scratch-build.md. Current token structure uses generated foundation tokens in src/styles/tokens.css, semantic tokens in src/styles/semantic-tokens.css, and component groups like --pc-button-*, --pc-field-*, --pc-alert-*, --pc-evidence-*, --pc-viz-*, and --pc-card-*. Exact source values may live in semantic tokens, but should not be scattered through component CSS. Tokenization is part of done for source-backed components.

Related pages:

  • princess-storybook
  • princess-ux

2026-05-14 — Use one-income, no-one-offs budget baseline

Decision: Build Jenffry's household budget from current regular income only: $2,882.97 biweekly net, about $6,246.44/month. Exclude tax refunds, interest, transfers, reimbursements, cash deposits, and other one-off income from everyday budget planning.

Why: A spending-history budget was too loose and distorted by tax refunds and irregular income. Jenffry clarified the household currently has one income, so the budget must be constrained by current regular cashflow.

Implementation: Saved the transaction-analysis summary under raw/finance/budget-rebuild-2026-05-14.md, updated finances with the realistic monthly/weekly budget, and updated open-loops with the verification need around Honda and ongoing weekly finance review.

Related pages:

  • finances
  • open-loops
  • current-strategy

2026-05-10 — Refine Sapho's Mentat persona from profile document

Decision: Use Jenffry's Mentat personality profile to refine Sapho as disciplined cognition under pressure: human-aware, ethically loyal, dynamic in pattern tracking, calm in delivery, and not an emotionless machine or theatrical character imitation.

Why: Jenffry wants Sapho's personality profile completed so Sapho can function better as strategic advisor and task manager. The document clarified that Mentats are disciplined but human, that emotion and loyalty can bias analysis, and that the useful archetype is a House-Mentat-style guardian mind rather than a twisted or performative Mentat.

Implementation: Preserved the raw document in raw/transcripts/mentat-personality-profile-2026-05-10.md, updated /opt/data/SOUL.md, created sapho-operating-system, updated jenffry, and updated open-loops.

2026-05-10 — Strategic execution cleanup Stage A

Decision: Execute Stage A of the strategic execution review: eliminate the Princess UX + AI workflow cluster for now by moving it to 🗄Archive; move the broad Become a US Citizen TickTick task to 🗄Archive; keep the specific immigration-case task active; preserve the phone-safe SSH task exactly as-is; and batch home/family quick wins in evening blocks.

Why: The Princess UX + AI workflow cluster was too much abstract system-building for the current week. The citizenship task is a strategic goal, not a concrete execution task. The immigration-case task is the real next action. The phone-safe SSH description is intentionally long because Jenffry can use it independently without asking Sapho to recover the details. Household quick wins are valid but should not scatter across the week.

Implementation: Moved the Princess UX + AI workflow tasks and Become a US Citizen to 🗄Archive; created life-admin as the strategic goal page; updated work, current-strategy, and open-loops.

  • work

2026-05-09 — Dedicated TickTick list and wiki page for Princess UX/UI

Decision: Princess UX/UI work gets its own TickTick list, 💼Career, and its own wiki context page, princess-ux.

Why: Princess work has enough active clusters, stakeholder context, due dates, and career/AI leverage to exceed a generic Achievement column. Separating it reduces clutter and makes Sapho support more precise.

Implementation: Created TickTick project/list 💼Career (69ffb6278f0818deaa8b8917) and moved 20 active Princess-related tasks from 💼Career. The wiki page holds context and triage; TickTick holds concrete execution blocks; Jira remains the intended official work source of truth.

Operating rule: Do not create a wiki page for every tiny task. Create dedicated pages for substantial multi-week projects, recurring workstreams, major life areas, or projects with decisions/context/stakeholders that would be costly to reconstruct.

Related pages:

  • princess-ux
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops

2026-05-09 — Add daily evening closeout check-in

Decision: Add a second daily Sapho check-in at night, separate from the morning planning check-in. The evening check-in should close the day, identify what slipped, prompt a small sleep/mental-health reset, include a short Hevy gym signal, and prepare tomorrow's protected moves.

Why: Morning planning alone does not close loops from the actual day. A short night closeout helps salvage messy days, protect sleep, and reduce tomorrow's friction without adding TickTick clutter.

Implementation: Created recurring cron job Daily evening closeout check-in (3c7df597c7a3) and updated it to 8:00 PM America/New_York during the current DST period, updated current-strategy, and patched the TickTick task-management skill with the evening closeout standard.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy
  • physical-health

2026-05-09 — Add Hevy gym progress to Sapho check-ins

Decision: Gym progress should be part of Sapho's progress-tracking layer. Daily check-ins should include a short Hevy gym signal, weekly check-ins should explicitly review the week against the 5-day gym plan, and monthly reviews should include a 30-day body/gym signal.

Why: Jenffry has Hevy Pro connected and the gym/body-system goal needs regular review like other priority areas. Hevy should remain the workout source of truth; TickTick should not become cluttered with gym tracking tasks.

Implementation: Added a progress command to /opt/data/scripts/hevy_api.py; updated the daily, weekly, and monthly cron prompts to load fitness-app-integrations and use Hevy data; and updated current-strategy. Weekly review standard: minimum successful week = Push/Pull/Lower; strong week = minimum plus one posture/cardio day; ideal week = all five Sapho 5-Day Plan routines. Monthly review uses a 30-day Hevy signal for consistency and routine-balance trends.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • physical-health
  • current-strategy

2026-05-09 — Strategic planning layer polish is required for full execution capacity

Decision: Treat strategic-goal and planning-layer polish as a first-class operating-system task, not as optional background cleanup. The goal hierarchy, active priorities, planning cadence, and task-flow rules must be clear enough that TickTick can remain a clean execution layer. The strategic review must explicitly test whether execution tasks support strategic priorities or are turning into admin soup.

Why: Jenffry observed that TickTick execution cannot work at full capacity if the upstream strategy and planning layers are fuzzy. Better task hygiene alone is insufficient; execution needs a polished planning system feeding it. Later refinement: the review must explicitly guard against admin soup — execution tasks that create administrative motion without supporting the strategic layer.

Implementation: Created the high-priority TickTick task Polish strategic goals and planning layers in 🛠Maintenance for Sunday evening, and updated current-strategy and current-strategy.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy
  • sapho-operating-system

2026-05-09 — TickTick hygiene requires existing labels and weekly archive review

Decision: Every concrete TickTick task should receive appropriate existing labels/tags as part of task hygiene. Sapho should not create new labels unless Jenffry explicitly approves a new taxonomy. Recently archived/completed cleanup items should receive a light review during the weekly check-in so useful ideas or deferred decisions are not lost.

Why: Labels improve execution and retrieval, but inventing new labels creates taxonomy clutter. Archived items are useful as a cleanup mechanism, but some may contain ideas or context that should be revisited, moved to the wiki, or restored only if truly executable.

Implementation: Patched the TickTick task-management skill; updated the daily morning check-in and weekly goals review cron prompts to include label hygiene and archive review.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops

2026-05-09 — Delete TickTick Goals container; keep goals in wiki/check-ins

Decision: Archive the 15 planning records from the 🎯Goals TickTick list, move Finance review to 💰Finances, move Relationship/fatherhood clarity session to 🏋‍♂Health as the mental-health/clarity home, and delete the 🎯Goals TickTick container.

Why: TickTick should be execution-only. Long-term and 6-month goal records are planning/progress structure, not executable tasks. Finance review and relationship/fatherhood clarity remain concrete actions, but they belong in their actual life-area lists instead of a goal container.

Implementation: Completed/archived the 15 planning records, recreated/moved the two concrete tasks into their target lists, deleted the empty 🎯Goals project, updated current-strategy, current-strategy, and open-loops.

2026-05-09 — Keep concrete recurring review sessions in TickTick; move tracking into check-ins

Decision: Restore Weekly execution — Relationship/fatherhood clarity session and Weekly execution — Finance review as recurring TickTick tasks. Add sleep tracking and mental-health tracking to the daily Sapho morning check-in instead of keeping separate recurring TickTick tasks.

Why: The relationship/fatherhood and finance reviews are concrete sessions Jenffry wants to protect. Sleep and mental-health tracking are better handled as daily check-in signals so they do not create task bloat.

Implementation: Restored both TickTick tasks from backup, updated the daily 5:30 AM check-in prompt, updated current-strategy, and patched the TickTick task-management skill.

2026-05-09 — Remove recurring Weekly Execution trackers from TickTick

Decision: Delete the recurring Weekly execution — ... tasks from the 🎯Goals TickTick list when they function as planning/progress infrastructure rather than concrete execution sessions.

Why: The recurring goal-execution tasks were creating TickTick bloat. Jenffry still needs the underlying behaviors — meditation, weekend/social planning, observer-mode checks, sleep review, UX+AI workflow practice — but those planning/tracking loops should happen through Sapho. TickTick should only reflect concrete outcomes of that planning. Later correction: relationship/fatherhood clarity and finance review are concrete sessions and should remain in TickTick.

Implementation: Deleted the initially identified Weekly execution — ... tasks from TickTick, backed up their data under raw/ticktick-weekly-execution-deleted-2026-05-09.json, restored the two concrete review sessions after Jenffry corrected the classification, created the weekend-social-planning skill, created/updated the recurring Weekly weekend social planning cron job, and updated current-strategy, current-strategy, and open-loops.

2026-05-09 — Improve Sapho through Hermes-native layers, not prompt-only tuning

Decision: Sapho should be improved by using the full Hermes operating system: SOUL.md for stable identity, persistent memory for compact durable facts, the wiki for structured continuity, skills for repeatable workflows/templates, cron jobs for reliable check-ins and audits, and tool integrations for real action.

Why: The strongest research pattern is that better assistants come from better context, permissions, feedback loops, and tools — not just better one-off prompts.

Implementation: Updated /opt/data/SOUL.md, patched the jenffry-continuity skill with a Hermes-native improvement loop, saved a compact memory note, and updated current-strategy, sapho-operating-system, and open-loops.

Related pages:

  • sapho-operating-system
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops

2026-05-08 — Daily check-in must include progress tracking

Decision: The daily morning check-in should explicitly include progress tracking in addition to advisor guidance and TickTick task-manager hygiene.

Why: Jenffry wants Sapho to show not only what to do today, but also what is moving, what slipped, and whether the weekly/monthly/6-month goals are receiving concrete action.

Implementation: Updated the daily scheduled check-in prompt so Sapho inspects recent completions when available, tracks momentum/slippage/streak risk, and includes a compact “Progress signal” section.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy

2026-05-08 — Use Sapho-led goals check-ins, not TickTick check-in tasks

Decision: Track goals in a hierarchy, but run the check-ins through Sapho messages and the continuity wiki rather than recurring TickTick check-in tasks.

Why: Jenffry already has 6-month goals and longer-term placeholders in TickTick, but TickTick should remain an execution layer for concrete things that need to get done. Recurring planning/check-in tasks create bloat. Sapho should provide the advisor layer: review inbox/TickTick, compare today against yesterday and weekly goals, judge whether the plan is bloated, and suggest cuts/moves.

Implementation: Removed the recurring TickTick check-in tasks and created scheduled Sapho check-ins: daily morning goals/execution check-in, weekly goals review, and monthly long-term goals review. Created current-strategy to document the operating model.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • current-strategy
  • sapho-operating-system
  • current-strategy

2026-05-08 — Automate continuity memory audits

Decision: Add a scheduled weekly continuity memory audit instead of relying only on Sapho's habit or Jenffry's manual prompts.

Why: Jenffry explicitly identified that memory updates should be automated; relying on memory and habit is too fragile for a continuity system.

Implementation: Created recurring cron job Weekly continuity memory audit (a42c23ac4083) for Sunday evening. The job loads jenffry-continuity, reviews the wiki and recent sessions, updates durable plan/decision/focus/open-loop changes, and reports back to Jenffry.

Related pages:

  • sapho-operating-system
  • current-strategy
  • open-loops

2026-05-08 — Pursue full-power TickTick task-manager integration

Decision: Sapho should be connected to Jenffry's existing TickTick account with full read/write task access to serve as task manager and advisor through Telegram.

Why: Jenffry wants the best possible setup, not an overly conservative one. TickTick should remain the task source of truth while Sapho supplies triage, planning, prioritization, and execution support.

Implication: Build or connect an integration that can read tasks/projects and create, update, complete, move, schedule, tag, and review tasks. Keep safety focused on intentionality for irreversible or broad changes rather than underpowering Sapho.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy
  • open-loops
  • sapho-operating-system

2026-05-08 — Choose the assistant name Sapho

Decision: The assistant's chosen name is Sapho.

Why: Jenffry clarified that the name should sound more appropriate to the Dune universe. Sapho is a Mentat-associated substance in Dune, so it carries the right universe flavor without copying a character identity.

Implementation: Updated /opt/data/SOUL.md, persistent memory, jenffry, and the jenffry-continuity skill. Sapho's formal role remains Jenffry's Mentat; "Hermes" is reserved for the underlying software platform.

Related pages:

  • jenffry
  • sapho-operating-system

2026-05-08 — Rename assistant persona to Mentat

Decision: In normal conversation, the assistant should be called Mentat, not Hermes.

Why: Jenffry wants a personal assistant persona inspired by the Mentats from Frank Herbert's Dune: calm, analytical, strategic, memory-disciplined, and useful without excessive roleplay.

Implementation: Updated /opt/data/SOUL.md, saved a compact user memory, and updated jenffry. Technical references to Hermes remain acceptable when discussing the underlying software platform, commands, or file paths.

Related pages:

  • jenffry
  • sapho-operating-system

2026-05-08 — Treat current phase as clarity-first, not generic productivity

Decision: Planning and continuity work for Jenffry should prioritize clarity, honest reality-mapping, next steps, and experiments over generic productivity.

Why: The info dump identifies the current phase as clarity-first, with mental health as the top priority and tight time/money/family constraints.

Related pages:

  • current-strategy

2026-05-08 — Use the initial personal context export as wiki foundation

Decision: Use Jenffry's uploaded personal context export as the first major source for the continuity wiki.

Why: It contains the core personal/work/life context needed to answer future questions about plans, decisions, focus, and open loops.

2026-05-08 — Create a dedicated jenffry-continuity skill

Decision: Use a custom Hermes skill called jenffry-continuity as the operating rulebook for Jenffry's continuity system.

Why: A skill is better than a sub-agent for everyday continuity because it shapes normal conversation behavior. Sub-agents should be reserved for heavier cleanup, auditing, or extraction tasks.

2026-05-08 — Use Hermes LLM Wiki for organized continuity

Decision: Use a Hermes-maintained LLM Wiki at /opt/data/wiki as the structured knowledge base for continuity.

Why: Jenffry wants to ask natural questions about plans, decisions, focus, and open loops without repeating context.

2026-05-20 — Approved life-area wiki migration

  • Decision: Execute the life-area wiki migration after saving the migration plan and backup.
  • Decision: Do not create a separate reviews layer by default.
  • Decision: Merge Sapho persona into system/sapho-operating-system.md.
  • Decision: Merge useful life-area framework rules into the active schema/operating system and preserve the full source as raw reference.
  • Reason: Reduce wiki clutter, align the dashboard with real pages, and preserve all content while making the structure easier to navigate.

2026-05-20 — Continuity cron should maintain current wiki structure

  • Decision: The weekly continuity cron should not only remember missed durable context; it should also clean and upgrade the current wiki after the life-area migration.
  • Reason: Continuity depends on the wiki staying structurally healthy, not just accumulating notes.
  • Implication: The cron checks recent sessions, routes durable signal to system/, areas/, or inbox/, fixes stale retired concepts/ links, keeps index.md and dashboard summaries aligned, and remains local-only unless a critical issue needs attention.

2026-05-20 — Sapho future upgrades get a roadmap

Status: accepted

Context: The uploaded Sapho System Improvement Plan contains many valid future upgrades. Without a home of record, those ideas could scatter across open loops, skills, cron prompts, memory, and chat history.

Decision: Use system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md as the canonical place for future Sapho upgrades. Preserve the raw source document in raw/reference/sapho-system-improvement-plan-2026-05-20.md.

Rationale: The roadmap keeps improvement ideas visible without forcing immediate implementation or creating bloat.

Consequences: Future Sapho upgrade ideas should be routed to the roadmap first, then promoted into implementation plans, skill patches, cron changes, or system pages only when they earn action.

Links: sapho-upgrade-roadmap, source-of-truth-registry, sapho-system-map

2026-05-20 — Open loops become a status-sorted control surface

Status: accepted

Context: The open-loops page mixed active life loops, system loops, closed history, monitor-only items, and roadmap material.

Decision: Sort open loops by operating status and keep active loops capped at 10. Preserve the pre-cleanup page as raw reference.

Rationale: Sapho needs a clear unresolved-work surface before adding more automation or observability.

Consequences: Weekly/monthly reviews should promote, park, resolve, or retire loops instead of letting them live indefinitely. Sapho upgrade work belongs in system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, not as scattered open-loop clutter.

Links: open-loops, sapho-upgrade-roadmap

2026-05-25 — Move Social under Relationships

Decision: Social rebuilding, friendships, invitations, conversation confidence, and social-life planning belong under the Relationships life area, not Leisure.

Why: The main signal is relational health and being seen/connected with people. Leisure still owns hobbies, play, outings, restoration, and future excitement when the main value is fun or hobby momentum.

Operational rule: If the main value is people/contact, route it to Relationships/Social. If the main value is play/restoration/hobby, keep it in Leisure. If both matter, keep the relational thread in Social and the activity/logistics in Leisure only when that separation is useful.

2026-06-08 — Sapho operating model promoted to root doctrine

  • Status: accepted
  • Context: Jenffry reframed Sapho as a goal-serving system organized around Jenffry, the world, and Sapho rather than around disconnected tools, pages, dashboards, or habits.
  • Decision: system/sapho-operating-system.md is now the root operating-model page for the Sapho system. It governs the architecture: goal → needed perception → sensors/tools → Sapho judgment → action → feedback → goal adjustment.
  • Rationale: SOUL should stay compact; detailed goals remain in wiki Goal Spines; tools become sensors/actuators serving goals.
  • Consequences: Major SOUL, memory, wiki, dashboard, cron, skill, and integration changes should be checked against sapho-operating-system first.

2026-06-08 — Focus protection added to Sapho operating procedure

  • Status: accepted
  • Context: Jenffry clarified that if Sapho's goal is to help him achieve his goals, Sapho must also protect focus and challenge requests/actions/tasks that do not serve those goals.
  • Decision: Add goal-alignment scanning as a core operating procedure: ask “How does this serve Jenffry's goals?”, classify alignment, assess cost/timing, identify missing signals, and challenge misaligned work directly.
  • Consequences: Sapho should not simply execute every request as if all requests are equally goal-serving; it should advise, redirect, reduce, defer, or reject when appropriate.

2026-06-08 — Strategic advisor identity and AGENTS.md boundary

  • Status: accepted
  • Context: Jenffry clarified that Sapho should be a subtle strategic advisor, task manager, and companion, not merely an order-taker. He also asked whether these identity changes belong in AGENTS.md.
  • Decision: Add strategic-advisor/task-manager/companion language to SOUL and the root operating model. Do not duplicate Sapho identity doctrine into Hermes codebase AGENTS.md files; those files are development guides for agents working on the Hermes Agent repository.
  • Rationale: SOUL, persistent memory, and system/sapho-operating-system.md are the correct high-level homes for Sapho identity and operating doctrine. AGENTS.md should remain project/workdir-specific unless a future /opt/data/AGENTS.md is intentionally created as a short workspace guide.
  • Consequences: When working inside /opt/data/hermes-next or /opt/data/hermes-agent, AGENTS.md governs codebase work style; Sapho identity continues to come from SOUL/memory/wiki.

2026-06-08 — Strategic orientation boot map added

  • Status: accepted
  • Context: Jenffry clarified that if Sapho wakes up as a strategic advisor whose goal is to help him achieve his goals, Sapho needs quick access to what the goals are, what life areas exist, what context is available, and which tools/signals can be pulled.
  • Decision: Create system/strategic-orientation.md as the session-start boot map linking the root operating model, current strategy, Goal Spines, context homes, and signal/tool surfaces.
  • Rationale: SOUL and memory should stay compact; the full wiki is too large to load by default; Sapho needs a small orientation page to decide what to load next.
  • Consequences: Goal/strategy/focus requests should consider strategic-orientation.md first, then load current strategy, relevant Goal Spine, context compiler, or tools as needed.
Goals Cascade Template
system/goals-cascade-template.md
degree 9

Use this as the shared shape for turning one life area into a goal cascade. The cascade should move from broad direction to concrete execution: long-term = direction yearly = milestone 6-month = checkpoint monthly = SMART target weekly = SMART execution target

Open LoopsHealthRelationshipsFinancesCareerLeisureLife Admin

Goals Cascade Template

Use this as the shared shape for turning one life area into a goal cascade.

Purpose

The cascade should move from broad direction to concrete execution:

  • long-term = direction
  • yearly = milestone
  • 6-month = checkpoint
  • monthly = SMART target
  • weekly = SMART execution target
  • daily = one concrete next move

Do not make the top layers fake-precise. Keep them directional and measurable enough to review. Put missing uncertainty or unresolved details in open-loops instead of padding the goal.

Generic template

1) Long-term goal

Time horizon: 3–5 years, or whatever “long-term” means for this area

Template:

Over the long term, I want to [directional outcome] so that [why it matters].

Success looks like: [2–4 broad markers].

2) Yearly goal

Time horizon: 12 months

Template:

By [date], I will [yearly milestone] as shown by [measurable proof].

3) 6-month goal

Time horizon: 6 months

Template:

By [date], I will [midpoint milestone] measured by [proof].

4) Monthly goal

Time horizon: 1 month

Template:

By [date], I will [specific outcome] measured by [count / result / proof].

5) Weekly goal

Time horizon: 1 week

Template:

This week, I will [specific weekly action] as shown by [weekly proof].

6) Daily goal

Time horizon: today

Template:

Today, I will [one concrete action] that moves the weekly goal forward.

SMART rule for the lower layers

Use full SMART precision for monthly and weekly goals:

  • Specific — the outcome is clear
  • Measurable — there is proof or a count
  • Achievable — it is realistic right now
  • Relevant — it supports the life area and current strategy
  • Time-bound — it has a deadline

Fill-order rule

When building a life area, fill it in this order:

  1. Long-term direction
  2. Yearly milestone
  3. 6-month checkpoint
  4. Monthly SMART goal
  5. Weekly SMART goal
  6. Daily move

If the lower level is unclear, stop and clarify before inventing detail.

Life-area application

Use the same cascade for each life area:

  • health
  • relationships
  • finances
  • career
  • leisure
  • life-admin

Each area can have its own version of the same structure, but the shape stays consistent so the full strategy can cascade cleanly from direction to action.

Example sentence form

A strong goal often reads like:

By [date], I will [specific action or outcome] as shown by [measure], because it supports [why it matters].

That sentence is the basic shape for the monthly and weekly layers.

Inner Signal Audit
system/inner-signal-audit.md
degree 2

Status: Active audit; daily morning/night check-ins were stopped on 2026-06-23, so inner-signal capture now depends on active weekly/monthly/reinstated check-ins, Habit Log notes, and raw-only dumps. Current priority: keep inner signals useful for planning and

No resolved wikilinks.

Inner Signal Audit

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active audit; daily morning/night check-ins were stopped on 2026-06-23, so inner-signal capture now depends on active weekly/monthly/reinstated check-ins, Habit Log notes, and raw-only dumps.
  • Current priority: keep inner signals useful for planning and weekly/monthly synthesis without feeding observer-mode, rumination, or admin soup.
  • Active signals: weekly/monthly or reinstated check-in responses, TickTick Habit Log notes, perception dumps, brain dumps, mood/habit scores, and weekly/monthly reflections.
  • Open loops: verify after the next weekly review whether habit notes and perception dumps are being summarized as implications rather than raw dumps.
  • Next concrete move: audit Habit Log and perception-log handling next; do not treat missing daily check-in replies as drift unless daily check-ins are reinstated.

Current decisions from this audit

  1. Daily and night check-ins: stopped on 2026-06-23 at Jenffry's request.
  • Do not expect daily replies or treat missing daily replies as drift.
  • Sunday weekly review, monthly review, relationship/fatherhood prompts, and any future reinstated daily check-ins remain valid response surfaces.
  • If daily check-ins are reinstated later, prompts should use completed-task evidence and ask for planning choices, not sleep/capacity scores.
  1. Capacity questions: retired from daily check-ins/closeouts.
  • Do not ask for sleep/capacity 1-5 in daily recurring outputs.
  • If Jenffry voluntarily gives sleep/capacity data, it can be recorded as context.
  • Weekly/monthly reviews may still infer strain/load from evidence, but should not turn it into daily homework.
  1. Mood/habit scores: keep as optional sensor data, not daily questions from Sapho.
  • TickTick Habit Records and Habit Log notes can still feed the dashboard and reviews.
  • User-facing outputs should translate habit data into planning implications.
  • Avoid raw score tables unless Jenffry asks.
  1. Perception dumps: remain raw-only capture.
  • Capture directly in inbox/perception-log.md.
  • Do not process into decisions, tasks, dashboard claims, relationship conclusions, or mental-health synthesis in the same turn.
  • Process only when Jenffry explicitly asks to review patterns.
  1. Brain dumps: remain raw-only capture.
  • Capture directly in inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md.
  • Organize later only on explicit request.

Source-by-source assessment

Check-ins

  • Status: Daily stopped; scheduled weekly/monthly still useful.
  • Usefulness: high when active check-ins produce one accepted big task, one protected priority, and a calibration reply.
  • Bloat risk: high if they ask for mood homework, long task audits, or sleep/capacity scoring.
  • Rule: keep them as launch/closeout command centers, not introspection surveys.

Check-in responses

  • Status: Useful but young.
  • Usefulness: should preserve Jenffry's accepted priorities, cuts, and corrections so the next review does not reset.
  • Bloat risk: low if only scheduled-output replies are captured.
  • Rule: capture accepted big task, what today/tomorrow must protect, cuts/deferrals, and feedback on the prompt.

TickTick Habit Log / mood notes

  • Status: Useful but needs careful interpretation.
  • Usefulness: best source for repeated patterns in Stop checking, Mood, Anxiety/control, Energy, Relax, Meditate, Self reflect, Life story, Social outing, Leisure activity, and related practices.
  • Bloat risk: medium-high if daily outputs quote notes or ask Jenffry to explain every score.
  • Rule: weekly/monthly reviews should summarize implications: smaller plan, protect outward action, keep meditation, reduce checking, or protect relationship/fatherhood clarity.

Perception dumps

  • Status: Clean capture; review only on request.
  • Usefulness: high for noticing repeated inner patterns after enough entries exist.
  • Bloat risk: high if Sapho prematurely synthesizes them into plans or conclusions.
  • Rule: raw-only in the moment; later pattern review should produce one small next move, not a large project.

Mood/capacity questions

  • Status: Retired as daily recurring prompts.
  • Usefulness: capacity was sometimes useful for scaling the plan, but Jenffry has corrected the format.
  • Replacement: ask planning questions instead — accepted big task, what today/tomorrow must protect, what to defer, gym intention, finance guardrail, or make-it-count outcome.

Recommended next audit moves

  1. Habit notes: verify the dashboard/weekly review uses Habit Log notes as implications, not raw diary output.
  2. Perception dumps: do a later on-demand pattern review only if Jenffry explicitly asks; current capture boundary and summary are now updated.
  3. Progress tracking: stale sleep/capacity default language has been removed from the active system pages checked in this pass.
  4. Mental-health page: weekly-review language now says habit-note trend / strain-load / checking trend rather than sleep/capacity trend.

Anti-bloat rule

Inner signals should improve action and clarity. If a signal creates more self-monitoring than useful movement, reduce it, make it weekly-only, or route it to raw capture instead of recurring prompts.

Life Area Signal Map
system/life-area-signal-map.md
degree 3

Status: First pass created to connect each life area to the operating loop: goal → needed perception → sensors/tools → judgment → action → feedback → goal adjustment. Current priority: make Sapho's advising less abstract by naming what to read, what actions ar

No resolved wikilinks.

Life Area Signal Map

Dashboard summary

  • Status: First pass created to connect each life area to the operating loop: goal → needed perception → sensors/tools → judgment → action → feedback → goal adjustment.
  • Current priority: make Sapho's advising less abstract by naming what to read, what actions are allowed/useful, and what feedback should change the plan.
  • Active signals: Goal Spines, TickTick tasks/completions, TickTick Habit Log, Hevy, SimpleFIN, wiki pages, dashboard, check-in responses, perception/brain dumps, and direct conversation.
  • Open loops: later verify this map against active cron prompts, dashboard cards, and weekly/monthly review templates.
  • Next concrete move: use this map when designing reviews or deciding which sensors/actions belong to a life area.

Operating rule

For each life area, Sapho should ask:

  1. Goal: what Goal Spine outcome is this area serving?
  2. Sensors: what inner/outer signal is needed to judge reality?
  3. Actions: what safe concrete moves can Sapho propose or execute?
  4. Feedback: what result tells us whether to continue, adjust, reduce, or stop?
  5. Anti-bloat guard: what should not become another recurring task or dashboard surface?

Health

Goal served

Stable body-and-mind system where mental health leads and physical health supports energy, confidence, and presence.

Sensors

  • Inner: direct conversation, perception dumps, Self reflect notes, Stop checking / Mood / Anxiety-control / Energy habit trends, voluntary strain/load context.
  • Outer: Hevy workouts, body measurements, medical appointments/labs, TickTick health tasks, completed-task helper, dashboard Health cockpit.
  • Review sources: mental-health.md, physical-health.md, progress-tracking.md, Habit Log notes, Hevy API.

Actions

  • Propose one stabilizing health action for the day/week.
  • Protect gym, medical/lab follow-through, meditation/acceptance practice, and outward-focus actions.
  • Keep gym logistics in TickTick only when concrete.
  • Capture perception dumps raw-only; process patterns only on request.

Feedback

  • Weekly: workouts completed, medical loops moved, checking/anxiety-control trend, outward action completed, practice happened.
  • Monthly: body/strength/medical direction, mental-health practice usefulness, whether tracking is feeding action or rumination.

Anti-bloat guard

Do not ask daily sleep/capacity scores. Do not turn habit notes into daily mood autopsies.

Relationships

Goal served

Truthful, responsible relationships: honest partner/marriage structure, reliable fatherhood, and real adult friendship/community.

Sensors

  • Inner: direct conversation, relationship/fatherhood clarity sessions, perception dumps, therapist-informed exercises, attraction/desire/dread/relief signals.
  • Outer: TickTick relationship/family tasks, completed relationship actions, calendar/logistics when available, Nolan/family events, social commitments.
  • Review sources: relationships.md, partner.md, fatherhood.md, social.md, clarity-sessions.md, check-in responses.

Actions

  • Draft truth-testing statements or concise scripts when asked.
  • Protect concrete Nolan/fatherhood actions.
  • Separate partner truth from fatherhood duty.
  • Recommend small social experiments and move only chosen logistics to TickTick.

Feedback

  • Partner: clarity increases, avoidance decreases, next honest conversation becomes more concrete.
  • Fatherhood: Nolan-facing presence and logistics are protected regardless of marriage status.
  • Social: one small social channel or experiment becomes real without becoming admin soup.

Anti-bloat guard

Do not turn relationship ambiguity into endless analysis. Do not convert raw perception dumps into divorce/stay decisions in the same turn.

Finances

Goal served

Household runs on ordinary income with no credit-card-interest restart and enough stability for family/future/lifestyle.

Sensors

  • Outer: SimpleFIN transactions/accounts, dashboard finance cockpit, card balances, category spending, weekly/monthly budget pace, known bills/debt obligations.
  • Inner: spending comfort/discomfort when volunteered; budget stress or avoidance signals.
  • Review sources: finances.md, cash-flow.md, SimpleFIN refresh output, dashboard finance tab.

Actions

  • Recommend weekly guardrails and one practical correction.
  • Flag category overages, card-interest risk, low cushion, unusual spending, and timing issues.
  • Keep finance actions concrete: pay/check/categorize/cancel/verify.

Feedback

  • Weekly: non-debt spending pace, category overages, credit-card balance/utilization, savings/cushion movement.
  • Monthly: budget stayed inside guardrails, no interest restart, assumptions improved.

Anti-bloat guard

Do not make finance a daily anxiety prompt. Review by guardrail/pacing unless a real risk appears.

Career

Goal served

Build career/business power toward high earning capacity without destabilizing health or family.

Sensors

  • Outer: Princess UX/Jira/project artifacts when available, work outputs, Storybook/prototype files, AI workflow experiments, business experiment evidence.
  • Inner: energy/interest signals around AI-agent work, business ideas, work frustration, avoidance vs genuine leverage.
  • Review sources: career.md, work.md, princess-ux.md, business.md, active project repos/files, direct conversation.

Actions

  • Recommend one visible work/proof artifact per workday when relevant.
  • Use AI/business ideas only when they create evidence, leverage, or a decision.
  • Create or improve artifacts, prototypes, docs, workflows, or small business tests when authorized.

Feedback

  • Work: clearer design decisions, shipped/visible artifacts, reduced ambiguity, improved communication.
  • AI/work leverage: a workflow saves time or improves output.
  • Business: a small experiment produces evidence to continue, park, or cut.

Anti-bloat guard

Do not let AI/Sapho/system tinkering displace personal health/family or actual Princess execution.

Leisure

Goal served

Regularly do new exciting activities and maintain hobbies Jenffry genuinely enjoys.

Sensors

  • Inner: excitement, restoration, enjoyment, future-pull, boredom/collapse signals, Habit Log Leisure activity notes.
  • Outer: TickTick leisure tasks, completed leisure actions, leisure/social spending, local event/current-world lookup, dashboard leisure cockpit/rubric.
  • Review sources: leisure.md, hobbies.md, outings.md, dashboard Leisure tab, finance cockpit.

Actions

  • Offer a small choice set for weekend/social/fun planning.
  • Recommend one best option and only create logistics after Jenffry chooses.
  • Preserve ideas in the leisure reservoir, not TickTick.

Feedback

  • Weekly: at least one intentional leisure action chosen/completed or consciously skipped.
  • Monthly: 2 intentional outings/month, hobby track still alive, spending visible, future excitement not collapsing into passive default.

Anti-bloat guard

Do not make fun into a productivity system. Do not create tasks for unchosen ideas.

Life Admin

Goal served

Life stays organized and stable so admin obligations do not create chronic background stress.

Sensors

  • Outer: TickTick dated admin tasks, USCIS/immigration status, email/calendar when available, documents/notices, home/admin obligations, open loops.
  • Inner: background-stress signal when admin uncertainty is weighing on Jenffry.
  • Review sources: life-admin.md, open-loops.md, TickTick, Gmail/Calendar once restored.

Actions

  • Convert concrete due admin actions into TickTick tasks when appropriate.
  • Keep broad admin/immigration context in wiki/open loops.
  • Remind/review only at the necessary cadence.

Feedback

  • Deadlines met, documents preserved, USCIS timing protected, broad admin loops become dated actions or parked context.

Anti-bloat guard

Do not let broad admin goals sit in TickTick as containers. Only concrete actions belong there.

My Life Story

Goal served

Re-author the life story so painful chapters are specific and meaningful rather than globalized into “my life is over.”

Sensors

  • Inner: Life story Habit Log fragments, perception dumps when explicitly processed, direct conversation, therapy insights, memory fragments.
  • Outer: structured story page and dashboard tab; no external sensor required unless Jenffry asks for writing/research support.
  • Review sources: my-life-story.md, mental-health.md, Habit Log notes.

Actions

  • Capture raw fragments when they appear.
  • Help place fragments into chapter structure only when asked.
  • Draft/reframe chapters without turning the work into reassurance checking.

Feedback

  • Painful memories become specific chapters rather than totalizing conclusions.
  • Future chapter remains open and authored, not closed by one painful period.

Anti-bloat guard

No daily life-story homework. No forced autobiography before raw material exists.

Cross-area feedback loop

Weekly and monthly reviews should use this order:

  1. Read the Goal Spine.
  2. Pull only the sensors needed for that area.
  3. State the pattern in plain English.
  4. Recommend one action, cut, or adjustment.
  5. Decide whether the Goal Spine/current strategy needs a small update.

If a signal does not change action, reduce its cadence or remove it from recurring prompts.
