Open Loops

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Cleaned into an active control surface; 2026-07-19 hygiene found active-loop count still within the max-10 rule, with relationship/fatherhood wording stale but too sensitive to auto-rewrite.
  • Current priority: keep active loops to a maximum of 10 and stop closed/parked/system items from competing with live priorities.
  • Active signals: relationship/fatherhood clarity, mental-health support profile, finance stability, Princess UX organization, execution-layer hygiene, immigration follow-up, social rebuild, career/business experiments, scheduled medical follow-up, and Sapho server storage pressure.
  • Open loops: active loops are below; Sapho 2.0 upgrades are tracked in sapho-upgrade-roadmap.
  • Next concrete move: use the 2026-07-19 weekly review to decide whether Mom housing/finance support or relationship trial wording deserves a canonical open-loop update.

Cleanup note

This page was restructured on 2026-05-20. The full pre-cleanup source is preserved at:

  • raw/reference/open-loops-pre-cleanup-2026-05-20.md

No loop was intentionally deleted; loops were sorted by operating status.

Weekly hygiene note — 2026-06-21: no major loops were closed automatically. Stale/duplicate pressure remains around relationship/fatherhood clarity appearing both as an Active loop and as the more specific “Scenario exercise” decision-needed loop; keep both for now because the broad practice layer and the next Aubrey-facing conversation prep are different altitudes. The old physical-health system-build wording was removed from the dashboard summary because the physical-health pipeline is already closed/monitoring.

Weekly hygiene note — 2026-06-23: renamed the specific relationship decision-needed loop away from stale “scenario exercise” wording because the page content has already moved into Aubrey-facing truth/separation conversation preparation. No relationship conclusion was closed or upgraded to a final decision.

Weekly hygiene note — 2026-06-23 late: low-risk Steward maintenance capture item CS-20260623-D was processed. The weekly timeout/storage issue was repaired and logged, so no active system loop remains for it. Relationship/fatherhood duplicate pressure remains staged; no sensitive conclusion, priority order, recurring review behavior, or dashboard-facing goal was changed.

Weekly hygiene note — 2026-06-28: duplicate pressure remains in relationship/fatherhood continuity because several high-sensitivity separation/Indiana/fatherhood signals are staged in system/continuity-steward-capture.md. No relationship conclusion, custody/legal conclusion, major loop closure, decision-log entry, recurring review behavior, or dashboard-facing goal was changed automatically. The workout-routine loop was refreshed to include the new twice-weekly muscle-frequency question without creating a separate duplicate loop.

Weekly hygiene note — 2026-07-05: relationship/fatherhood duplicate pressure was reduced in the capture page by compressing overlapping late-June signals into staged clusters. No active loop was closed, no decision-log entry was created, and stale June Goal Spine dates in relationship pages were left for a proper goals/review update rather than edited automatically.

Weekly hygiene note — 2026-07-12: no active loop was closed automatically. Capture backlog cleanup moved the 2026-07-07 relationship trial-period candidate to staged review, which may make the Aubrey-facing truth/separation open-loop wording stale; this needs review-owned judgment rather than an automatic sensitive conclusion. The workout-routine candidate was deferred rather than opened as a duplicate loop because the existing workout-routine loop already owns that question. Finance and relationship Goal Spine dates remain visibly stale and should be refreshed through weekly/monthly review, not by this hygiene pass.

Weekly hygiene note — 2026-07-19: no active loop was closed automatically. The server-storage capture item is already represented by Sapho server storage pressure; health follow-up is already represented by Testosterone and lipid follow-up plus the physical-health owner page; and the OFSEC social framework is already on social, so no duplicate loops were opened. Staged issue for review: Mom housing/application and Mom housing cost-boundary captures may need either a temporary family/life-admin loop or a finance-boundary update if still unresolved. Relationship trial-period captures may supersede the older Aubrey-facing truth/separation wording, but that remains too sensitive for automatic rewrite.

Active Open Loops — max 10

Relationship/fatherhood clarity

Status: open — practice layer defined

Question: What observations, questions, scenarios, possible futures, boundaries, regrets to avoid, and next actions would create real clarity without circular analysis?

Status update: 2026-05-11 intake clarified that the 12-month goal cannot be fully defined in advance. Success means achieving clarity and being comfortable with it, whatever the final family/relationship structure is. The practice layer is written clarity sessions: answer prompts, play out futures, test what acting kindly/not abandoning himself means, identify regrets to avoid, protect Nolan from chaos, and choose one next observation/action.

Suggested next action: Complete the TickTick Relationship/fatherhood clarity session; use the new weekly Sapho clarity prompt to make the work recurring without turning the goal structure into TickTick bloat.

Prepare Sapho for mental-health support role

Status: open — active upgrade in progress

Question: What should Sapho know about Jenffry's mental-health issues, warning signs, helpful responses, unhelpful responses, boundaries, and escalation rules?

Status update: 2026-05-11 intake captured observer mode, acceptance/commitment toward current life, and fear/acceptance around possible recurrence of the late-January weed incident.

Status update: 2026-05-24 Jenffry marked the physical-health goals/dashboard/pipeline complete and chose mental health as the next area to bring to the same clarity level. mental-health now has a SMART cascade built around fear as the core mechanism and the strategy: ignore, redirect, accept, and embrace.

Status update: 2026-05-25 TickTick mental-health tracking was finalized as 15 habit trackers: Stop checking, Therapy, Physical activity, Healthy diet, Walk, no phone, Social outing, Mood, Sleep, Anxiety, Energy, Relax, Leisure activity, Life story, Self reflect, and Meditate. Anxiety uses Option B: 1 = poor anxiety control, 5 = strong anxiety control. Habit Log notes are written to /opt/data/wiki/private/health/mood-tracking-notes.md and intentionally included in the public dashboard.

Suggested next action: Use the 15-habit tracker for one week, then review which signals are useful versus noisy/admin-heavy.

Finance stability

Status: open — income-based budget created; verification and execution remain

Question: Can the household budget stay stable for a clean month without tax refund or one-off income distortion, without credit-card interest restarting, and with enough monthly excess/enjoyment money to stop feeling purely survival-based?

Status update: 2026-05-11 finance goal pass clarified that savings are strong: about $36k emergency fund and over $500k in 401k. The real issue is monthly budget comfort and cashflow.

Status update: 2026-05-31 SimpleFIN refresh shows the budget is still strained at the end of May: monthly included spending is $7,154 against $6,246 income, this week's non-debt spending is $563 against a $376 pace, and usable remaining is effectively zero. The current pressure is controllable spending categories, not card utilization. See finances.

Suggested next action: Continue weekly Finance review; compare spending against the income-based caps, keep May controlled, verify whether Honda truly stopped after the April payoff-sized payment, and refine the path toward ~$400/month comfortable excess.

Princess UX/UI work organization

Status: open — success buckets defined

Question: What structure will let Sapho help Jenffry organize Princess UX/UI work without duplicating Jira or creating TickTick admin soup, while supporting senior UX growth?

Status update: 2026-05-11 goals-layer pass defined five Princess UX/career success buckets: work quality, execution, influence, portfolio/visible proof, and AI/work leverage. The next need is selecting 2–3 concrete proof projects/case-study candidates and a weekly practice/review cadence.

Status update: 2026-05-14 operating-system cleanup moved the overdue Princess UX/UI dated pile out of May 13 and spread it across Fri May 15, Mon May 18, and Tue May 19. No Princess tasks were deleted or archived; this was a reversible overload-clearing pass. Remaining issue: confirm which items are already done, blocked, should move to Jira, or should be collapsed/archived after Jenffry reviews real work status.

Suggested next action: Use princess-ux as the context layer. Keep Jira as the work source of truth, TickTick as dated execution blocks, and Sapho/wiki for synthesis. First clarify the official Jira structure and highest-stakes Princess clusters, then organize work into clarify/design/review/handoff/systemize buckets. Treat AI workflow as a bounded real-work experiment, not an abstract project.

Strategic review: execution layer versus strategic layer

Status: open

Question: Is the current TickTick execution layer genuinely supporting the strategic layer, or has it become admin soup?

Status update: Stage A executed on 2026-05-10. The Princess UX + AI workflow cluster was moved to 🗄Archive; the phone-safe SSH task was intentionally left long because Jenffry wants the self-service details in TickTick; the broad Become a US Citizen task was moved to 🗄Archive; and home/family quick wins should be batched in evening blocks.

Status update: 2026-05-25 dashboard polish clarified the next goals-system improvement: future weekly/monthly SMART goals should have automatic tracking sources when possible, such as TickTick task completions, habits, or dashboard source data, instead of hardcoded progress percentages.

Suggested next action: Continue protecting the execution layer against admin soup during weekly reviews. Future cleanup should focus on newly accumulated abstract/system-building tasks, broad goals without next actions, excessive household quick wins outside evening batches, and hardcoded goals that need real tracking signals.

Immigration and citizenship path

Status: open — USCIS replied; next request due in about 2 months

Question: What is actionable for Jenffry's immigration case after more than two years pending, and what is the longer citizenship path after that?

Current 12-month definition: green card if the process allows it; otherwise no passive waiting — confirm case status, normal processing time, documents/notices, escalation options, and take the reasonable next action available.

Status update: USCIS replied by email. Jenffry now needs to submit another request in about 2 months rather than waiting on the old follow-up window.

Suggested next action: Keep the broad citizenship goal in life-admin, not as an active TickTick container task. Preserve the email reply and set the next USCIS request for about 2 months out. If a non-delay issue appears — lost document, USCIS error, missed notice, approved expedite stuck, or urgent humanitarian issue — consider earlier Ombudsman/attorney/congressional escalation.

Social rebuild

Status: open — 12-month markers partly defined

Question: How can Jenffry protect hobbies, weekends, and future excitement so leisure restores agency rather than becoming avoidance, while social rebuilding lives under Relationships?

Status update: 2026-05-25 taxonomy correction: social rebuilding, friendships, invitations, and social confidence now belong under relationships / social. Leisure still tracks hobbies, family/solo outings, restoration, and future excitement. Older 2026-05-11 markers about friends/social outings should be interpreted as Relationships/Social markers, not Leisure markers.

Suggested next action: Use leisure for one concrete hobby/restoration activity at a time and social for one concrete social experiment at a time. TickTick should get only concrete outcomes, not recurring planning clutter.

Sapho server storage pressure

Status: open — operational risk

Question: What safe cleanup/retention action should reduce /opt/data disk pressure without risking wiki, sessions, credentials, active source trees, backups needed for restore, or project files?

Status update: 2026-07-15 planning readout showed /opt/data at 91% used with about 3.3 GB available. Jenffry flagged the Sapho server space issue as needing to be solved. This belongs under Sapho System / Life Admin, not Health.

Status update: 2026-07-19 maintenance found /opt/data at 94% used before cleanup. The cleanup script was patched to include the user-home npm cache, then rerun; it improved space to about 3.3 GB free / 92% used. Risk remains high because large active source/tool directories, Android local tooling, backups, and Hermes source trees dominate remaining usage.

Suggested next action: Do a scoped storage review for large active source/tool directories before deleting anything else; keep wiki, sessions, state, credentials, backup safe repo, and active project files protected.

Business/career direction

Status: open — success buckets drafted

Question: What concrete experiments will help choose the next business/career direction instead of endlessly comparing imagined futures?

Status update: 2026-05-11 goals-layer pass set this area to follow a Princess-like bucket structure: concrete experiments, work leverage, business-development option value, visible proof/assets, and bandwidth/mental-health fit. Blaze remains attractive but inactive unless experiments show demand, capability, and bandwidth.

Suggested next action: Choose 1–3 small experiments or proof assets related to AI leverage, senior UX growth, leadership, and possible business directions using career.

Needs Decision

Expose dashboard task-score API for cross-device saving

Status: open — local backend implemented

Question: What public HTTPS endpoint should the GitHub Pages dashboard use so dashboard-only task scores save from both phone and computer?

Status update: 2026-05-29 implemented local SQLite storage at /opt/data/task_scores/task_scores.sqlite3, API script /opt/data/scripts/sapho_task_score_api.py, visible dashboard 1–5 score controls, and watchdog cron Sapho task score API watchdog (a92bd543e90d) for the local API on 127.0.0.1:8787. 2026-05-31 adds a Cloudflare Workers + D1 persistence path for dashboard state. TickTick remains untouched. The remaining gap is a deployed HTTPS API URL; without it, GitHub Pages can render score fields but cannot save from phone/computer.

Suggested next action: choose the public API exposure path: small hosted service, secure tunnel, or Netlify/Supabase-style backend, then set SAPHO_TASK_SCORE_API_URL and republish.

Prepare Aubrey-facing relationship truth conversation

Status: open

Question: How should Jenffry prepare an honest, compassionate Aubrey-facing truth/separation conversation without private shorthand, false hope, or fatherhood avoidance?

Status update: The therapist gave a concrete exercise for the next relationship clarity session. Jenffry should create four scenarios: selfish while staying in the marriage, selfless while staying in the marriage, selfish while leaving the marriage/not living in it, and selfless while leaving the marriage/not living in it.

Status update: 2026-06-15 relationship clarity update added a new signal after Aubrey and Nolan returned from Indiana: Jenffry felt genuine happiness at their return, still lacks physical attraction and sometimes feels repulsion, softened/cowered under confrontation, and now finds a final attempt less immediately aversive. The next decision question is whether a bounded final attempt can be honest and informed, not just avoidance or guilt.

Status update: 2026-06-16 upgraded decision pass clarified the current hinge: Jenffry is not ready to break the family structure until he tests whether staying can be chosen without self-betrayal, resentment, or false romantic performance. The most live uncertainty he chose is whether he is chasing fantasy freedom. Aubrey's informed-consent fairness question remains unresolved. Status update: 2026-06-18 clarity pass shifted the live state: Jenffry said the attempt path is not honest; doing nothing risks false hope; realistic separation still seems more honest than false husbandhood; and the remaining reservation is fear. The next work is no longer broad divorce analysis but preparation for a serious Aubrey-facing truth/separation conversation with compassion, anti-cave safeguards, and shared language rather than private “final attempt” framing.

Suggested next action: Prepare the Aubrey-facing truth/separation conversation: refine the short script in shared language, build anti-cave responses for likely reactions, choose a protected timing/container, and outline immediate responsibilities for Nolan, money, housing, and support without pretending every logistical answer is solved.

Formalize Sapho model-routing policy matrix

Status: open — return later

Question: Should Sapho create an explicit model-routing matrix before considering any custom auto-router, covering job/session type, default model, allowed fallback, forbidden downgrade, review cadence, and reason?

Status update: 2026-05-17 discussion concluded that the first step should be a visible policy-based routing matrix rather than jumping to OpenRouter auto-router or a custom live model router. Candidate rows include live mental health/relationship/family, monthly goals/full weekly review, evening closeout, memory/lean audits, mechanical status checks, and unknown/default routing.

Suggested next action: Build the matrix, audit all cron jobs against it, and only then decide whether to add a cron-only policy enforcer or a true live message router.

Improve Sapho outer-signal integrations

Status: open — audit redone with web/current-world research

Question: Which new sensors or integration improvements would most improve Sapho's goal-serving perception without creating privacy risk, token waste, or admin soup?

Status update: 2026-06-08 redid outer-signal-audit from an improvement perspective. Current conclusion: Sapho should first restore Google Calendar read-only, then Gmail narrow read/search if useful, then design a local signal inbox/event ledger before adding more always-on feeds. Candidate later sensors include Apple Health/wearable export, meeting transcripts, Readwise, YNAB/Actual Budget, Home Assistant, food/grocery receipt parsing, local event discovery, and browser recipes.

Suggested next action: Use a staged proof sequence: Calendar read-only reauth → signal inbox/event-ledger schema → one health sensor proof. Keep all new integrations read-only until they repeatedly prove value.

Define Sapho permissions and recurring templates

Status: partly closed on permissions; templates still open

Permission decision: Jenffry wants Sapho to have full permissions by default. Permissions are not the blocker. Sapho should act with disciplined judgment, especially around broad/destructive/sensitive actions, but should not treat lack of permission as a reason for passivity.

Template calibration rule: every recurring check-in should include a brief question asking how to improve that check-in. Use the answers to patch skills/templates and reduce noise over time.

Suggested next action: Continue calibrating output templates from real active check-ins. Daily morning/night check-ins were stopped on 2026-06-23, so template calibration now applies to the Sunday weekly review, monthly review, relationship/fatherhood prompts, and any future reinstated daily check-ins. Active user-facing recurring check-ins should include a self-improvement question, tracking questions, and progress review at the right scale. Weekly planning must cover every day of the coming week, including weekend, while using goal-oriented suggestions, pushback, and anti-overstacking guards. Monthly review must include a long-term planning session before setting monthly targets. Current weekly-review template should use clearer top-level sections; make Gym review and Financial review distinct major sections; ignore normal 9–5 Princess UX/UI work in personal weekly planning; and use one big important task plus one quick win per day as the default daily load rule. The continuity memory audit should stay background/local-only unless a critical issue needs direct attention.

Build Sapho workspace blueprint / internal-systems dashboard

Status: active — full wiki browser added to the hosted dashboard; backup/archive mirror still undecided

Question: How should Sapho make the continuity/wiki/skills/cron/TickTick system legible as a folder/workspace-style map, so Jenffry can see the whole operating system without relying on hidden context?

Status update: 2026-05-18 comparison against the folder/workspace video found strong alignment in philosophy — markdown continuity, separated context, reusable skills, and routing AI to the right files — but a clear weakness in Sapho's legibility. Current routing is split across wiki pages, skills, cron prompts, memory, scripts, and TickTick. The missing layer is an at-a-glance workspace blueprint or dashboard showing life-area rooms, raw capture containers, review cadences, routing rules, and system health.

Status update: Initial blueprint page now exists as sapho-operating-system with room map, routing rules, and weekly review touchpoints.

Status update: A concrete implementation page now exists as dashboard to guide the first web dashboard build.

Status update: The hosted dashboard now renders the full live wiki with a graph view and page browser. The remaining question is whether to add the backup/archive mirror into the main view or keep the graph focused on the live wiki only.

Suggested next action: Decide whether to add a backup/archive toggle or keep the live wiki graph uncluttered.

Needs Information

Capture January mental-health incident context

Status: partly open — sensitive/foundational

Question: What happened during the January weed-related mental-health incident, and what context does Sapho need to better support Jenffry's mental-health planning and conversations?

Status update: 2026-05-11 intake captured the current meaning for goals work: Jenffry is afraid it can happen again, is working on accepting that recurrence is possible, and wants to commit that if it happens again he can work through it because he survived it once.

Suggested next action: If/when useful, capture fuller incident context, warning signs, helpful responses, unhelpful responses, and escalation boundaries. Sapho should preserve useful context without treating itself as a therapist or medical authority.

Testosterone and lipid follow-up

Status: partly resolved — follow-up scheduled

Question: What testosterone/urology plan makes sense with medical input after the repeat testosterone test?

Status update: 2026-07-15 Jenffry reported cardiology follow-up is done: he had an ultrasound and everything was good. He also already took a repeat testosterone test and it was better than the first one. The next follow-up is already scheduled as the urology appointment.

Suggested next action: Attend the scheduled urology appointment and prepare questions for that clinician visit; do not treat AI as medical authority.

Review gpt-5.4-mini quality for background continuity/system audits

Status: open — monitor after next runs

Question: Does running the weekly continuity memory audit and weekly Sapho lean-system audit on openai-codex / gpt-5.4-mini preserve enough judgment quality, or does it create missed context, shallow recommendations, bad pruning, or operating-system drift?

Status update: 2026-05-17 model-routing decision set both jobs to gpt-5.4-mini to preserve gpt-5.5 capacity while keeping important background audits inside the Codex/OpenAI family. This is a deliberate trial, not a final settled routing rule.

Suggested next action: After the next 1–2 runs, inspect the local cron outputs for quality: whether memory recommendations are conservative and useful, whether lean-system audit avoids bloat and false optimizations, and whether either job should move back to gpt-5.5 or to DeepSeek V4 Pro.

Parked / Later

Solidify Jenffry's goals layer

Status: open — first pass complete; execution/refinement remains

Question: What are Jenffry's current goals, and how should they cascade from long-term direction to 12-month outcomes, 6-month goals, monthly targets, weekly commitments, and concrete TickTick tasks?

Why it matters: Sapho's core roles are strategic advisor and task manager. Those roles require a reliable model of Jenffry's goals, capabilities, limitations, and context. The current goal information is incomplete, which weakens advice and task management.

Status update: 2026-05-10 voice intake captured the first 12-month goals layer by life area. 2026-05-11 voice intake refined the mental-health goal into mental-health, completed first-pass definitions across the earlier eight-domain draft in current-strategy, and added a Hevy-based physical-health baseline/target page. 2026-05-18 restructure collapsed the goals layer into the final six life areas, now named Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, and Life Admin.

Suggested next action: Treat broad goal definition as good enough for this pass. Next work is execution and calibration: monthly targets, weekly commitments, TickTick alignment, and lightweight progress signals without creating admin soup.

Define concrete 12-month goal metrics

Status: open — first pass complete; calibration remains

Question: What concrete measures define the 12-month goals so they are not fuzzy?

Status update: first-pass observable 12-month markers now exist across the final six life areas in current-strategy. Health contains mental-health and physical-health markers; Relationships contains partner / marriage, fatherhood, and social connection practice-layer structures; Finances contains budget and investing; Career contains work and business; Leisure contains hobbies, outings, restoration, and future excitement; Life Admin contains concrete admin/immigration follow-through.

Suggested next action: Convert the first-pass goals into monthly/weekly progress signals without tracking bloat. Recalibrate physical-health targets after 4–6 weeks of Hevy data on the Sapho plan; refine finance comfort, Princess proof projects, career/business experiments, work-relationship target, and mental-health support boundaries during reviews.

Create a bucket list

Status: open

Question: What experiences, trips, hobbies, milestones, family memories, social adventures, and personal achievements does Jenffry want to make room for over the next several years?

Why it matters: A bucket list can strengthen the Leisure and future-excitement layer without turning everything into productivity. It should help identify what is actually worth protecting time, money, and planning energy for.

Suggested next action: Do a first-pass capture session: collect ideas without filtering, then sort them into near-term, someday, family, solo, social, travel, hobby/skill, and achievement-style items. Only turn specific chosen items into TickTick tasks when they are ready to become concrete plans.

Complete Sapho personality profile

Status: partly complete — first personality profile ingested on 2026-05-10

Question: What additional persona details should shape Sapho's tone, judgment, boundaries, and working style?

Status update: Jenffry sent a Mentat personality profile. Sapho preserved the raw document, updated /opt/data/SOUL.md, created sapho-operating-system, and recorded the applied model: disciplined cognition under pressure, not emotionless-machine behavior; ethical loyalty; dynamic pattern tracking; awareness of emotional bias.

Suggested next action: Keep this loop open only for additional Jenffry-specific Sapho behavior preferences, not for more Dune lore unless it changes actual operating behavior.

Continue learning Jenffry's operating profile

Status: open — ongoing

Question: What information about Jenffry's capabilities, limitations, constraints, preferences, energy patterns, relationships, work context, and decision patterns would make Sapho a better strategic advisor and task manager?

Suggested next action: Keep a standing, low-clutter intake loop. Save durable signal when it improves future advice or execution; avoid saving noisy one-off details.

System Open Loops

Sapho operating-system improvement review

Status: active — first lean/bloat pass completed 2026-05-17

Question: Is Sapho's layer above the code — persona, memory, wiki, skills, scheduled check-ins, TickTick hygiene, and review cadence — reducing fog and increasing agency, or creating its own admin load?

Current assessment: The architecture is strong and now has a lean-governance layer. The first bloat pass found the main risk in the skills reference layer and recurring cron prompt length, not in the wiki itself. Sapho consolidated overlapping TickTick review-calibration references into ticktick-task-management/references/review-system-governance-2026-05-17.md, kept the canonical review-templates-2026-05-17.md, removed absorbed reference files, shortened user-facing review cron prompts to point at canonical templates/governance, removed the paused duplicate weekend/social cron job, and created weekly local-only lean audit job 292f4883ad21.

Suggested next action: Let the weekly lean audit run quietly. It should only compress or remove when durable context is preserved and the change is likely to improve token usage, response speed, prompt size, or recurring-output clarity. Continue staged TickTick overload cleanup separately; do not let lean governance become another user-facing admin process.

Define what belongs in the wiki

Status: open

Question: What exact kinds of personal/work information should Hermes save into the wiki versus short-term chat or built-in memory?

Suggested next action: As real conversations happen, save only things that improve continuity, clarity, or action.

Monitor Only

Email inbox and calendar integration for check-ins

Status: restored again on 2026-06-12 — monitor only

Result so far: Google Workspace OAuth is connected for Jenffry's Google account with Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Contacts scopes. Gmail and Calendar were re-verified on 2026-06-12 after a revoked-token failure, and the daily/weekly check-in jobs may use inbox and calendar attention when useful.

Current boundary: Google Workspace is a read/perception surface for check-ins unless Jenffry explicitly authorizes a send/modify action. Future failures should be treated as integration degradation, not as a reason to fabricate email/calendar state.

Suggested next action: No re-auth needed unless the failure recurs. If a future check-in reports Google failure, first retry with the known dependency prefix (PYTHONPATH=/opt/data/google-python-deps) before assuming OAuth is revoked.

OpenRouter fallback provider

Status: superseded/closed — OpenRouter disabled on 2026-05-26

Question: Should Sapho use OpenRouter as a backup provider when the primary OpenAI Codex route is unavailable/rate-limited?

Current result: earlier OpenRouter fallback testing was superseded. Current Sapho routing keeps OpenRouter out of the active provider/fallback chain because OpenRouter credit limits caused repeated auxiliary/compression failures. /opt/data/config.yaml should have fallback_providers: [], and the OpenRouter key should remain disabled/commented unless Jenffry explicitly reopens the decision.

Suggested next action: If OpenAI/Codex credits are exhausted, treat that as a Codex/OpenAI availability problem or a new provider-selection decision, not as an automatic OpenRouter fallback issue.

Retired / Closed

Dashboard GitHub Pages stale publish failure

Status: closed on 2026-07-05

Result: The Daily dashboard source refresh job (b7a5b1d24d5c) was failing at the GitHub Pages publish step. Initial cron output hid the real Git stderr, so /opt/data/scripts/publish_sapho_dashboard.py was patched to preserve subprocess stdout/stderr in future failures. Verification with the script's askpass environment showed credentials were present and the real failure was main -> main (stale info) from git push --force-with-lease. The publisher was patched to run git fetch origin main immediately before the force-with-lease push, then the direct refresh script was rerun successfully and verified generated plus live dashboard markers.

Next action: no user action required unless the next scheduled Daily dashboard source refresh fails again.

Continuity Steward weekly repair and storage health

Status: closed on 2026-06-23

Result: Jenffry asked Sapho to repair the failed weekly improvement loop and storage issue. The repair was completed the same day: the prior weekly cron failure was identified as a 120-second script timeout even though the nested Steward run had completed; cron.script_timeout_seconds was raised to 900; /opt/data/scripts/continuity_steward.py improve was manually rerun and exited 0; storage cleanup improved /opt/data from about 702 MB free / 99% used to about 5.8 GB free / 84% used; backup archives and modified script syntax were verified. See log entry “2026-06-23 — Sapho storage and weekly Steward repair.”

Body system / physical-health pipeline

Status: closed on 2026-05-24 — completed and moved to monitoring

Result: Jenffry marked the physical-health goals, physical-health dashboard, and whole physical-health system/pipeline complete. The canonical source is physical-health, Hevy remains the source of truth, and the dashboard/direct refresh pipeline now maintains the live physical-health display. Remaining physical-health work is normal execution, weekly/monthly review, medical input, and later calibration — not an open system-build loop.

Move goal structure out of TickTick if it creates bloat

Status: closed on 2026-05-09

Result: Jenffry approved archiving the 15 planning records from the 🎯Goals TickTick list, moving Finance review to 💰Finances, moving Relationship/fatherhood clarity session to 🏋‍♂Health as the mental-health/clarity home, and deleting the empty 🎯Goals TickTick container. Long-term and 6-month goal structure now belongs in the wiki/check-ins, not TickTick.

Verify self-improving skill notes loop

Status: closed on 2026-05-17 — inspected

Question: Is Hermes Agent's self-improving skill loop actually capturing useful notes from repeated review/check-in runs and turning them into better skills/templates, or is Sapho only relying on memory/habit?

Finding: The loop is working in-turn. Recent check-in/review feedback produced concrete updates to governing skills, reusable templates, cron prompts, wiki decisions/open loops/logs, and concise reference files. Evidence includes same-day patches to jenffry-continuity, ticktick-task-management, personal-finance-budgeting, health-and-fitness-planning, and weekend-social-planning, plus the reusable review templates and tracking dashboard calibration.

Weakness: The loop is not yet fully autonomous or lean. It depends on Sapho recognizing reusable corrections during the turn, and the growing number of reference files/prompts could itself become bloat.

Result: Original inspection loop closed. Carry forward the governance problem into the Sapho-system bloat/lean review: keep the improvement loop, but audit whether skill/reference/template additions should be merged, shortened, or pruned.

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: Replaced reliance on habit with a scheduled weekly continuity memory audit cron job.

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: TickTick OAuth credentials were stored in /opt/data/.env, authorization completed with tasks:read tasks:write, project listing was verified, and write access was verified by creating and completing a harmless test task in 🛠Maintenance. Sapho can now read and write TickTick tasks through Telegram.

degree 6

Status: Redone on 2026-06-08 from Jenffry's corrected perspective: outer-signal audits must evaluate utilization, underuse, improvement potential, and new sensors/tools, not only list current connections. Current priority: turn Sapho's signals into a goal-serv

Strategic OrientationCurrent Strategy

Outer Signal Audit

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Redone on 2026-06-08 from Jenffry's corrected perspective: outer-signal audits must evaluate utilization, underuse, improvement potential, and new sensors/tools, not only list current connections.
  • Current priority: turn Sapho's signals into a goal-serving perception system: use restored Google Calendar/Gmail deliberately, improve use of existing TickTick/Hevy/SimpleFIN/wiki/dashboard/cron/web signals, and evaluate new integrations in read-only proof steps before adding automation.
  • Active signals: TickTick tasks/habits, Hevy, SimpleFIN/finance cockpit, Google Calendar/Gmail, wiki/Goal Spines, dashboard, cron jobs, web search/current-world lookup, browser/current facts, Telegram/direct conversation.
  • Open loops: TickTick has an undated-task hygiene pocket; SimpleFIN importer reports one warning without user-facing detail; recurring review prompts still need alignment against strategic-orientation; new candidate sensors need scoped proofs before becoming standing feeds.
  • Next concrete move: use Google Calendar/Gmail in read-only planning/check-in proofs before adding any automation; then design a lightweight signal inbox/event ledger and test one health sensor such as Apple Health/wearable export.

Purpose

This audit answers:

What does Sapho currently perceive about Jenffry and the world, how well are we using those signals, and what new tools/sensors would materially improve goal service?

This is not just an inventory. A good outer-signal audit must ask:

  1. Utilization: are we using each available tool/signal at the right moments?
  2. Underuse: what high-value source exists but is not feeding reviews/advice/actions?
  3. Improvement potential: what extra value could be extracted from tools already connected?
  4. New sensors: what additional integrations or outside-world lookups would materially improve goal service?
  5. Cost/friction: would adding or using the signal create privacy, token, attention, or admin-bloat costs that outweigh the benefit?

Classification:

  • Clean: live, current, goal-relevant, and usable now.
  • Clean with watchpoint: usable, but has a known caveat.
  • Degraded: connected in theory but not currently reliable.
  • Underused: available or feasible but not yet feeding the operating loop well.
  • Candidate sensor: potentially useful, but requires proof before standing use.
  • Do not add yet: too much risk/friction for current goal value.

Executive read

Mentat assessment: Sapho already has a strong core stack. The missed opportunity is not simply “add more tools.” It is to make the existing and possible signals behave like a coherent perception layer:

Sensor → normalized signal → severity/dedupe → Sapho judgment → safe action/proposal → feedback

The current strongest live signals are:

  1. TickTick — concrete execution tasks and mental-health habit definitions.
  2. Hevy — physical-health/workout/body-system evidence.
  3. SimpleFIN — finance cockpit and budget-risk evidence.
  4. Wiki/Goal Spines — canonical goals, decisions, context, and source ownership.
  5. Dashboard — visible cockpit rendering source-owned state.
  6. Cron — recurring reviews, refreshes, backups, and watchdogs.
  7. Web search/current-world lookup — outside visibility: current facts, external tools, methods, prices, options, venues, docs, and comparison points.
  8. Telegram/direct conversation — Jenffry's live inner signals, commands, corrections, and goal truth.

The biggest immediate gap was Google Workspace: credentials existed but auth was revoked. It was restored on 2026-06-12 and verified with a valid auth check, Calendar read, and Gmail labels read.

The next structural gap is a single lightweight signal inbox / event ledger where tool outputs are normalized into “what changed, severity, source, confidence, proposed action, status.” Without that, each integration risks becoming another scattered feed.

Current signal inventory and improvement read

TickTick tasks — clean with watchpoint

  • Status: Clean with watchpoint
  • Last probe: 6 active projects, 49 active tasks, 0 overdue by guard scan, 1 today item, 21 undated active tasks.
  • Goal service: execution layer for concrete tasks, dated actions, appointments, reminders, and protected review sessions.
  • Current use: strong for daily/weekly planning, but still at risk of becoming a task pile unless reviews classify vague/undated items through the action firewall.
  • Underuse/improvement:
  • Track repeated deferrals and stale undated items as a signal, not just clutter.
  • Surface “tasks that do not support current Goal Spines” during weekly/monthly reviews.
  • Use a severity model: overdue/blocked/dated-soon/undated/stale/repeatedly-deferred.
  • Next proof step: run a one-time undated-task hygiene pass and classify items as schedule, intentional park, wiki/open-loop, archive, or close.

TickTick habits / Habit Records — clean with private-API caveat

  • Status: Clean with watchpoint
  • Last probe: all 15 intended mental-health habits are present.
  • Goal service: inner-signal support for mental health, energy, sleep, anxiety-control, checking, self-reflection, social/leisure/life-story signals.
  • Current use: dashboard and weekly/monthly reviews can use the signal, but daily interpretation must stay light to avoid observer-mode/checking.
  • Underuse/improvement:
  • Use habit trends as weekly/monthly pattern evidence, not a daily mood autopsy.
  • Add correlation only at review altitude: sleep ↔ anxiety-control ↔ workouts ↔ social/leisure actions.
  • Keep raw notes private/controlled; publish only what Jenffry intentionally wants visible.

Hevy — clean

  • Status: Clean
  • Last probe: API OK; 155 workouts; last workout 2026-06-06; last 7 days had 2 workouts; last 30 days had 14 workouts.
  • Goal service: physical-health source of truth for workouts, routine balance, strength/cardio/body-system review.
  • Current use: strong weekly/monthly source; dashboard pulls workout/strength/body data.
  • Underuse/improvement:
  • Add a recovery/consistency sensor: days since last workout, routine imbalance, muscle-group gaps, PR/volume trends.
  • Use Hevy to inform weekly plan: whether to protect lifting, deload, cardio, or recovery.
  • Avoid duplicating gym tracking into TickTick; TickTick should hold only chosen gym logistics when needed.

SimpleFIN / finance cockpit — clean with watchpoint

  • Status: Clean with watchpoint
  • Last probe: import OK; 15 accounts, 517 transactions, max transaction date 2026-06-08; importer summary reported one warning/error without useful detail.
  • Goal service: finance stability, weekly/monthly budget pacing, credit-card balance awareness, no credit-card-interest restart.
  • Current use: good cockpit source, but interpretation can improve.
  • Underuse/improvement:
  • Add anomaly detection: unusual vendor/amount/category, low balance, high spend rate, credit-card-interest risk.
  • Add warning-detail logging so the one importer warning is not invisible.
  • Consider a budget-intent layer later, such as YNAB or Actual Budget, only if Jenffry will actually use it. SimpleFIN shows what happened; a budget layer shows what was intended.

Google Workspace: Calendar/Gmail — clean with approval boundary

  • Status: Clean with approval boundary
  • Last probe: restored on 2026-06-12 after TOKEN_REVOKED: invalid_grant; auth check returned AUTHENTICATED, Calendar read returned successfully, and Gmail labels read returned 19 labels.
  • Goal service: calendar deadlines/appointments, family logistics, work meetings, email obligations, receipts/statements, bills, travel, coordination, follow-ups.
  • Current impact: daily/weekly check-ins can use Calendar/Gmail as a live outer-world signal when useful.
  • Approval boundary:
  • Calendar/Gmail reads are allowed when relevant to planning, check-ins, and narrow user requests.
  • Never send email, create/delete calendar events, delete/share Drive files, or modify Docs/Sheets without explicit confirmation.
  • Full email-body reads should stay narrow and purpose-bound; prefer metadata/search first.
  • First proof step: produce a next-7-days calendar digest plus an important-unread email count by sender/category when Jenffry asks for a planning/check-in read.

Wiki / Goal Spines / files — clean, but needs event/state discipline

  • Status: Clean
  • Last probe: 22 area pages, 15 pages with Goal Spines, all core pages exist.
  • Goal service: source-owned strategic context, goal canon, decisions, open loops, raw capture, current strategy, and orientation.
  • Underuse/improvement:
  • Use wiki freshness as a signal: stale Goal Spine, stale active weekly/monthly goal, outdated dashboard summary, unresolved open-loop aging.
  • Add source/confidence/provenance to major proactive claims when possible.
  • Keep Strategic Orientation short; route details to owner pages.

Dashboard — clean after repair

  • Status: Clean after repair
  • Last probe: local/live dashboard had key markers for Strategic Orientation, Sapho Operating System, Finance, Mental health, Physical health, Strength ladder, and Hedonistic Calculator.
  • Goal service: cockpit for navigation, goal movement, signal quality, and current status display.
  • Underuse/improvement:
  • Add a visible but lean “Signal quality” or “System status” panel only if it helps decisions, not as admin decoration.
  • Show degradation states when they affect advice: Google degraded, SimpleFIN warning, cron failure, dashboard publish failure.
  • Keep dashboard display separate from source-of-truth ownership.

Cron jobs — clean with alignment backlog

  • Status: Clean with alignment backlog
  • Last probe: 12 jobs total, 11 enabled, recent enabled jobs OK; monthly long-term goals review scheduled but not yet run.
  • Goal service: recurring reviews, source refreshes, backups, watchdogs, and maintenance.
  • Underuse/improvement:
  • Patch review prompts so they start from strategic-orientation, current-strategy, and relevant Goal Spines.
  • Add explicit “what changed since last run?” and “what needs attention?” framing.
  • Use severity/dedupe so recurring jobs do not repeatedly alert about the same known issue.
  • Keep routine source refresh script-first/no-agent where possible.

Web search / current-world lookup — clean standing sensor

  • Status: Clean standing sensor
  • Research result: current sources on Hermes, OpenClaw, planning assistants, and integration ecosystems show the same broad pattern: valuable agents combine reasoning with real integrations, scheduled/proactive checks, skills, browser/web access, memory, and user-in-the-loop approval.
  • Goal service: prevents Sapho from being locked inside local data; supplies current external facts, public research, tool options, prices, docs, methods, venues, and comparison points.
  • Underuse/improvement:
  • Use web search in audits, tool recommendations, local activity planning, current docs/version questions, prices, buying decisions, and public-research questions.
  • Pair search with local context; do not dump generic web recommendations divorced from Jenffry's goals.
  • Search should feed “what new sensor/tool is worth considering?” in every major system audit.

Browser/current facts — on demand

  • Status: On demand
  • Goal service: interactive websites, dynamic pages, visual verification, account flows, docs that need browser execution, public pages that web extract cannot handle.
  • Underuse/improvement:
  • Create browser recipes for known safe reads: dashboard live check, Google auth status, web app visual checks, event pages, account status pages.
  • Add browser safety classes: read-only allowed, ask-before-form-fill, never-without-explicit-approval for purchases/account changes/medical/legal/financial actions.

Telegram/direct conversation — clean but needs unresolved-commitment extraction

  • Status: Clean
  • Goal service: primary interface for commands, voice dumps, corrections, inner signals, decisions, and quick approvals.
  • Underuse/improvement:
  • Detect unresolved commitments in conversations: “look into,” “remind me,” “we should,” “increase priority,” “schedule,” “ask me later.”
  • Route them through the correct lane: raw capture, TickTick task, wiki open loop, or Sapho roadmap.
  • Avoid turning every voice note into a task; use the action firewall.

External research: what similar systems suggest

Hermes ecosystem findings

Sources:

  • Hermes integrations docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/integrations/
  • Hermes skills docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/skills
  • Hermes bundled skills catalog: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/reference/skills-catalog
  • Awesome Hermes Agent: https://github.com/0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent

Useful findings:

  • Hermes is designed for exactly this kind of expansion: MCP servers, plugins, webhooks, browser automation, messaging platforms, skills, cron, profiles, and external skill directories.
  • Built-in skill categories already cover Apple tools, email, GitHub, productivity tools, research, smart home, social media, Spotify, maps, OCR/documents, Google Workspace, Notion, Airtable, Linear, and more.
  • The skills system is a major advantage: it lets Sapho encode recurring workflows, pitfalls, and verification steps without bloating always-loaded memory.
  • The community ecosystem points toward GUI/workspace tools, plugins, external skill libraries, memory plugins, tracing/observability, and MCP-based integrations.

Sapho implication:

  • Do not build custom integrations first if a Hermes skill/MCP/plugin path already exists.
  • Use the sequence: skill/docs check → small read-only proof → source-of-truth rule → recurring review integration → optional action automation.

OpenClaw / similar-agent findings

Sources:

  • OpenClaw repo/site/docs: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw, https://openclaw.ai/, https://docs.openclaw.ai/start/openclaw
  • OpenClaw guide: https://milvus.io/blog/openclaw-formerly-clawdbot-moltbot-explained-a-complete-guide-to-the-autonomous-ai-agent.md
  • Awesome OpenClaw Agents: https://github.com/mergisi/awesome-openclaw-agents

Useful findings:

  • OpenClaw's workspace-file model uses files like SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, HEARTBEAT.md, and task/context files to shape behavior.
  • Its proactive heartbeat pattern is a useful analogy for Sapho: scheduled wakeups should inspect known signals, report only meaningful deltas, and stay quiet when all is normal.
  • OpenClaw agent-template collections show common agent roles: productivity, finance, health, legal, development, business, marketing, data, DevOps, customer success, voice, and personal assistants.
  • The best transferable idea is not copying templates; it is creating clear per-domain heartbeat/checklist files that define what to inspect, what is normal, what is notable, what requires approval, and what should never be done automatically.

Sapho implication:

  • Create lightweight domain signal policies rather than new noisy dashboards:
  • health heartbeat/checklist
  • finance heartbeat/checklist
  • task/execution heartbeat/checklist
  • system reliability heartbeat/checklist
  • Each should produce normalized events or concise review inputs, not long messages.

Other agent/productivity patterns

Relevant patterns from Browser Use, Huginn, Lindy, Open Interpreter, OpenHands, QwenPaw, and AI planning assistants:

  • Huginn pattern: separate sensor collection from LLM interpretation; normalize events first.
  • Lindy pattern: detect admin item → draft/propose → ask for approval → follow up.
  • Browser Use pattern: browser automation is powerful but brittle; use domain allowlists and structured observations.
  • OpenHands pattern: changes should create auditable artifacts/diffs/logs, not silent mutation.
  • Open Interpreter pattern: local computer control requires approval boundaries.
  • QwenPaw/OpenClaw pattern: skill security, file access controls, scheduling, and multi-channel agents become safety-critical as integrations grow.

Sapho implication:

  • The right next architecture is a signal inbox/event ledger plus approval-first action queue, not uncontrolled proactive autonomy.

Candidate new sensors and integration opportunities

Ranked by likely value for Jenffry now.

1. Restore Google Calendar read-only

  • Value: very high.
  • Serves: life admin, family logistics, work meetings, health appointments, weekly planning.
  • Risk: low-to-medium; calendar is sensitive but less risky than full email.
  • First proof: reauth minimal read-only scope; produce next-7-days agenda and compare against TickTick.

2. Restore Gmail narrow read/search

  • Value: high.
  • Serves: bills, receipts, medical/admin follow-ups, travel, work/life admin, missed obligations.
  • Risk: high privacy; use narrow search/metadata before bodies.
  • First proof: important unread count by sender/category; recent receipt/bill search; no auto-send.

3. Signal inbox / event ledger

  • Value: very high structural improvement.
  • Serves: all domains.
  • Risk: low if local-only and summary-first.
  • First proof: local JSON/SQLite or markdown ledger with source, timestamp, event type, severity, confidence, dedupe key, proposed action, status.
  • Sources to feed first: TickTick overdue/stale, SimpleFIN anomaly, Hevy staleness, Google degraded/recovered, cron failure, dashboard publish failure.

4. Apple Health / wearable export

  • Value: high if Jenffry uses iPhone/Apple Watch/Oura/Fitbit.
  • Serves: mental health, sleep, energy, workout recovery, physical health.
  • Risk: sensitive health data; avoid over-analysis.
  • First proof: choose source. If Apple Health, use Health Auto Export or similar to ingest 14 days of sleep, HRV/resting HR, steps, workouts, mindfulness, mood/state-of-mind if available. If Oura/Fitbit, validate API and fetch last 7 days.

5. Meeting notes/transcripts

  • Value: high for work leverage if meetings are important.
  • Serves: career/work, follow-ups, decision capture.
  • Risk: consent/privacy; do not use for therapy/family/sensitive calls without explicit decision.
  • First proof: Fireflies or Fathom webhook/API for one low-stakes work meeting; extract action items and decisions.

6. Readwise Reader / highlights

  • Value: medium-high for career, learning, leisure, idea synthesis.
  • Serves: career/work leverage, hobbies, future excitement.
  • Risk: personal reading history; paid service may be needed.
  • First proof: validate Readwise token and generate weekly “ideas to action” digest from recent highlights/saved articles.

7. Budget-intent layer: YNAB or Actual Budget

  • Value: medium-high if Jenffry will use budgeting actively.
  • Serves: finance stability and no credit-card-interest restart.
  • Risk: duplicated finance system if not adopted; sensitive data.
  • First proof: only after deciding a budgeting source. Fetch categories/month summary and compare with SimpleFIN actuals.

8. Home Assistant

  • Value: medium if Jenffry already has smart-home devices.
  • Serves: home/family routines, chores, environment, comfort, automations.
  • Risk: home-network/security and accidental control actions.
  • First proof: read-only state digest from Home Assistant REST API; no control until a use case earns it.

9. Food/grocery signal

  • Value: medium; could support health and finance.
  • Serves: nutrition, grocery cost, eating-out/grocery denominator, family logistics.
  • Risk: manual logging burden and privacy; grocery APIs often restricted.
  • First proof: combine email receipt parsing after Gmail restore with Open Food Facts/USDA lookup for occasional barcode/text queries.

10. Events/social discovery

  • Value: medium for leisure/social/future excitement.
  • Serves: social rebuilding, hobbies, family outings, leisure.
  • Risk: noisy recommendations; some event APIs are paid/limited.
  • First proof: weekly web-search digest with 3 local options based on chosen interests; create TickTick tasks only for selected logistics.

11. Location history / places reflection

  • Value: low-to-medium and sensitive.
  • Serves: leisure/social/family/time-use reflection.
  • Risk: very sensitive; no clean Google Maps Timeline API; can become surveillance.
  • First proof: optional one-time Google Takeout import only if Jenffry explicitly wants place/time reflection.

12. Notion/Obsidian external notes

  • Value: conditional.
  • Serves: work/career/life-admin if Jenffry already uses them.
  • Risk: duplication with current wiki.
  • First proof: do not add unless there is an existing active vault/workspace. Current Sapho wiki already owns this role.

Better-use plan for existing tools

Normalize signals before interpreting them

Every recurring sensor should produce, explicitly or implicitly:

  • Source
  • Timestamp
  • Event type
  • Severity: FYI / notable / needs decision / urgent / degraded
  • Confidence
  • Dedupe key
  • Proposed action
  • Status: new / acknowledged / acted / parked / resolved

Make reviews delta-based

Recurring check-ins should ask:

  • What changed since last run?
  • What matters for this week's/month's goals?
  • What is degraded or blocked?
  • What can Sapho safely do?
  • What needs Jenffry approval?
  • What should be ignored to prevent admin soup?

Keep action permissions staged

  1. Read-only sensing — safe first step.
  2. Draft/propose — Sapho prepares but does not send/change.
  3. Approved write — scoped, reversible changes after Jenffry approval.
  4. Standing automation — only after repeated value and clear guardrails.

Never automate financial transfers, medical/legal submissions, relationship-critical messages, purchases, or broad destructive/system changes without explicit per-action approval.

Recommended implementation sequence

Phase 1 — Repair and structure the core perception layer

  1. Restore Google Calendar read-only.
  2. Restore Gmail narrow read/search only after Calendar works.
  3. Design the local signal inbox/event-ledger schema.
  4. Patch daily/weekly/monthly review prompts to use Strategic Orientation, Goal Spines, and signal-quality notes.
  5. Add SimpleFIN warning detail and Hevy staleness/recovery summaries.

Phase 2 — Add one body/health sensor

  1. Ask which wearable/phone health source Jenffry actually uses.
  2. Test Apple Health export, Oura, or Fitbit with 7–14 days of data.
  3. Add only weekly/monthly interpretation; avoid daily over-analysis.

Phase 3 — Add work/leverage sensors only if they serve the current month

  1. Meeting notes/transcripts for work, with consent boundaries.
  2. Readwise/highlights for learning and idea-to-action synthesis.
  3. Browser recipes for repeatable account/status checks.

Phase 4 — Add optional life-enrichment sensors

  1. Local events/social discovery digest.
  2. Food/grocery receipt parsing and barcode lookup.
  3. Home Assistant read-only state if already available.

Do not add yet

  • Always-on location tracking.
  • Broad Gmail body ingestion without narrow use cases.
  • Browser agents that act on financial/medical/legal/account pages without explicit approval.
  • A second task/planning app competing with TickTick unless TickTick stops serving execution well.
  • More dashboards before the signal inbox/event model is clear.
  • New feeds that create weekly noise but no decisions.

  • Hermes skills docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/skills
  • Hermes bundled skills catalog: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/reference/skills-catalog
  • Awesome Hermes Agent: https://github.com/0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent

Audit verification

  • Read existing Strategic Orientation, Current Strategy, and prior Outer Signal Audit.
  • Used web search/extract for Hermes Agent docs, skills catalog, Awesome Hermes Agent, OpenClaw docs/repo/ecosystem, and external integration candidates.
  • Compared external patterns against Jenffry's current goals and Sapho's source-of-truth rules.
  • Preserved the previous live-probe findings where still relevant instead of inventing new API status.
  • Reframed recommendations as staged read-only proofs before automation.
Progress Tracking

Progress Tracking

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active lightweight tracking page for daily signals and weekly trend summaries.
  • Current priority: keep tracking useful without feeding rumination or admin soup.
  • Active signals: TickTick habit scores/notes, observer-mode / rumination when relevant, presence / future excitement, gym intention/completion, finance guardrail, relationship/fatherhood clarity, career proof, leisure/social restoration, life-admin follow-through, and make-it-count outcome. Daily recurring outputs no longer ask for sleep/capacity scores.
  • Open loops: summarize the current week during weekly review, display trend infographics on the dashboard, and adjust prompts if signals are noisy or not useful.
  • Next concrete move: keep daily tracking compact, use weekly review for broader area signals, and let the dashboard show the tracking cockpit instead of duplicating status by hand.

This page is the home for daily/weekly progress signals. The dashboard should read this page rather than storing progress widgets inside system/dashboard.md.

Operating rule

Daily check-ins may ask for short tracking answers. Those answers should feed the weekly dashboard and monthly review. If a question is not useful, it should be removed or replaced during reviews.

Use a 1–5 scale by default instead of green/yellow/red:

  • 1 = poor / high friction / needs protection
  • 2 = weak / strained
  • 3 = workable / neutral
  • 4 = good / stable
  • 5 = strong / notably good

When the metric is a negative signal, label it clearly so the scale is not confusing. Example: “observer-mode intensity: 1 low, 5 high.”

Tracking framework

Tracking has two layers:

  1. Daily minimum signal — only 1–2 planning prompts in a check-in, such as accepted big task, what today/tomorrow must protect, what to defer, gym intention, finance guardrail, or make-it-count outcome. Do not ask for sleep/capacity scores.
  2. Weekly area signal — broader life-area measures summarized during weekly review and displayed as infographics on the dashboard.

Daily signal set

Use these when they are relevant to the day:

  1. Accepted big task or make-it-count outcome.
  2. What today/tomorrow must protect.
  3. What to defer if overloaded.
  4. Observer-mode / rumination intensity only if clearly relevant, where 1 = low and 5 = high.
  5. Gym intention/completion: planned, completed, skipped, or substituted.
  6. Finance guardrail: stayed within today’s guardrail, yes/no/unclear.

Weekly life-area signal set

Use these during weekly review or dashboard synthesis, not as daily homework:

  • Health: mental-health split from physical-health; TickTick habit trends; strain/load when voluntarily given or inferable; rumination/observer-mode; presence/future-excitement; gym sessions; pickleball/activity substitution; cardio/mobility; weight/waist/abdomen measurements; strength-goal anchors; medical/lab follow-through.
  • Relationships: relationship/fatherhood clarity session progress, fatherhood presence, partner/marriage repair/strain signal, avoided circular analysis.
  • Finances: weekly spending guardrail, surplus/deficit comfort, debt/loan autopay status, no-credit-card-interest guardrail.
  • Career: Princess UX proof/workstream progress, senior-UX practice, AI/work leverage experiment, work spillover risk.
  • Leisure: social contact, planned outing, hobby/future-excitement action, restoration rather than avoidance.
  • Life Admin: immigration waypoint, appointments, home/admin obligations, unresolved paperwork.
  • Sapho system: dashboard freshness, open-loop load, wiki/source-of-truth cleanliness, cron/backup health.

Dashboard infographic display rules

The dashboard should render tracking as a cockpit, not a second database:

  • pull daily trend numbers from this page’s Daily log
  • pull area status from each page’s own ## Dashboard summary
  • pull unresolved-load distribution from open-loops.md
  • use bars/rings/coverage cards for quick scanning
  • keep the raw detail inside the owning wiki pages

Active daily tracking prompts

Keep the daily set small. Ask only 1–3 of these when relevant:

  1. Accepted big task or make-it-count outcome.
  2. What today/tomorrow must protect.
  3. What to defer if overloaded.
  4. Observer-mode / rumination intensity only if clearly relevant, where 1 = low and 5 = high.
  5. Gym intention/completion: planned, completed, skipped, or substituted.
  6. Finance guardrail: stayed within today’s guardrail, yes/no/unclear.

Daily questions should be adjusted from the active weekly/monthly focus. Do not ask all metrics every day.

Current week

Week: 2026-07-20 to 2026-07-26

Active tracking focus

Use the proposed weekly goal in current-strategy as the tracking spine until Jenffry accepts, cuts, or corrects it:

  • Medical/health: reschedule the missed ENT follow-up; protect three body sessions, with minimum success as two lifts plus one cardio/pickleball/walk substitute.
  • Relationships/fatherhood: one Aubrey/fatherhood container move — prep, timing choice, or conversation if contained — while protecting family presence.
  • Finance/life admin: review Mom housing cost reality and weekly finances; hold a spending brake after last week's over-pace result.
  • Leisure/social: choose one intentional low-cost leisure/social anchor only; park extra events unless deliberately selected.
  • Daily planning signal: one big task, one quick win, and what to defer if overloaded.
  • Mental-health habits remain available as background TickTick/dashboard signal; do not turn them into daily question clutter unless Jenffry volunteers a relevant pattern.

Last completed week signal — 2026-07-13 to 2026-07-19

  • Movement: cardiology follow-up completed with good ultrasound result; repeat testosterone follow-up is already scheduled as urology; two Hevy workouts plus pickleball/body activity kept the body rhythm alive.
  • Slips/risks: ENT follow-up was missed and still needs rescheduling; weekly non-debt spending was over pace; habit check-in data was empty/degraded rather than proof of noncompliance.
  • Planning lesson: center the next week on ENT + body rhythm + finance reality, keep relationship/fatherhood to one contained move, and do not let task cleanup replace the goal-serving moves.

Daily log

Use one compact row per day. Do not overfill this page.

  • Monday: 2026-06-01 — TickTick reviewed; day felt partial with family-invite and phone-safe SSH spillover; make-it-count: keep tomorrow to one big task plus one quick win; note: gym bag prep and trash were the other visible same-day items.
  • Tuesday: 2026-06-02 — TickTick reviewed; day felt partial with the 7pm Dr. Kahane appointment and Life Admin spillover still open; make-it-count: keep tomorrow to one big task plus one quick win and finish the Sapho Dashboard / birthday-invite pair; note: overdue pile is mostly Life Admin plus one Relationships item.
  • Tuesday: 2026-05-26 — TickTick reviewed; day felt overloaded/partial with overdue spillover; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's health baseline appointment and keep tomorrow to one big task plus one quick win; note: mental-health and finance signals not directly reported.
  • Wednesday: 2026-06-03 — TickTick reviewed; day felt partial with family-invite, dashboard, and finance spillover still open; make-it-count: keep tomorrow to one big task plus one quick win; note: overdue still contains life-admin and finance carryover.
  • Thursday: 2026-06-04 — TickTick reviewed; day felt partial with two due-today Life Admin tasks and eight overdue spillover still open; make-it-count: finish the Sapho Dashboard tomorrow and keep the rest of the day to one big task plus one quick win; note: no extra daily score prompt needed.
  • Thursday: 2026-05-28 — TickTick reviewed; day felt partial with 5 overdue spillover and no due-today tasks; make-it-count: repair Sapho Dashboard tomorrow; note: backlog is mostly Life Admin plus one system/career item.
  • Friday: 2026-06-05 — TickTick reviewed; day partial with Gym still open and two overdue items (Nolan's one year birthday party invite, Finish Sapho Dashboard); make-it-count: finish the Sapho Dashboard tomorrow and keep tomorrow to one big task plus one quick win; note: no extra daily score prompt needed.
  • Saturday: 2026-05-30 — TickTick reviewed; day felt partial/overloaded with two same-day open items still pending and tomorrow spillover queued; make-it-count: land the family invite and protect tomorrow’s finance/system follow-through; note: Overdue view was empty, but the separate date-only guard still found same-day spillover.
  • Saturday: 2026-06-06 — TickTick reviewed; day felt partial with one same-day leisure task still open plus overdue family-invite and dashboard spillover; make-it-count: carry tomorrow to one big task plus one quick win; note: no extra daily score prompt needed.
  • Sunday: 2026-06-07 — TickTick reviewed; day felt partial with Finish Sapho Dashboard slipping past its 7:45 PM slot and a late-night family pickup still open; make-it-count: keep tomorrow to one big task plus one quick win and protect the health/family week; note: no extra daily score prompt needed.
  • Monday: 2026-06-08 — TickTick reviewed; day felt partial with five visible dated tasks still open for today and tomorrow carrying a dense health/admin/leisure pile; make-it-count: keep tomorrow to one big task plus one quick win; note: no extra daily score prompt needed.
  • Tuesday: 2026-06-09 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial with Finish Sapho Dashboard, Pick up Aubrey from the airport, and Send Nolan invite still open; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's health/family anchor and clear the dashboard carryover before adding new leisure/system work; note: gym bag prep and other dated clutter are still alive.
  • Wednesday: 2026-06-10 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial but health/family/admin quick wins moved: Gym, gym bag, trash, and Nolan invite completed; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's health/family rhythm and do not let dashboard/system/leisure spillover crowd the one-big-task rule; note: Overdue is empty, but same-day dated guard still shows six open items.
  • Thursday: 2026-06-11 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial/overloaded with no completed-task entries and nine dated open items; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's health baseline and one family/future-excitement move, then defer dashboard/system and leisure backlog if overloaded; note: overdue guard shows six carryovers from 2026-06-10 plus Gym, D&D, and trash still dated today.
  • Friday: 2026-06-12 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial/restorative with Gym and trash completed while pickleball remains unconfirmed; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's pickleball or best health/social substitute first, then keep Life Admin to one quick win; note: active TickTick still shows a dated Gym item plus the Sapho signal-integration review carryover, so tomorrow should avoid inheriting invisible clutter.
  • Saturday: 2026-06-13 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial/restorative with friendship-content and lawn completed, but pickleball remains unconfirmed and the weekly reset did not visibly close; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's weekly-review reset and keep the carryover to Gym, Sapho signal-integration review, and one leisure reset only; note: date guard still shows two overdue carryovers plus one same-day VR task.
  • Sunday: 2026-06-14 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial/restorative with Church and Gym completed, Pickleball & park with Nolan marked won't-do/cancelled, and no overdue guard items; make-it-count: start Monday with health-baseline rhythm, protect Dr. Stillman and the ENT/mouth-guard follow-up, and do not let optional leisure or gym-bag prep crowd the one-big-task rule; note: Today still had Prepare gym bag and UFC leisure open at closeout.
  • Monday: 2026-06-15 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial/needs recovery with zero completed-task entries and six dated guard items still open or overdue; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's health-baseline rhythm by choosing either Gym or the ENT/mouth-guard follow-up as the big move, then use trash/gym-bag as the quick win; note: defer the optional UFC/leisure carryover if overloaded.
  • Tuesday: 2026-06-16 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial with trash, gym-bag prep, and one leisure reset completed, while Gym was cancelled/won't-do and ENT/mouth-guard follow-up remains open; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's health-baseline rhythm by choosing the ENT/mouth-guard follow-up or a body-system session as the one big move, then keep trash/gym-bag as the quick win only; note: no overdue guard items, but two same-day health items still shape tomorrow.
  • Wednesday: 2026-06-17 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial/needs recovery with zero completed-task entries and four dated guard items still open or overdue; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's health baseline by choosing the ENT/mouth-guard follow-up or Gym as the one big move, then use trash or gym-bag prep as the quick win; note: do not let the weekly stack expand before the Jun 19 testosterone test.
  • Thursday: 2026-06-18 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial but body-system movement happened with Gym completed twice in the feed; gym-bag and one trash entry were cancelled/won't-do while active dated carryover still shows ENT/mouth-guard, D&D, and Take out the trash; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's Jun 19 testosterone test as the one big health-baseline move; note: keep Dr. Stillman/support-group/gym as optional or bounded so Friday does not become a full-stack catch-up day.
  • Friday: 2026-06-19 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial but health-baseline progress was strong with testosterone test, Gym, dandruff treatment check, trash, finance/trial tasks, and Aubrey appointment completed; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's quick finance cancellation and one real relationship/body anchor; note: overdue/date guard still shows Get prescription and buy mouth guard plus Dr. Stephanie Stillman as the meaningful carryover.
  • Saturday: 2026-06-20 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial/restorative with mouth guard/prescription and Babe's Pilates/yoga completed, while Cancel ElevenLabs and Social skills call still show dated today; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's weekly reset and do not let the finance cancellation or loose leisure/social follow-up become invisible clutter; note: no overdue guard items, but two same-day open items remain.
  • Sunday: 2026-06-21 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial/transition with 0 true completed-task entries and three dated open items: Cancel ElevenLabs, Social skills call, and Prepare gym bag; make-it-count: protect Monday gym as the weekly-goal start, clear the finance cancellation as the quick win, and defer loose social/admin clutter if overloaded; note: closeout ran earlier than the nominal 9:30 PM window.
  • Monday: 2026-06-22 — TickTick reviewed at closeout; day partial but weekly health rhythm started with Gym completed; make-it-count: protect tomorrow's pickleball/cardio or best health/social substitute, clear Cancel ElevenLabs as the quick win, and avoid letting trash/gym-bag prep turn into invisible clutter; note: no overdue guard items, but three same-day items remained open at closeout.

Suggested entry format:

  • YYYY-MM-DD — TickTick mental-health habits updated; gym ___; finance ___; make-it-count: ___; note: ___

Weekly trend summary

Previous week, 2026-05-18 to 2026-05-24:

  • Sleep trend: mostly workable, with 3–4/5 entries; not enough full daily data for a clean trend.
  • Capacity / mental-health trend: stable-to-workable; one low-capacity rest day was handled as recovery rather than failure.
  • Observer-mode / rumination trend: low-to-moderate where recorded, but incomplete data.
  • Gym trend: strong week from Hevy — 4 workouts, 68 working sets, average 51.1 minutes; missed ideal 5-day plan but exceeded the minimum.
  • Finance guardrail trend: strained — week non-debt spending reached 82% of the $525.54 cap by the latest 2026-05-22 transaction data; monthly usable remaining is negative.
  • Goal-alignment trend: health and body-system execution moved; finance and relationship/fatherhood clarity need tighter protection next week.
  • Main lesson for next week: physical-health system work is complete enough to move into normal review; next strategic build is the mental-health support profile, observer-mode reset, and future-excitement/practice pipeline while keeping tracking light.

Last completed week summary

Week: 2026-06-01 to 2026-06-07

  • Strain/load signal: mixed and under-observed; several days were partial or overloaded, but there is no sign of collapse. Future daily prompts should ask planning questions rather than sleep/capacity scores.
  • Gym trend: strong enough — 3 workouts in 7 days, 61 working sets, minimum successful week met, and the last workout was 2026-06-06.
  • Finance trend: strained — monthly included spending is at 64% of the June budget by 2026-06-05 data, and weekly non-debt spending is 139% of pace; auto, shopping/household, and misc/buffer are the main pressure points.
  • Task-load trend: improved after Sunday cleanup; the day now holds only one big task plus one quick win instead of a spillover pile.
  • Main lesson: protect medical follow-through, keep the finance guardrail visible, and let system/dashboard work proceed without crowding out family.

Weekly review responsibilities

Weekly reviews should:

  • summarize the daily signals from this page or recent check-in notes
  • identify trend direction: improving, stable, worsening, noisy, or insufficient data
  • connect trends to weekly goal alignment and next-week planning
  • update the active tracking prompts if a question is not producing useful signal
  • keep the one-big-task + one-quick-win rule visible in the coming week plan

Monthly review responsibilities

Monthly reviews should:

  • summarize weekly trends across the month
  • identify whether tracked signals support or threaten long-term goals
  • decide which metrics to keep, drop, add, or change for the next month
  • avoid over-measuring mental health in a way that feeds rumination

Anti-bloat guard

If tracking starts feeling like homework, reduce it. Current default: no sleep/capacity score questions in daily recurring outputs; use one planning signal plus weekly/monthly synthesis.

  • mental-health
