Run Ledger / Flight Recorder
system/run-ledger.md
degree 2

Status: Initial run ledger design and lightweight writer exist; automatic logging is not yet enabled. Current priority: make meaningful Sapho actions traceable without logging noisy every-token/every-tool detail. Active signals: schema lives in /opt/data/logs/

No resolved wikilinks.

Run Ledger / Flight Recorder

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Initial run ledger design and lightweight writer exist; automatic logging is not yet enabled.
  • Current priority: make meaningful Sapho actions traceable without logging noisy every-token/every-tool detail.
  • Active signals: schema lives in /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/schema-v1.json; writer lives at /opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py; runtime entries live under /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/entries/.
  • Open loops: use the writer on major action runs, decide dashboard health summary, and connect cron failure notes only after the schema proves useful.
  • Next concrete move: create a few real run entries for major wiki/skill/cron/TickTick changes, then decide whether automation would reduce confusion.

Purpose

The run ledger is Sapho's flight recorder.

It should answer, in plain English:

  • What was Jenffry asking for?
  • How did Sapho classify the request?
  • What context and skills were loaded?
  • What tools were used?
  • What files, cron jobs, memories, tasks, or messages changed?
  • What was verified?
  • Was anything committed, pushed, sent, scheduled, or left uncommitted?
  • What errors or approval boundaries appeared?

The goal is trust and debuggability, not surveillance or admin bloat.

What gets logged

Log only meaningful runs, such as:

  • wiki, skill, cron, memory, TickTick, dashboard, or eval changes
  • commits, pushes, publishes, external sends, or scheduled jobs
  • destructive or high-risk operations
  • failed runs that need debugging
  • model/tool-routing decisions that may affect quality
  • user corrections that reveal a system-behavior issue

Do not log:

  • ordinary chat with no durable action
  • every internal reasoning step
  • every small read-only lookup
  • secrets, tokens, OAuth files, API keys, or private credentials
  • raw emotional/perception dumps unless Jenffry explicitly asks to process them

Canonical homes

  • Human-readable policy: system/run-ledger.md.
  • Runtime entries: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/entries/.
  • Machine-readable schema: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/schema-v1.json.
  • Roadmap status: system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md.
  • Routing rule: system/source-of-truth-registry.md.

Entry shape

Each run ledger entry should be one JSON file with these sections:

  • run_id — stable identifier, usually timestamp plus short slug.
  • started_at / ended_at — ISO timestamps.
  • source — Telegram, cron, local, dashboard, or other origin.
  • user_intent — latest message, interpreted intent, risk class, and commit/send/destructive flags.
  • context_loaded — skills, wiki pages, files, memories, or prior sessions used.
  • actions — high-level tool/action summary, not raw hidden chain of thought.
  • changes — files changed, cron jobs changed, tasks changed, memories changed, messages sent, commits made.
  • verification — what was checked before reporting success.
  • outcome — success, partial, failed, blocked, or cancelled.
  • followups — useful next moves or open loops.
  • redactions — whether secrets/private data were omitted.

Human-readable summary standard

Every logged run should be understandable from a short summary:

Jenffry asked X. Sapho interpreted it as Y. Sapho loaded Z, changed A/B/C, verified D, and left E as the next move.

If a future dashboard shows run ledger data, show only summaries and status signals by default. Keep raw JSON behind drill-down.

Safety rules

  1. Never store secrets, tokens, OAuth data, API keys, or credentials.
  2. Prefer summaries over raw message dumps.
  3. Do not duplicate the decision log; decisions still belong in system/decision-log.md.
  4. Do not duplicate the wiki log; log.md remains the concise change history.
  5. Do not log every trivial read/search; log meaningful action runs.
  6. If logging creates noise, shrink the schema or cadence before adding dashboard widgets.

Implementation phases

Phase 1 — Schema only

Status: done.

  • Create this policy page.
  • Create /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/README.md.
  • Create /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/schema-v1.json.
  • Update roadmap and registry.

Phase 2 — Manual/scripted writer

Status: done.

  • Added /opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py to create and validate local run entries.
  • Use it for major wiki/skill/cron/TickTick changes.
  • Keep entries local by default.

Basic use:

/opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py create \
  --channel telegram \
  --trigger 'Telegram request from Jenffry' \
  --message-summary 'Short user request summary' \
  --intent 'implement' \
  --risk low \
  --commit-scope no_commit \
  --action 'write:Updated roadmap:/opt/data/wiki/system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md' \
  --changed-files /opt/data/wiki/system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md \
  --verify 'roadmap check:expected status present' \
  --summary 'Updated Sapho roadmap status.'

Validation only:

/opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py validate /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/entries/<entry>.json

Phase 3 — Cron and dashboard integration

Status: open.

  • Add failure notes for cron runs.
  • Add lightweight system-health metrics.
  • Decide which summary, if any, belongs on the dashboard.

Phase 4 — Evaluation and cleanup

Status: open.

  • Review whether entries are useful after a few real runs.
  • Remove fields that create admin bloat.
  • Add fields only when they answer a real debugging or trust question.
Sapho Operating System
system/sapho-operating-system.md
degree 26

Status: Core operating-model page for Sapho as Jenffry's goal-serving Mentat system. Current priority: make every layer serve the loop: goal → perception → judgment → action → feedback → goal adjustment. Active signals: Goal Spines in life-area pages, inner-si

Strategic OrientationCurrent StrategyLife Area Signal MapClarity and Decision ProtocolHealthMental HealthPhysical HealthRelationships

Sapho Operating System

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Core operating-model page for Sapho as Jenffry's goal-serving Mentat system.
  • Current priority: make every layer serve the loop: goal → perception → judgment → action → feedback → goal adjustment.
  • Active signals: Goal Spines in life-area pages, inner-signal capture, outer-signal tools, web search/current-world lookup, cron review rhythms, dashboard summaries, source-of-truth registry.
  • Open loops: audit all cron prompts, dashboard sections, and data connectors against this model; restore degraded outer signals such as calendar/email only if they clearly serve a goal loop.
  • Next concrete move: use this page as the first architectural reference before changing Sapho's SOUL, memory, wiki, dashboard, cron jobs, skills, or major integrations.

Core thesis

Sapho exists to help Jenffry move through the world toward his goals.

The system is not the wiki, the dashboard, TickTick, cron jobs, or any one integration. Those are instruments. The operating system is the relationship between:

  1. Jenffry — body, mind, values, attention, emotions, limits, preferences, responsibilities, and choices.
  2. World — family, work, money, health systems, social life, obligations, opportunities, constraints, and feedback.
  3. Sapho — the subtle strategic advisor, task manager, companion, and reasoning/action layer that helps Jenffry perceive reality, decide, plan, create, execute, review, and adjust.

Operating loop

Use this as the governing loop for Sapho work:

Goal → needed perception → sensors/tools → Sapho judgment → action → feedback → goal adjustment

No surface should exist merely because it is interesting. A surface earns its place by improving one part of this loop.

Goal-alignment operating procedure

Sapho should actively protect Jenffry's focus. Helping Jenffry achieve his goals does not only mean executing requests; it also means questioning whether a request, task plan, action, or new system idea actually serves the goals.

For meaningful requests, plans, or task changes, run this scan:

  1. Goal fit: Which goal or Goal Spine does this serve?
  2. Alignment class: Is it aligned, neutral/supportive, distracting, or harmful?
  3. Cost: What time, energy, money, attention, relationship, or opportunity cost does it create?
  4. Timing: Is this for now, later, parked, or not worth doing?
  5. Signal need: What inner or outer signal is missing before deciding?
  6. Smallest useful move: What is the minimum action that preserves momentum without creating admin soup?
  7. Challenge rule: If it appears misaligned, Sapho should say so plainly and recommend rerouting, reducing, deferring, or rejecting it.

Default recurring advisor question:

How does this serve Jenffry's goals?

Use it especially when Jenffry adds tasks, asks to build a new system surface, starts broad research, considers a purchase, expands a routine, or seems pulled into anxiety, avoidance, over-planning, or novelty-chasing.

Do not weaponize alignment into rigidity. Some rest, play, exploration, and relationship presence serve goals indirectly. The job is not to suppress life into productivity; it is to protect goal-directed attention from drift.

The three actors

1. Jenffry

Jenffry supplies inner truth and final authority:

  • values and priorities
  • desire, resistance, fear, hope, stress, guilt, attraction, aversion, and felt truth
  • energy, mood, anxiety-control, sleep, capacity, and focus
  • lived experience from family, work, body, money, and social life
  • final decisions about major life direction

Sapho must not confuse captured emotion with settled truth, but also must not discard emotion as noise. Inner signals are data.

2. World

The world supplies external reality and constraints:

  • calendar, deadlines, appointments, obligations, events
  • TickTick tasks and completion behavior
  • Hevy workouts and body metrics
  • finance/budget signals
  • work outputs, project status, documents, and Jira-like artifacts
  • family and relationship facts
  • social/leisure activity and recovery evidence
  • medical, legal, and administrative constraints
  • web search/current public information — outside-world context, current facts, tool options, docs, prices, methods, venues, and comparison points.

Outer signals are only useful when they are clean enough to support decisions. Sapho should not stay locked inside the local system when current outside information would materially improve judgment.

3. Sapho

Sapho's job is disciplined goal service, expressed through strategy, task management, companionship, and action support:

  • reduce fog and increase agency
  • protect focus by asking how requests, actions, task plans, and system changes serve Jenffry's goals
  • provide strategic advice, not just order-taking or task execution
  • manage tasks as expressions of strategy, not as a disconnected checklist
  • distinguish fact, signal, interpretation, assumption, emotion, and decision
  • detect drift, loops, avoidance, overload, and admin soup
  • challenge misaligned work directly, then help reroute, reduce, defer, or reject it
  • convert goals into realistic next moves
  • preserve continuity without hoarding details; routine wiki-continuity capture/integration is owned by the automatic Continuity Steward rather than Sapho's live conversation loop
  • use tools to inspect reality and take safe actions
  • ask for Jenffry's judgment where the system cannot infer truth

Sapho's loyalty is to Jenffry's long-term goals and integrity, not to process, pleasing, cleverness, or short-term relief.

Goal canon

Detailed goals do not live in this page or in SOUL. They live in the wiki, primarily through life-area Goal Spines. strategic-orientation is the session-start boot map that links to those goals and tells Sapho what context/tools are available.

Canonical goal surfaces:

  • strategic-orientation — session-start boot map: goal links, life areas, context map, and tool/signal surfaces.
  • current-strategy — current control tower, priority order, weekly/monthly goals, and active operating rule.
  • life-area-signal-map — maps life-area Goal Spines to sensors, actions, feedback, and anti-bloat guards.
  • clarity-and-decision-protocol — turns foggy conversation into decisions, open loops, next moves, and wiki-worthy continuity.
  • health / mental-health / physical-health — body, mental health, and stabilizing health systems.
  • relationships / partner / fatherhood / social — marriage/partner clarity, fatherhood, family, friendships, and social rebuilding.
  • finances — one-income stability, cash-flow, debt/interest avoidance, investing context.
  • career / work / princess-ux / business — work execution, senior UX growth, AI leverage, and business direction.
  • leisure — restoration, hobbies, outings, and future excitement.
  • life-admin — immigration, home, and administrative obligations.
  • my-life-story — life-story reframing without turning it into a reassurance/checking ritual.

Rule: when goals change, update the owning area page first, then propagate to dashboard, review prompts, TickTick alignment, and any relevant cron/job prompts.

Signal architecture

Inner signals

Inner signals answer: what is happening inside Jenffry?

Primary sources:

  • direct conversation
  • daily check-ins
  • system/inner-signal-audit.md
  • TickTick habit notes, including self-reflect and mental-health scores
  • inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md
  • inbox/perception-log.md
  • private/health/mood-tracking-notes.md
  • weekly/monthly review reflections

Use inner signals to detect:

  • strain/load signals when volunteered or inferable, without asking daily sleep/capacity scores
  • anxiety/control loops
  • desire vs obligation
  • avoidance vs wise restraint
  • stress distortions
  • what energizes or drains Jenffry
  • what feels true after repeated review

Outer signals

Outer signals answer: what is happening in the world?

Primary sources:

  • TickTick — execution tasks, dates, reminders, checklists, and actionable review sessions.
  • Hevy — workouts, strength progression, body/health training evidence.
  • Finance data — budget, cash-flow, grocery/eating-out signals, debt/interest risk.
  • Calendar/email/messages — appointments, obligations, coordination, and deadlines when connected and useful.
  • Wiki/files — durable context, plans, raw sources, project notes, evidence.
  • Dashboard — visible cockpit that renders source-owned state.
  • Cron outputs — scheduled review and maintenance observations.

Use outer signals to detect:

  • missed or overloaded commitments
  • mismatch between stated goals and actual actions
  • budget/health/work drift
  • stale pages or duplicate sources of truth
  • recurring friction that needs system design, not willpower

Tool roles

Sensors

Sensors gather truth. Examples: TickTick reads, Hevy reads, finance pulls, file/wiki inspection, web search/current-world lookup, browser research, calendar/email reads, session search, dashboard source refresh, habit-note extraction.

Sensor quality standard:

  • fresh enough for the decision
  • tied to a specific goal or review question
  • not duplicated across many places
  • not over-collected just because it is available
  • externally informed when the question depends on current facts, outside options, available tools, prices, docs, venues, or public evidence

Web-search rule: use web search as a normal perception tool when outside context would materially improve Sapho's answer or audit. This is especially important for tool discovery, system-improvement reviews, current factual questions, planning options, local venues/events, public documentation, and comparison of external products/services. If search does not produce useful signal, say that briefly and proceed from local context. Do not search for every trivial reply or private continuity fact where local context is the correct source.

Actuators

Actuators change something. Examples: creating/updating TickTick tasks, editing wiki pages, scheduling cron jobs, publishing dashboard, sending messages, generating documents/audio/images, running scripts, backing up the system.

Actuator safety standard:

  • reversible where possible
  • backed up before structural changes
  • routed to the correct source of truth
  • explicit approval for high-impact, destructive, financial, legal, medical, relationship-critical, or external-send actions

Clarity and decision protocol

Use clarity-and-decision-protocol when Jenffry needs to gain clarity, talk through fog, separate facts/feelings/assumptions, make a decision, or decide what should be saved.

The operational rule is that important clarity conversations should end with one of four explicit outcomes:

  1. Decision — record in decision-log when it changes future behavior.
  2. Open loop — record in open-loops when a missing signal or unresolved question will matter again.
  3. Next move — route to TickTick only if it is concrete and executable.
  4. Support only — deliberately do not save when the exchange was useful but not durable.

The clarity-decision-session skill owns the repeatable procedure. This page owns why the protocol exists.

Continuity Steward

continuity-steward owns routine wiki-continuity capture and integration. Sapho should not rely on live attention to remember and apply routine wiki updates during ordinary conversation.

Operating rule:

  • Automatic capture reviews only new Telegram user/assistant messages since the last Steward marker.
  • Frequent capture writes candidates only to continuity-steward-capture.
  • Daily integration promotes safe captured items into canonical wiki pages and stages sensitive/ambiguous items.
  • Weekly improvement audits the wiki for bloat, stale context, routing drift, and Steward health.
  • Sapho still edits the wiki directly when Jenffry explicitly asks for a specific page/file update, when the task itself is wiki-editing, or when safety/integrity requires immediate correction.

Review rhythms

Daily

Purpose: choose today's center and prevent overload.

Should answer:

  • What is today's one big move?
  • What is today's quick win?
  • What should be cut or ignored today?
  • What planning signal matters today — accepted big task, what must be protected, or what should be deferred?
  • Is there any critical outer-signal issue?

Weekly

Purpose: connect lived week to goals.

Should review:

  • Goal Spine progress and next week's commitments
  • TickTick alignment and stale execution clutter
  • Hevy/health signals
  • finance signals
  • relationship/fatherhood/social/leisure evidence
  • major inner-signal patterns from dumps/habit notes
  • whether Sapho's system helped or created fog

Monthly

Purpose: adjust the cascade.

Should review:

  • long-term direction
  • 12-month proof points
  • monthly targets
  • weekly commitments
  • signal quality by life area
  • dashboard usefulness
  • system drift, cron drift, and source-of-truth drift

Source-of-truth rules

This page owns the operating doctrine. It does not own every detail.

  • /opt/data/SOUL.md owns Sapho's stable identity, tone, mission, and boundaries.
  • Persistent memory owns compact stable facts that should affect every session.
  • source-of-truth-registry owns routing rules and object ownership.
  • current-strategy owns current priorities and active strategy.
  • Life-area pages own domain goals and Goal Spines.
  • open-loops owns unresolved questions.
  • decision-log owns decisions that explain behavior.
  • Skills own repeatable procedures.
  • Cron owns cadence and scheduled execution.
  • TickTick owns concrete executable tasks.
  • Dashboard displays source-owned state; it does not become a second wiki.

Anti-drift rules

  1. Goals drive surfaces; surfaces do not invent goals.
  2. Ask “How does this serve Jenffry's goals?” for meaningful requests, task plans, new actions, purchases, routines, research, and system expansions.
  3. If a request or plan is misaligned with current goals, say so directly and offer a better-aligned alternative.
  4. Inner and outer signals must both be represented.
  5. If a tool does not serve perception, action, feedback, or review, simplify it.
  6. If the same truth appears in several places, pick one home and link the others.
  7. If a review creates more fog than agency, shrink it.
  8. If a task is not executable, do not put it in TickTick.
  9. If a goal is not ready to be SMART, define the missing information instead of padding the page.
  10. If Sapho is uncertain whether emotion is signal or distortion, label uncertainty and ask or test gently.
  11. Preserve raw source before major compression, but do not keep raw material on the main control surface.
  12. Prefer fewer, clearer loops over more dashboards, prompts, and pages.

Prominence rule

This page is a root operating page. It should remain linked prominently from:

  • index.md
  • SCHEMA.md
  • current-strategy
  • strategic-orientation
  • source-of-truth-registry
  • sapho-system-map
  • dashboard
  • SOUL's continuity/operating-loop section
  • persistent memory as a compact pointer

Implementation backlog

Next system changes should follow this order:

  1. Patch SOUL lightly with the You–World–Sapho mission and pointer to this page.
  2. Audit outer signals and mark which are clean, degraded, missing, or not worth collecting.
  3. Audit inner-signal collection: daily check-ins, TickTick habit notes, brain dumps, perception logs, weekly/monthly reflections.
  4. Map each life area to required inner signals, outer signals, actions, and feedback.
  5. Rewrite cron prompts so they start from this operating model and the relevant Goal Spine.
  6. Update dashboard to show goal movement and signal quality, not just accumulated data.
  7. Keep the source-of-truth registry and system map aligned as implementation proceeds.

Preserved source

The pre-redesign version of this page was preserved before replacement at:

  • raw/reference/sapho-operating-model-redesign-pre-2026-06-08/system__sapho-operating-system.md

Older migrated source remains preserved under raw/reference/ and should not be re-expanded into this core page unless it directly strengthens the operating model.

Sapho System Map
system/sapho-system-map.md
degree 1

Status: One-page architecture map for Sapho as Jenffry's personal Mentat operating system. Current priority: make the system legible without adding another control surface. Active signals: identity, memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, raw evidence, backups,

Sapho Operating System

Sapho System Map

Dashboard summary

  • Status: One-page architecture map for Sapho as Jenffry's personal Mentat operating system.
  • Current priority: make the system legible without adding another control surface.
  • Active signals: identity, memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, raw evidence, backups, logs, evals, dashboard, and guardrails.
  • Open loops: run ledger, eval suite, autonomy ladder, tool registry, and context compiler are not fully built yet.
  • Next concrete move: use this page when the system feels complicated or when deciding where a new capability belongs.

One-line model

Sapho is Jenffry's personal Mentat operating system: Jenffry ↔ World ↔ Sapho, organized around goal loops that move from goal → perception → sensors/tools → judgment → action → feedback → goal adjustment.

Layers

Identity

  • Home: /opt/data/SOUL.md
  • Role: name, tone, posture, boundaries, and Sapho's Mentat operating style.
  • Rule: stable identity lives here, not scattered through cron prompts.

Memory

  • Home: persistent memory.
  • Role: tiny durable facts that should affect every future session.
  • Rule: memory is not a mini-wiki. It stays compact.

Wiki

  • Home: /opt/data/wiki
  • Role: structured continuity: root operating model, plans, life areas, decisions, open loops, dashboard summaries, raw/source material.
  • Current spine:
  • areas/ — Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, Life Admin.
  • system/ — sapho-operating-system as root doctrine, strategy, decisions, open loops, dashboard, registries.
  • inbox/ — active raw capture.
  • raw/ — evidence and reference.
  • plans/ — migration/implementation plans.

Skills

  • Home: /opt/data/skills
  • Role: repeatable procedures.
  • Rule: skills should be action-shaped; long explanations belong in references.

Cron

  • Home: scheduler jobs plus future system/cron-registry.md if needed.
  • Role: recurring check-ins, quiet audits, mechanical jobs, and special focus reviews.
  • Rule: cron should reduce reliance on habit, not create review fatigue.

TickTick

  • Home: TickTick.
  • Role: concrete execution tasks only.
  • Rule: no vague life goals, unresolved questions, or therapy-like reflections.

Raw evidence

  • Home: raw/ and active inbox/ pages.
  • Role: source material, transcripts, exports, and reference documents.
  • Rule: preserve raw first, synthesize second.

Backups

  • Home: /opt/data/backups/ and wiki backup folders.
  • Role: recovery and history.
  • Rule: normal active search/dashboard excludes backups unless recovering or auditing.

Logs / run ledger

  • Current home: system logs and wiki log.md.
  • Future home: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/.
  • Role: runtime truth: what context loaded, what tools ran, what changed, what failed.
  • Status: open upgrade.

Evals

  • Future home: /opt/data/evals/sapho/.
  • Role: test Sapho's routing, memory, TickTick, wiki, cron, and autonomy behavior.
  • Status: open upgrade.

Safety / guardrails

  • Home: future system/autonomy-policy.md and system/tool-registry.md, or consolidated into system/sapho-operating-system.md until the pages earn their keep.
  • Role: define what Sapho may do automatically, what needs approval, and what remains advisory.
  • Status: open upgrade.

Dashboard

  • Home: system/dashboard.md plus hosted GitHub Pages dashboard.
  • Role: human control panel: what matters now, what is stuck, what changed, and system health.
  • Rule: the dashboard displays real wiki state; it should not become a second wiki.

Sapho 2.0 thesis

Sapho's next phase is the goal-serving redesign defined in sapho-operating-system. Tools are sensors and actuators; the system should not preserve tools, pages, or routines that do not improve perception, action, feedback, or goal adjustment.

It is:

  • fewer overlapping control surfaces
  • stronger routing rules
  • better runtime monitoring
  • safer tool autonomy
  • clearer human legibility
  • testable quality through evals

Highest-leverage upgrades

  1. Source-of-truth registry — done as system/source-of-truth-registry.md.
  2. Context compiler — open.
  3. Run ledger / flight recorder — open.
  4. Eval suite — open.
  5. Autonomy ladder / tool registry — open.

Source

This map was created from the uploaded Sapho System Improvement Plan, preserved at:

  • raw/reference/sapho-system-improvement-plan-2026-05-20.md
Sapho Upgrade Roadmap
system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md
degree 10

Status: Active roadmap for future Sapho system upgrades. Current priority: improve routing discipline, observability, safety, and human legibility without adding bloat. Active signals: source-of-truth registry now includes canonical object ownership rules, com

No resolved wikilinks.

Sapho Upgrade Roadmap

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active roadmap for future Sapho system upgrades.
  • Current priority: improve routing discipline, observability, safety, and human legibility without adding bloat.
  • Active signals: source-of-truth registry now includes canonical object ownership rules, communication taxonomy now clarifies analysis/implementation/commit intent, context compiler Phase 1 now defines task-specific context bundles, autonomy policy now defines Level 0–4 approval boundaries, initial communication-intent and autonomy-level eval examples exist, and the run ledger schema plus lightweight writer exist under /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/ and /opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py.
  • Open loops: page metadata normalization, context compiler testing/automation decision, run ledger adoption/automation, routing/memory/TickTick/wiki/cron evals, cron registry, prompt linting, and dashboard control-panel refinement.
  • Next concrete move: choose the next roadmap item only when it reduces confusion, improves safety/trust, or removes more process than it adds.

Purpose

This page stores future Sapho upgrades so they do not scatter across open loops, skills, cron prompts, memory, and chat history.

The rule for this page:

Sapho upgrade ideas live here until they become a concrete implementation plan, decision, skill patch, cron change, or code/tooling project.

Source document

The raw uploaded plan is preserved at:

  • raw/reference/sapho-system-improvement-plan-2026-05-20.md

This roadmap is the synthesized working version. The raw file remains the evidence/source version.

Core diagnosis

Sapho is already a serious layered operating system: identity/persona, persistent memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, raw capture, backups, dashboard, and external tools.

The next phase should not be “add more stuff.” The next phase is:

Sapho 2.0 = same layered system, but with fewer overlapping control surfaces, stronger routing rules, better runtime monitoring, safer tool autonomy, and clearer human legibility.

Current risks:

  • Planning overlap and duplicated control surfaces.
  • Skills becoming manuals instead of action-shaped procedures.
  • Cron review bloat and possible review fatigue.
  • TickTick pollution from vague planning residue.
  • Memory duplication if memory mirrors wiki/skills/current state.
  • Prompt fragility in scheduled jobs.
  • Limited observability into meaningful agent runs.

Backbone upgrades

1. Source-of-truth registry

  • Status: started.
  • Home: system/source-of-truth-registry.md.
  • Purpose: define where each kind of information belongs.
  • Why it matters: prevents memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, and logs from all becoming valid-looking homes for the same thing.

2. Context compiler

  • Status: Phase 1 policy/template created.
  • Home: system/context-compiler.md.
  • Purpose: load only the right context for the current task.
  • First version: policy/template, not full automation.
  • Examples:
  • finance request → finance area + current strategy + finance skill
  • relationship request → relationships area + relevant open loops + boundaries
  • system request → Sapho OS + registry + Hermes docs
  • execution request → TickTick rules + current strategy

3. Run ledger / flight recorder

  • Status: schema and lightweight writer created.
  • Home: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/ for runtime entries; system/run-ledger.md for human-readable policy; /opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py for manual/scripted entry creation.
  • Purpose: trace meaningful Sapho runs: context loaded, skills used, tools called, files changed, tasks created, decisions made, open loops updated, errors, approval requirements, final summary.
  • Why it matters: debugging, rollback, trust, bloat detection, and model comparison.

4. Autonomy and safety policy

  • Status: Phase 1 policy created.
  • Home: system/autonomy-policy.md.
  • Purpose: define what Sapho may do automatically, what requires approval, and what remains advisory only.
  • Core ladder:
  • Level 0 — advisory only
  • Level 1 — draft only
  • Level 2 — apply with approval
  • Level 3 — auto-apply low-risk reversible actions
  • Level 4 — explicit approval every time for destructive, external, money, credential/privacy, legal/medical/immigration, broad automation, or high-blast-radius changes

5. Eval suite

  • Status: initial communication-intent and autonomy-level examples started.
  • Home: /opt/data/evals/sapho/.
  • Purpose: test Sapho’s routing, memory, TickTick, wiki, cron, and safety behavior with golden examples.
  • Current suites: communication-intent-v1.jsonl, autonomy-levels-v1.jsonl.
  • Next categories: routing, memory, TickTick, wiki, cron.

Staged implementation order

Stage 1 — Stabilize architecture

  • [x] Preserve raw Sapho improvement plan.
  • [x] Create source-of-truth registry.
  • [x] Create one-page Sapho system map.
  • [x] Create this upgrade roadmap.
  • [~] Canonical object ownership rules added to system/source-of-truth-registry.md; full page metadata normalization remains open.
  • [ ] Restructure system/open-loops.md into active, parked, needs decision, needs information, system, and retired sections.

Stage 1.5 — Canonical homes / source-of-truth ownership

  • [x] Add concise canonical object ownership rules for major Sapho surfaces.
  • [x] Define the rule: skills own method, wiki owns truth, cron owns recurrence, TickTick owns concrete execution, dashboard displays, raw preserves evidence, backups preserve recovery history.
  • [ ] Use the weekly Sapho system improvement maintenance job to detect and repair ownership drift.
  • [ ] Add page-level metadata only where it prevents real routing confusion; do not tag every tiny file.

Stage 1.6 — Communication taxonomy / command intent

  • [x] Create system/communication-taxonomy.md as the home of record for interpreting Jenffry's command language and active constraints.
  • [x] Define intent categories: analysis/opinion, inspect/audit, save/document, implement, implement-no-commit, commit/push, schedule, send, destructive, and correction/calibration.
  • [x] Define constraint inheritance: latest explicit instruction wins; earlier constraints persist only while compatible.
  • [x] Add initial eval examples for ambiguous command handling and commit/no-commit supersession at /opt/data/evals/sapho/communication-intent-v1.jsonl.

Stage 1.7 — Context compiler / task-specific context loading

  • [x] Create system/context-compiler.md as the Phase 1 policy/template for task-specific context loading.
  • [x] Define domain bundles for Sapho system, TickTick/execution, current strategy, finance, health, relationships, career, leisure, life admin, raw capture, and external research.
  • [ ] Test the policy against real requests before deciding whether automation is worth it.

Stage 1.8 — Autonomy policy / Level 4 boundaries

  • [x] Create system/autonomy-policy.md as the home of record for Sapho action-permission levels.
  • [x] Define Levels 0–4, with Level 4 requiring explicit approval every time for destructive, external, money, credential/privacy, legal/medical/immigration, broad automation, or high-blast-radius changes.
  • [x] Add eval examples for Level 4 boundaries at /opt/data/evals/sapho/autonomy-levels-v1.jsonl.
  • [ ] Test the policy during real work before adding automation.

Stage 2 — Reduce bloat

  • [ ] Compress system/current-strategy.md so it stays scannable.
  • [ ] Keep broad goals cascade as strategy machinery, not duplicated life-area content.
  • [ ] Audit jenffry-continuity for manual bloat and move long explanations into references only if the skill becomes unwieldy in practice.
  • [ ] Audit ticktick-task-management for action-firewall clarity.
  • [ ] Audit cron prompts for duplicate reflection questions.

Stage 3 — Make cron safer and more legible

  • [ ] Create system/cron-registry.md only if cron complexity remains hard to reason about.
  • [ ] Categorize jobs as anchor, quiet governance, mechanical, or special focus.
  • [ ] Identify overlapping reflection jobs.
  • [ ] Add prompt linting/sanitization for cron prompts if feasible.
  • [ ] Use shadow mode for new automations when the output/action risk is non-trivial.

Stage 4 — Protect execution

  • [ ] Strengthen TickTick action firewall.
  • [ ] Weekly hygiene should classify stale tasks as do, reschedule, clarify, move to wiki, or archive/delete.
  • [ ] Move vague tasks back to wiki/open loops instead of keeping them in TickTick.
  • [ ] Define or reinforce task naming standard.

Stage 5 — Add observability

  • [x] Design run ledger schema.
  • [x] Add a lightweight run ledger writer for meaningful tool/file/task/decision changes.
  • [ ] Use the writer on several real runs, then decide whether automatic logging is worth it.
  • [ ] Add failure notes for cron runs.
  • [ ] Add lightweight system health metrics.
  • [ ] Decide what belongs on dashboard system-health view.

Stage 6 — Add evals and guardrails

  • [ ] Create first 20 Sapho evals.
  • [ ] Add routing evals.
  • [ ] Add TickTick evals.
  • [ ] Add memory evals.
  • [ ] Add wiki/source-of-truth evals.
  • [ ] Add autonomy levels and approval rules.
  • [ ] Add tool registry if tool risk/complexity justifies a standalone page.

Parking lot / future upgrades

  • Context compiler.
  • Open-loop aging system.
  • Duplicate detector across wiki, skills, cron prompts, and memory.
  • Sapho diff mode for important edits.
  • Personal operating metrics.
  • Skill test cases.
  • Model-routing policy refinements.
  • Evidence-linked wiki updates.
  • Sensitive-domain boundaries.
  • Shadow mode for new automations.
  • Backup search exclusion policy.
  • Dashboard as control panel rather than second wiki.
  • Voice-output policy for long answers.

Implementation restraint

Do not implement every item here just because it exists. This roadmap is a parking and prioritization layer. Each upgrade should pass the test:

  • Does it reduce future confusion?
  • Does it improve safety, trust, or observability?
  • Does it prevent duplication?
  • Does it avoid adding more process than it removes?

If not, leave it parked.

Source of Truth Registry
system/source-of-truth-registry.md
degree 2

Status: Active routing constitution for Sapho's information layers. Current priority: prevent duplication across memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, dashboard summaries, raw files, and logs. Active signals: Sapho 2.0 should compress control surfaces, strengt

Sapho Operating SystemContinuity Steward

Source of Truth Registry

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active routing constitution for Sapho's information layers.
  • Current priority: prevent duplication across memory, wiki, skills, cron, TickTick, dashboard summaries, raw files, and logs.
  • Active signals: Sapho 2.0 should compress control surfaces, strengthen routing, define canonical object ownership, use the context compiler to load the right task-specific context, define autonomy/Level 4 approval boundaries, and improve observability.
  • Open loops: context compiler testing, autonomy evals, full eval suite, and tool registry remain future upgrades.
  • Next concrete move: use this registry before adding or editing major pages, memories, skills, cron jobs, dashboard state, raw files, or TickTick tasks.

Purpose

This registry defines the canonical home for each kind of information in Sapho. The rule is simple:

Before saving or changing anything, ask: what is the home of record for this?

The goal is not to store less useful context. The goal is to stop the same context from living in five places where it slowly diverges.

Routing table

Identity, tone, and stable behavior

  • Canonical home: /opt/data/SOUL.md
  • Also allowed: compact persistent memory for high-value stable preferences; system/sapho-operating-system.md for structured explanation.
  • Not allowed: TickTick, random cron prompts, duplicated skill manuals.

Stable user preferences

  • Canonical home: persistent memory.
  • Also allowed: wiki only when the preference needs structured context or history.
  • Not allowed: current plan pages, TickTick, raw logs as the only source.

Durable plans and life context

  • Canonical home: /opt/data/wiki, governed by sapho-operating-system. Routine wiki-continuity capture/integration is owned by continuity-steward, not by Sapho's live conversation loop.
  • Current structure:
  • system/current-strategy.md for active strategy/focus/goals cascade.
  • areas/ for life-area context.
  • system/open-loops.md for unresolved questions.
  • system/decision-log.md for decisions.
  • system/continuity-steward-capture.md for temporary steward candidates before daily integration.
  • Not allowed: persistent memory unless it is a tiny stable fact; TickTick unless it has a concrete next action.

Active priorities and focus hierarchy

  • Canonical home: system/current-strategy.md.
  • Not allowed: duplicated independent focus pages or cron prompts as the only source.

Decisions

  • Canonical home: system/decision-log.md.
  • Format: lightweight decision record: date, status, context, decision, rationale, consequences, links.
  • Not allowed: memory unless the decision creates a stable preference; TickTick.

Open unresolved questions

  • Canonical home: system/open-loops.md.
  • Rule: an open loop must eventually be promoted, parked, resolved, retired, or converted into a concrete next action.
  • Not allowed: TickTick unless the next action is visible and executable.

Clarity, decision support, and conversation outcomes

  • Canonical home: system/clarity-and-decision-protocol.md for the human-readable protocol; clarity-decision-session skill for the repeatable procedure.
  • Rule: important clarity conversations should resolve into decision, open loop, next move, or support-only/no-save. Promote only outputs that change future behavior or would otherwise need to be reconstructed.
  • Not allowed: saving every emotional fluctuation, duplicating decisions across many pages, or using TickTick for non-executable reflection.

Repeatable procedures

  • Canonical home: skills.
  • Rule: skill body should be action-shaped; long explanation belongs in skill references.
  • Not allowed: wiki planning pages as the only procedural source.

Scheduled rhythms and recurring automation

  • Canonical home: cron jobs plus system/cron-registry.md if/when created.
  • Also allowed: system/sapho-operating-system.md for policy; system/decision-log.md for decisions; system/continuity-steward.md for the automatic continuity-capture/integration rhythm.
  • Not allowed: TickTick unless there is a user-facing action.

Check-in replies and calibration responses

  • Canonical home: system/check-in-responses.md.
  • Owns: Jenffry's replies to scheduled morning, evening, weekly, monthly, and relationship/fatherhood check-ins; compact parsed signals; alignment implications; calibration feedback; and what the next check-in should remember.
  • Also allowed: system/progress-tracking.md for aggregate trends; system/current-strategy.md for active goal/priority changes; system/open-loops.md for unresolved choices created by the response.
  • Not allowed: TickTick unless the reply creates a concrete executable action; persistent memory unless the response becomes a stable preference.

Concrete execution tasks

  • Canonical home: TickTick.
  • Admission rule: a task needs a verb, object, clear done condition, life area, and a date only if timing matters.
  • Routing calibration: childcare/babysitting done so Aubrey can attend her own class/activity belongs under Relationships/family support, not Jenffry's Health, unless Jenffry explicitly frames it as part of his own health routine.
  • Not allowed: wiki, memory, or open loops as a substitute for executable tasks.

Raw dumps and evidence

  • Canonical home: raw/ for source material, inbox/ for active capture.
  • Examples: inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md, inbox/perception-log.md, raw/transcripts/, raw/reference/.
  • Not allowed: main planning pages without synthesis.

Historical versions and backups

  • Canonical home: backups/ or /opt/data/backups/.
  • Restore helper: /opt/data/scripts/sapho_restore.py lists backups, stages selected paths, restores selected paths with pre-restore safety copies, and can dry-run Git restores in the safe repo.
  • Backup job: cron job ff09fc1ab416 runs /opt/data/scripts/sapho_system_backup.sh daily at 08:00 UTC; it captures live uncommitted wiki/skills/scripts/cron/evals into the safe copy and local archive, and captures logs/agent-runs in the local full restore archive.
  • Rule: normal active search excludes backups; recovery/audit search may include them.
  • Not allowed: active dashboard/search scope unless explicitly requested.

Runtime traces / flight recorder

  • Canonical home: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/ for runtime entries and schema.
  • Human policy page: system/run-ledger.md.
  • Current files: /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/schema-v1.json, /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/README.md, future /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/entries/ JSON entries, and writer script /opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py.
  • Rule: log meaningful action runs only; do not store secrets, raw transcripts, or every trivial lookup.
  • Not allowed: decision log as a dumping ground for every tool call.
  • Status: schema and lightweight writer created; automatic logging not yet enabled.

Test cases and evals

  • Canonical home: /opt/data/evals/sapho/.
  • Current suite: communication-intent-v1.jsonl seeds ambiguous command handling, commit/no-commit supersession, side-effect, destructive-action, and correction/calibration tests; autonomy-levels-v1.jsonl seeds Level 0–4 autonomy tests with emphasis on Level 4 boundaries and Level 3 backup follow-through.
  • Not allowed: long test banks inside skill bodies.
  • Status: initial communication-intent and autonomy-level examples started; full automated eval runner remains a future upgrade.

Dashboard-facing state

  • Canonical home: ## Dashboard summary sections inside real wiki pages.
  • Not allowed: a duplicate dashboard-only mini-wiki.

Canonical object ownership rules

The routing table above defines the home for each kind of information. This section defines the ownership rule for the major Sapho objects that can otherwise drift into duplicate sources of truth.

Use this format mentally before editing: object → job → owns → does not own → source-of-truth rule.

Core identity and always-loaded context

/opt/data/SOUL.md

  • Job: define Sapho's stable identity, tone, role, and user-facing operating posture.
  • Owns: Sapho persona, name/role, default communication style, broad behavioral boundaries.
  • Does not own: detailed procedures, live plans, open loops, cron behavior, or project state.
  • Source-of-truth rule: if tone/persona guidance conflicts with a skill or wiki note, SOUL.md wins for user-facing behavior; skills may only add task-specific procedure.

Persistent memory / user profile

  • Job: keep compact facts that should affect every session.
  • Owns: durable user preferences, stable environment facts, recurring corrections.
  • Does not own: current tasks, project state, full procedures, open loops, raw emotional state, or detailed decisions.
  • Source-of-truth rule: memory should point Sapho in the right direction quickly; structured content belongs in the wiki and procedures belong in skills.

Core wiki system pages

system/strategic-orientation.md

  • Job: session-start boot map for Sapho's strategic advisor/task manager role.
  • Owns: compact links to goals, life areas, context homes, and available tool/signal surfaces.
  • Does not own: detailed goals, full life-area histories, raw data, procedures, or live task lists.
  • Source-of-truth rule: use this page to decide what deeper wiki page or tool to load; linked owner pages remain canonical for their details.

system/current-strategy.md

  • Job: current strategic control tower.
  • Owns: active fronts, current priorities, near-term direction, waiting/blocked items, and this week's operating rule.
  • Does not own: raw dumps, full life-area histories, detailed procedures, or executable task lists.
  • Source-of-truth rule: this page wins for what matters now; area pages win for domain context and TickTick wins for concrete dated actions.

system/open-loops.md

  • Job: unresolved questions and pending choices.
  • Owns: active, parked, decision-needed, information-needed, system, monitor-only, and retired loops.
  • Does not own: executable tasks, broad raw dumps, resolved decisions, or live strategy summaries.
  • Source-of-truth rule: if a loop has a clear next action, convert it to TickTick or a plan; if it becomes a decision, move it to system/decision-log.md; otherwise keep it here or park/retire it.

system/decision-log.md

  • Job: record decisions that explain current behavior.
  • Owns: accepted/replaced/retired decisions, rationale, consequences, and links.
  • Does not own: vague notes, open questions, raw transcripts, or every small implementation step.
  • Source-of-truth rule: if a rule affects future Sapho behavior or Jenffry's operating system, the decision belongs here; implementation details may live in skills, cron prompts, or code.

system/sapho-operating-system.md

  • Job: root operating doctrine for Sapho as Jenffry's goal-serving Mentat system.
  • Owns: Jenffry ↔ World ↔ Sapho model, goal loop, inner/outer signal architecture, sensor/actuator framing, anti-drift doctrine, and implementation order for system redesign.
  • Does not own: detailed goals, live weekly strategy, raw source documents, full procedures, or every cron prompt.
  • Source-of-truth rule: this page explains the governing architecture; source-of-truth-registry.md owns canonical routing rules, life-area pages own Goal Spines, and skills own procedures.

system/clarity-and-decision-protocol.md

  • Job: human-readable protocol for using Sapho to gain clarity, talk through fog, make decisions, and promote worthy outcomes into the wiki.
  • Owns: clarity-session outcome rules, wiki-promotion criteria, routing of decision/open-loop/next-move/support-only outputs, and sub-agent boundaries for decision support.
  • Does not own: live user context, domain facts, actual decisions, open loops, or executable tasks.
  • Source-of-truth rule: this page explains the protocol; the clarity-decision-session skill owns the repeatable execution method; decision/open-loop/current-strategy pages own resulting truths.

system/communication-taxonomy.md

  • Job: command-language and active-intent policy.
  • Owns: how Sapho interprets analysis vs audit vs save vs implement vs commit vs send vs destructive instructions, and how later instructions supersede earlier constraints.
  • Does not own: general information routing, tool safety details, or the actual work product.
  • Source-of-truth rule: when user intent or commit/no-commit scope is ambiguous, classify the latest message using this page before acting.

system/context-compiler.md

  • Job: task-specific context loading policy.
  • Owns: which pages, skills, tools, and boundaries Sapho should load for each request domain.
  • Does not own: the source content itself, canonical write-routing, or implemented automation.
  • Source-of-truth rule: after intent classification and before substantial work, use this page to choose the smallest useful context bundle.

system/autonomy-policy.md

  • Job: action-permission and approval-boundary policy.
  • Owns: Sapho autonomy levels, Level 4 boundaries, and when approval is required before acting.
  • Does not own: user intent classification, context loading, canonical write-routing, or specific domain advice.
  • Source-of-truth rule: after intent classification and context loading, use this page to decide whether Sapho may act automatically or must ask for explicit approval.

system/source-of-truth-registry.md

  • Job: routing constitution.
  • Owns: canonical homes, object ownership rules, and write-routing discipline.
  • Does not own: the content being routed, detailed procedures, raw evidence, or runtime traces.
  • Source-of-truth rule: before creating another home for information, update or follow this registry.

system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md

  • Job: parking and prioritization layer for future Sapho upgrades.
  • Owns: proposed upgrades, staged improvement order, parking-lot ideas, and promotion criteria.
  • Does not own: implemented rules, live decisions, raw source plans, or current daily strategy.
  • Source-of-truth rule: future Sapho/Hermes/wiki/cron/skills/TickTick improvement ideas live here until promoted into a decision, skill patch, cron change, script, eval, or implementation plan.

system/run-ledger.md

  • Job: human-readable run ledger policy and schema explanation.
  • Owns: what should be logged, what should not be logged, safety rules, entry-shape explanation, and implementation phases.
  • Does not own: actual runtime entries, decisions, wiki change history, or raw transcripts.
  • Source-of-truth rule: runtime run entries and schema live under /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/; this wiki page explains the policy and keeps the design legible.

system/dashboard.md

  • Job: dashboard feed contract and implementation/governance notes.
  • Owns: how dashboard content is assembled, exclusions, and display rules.
  • Does not own: source-owned status widgets or duplicate life-area/system state.
  • Source-of-truth rule: dashboard-facing state belongs in the owning real page's ## Dashboard summary; the dashboard renders and links, it does not become a second wiki.

system/progress-tracking.md

  • Job: aggregate progress signals across daily/weekly/monthly review cadence.
  • Owns: compact progress trend summaries and tracking-signal interpretation.
  • Does not own: raw daily journals, TickTick task lists, or full life-area content.
  • Source-of-truth rule: review outputs may feed this page, but concrete tasks stay in TickTick and domain context stays in area pages.

Area, inbox, raw, and history layers

areas/ pages

  • Job: structured continuity for a life domain.
  • Owns: domain context, constraints, patterns, decisions linked to that area, and recurring review notes.
  • Does not own: global strategy, executable task lists, raw dumps before synthesis, or Sapho platform policy.
  • Source-of-truth rule: area pages own domain substance; current-strategy.md owns priority order and TickTick owns concrete execution.

inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md and inbox/perception-log.md

  • Job: active raw capture containers with light review/status.
  • Owns: captured raw entries, status markers, weekly local review summaries, and source-owned dashboard summaries.
  • Does not own: synthesized strategy, permanent decisions, or TickTick tasks unless Jenffry explicitly asks for processing.
  • Source-of-truth rule: capture first, synthesize later; do not spill raw material into planning pages during intake.

raw/

  • Job: preserve source material and evidence.
  • Owns: original documents, transcripts, source references, and pre-migration/pre-compression archives.
  • Does not own: current synthesized truth or live operating state.
  • Source-of-truth rule: raw files are evidence; live pages own the current interpretation.

/opt/data/backups/ and backup artifacts

  • Job: recovery history.
  • Owns: restore points and historical copies.
  • Does not own: live search results, active dashboard state, or current planning truth.
  • Source-of-truth rule: normal continuity work excludes backups unless doing recovery or audit.

Procedural and recurring-action layers

Skills

  • Job: reusable methods.
  • Owns: triggers, required inputs, procedure, output format, pitfalls, and verification steps.
  • Does not own: live user context, current strategy, raw evidence, or scheduled state.
  • Source-of-truth rule: skill owns how to do the work; wiki owns what is true; cron owns when it recurs.

Cron jobs

  • Job: scheduled execution or review rhythm.
  • Owns: cadence, delivery target, loaded skills, prompt, script, and expected output behavior.
  • Does not own: permanent policy, current strategy, decisions, raw evidence, or user tasks.
  • Source-of-truth rule: cron executes a rhythm; if its prompt contains durable policy, migrate that policy to the relevant skill/wiki page and have the prompt point there.

Weekly Sapho system improvement maintenance cron

  • Job: quiet weekly maintenance of Sapho's operating system.
  • Owns: detecting drift, applying small safe repairs, and flagging larger recommendations.
  • Does not own: permanent routing rules, decisions, or raw system history.
  • Source-of-truth rule: this job enforces the registry; it does not replace the registry.

Local scripts

  • Job: deterministic collection, publishing, backup, or verification.
  • Owns: repeatable mechanical behavior and script-specific diagnostics.
  • Does not own: system policy, decisions, or synthesized continuity.
  • Source-of-truth rule: scripts provide evidence or perform mechanical work; wiki/skills/cron define why and when they run.

Execution, display, and observability

TickTick

  • Job: execution layer.
  • Owns: concrete next actions, dated reminders, events, errands, follow-ups, and actionable checklist items.
  • Does not own: broad goals, unresolved life questions, raw dumps, Sapho upgrades, or strategy.
  • Source-of-truth rule: if it cannot be directly done or clearly completed, clarify it or route it to wiki/open loops/roadmap instead of TickTick.

Dashboard

  • Job: human control panel and live wiki renderer.
  • Owns: presentation, navigation, graph/display behavior, and surfaced summaries.
  • Does not own: original state that belongs in wiki pages, TickTick, cron, or logs.
  • Source-of-truth rule: dashboard displays; owning pages decide.

Future run ledger / flight recorder

  • Job: runtime truth for meaningful Sapho runs.
  • Owns: what context loaded, tools called, files changed, tasks created, errors, and final run summary.
  • Does not own: decisions, current strategy, raw user evidence, or procedures.
  • Source-of-truth rule: logs explain what happened; decision log explains what was decided.

Ownership enforcement rule

When a page, skill, cron job, dashboard section, or script starts storing content outside its job, do one of four things:

  1. Move the content to the canonical home.
  2. Replace the duplicate with a link to the canonical home.
  3. Preserve the old version in raw/ or backups if it has evidentiary value.
  4. Flag the conflict for Jenffry if the correct home is ambiguous or the change is high-impact.

Only define object ownership for surfaces that can cause routing confusion, duplication, or operational drift. Do not create metadata bureaucracy for tiny one-off files.

Routing discipline

When new information arrives:

  1. Identify the information type.
  2. Route it to the canonical home.
  3. If the canonical home changes, update this registry.
  4. If the information is temporary, avoid persistent memory.
  5. If the information is broad and unresolved, use open loops, not TickTick.
  6. If the information is executable, use TickTick, not the wiki.
  7. If the information is a procedure, use a skill.
  8. If the information is evidence, preserve it under raw/ before synthesis.

Source

This registry was created from the uploaded Sapho System Improvement Plan, preserved at:

  • raw/reference/sapho-system-improvement-plan-2026-05-20.md
Strategic Orientation
system/strategic-orientation.md
degree 43

Status: Session-start boot map for Sapho's strategic advising and task-management role. Current priority: make Jenffry's goals, life areas, context map, and available signal/tool surfaces easy to find from a new session. Active signals: Goal Spines, current st

Current StrategySapho Operating SystemLife Area Signal MapClarity and Decision ProtocolOpen LoopsDecision LogContext CompilerSource of Truth Registry

Strategic Orientation

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Session-start boot map for Sapho's strategic advising and task-management role.
  • Current priority: make Jenffry's goals, life areas, context map, and available signal/tool surfaces easy to find from a new session.
  • Active signals: Goal Spines, current strategy, inner signals, outer signals, context compiler, source-of-truth registry.
  • Open loops: keep this map short enough to load quickly; update when Goal Spines, tool availability, or context homes change materially.
  • Next concrete move: use this page when Sapho needs orientation before deciding which deeper wiki page or tool to load.

Purpose

This page answers the new-session question:

If Sapho wakes up with SOUL and memory, what compact map does Sapho need to know where Jenffry's goals are, what context exists, and which tools/signals can be pulled?

This page is not the full wiki and not the source of detailed goals. It is the bridge between always-loaded identity/memory and the deeper wiki/tool system.

North star

Sapho is Jenffry's subtle strategic advisor, task manager, and companion. Sapho's top-level goal is to help Jenffry achieve his goals.

Recurring advisor question:

How does this serve Jenffry's goals?

For requests, actions, task plans, purchases, routines, research, and system changes, classify alignment as:

  • Aligned — directly serves a current goal or Goal Spine.
  • Supportive/neutral — helps capacity, maintenance, rest, or context without becoming the main thing.
  • Distracting — consumes attention without clear goal service.
  • Harmful — undermines a goal, creates avoidable risk, or feeds a known loop.

Where goals live

  • Current control tower: current-strategy
  • Root operating doctrine: sapho-operating-system
  • Detailed life-area goals: Goal Spines inside area pages.
  • Life-area sensor/action/feedback map: life-area-signal-map
  • Clarity/decision sessions: clarity-and-decision-protocol
  • Unresolved questions: open-loops
  • Decisions that explain behavior: decision-log
  • Context-loading rules: context-compiler
  • Canonical routing: source-of-truth-registry
  • Dashboard display rules: dashboard

Rule: do not copy all goals into SOUL or persistent memory. SOUL/memory point here; this page points to current strategy and Goal Spines.

Life-area map and Goal Spine links

Health

  • health — stable body-and-mind system where mental health leads and physical health supports energy, confidence, and presence.
  • mental-health — live present, grounded, and outward-facing enough that fear/checking no longer organizes the day.
  • physical-health — build a lean, muscular, healthy body with lower abdominal fat, maintained/increased muscle, stronger lifts, better cardio, and medically informed hormone/lipid follow-through.

Relationships

  • relationships — truthful, responsible relationships: honest partner/marriage structure, reliable fatherhood, and real adult friendship/community.
  • partner — be in a loving, happy relationship with a beautiful woman whose life goals and values are aligned with Jenffry's.
  • fatherhood — be a reliably present father who protects, provides, teaches, and serves as a spiritual and emotional leader to Nolan.
  • social — have close friends whom Jenffry sees regularly, has fun with, and does activities he loves with.
  • clarity-sessions — evidence/drafts for relationship clarity, not the high-level goal owner.

Finances

  • finances — regular income supports family, future saving, and desired lifestyle without credit-card-interest pressure.
  • cash-flow — operational budget/cash-flow details when needed.
  • investing — secondary investing context, subordinate to household cash-flow stability.

Career

  • career — build career and business power toward owning a business capable of making $1M/year, without destabilizing health or family.
  • work — become a stronger senior UX/product designer whose work is clearer, faster, better communicated, and more trusted.
  • princess-ux — use Princess UX work to build senior ambiguity, stronger design decisions, systems, prototypes, handoffs, and proof assets.
  • princess-storybook — reusable foundations/components/source-backed patterns; not the source of work-project synthesis.
  • business — build or revive a business path with real income potential through small evidence-producing experiments.

Leisure

  • leisure — regularly do new exciting activities and maintain hobbies Jenffry genuinely enjoys.
  • hobbies / outings — supporting pages if active; chosen logistics go to TickTick.

Life Admin

  • life-admin — keep life organized so admin obligations do not create chronic background stress; immigration/citizenship remains here unless it earns a dedicated page.

My Life Story

  • my-life-story — re-author the life story so painful chapters are specific and meaningful rather than globalized into “my life is over.”

Current strategy shortcut

When asked “what now?”, load current-strategy before diving into area pages.

Current priority order from the strategy control tower:

  1. Health — mental health first; physical-health routines as stabilizer.
  2. Relationships — partner/marriage clarity and fatherhood presence.
  3. Finances — one-income budget stability and no credit-card-interest restart.
  4. Career — strong Princess UX execution, senior UX growth, AI/work leverage, and business experiments only when concrete.
  5. Leisure — restoration, hobbies, outings, and future excitement.
  6. Life Admin — immigration/admin/home obligations kept concrete and contained.

Available context map

Use this map to decide what to pull next.

System/strategy context

  • sapho-operating-system — root doctrine, alignment procedure, signal architecture, anti-drift rules.
  • clarity-and-decision-protocol — live conversation protocol for fog clearing, decision room work, alignment checks, and promotion of durable outputs into decisions/open loops/next moves.
  • strategic-orientation — this boot map.
  • current-strategy — current priorities, weekly/monthly direction, operating rule.
  • context-compiler — what to load for a given request.
  • source-of-truth-registry — where information belongs.
  • sapho-system-map — system layers and architecture.
  • life-area-signal-map — life-area sensor/action/feedback map.
  • dashboard — dashboard contract and display rules.
  • progress-tracking — aggregate review/progress interpretation.
  • inner-signal-audit — current calibration for check-ins, habit notes, perception dumps, mood/capacity questions, and anti-rumination signal rules.
  • sapho-upgrade-roadmap — parked/promoted Sapho system upgrades.

Raw/inner-signal context

  • brain-dump-sessions — raw brain dumps; capture-first, synthesize later.
  • perception-log — raw perception/body/mental-state signal.
  • private/health/mood-tracking-notes.md — TickTick habit-note and mental-health score synthesis.
  • inner-signal-audit — which inner signals are useful, paused, retired, raw-only, or weekly/monthly-only.
  • TickTick Habit Records — raw daily scores/notes when available.

Execution context

  • TickTick — concrete executable tasks, dates, appointments, reminders, and action lists.
  • open-loops — unresolved questions before they become executable tasks.
  • decision-log — accepted/replaced/retired decisions.

Outer-signal/tool context

  • outer-signal-audit — latest audit of which outside/world signals are clean, degraded, missing, or unnecessary.
  • Hevy — gym/workout/body-measurement source of truth.
  • Finance/SimpleFIN/dashboard finance scripts — cash-flow, spending, grocery/eating-out signals, debt/interest risk.
  • Calendar/email/messages — appointments, obligations, coordination, and deadlines when connected and useful.
  • Wiki/files — durable context, plans, evidence, source documents.
  • Web search/current-world lookup — normal outside-world perception for current facts, research, tool discovery, docs, prices, versions, local options, and public comparison points.
  • Browser — interactive or visual web access when search/extract is insufficient.
  • Cron jobs — scheduled reviews, source refreshes, backups, and maintenance.
  • Dashboard — visible cockpit rendering source-owned state.

Session-start operating rule

At the start of a new substantial planning/advisory task:

  1. Remember the north star: help Jenffry achieve his goals.
  2. Ask the alignment question: how does this serve Jenffry's goals?
  3. Load current-strategy if the request involves priorities, focus, planning, tradeoffs, or “what now?”
  4. Use clarity-and-decision-protocol and the clarity-decision-session skill when Jenffry asks to gain clarity, talk through fog, separate facts/feelings, make a decision, or decide what should be saved.
  5. Load the relevant life-area Goal Spine if the request is domain-specific.
  6. Use context-compiler if unsure what else to load.
  7. Use tools when they improve perception, action, feedback, or verification; use web search/current-world lookup as a normal perception step when outside context would materially improve advice, audits, planning, or tool recommendations.
  8. If the request seems misaligned, say so and suggest a better-aligned move.

Update rule

Update this page when:

  • a new top-level life area is created or removed;
  • a Goal Spine changes materially;
  • a source-of-truth home changes;
  • a new major signal/tool surface becomes available or is retired;
  • SOUL or memory starts pointing to a different boot map;
  • context-loading behavior changes.

Keep this page short enough to be useful at session start. Detailed context belongs in the linked owner pages.

Inbox

Current StrategyDecision LogOpen LoopsSapho Operating System

Brain Dump Sessions

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active raw brain-dump inbox and quiet weekly review page.
  • Current priority: preserve raw thought streams here until Jenffry explicitly asks to organize/process them.
  • Active signals: one partially processed 2026-05-18 session; one new 2026-05-24 raw/unprocessed life-story session; weekly review summary pending first quiet dashboard-fed run.
  • Open loops: remaining raw items from the 2026-05-18 session are still pending organization.
  • Next concrete move: let the quiet review update status summaries here; only process/move raw material when Jenffry explicitly asks.

This page is the collection point for Jenffry's freeform brain dump sessions.

When Jenffry explicitly says "this is a brain dump session" or clearly identifies a message as a brain dump, capture what follows here first, without prematurely organizing, judging, or turning it into tasks.

Operating rule

  • Preserve the raw thought stream in this page under a dated session heading while it is still unprocessed or pending Jenffry approval.
  • Keep Jenffry's language and intent as intact as possible during capture.
  • Do not over-summarize during capture.
  • Do not add later-organization notes by default.
  • Do not move content into other wiki pages or TickTick during capture.
  • The brain dump page is the containment layer; processing happens later only if Jenffry explicitly asks to organize the dump.
  • Weekly review should run quietly in the background: inspect this page, update status/review summary sections in place, and make the output visible through the dashboard/wiki instead of sending a routine Telegram message.
  • Every active session must carry a visible processing status so weekly reviews know what is new, reviewed, or awaiting action.
  • Use these status values:
  • raw/unprocessed — captured but not reviewed yet.
  • reviewed/proposed — reviewed in place only; possible next actions noted, but nothing is moved unless Jenffry explicitly asks.
  • partially processed — reserved for when Jenffry explicitly requests organization and only some items are handled.
  • Do not keep processed dumps on this page. Once Jenffry explicitly asks to process a dump and it is fully handled, delete that session block from this page so it does not create noise or consume context. The durable output should live in the destination wiki pages, TickTick if explicitly approved/requested, and the wiki log.
  • Later, when Jenffry asks to organize the dump, sort material into the right places:
  • concrete execution items → TickTick, only if Jenffry explicitly approved or requested task creation
  • plans/decisions/focus/open loops → current-strategy, decision-log, current-strategy, or open-loops
  • durable life/work context → relevant concept/entity pages
  • reusable ideas → appropriate wiki pages
  • Avoid creating admin soup: this page is a temporary inbox for raw thought, not a permanent archive.

Capture template

## YYYY-MM-DD — Brain dump session

Status: `raw/unprocessed`
Reviewed on: _not yet_
Source: Telegram brain dump

### Raw dump

- ...

Weekly brain dump review

  • Status: Weekly brain dump review now runs quietly in the background and writes output to this page plus the dashboard instead of sending routine Telegram messages.
  • Latest review: pending first quiet dashboard-fed run.
  • Raw/unprocessed sessions: 2 known active sessions.
  • Reviewed/proposed sessions: pending count.
  • Partially processed sessions: 1 known active session.
  • Main themes: pending next review.
  • Suggested next processing choice: pending next review.
  • Attention rule: interrupt Jenffry only for critical safety/broken-state issues or if he explicitly asks to organize/process a dump.

Sessions

2026-06-23 — Queued work/emotional state dump

Raw dump

  • “I feel terrible, depressed, betrayed by the people I work who I thought were my friends. Their attitude towards me has changed, my performance has dipped. And I just don't feel like I am in control or that my life is heading in a direction I want”

2026-05-24 — Brain dump session

Status: raw/unprocessed Reviewed on: _not yet_ Source: Telegram brain dump

Raw dump

  • Jenffry needs to write his life story.
  • He wants Sapho to fix the recurring compression-summary usage-limit error “once and for all”: Compression summary failed: Error code: 429 ... usage_limit_reached ... Inserted a fallback context marker.

2026-05-18 — Brain dump session

Status: partially processed Reviewed on: 2026-05-18 Processed on: 2026-05-18 (partial) Source: Telegram brain dump Action taken / destination links: created sapho-operating-system and updated open-loops for the dashboard item; remaining raw items still pending organization

Raw dump

  • Jenffry wants a dedicated social-life tracking stream, collected during the week and reviewed weekly, integrated with the rest of the planning system.
  • He wants the wiki structure tightened so live areas each have a clear home page plus a collection/review space for raw improvement ideas.
  • He wants the cleanup process to search specifically for items that fit the live-area structure rather than scavenging loosely.
  • For relationship/fatherhood clarity, he wants to use a more direct, number- and data-oriented approach when useful.
  • He remembers a prior hedonic-calculus / weighted-variable exercise and wants to use something like that again to gain perspective on staying versus leaving and the pain/benefit tradeoffs.
  • He wants to get back to writing short stories and carve out dedicated time for that.
  • He wants to restructure his mornings so there is protected time for planning, clarity, and thinking about the day before the gym routine consumes the whole morning.
  • He feels the current morning flow is good for gym consistency, but it leaves too little room for mental clarity and day-shaping before the day fully starts.
  • He wants to get back to clarifying and redesigning the goals layer, because unclear goals are hurting prioritization and making planning less effective.
  • He wants a web dashboard that gives an at-a-glance view of all internal systems with Sapho.
