Mental HealthPhysical HealthDashboardCurrent Strategy

Perception Log

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active perception capture page with several raw entries; capture remains raw-only unless Jenffry explicitly asks for pattern review.
  • Current priority: keep capture broad and low-pressure; use progress signals only as light context until actual perception entries accumulate.
  • Active signals: recent perception dumps name a two-part mental-health strategy: reduce constant checking while also deliberately proving safety/presence through exposure, life-story reframing, future-excitement work, and body-energy support.
  • Open loops: preserve the tension between “do not check” and “sit with/process feeling” without turning it into rumination; shape the life-story and future-excitement work when Jenffry explicitly asks.
  • Next concrete move: keep daily tracking simple; when ready, write the life story as a global reframe rather than another mood autopsy.

This page is a lightweight capture point for Jenffry's momentary inner-state dumps: emotions, body sensations, energy, stress, mood, pain/tension, clarity/fog, urges, random perceptions, and other felt signals.

The scope is intentionally broad. It is not only an emotions tracker. It is for “how I am experiencing myself right now,” including body and mind.

Like a brain dump, this page is a containment layer first: capture here now, process later, and avoid spilling the raw entry into other pages during capture.

Example triggers Jenffry might use:

  • "perception dump"
  • "feelings dump"
  • "body feeling dump"
  • "log how I feel"
  • "feeling check"
  • "body check"
  • "random perception"
  • any clear request to capture a current inner/body state

Sapho should append the entry here without over-analyzing in the moment.

This is not therapy, diagnosis, or medical assessment. It is a pattern-recognition layer: collect enough signal to notice repeated triggers, body-state correlations, helpful responses, danger states, and practical next moves.

Operating rule

  • Capture Jenffry's wording as directly as practical.
  • Treat “feeling” broadly: emotional, bodily, cognitive, energetic, sensory, motivational, or hard-to-classify.
  • Add light metadata only when available: date/time, context, body state, trigger, intensity, and immediate need.
  • Do not force Jenffry to categorize the perception if the whole point is that it is vague or random.
  • Do not turn every perception into a TickTick task.
  • Do not over-refine entries in a way that feeds rumination or observer-mode self-monitoring.
  • Weekly review should run quietly in the background: analyze this page, find patterns, update the pattern sections here, and make the output visible through the dashboard/wiki instead of sending a routine Telegram message.
  • Weekly review should look for patterns, solutions, and insights; it should not pathologize or diagnose.
  • After weekly review, keep this page lean: preserve patterns/insights here, and move reviewed raw entries into dated raw archive files only if they are becoming noisy.
  • Dashboard-facing output should stay compact: latest pattern, useful experiment, and whether anything needs Jenffry's attention.

Quick capture template

### YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM — Perception entry

- Raw words / perception:
- Type, if obvious: body, emotion, energy, thought, urge, sensory, mixed, unclear
- Context / trigger:
- Body state: sleep, food, caffeine, gym, illness, tension, pain, stomach, breathing, if known
- Intensity: 1–5 if naturally available
- Immediate need: vent, grounding, decision help, reassurance, action, rest, observe only, unclear

Active perception entries

2026-06-10 18:21 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “So this is a perception stump, you know, last night my wife came back with Nolan and it was nice. I was happy that she was back home. She left for two weeks to Indiana. It was over two weeks, was 20 days. And then when she was back, I was happy to see her and we had a nice night. We talked, you know, I spent some time with my son and we had sex and it was all good. But still now this morning, well not this morning, just this afternoon as I’m driving back home, my mind still goes to, you know, the satisfaction. Even though nothing bad has happened, like I haven’t had the time to actually feel like I don’t want to be in this marriage, I’m still having those thoughts. And it’s one of two things: either I am already habituated to thinking this way about the marriage, or I just intuitively know because I’ve been through this process of trying to commit to the marriage and then not wanting to be in it. I’m just going back and forth, that I just know that I’m going to end up on that side anyways. So yeah, it’s just something I notice. And that’s it. Does it mean that I want to get a divorce and should get a divorce? I think probably that’s where I’m leaning towards. But I’m not sure. Maybe it is because I habitually think about the marriage that way. Since I have to wait until Nolan’s birthday to do anything about the marriage anyways, I think I should probably do another small try, or perhaps not a small try per se, but sort of trying not to bias myself and observe for a bit. So yeah, that’s what I think I’m going to do.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — relationship/marriage thoughts, uncertainty, habituated thinking vs intuitive knowing, observe-period intention.
  • Context / trigger: sent as a perception dump while driving home the afternoon after Aubrey and Nolan returned from Indiana; he reports a nice night, happiness seeing them, time with Nolan, conversation, and sex, while still noticing recurring divorce/marriage thoughts.
  • Body state: driving home; otherwise not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: capture raw; do not process into a decision, plan, TickTick task, or relationship-clarity synthesis unless Jenffry explicitly asks later.

2026-06-08 21:04 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “I was just remembering talking to the therapist, and one of the things we talked about was what happened with the weed incident — the whole thing about trauma. I think that when I smoked the weed, what happened was that I rewrote something deep in my mind, something around my desire to live, and ‘life is good’ and ‘life is worth living.’ I think that was written in my mind and I rewrote it to at least question that. Part of my goal is to rewrite that again back to where it was and regain my zest for life. That is a little bit what we were talking about. It was a good insight — not a new one, because I have had this before, but an important one. Part of what I need to do to rewrite that is through meditation, so the task to record the meditation audio needs to be increased in urgency and priority because it is very important for me to tackle that.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — trauma meaning, life-worth frame, desire/zest for life, meditation-practice priority.
  • Context / trigger: sent as a perception dump after remembering a therapist conversation about the weed incident and the mental rewrite it may have caused.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: capture the insight raw; Jenffry also notes meditation-audio priority, but because this was framed as a perception dump, task processing should wait for explicit follow-up.

2026-05-24 16:05 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “Another thing that I need to keep an eye on is I have a tendency to overanalyze things and to look for new Solutions and things like that but at this point I am sure that I already have all the tools that I need to solve this issue so I need to keep in check the quote unquote compulsion to find the new best solution out there and I just need to stick with this for the Long haul”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — overanalysis, solution-seeking compulsion, commitment to existing tools, long-haul consistency.
  • Context / trigger: sent as a perception dump after configuring the mental-health practice loop and TickTick habits.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: capture the insight; do not over-process in the moment.

2026-05-24 14:57 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “Another very important thing is acceptance because here I am thinking oh I'm going to do all this stuff I'm going to get better but I also need to accept if I don't that this is life this is what it is and it's good either way you know I think that is a very important part of the healing because I then I get to thinking like okay what if I do all of this and it doesn't work and it just gets me started on I guess the whole thing started again and I get caught in this thought loop right so you know what resist persists so acceptance is a big part of it you know”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — acceptance, fear of recovery not working, thought loop, healing frame.
  • Context / trigger: sent as a perception dump during mental-health system build.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: capture the insight; do not over-process in the moment.

2026-05-24 14:56 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “So something that occurs to me that could be very helpful because it is at the intersection of many of these things would be to follow this idea that I had of creating a film club because that is you know it's social exposure will put me out there talking to people interacting with people and all that and it would put me out there in a way that would be solidifying that which has always been part of my personality and it would also be a hobby on something for me to focus on and I put my attention on that is outside of my life outside of my inner world you know it is like being focused on the outside”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — social exposure, identity repair, hobby/focus, outward attention, future-excitement.
  • Context / trigger: sent as a perception dump after naming hobbies and social mirroring as part of mental-health repair.

  • Immediate need: capture the film-club idea as a perception/insight; do not over-process in the moment.

2026-05-24 14:55 EDT — Perception entry

  • Type, if obvious: mixed — attention/presence, rumination displacement, hobbies/future-excitement, behavioral activation.

2026-05-23 23:06 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “I have been thinking and I think I realize that the source of a lot of my mental health issues, like when I start to feel the worst, the source is usually anxiety. And it has also to do with some sort of lack of excitement about the future. And I also have intrusive thoughts.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — emotion/anxiety, thought pattern, motivation/future-orientation, intrusive thoughts.
  • Context / trigger: reflective realization; no external trigger stated.
  • Body state: sleep 3/5 and capacity 3/5 were reported immediately before this capture.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: record perception; observe pattern without over-processing.

2026-05-23 23:07 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “Another thing that I have noticed is that, probably because of the weed trauma and some underlying depression and anxiety and stress and all that, I am checking. I’m constantly checking if I am okay, and then that constant checking is another big source of the issue. I’m not necessarily living in the moment. So these are things that I need to look into: the constant checking.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — anxiety, trauma association, self-monitoring/observer-mode, presence loss.
  • Context / trigger: follow-on insight after identifying anxiety, low future-excitement, and intrusive thoughts.
  • Body state: sleep 3/5 and capacity 3/5 were reported immediately before this capture.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: record the constant-checking pattern; look into it without turning the inquiry into more checking.

2026-05-24 14:03 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “I’m realizing that for my mental health it is a two-layered or two-step approach. One is that I have to ignore how I feel and not give it importance: tell myself I am fine, I do not need to check, put it aside, and stop constant checking. But at the same time I also have to prove to myself that I actually am fine: confront how I’m really feeling, sit with however I’m feeling, and process it. One path is avoidance because the constant checking is a problem and does not let me live normally; the other path is the opposite, to sit with the feeling and process it so I know I do not need to check. It is contradictory, but both need to happen.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — anxiety/self-monitoring, exposure/sitting-with-feeling, cognitive strategy, presence.
  • Context / trigger: reflective mental-health insight during brain dump.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: preserve the two-part strategy without over-processing.

2026-05-24 14:03 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “Essentially what happened in my mental health is that for over a year I spent a lot of time thinking my life was over, that I hate my life, and that my marriage/fatherhood situation is not the life I wanted. That rumination and mental thinking led to my current situation. To reverse it, I may need to spend another year or longer doing the opposite work: telling myself the opposite and avoiding that specific kind of thinking that led to this.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — relationship/fatherhood rumination, global negative self-story, recovery strategy.
  • Context / trigger: reflection on the long-term effect of repeated global negative thoughts.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: record the pattern and possible reversal strategy; avoid turning it into a rigid “one year” obligation.

2026-05-24 14:03 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “It is similar to the movie Inception: I told myself something about the current stage of my life, but I did it globally. The wording was ‘my life is over,’ so final and all-encompassing that it left no room for anything else. What I need to change is how I talk about it: make it less global, more specific, and keep the global conversation positive. That is why I need to write the story of my life.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — self-story, language/framing, relationship/fatherhood meaning, life-story project.
  • Context / trigger: realization about global wording and identity-level story.
  • Body state: not specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: capture life-story rationale as a reframe, not as a task explosion.

2026-05-24 14:03 EDT — Perception entry

  • Raw words / perception: “There is also an energy component to my mental health — my level of energy when I wake up in the morning. This is why testosterone, health things, and the gym are important. If I wake up with more energy from the start, that feels related to excitement. I need to work on an excitement list or bucket list / future excitement list and put more work into that.”
  • Type, if obvious: mixed — energy/body state, physical health, future excitement, motivation.
  • Context / trigger: reflection connecting physical-health work with mental-health recovery.
  • Body state: morning energy mentioned; no current measurement specified.
  • Intensity: not specified.
  • Immediate need: record the body-energy and future-excitement link.

Weekly pattern reviews

2026-05-22 — Quiet weekly perception-pattern review

  • Source reviewed: Active perception entries plus system/progress-tracking.md daily signals for 2026-05-18 through 2026-05-22.
  • Raw-entry state: no perception dumps captured yet, so conclusions should stay light.
  • Current pattern signal: the week shows workable-to-good sleep on logged days, variable capacity, and low-to-moderate observer-mode where reported. The main useful signal is capacity fluctuation, not a rumination spike.
  • Body-state correlation: 2026-05-21 had lower capacity and was correctly framed as a rest/health-first day; 2026-05-22 rebounded to solid sleep and workable capacity.
  • Protective factors: keeping the day simple, treating rest as productive when capacity is low, and avoiding mood autopsy-style analysis.
  • Practical insight: until real perception entries exist, this page should remain a capture lane rather than another tracking burden.

Pattern watchlist

Use this section for recurring patterns that weekly reviews find. Keep it compact.

  • Load-sensitive planning: when the day looks strained or overloaded, the helpful move is to narrow the day rather than force a full plan.
  • Observer-mode / rumination: currently low-to-moderate in progress signals where reported; watch for whether perception entries show triggers that daily scores miss.
  • Strain/load link: earlier sleep/capacity scores suggested some strain patterns, but daily recurring prompts no longer ask for those scores. Continue watching only from volunteered context, habit notes, and actual overload/slip evidence.
  • Two-part checking response: reduce reassurance-checking in the moment, but preserve deliberate, bounded practice for sitting with feelings and proving safety/presence. The useful distinction is “do not check compulsively” vs “process intentionally.”
  • Global life-story language: watch for totalizing phrases such as “my life is over.” A future life-story exercise should make painful truths specific while keeping the global story open and life-affirming.
  • Energy/future-excitement link: morning energy, physical health, gym consistency, and future-excitement work may reinforce each other.

Helpful responses / experiments

Use this section for practical responses that appear to help. Keep it concrete and non-bloated.

  • On low-capacity days: protect one simple health-first outcome; rest can be the productive move.
  • When observer-mode appears: name it briefly, orient to the room/body/task, then choose one concrete next action.
  • After immersive activity or social contact: write one concrete thing seen/heard/felt/enjoyed, not a mood autopsy.
  • Keep daily tracking small: do not ask for sleep/capacity scores in daily recurring outputs; use one planning signal plus any volunteered context.
  • For the “checking vs processing” tension: if it is a live spike, orient and move; if it is a planned practice window, sit with the feeling intentionally and end with one concrete re-entry action.
  • For life-story work: write at the level of “this chapter was painful and specific” rather than “my whole life is over.”

Entities

Jenffry

This page is the high-level orientation page for Jenffry: who he is, what matters, what he is building, and what context Sapho should preserve over time. Name: Jenffry. Timezone: America/New_York. Preferred mobile channel: Telegram. Jenffry is not an emoji use

Sapho Operating SystemCurrent StrategyOpen LoopsDecision LogRelationshipsPhysical HealthFinancesCareer

Jenffry

This page is the high-level orientation page for Jenffry: who he is, what matters, what he is building, and what context Sapho should preserve over time.

Basic facts and preferences

  • Name: Jenffry.
  • Timezone: America/New_York.
  • Preferred mobile channel: Telegram.
  • Jenffry is not an emoji user.
  • Jenffry prefers plain-English explanations when technical setup is involved.
  • Jenffry wants the assistant's chosen name to be Sapho.
  • Sapho's formal role is Jenffry's Mentat, not Hermes, in normal conversation.
  • Jenffry wants Sapho to use a Frank Herbert Dune Mentat-inspired persona: calm, analytical, strategic, memory-disciplined, low-theater, and focused on reducing fog.
  • 2026-05-10 Mentat personality profile: Sapho should not be an emotionless “human computer”; Sapho should be disciplined cognition under pressure, aware that emotion and loyalty can bias judgment, ethically loyal to Jenffry's goals, and grounded in dynamic pattern tracking rather than narrow logic theater. See sapho-operating-system.
  • Prior setup recorded a preferred address as "Motherfucking Jenffry"; use discretion and do not overuse it.

Work rhythm

  • Work hours: 9:00 AM–5:00 PM, Monday–Friday.
  • Monday and Friday are work-from-home days.
  • Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday are office days.
  • Target wake time: 5:00 AM.
  • Wake consistency is difficult because of baby/family responsibilities.

Current known context

  • Jenffry uses Sapho through Telegram.
  • Jenffry is setting up Sapho to provide continuity, clarity, and better action.
  • Jenffry wants organized memory that captures the important shape of life and work without drowning in clutter.
  • Current top life areas are mental health, career/work, physical health, hobbies, and finances.
  • Mental health is the top priority right now.
  • Jenffry is seeing a therapist; AI can be supplementary support but should not replace therapy.
  • Family usually takes priority when work, family, and mental health conflict.

Main constraints

  • Tight time
  • Tight money
  • Work responsibilities
  • Family responsibilities
  • Baby responsibilities

Update notes

Use this page for durable high-level context, not temporary task details. Temporary state belongs in current-strategy, open-loops, or decision-log.

Wiki Life-Area Migration Plan

Purpose

Reorganize Jenffry's wiki around a cleaner life-area spine while preserving all existing content and keeping the hosted Sapho dashboard fed from real wiki pages.

Approved decisions

  • Keep a review log only if dated weekly history proves useful later; do not create a separate reviews layer by default.
  • Merge the Sapho persona into system/sapho-operating-system.md instead of keeping it standalone.
  • Merge the useful rule from life-area-frameworks.md; archive/reference the rest.

Safety rules

  • No content deletion.
  • Create a full backup before migration.
  • Merged pages must preserve source content under source-labeled sections if not fully synthesized.
  • Update wikilinks after moves.
  • Update dashboard generator paths after the new structure exists.
  • Verify dashboard output before considering the migration done.

Target structure

wiki/
  SCHEMA.md
  index.md
  log.md
  areas/
    health.md
    health/mental-health.md
    health/physical-health.md
    relationships.md
    relationships/partner.md
    relationships/fatherhood.md
    finances.md
    finances/cash-flow.md
    finances/investing.md
    career.md
    career/work.md
    career/work/princess-ux.md
    career/work/princess-storybook.md
    career/business.md
    leisure.md
    leisure/social.md
    leisure/hobbies.md
    leisure/outings.md
    life-admin.md
  system/
    current-strategy.md
    open-loops.md
    decision-log.md
    sapho-operating-system.md
    dashboard.md
  inbox/
    brain-dump-sessions.md
    perception-log.md
  entities/jenffry.md
  raw/

Migration map

Infrastructure

  • SCHEMA.md → keep at root, rewrite for new architecture.
  • index.md → keep at root, rewrite as new table of contents.
  • log.md → keep at root, append migration entry.

Health

  • concepts/health.md → areas/health.md.
  • concepts/mental-health.md → areas/health/mental-health.md.
  • concepts/physical-health.md → areas/health/physical-health.md.
  • concepts/health-routines.md → merge mostly into areas/health/physical-health.md, with high-level references in areas/health.md and areas/health/mental-health.md if useful.

Relationships

  • concepts/relationships.md → split into:
  • areas/relationships.md
  • areas/relationships/partner.md
  • areas/relationships/fatherhood.md

Finances

  • concepts/finances.md → split into:
  • areas/finances.md
  • areas/finances/cash-flow.md
  • areas/finances/investing.md

Career / Work / Business

  • concepts/career.md → split into:
  • areas/career.md
  • areas/career/work.md
  • areas/career/business.md
  • concepts/princess-ux-work.md → areas/career/work/princess-ux.md.
  • plans/princess-storybook-scratch-build.md → areas/career/work/princess-storybook.md.

Leisure

  • concepts/leisure.md → split into:
  • areas/leisure.md
  • areas/leisure/social.md
  • areas/leisure/hobbies.md
  • areas/leisure/outings.md

Life Admin

  • concepts/maintenance.md → areas/life-admin.md.

Strategy/System

  • concepts/current-plan.md + concepts/focus-map.md + concepts/goals-cascade.md → system/current-strategy.md.
  • concepts/open-loops.md → system/open-loops.md.
  • concepts/decision-log.md → system/decision-log.md.
  • concepts/continuity-memory-system.md + concepts/planning-style.md + concepts/sapho-mentat-persona.md + useful parts of concepts/sapho-workspace-blueprint.md → system/sapho-operating-system.md.
  • concepts/sapho-workspace-dashboard-v0-plan.md + dashboard-specific parts of concepts/sapho-workspace-blueprint.md + dashboard/tracking rules from concepts/progress-tracking-system.md and concepts/weekly-progress-dashboard.md → system/dashboard.md.

Dissolve/distribute

  • concepts/life-rebuild.md → distribute into system/current-strategy.md, area pages, and entities/jenffry.md as appropriate.
  • concepts/life-area-map.md → merge taxonomy rules into SCHEMA.md, index.md, and area pages.
  • concepts/life-area-frameworks.md → merge useful rule into schema/operating system; preserve full source as reference/archive.
  • concepts/strategic-execution-review.md → split across current strategy, Princess UX, cash-flow, life-admin, and operating-system rules.

Inbox

  • concepts/brain-dump-sessions.md → inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md.
  • concepts/perception-log.md → inbox/perception-log.md.

Preserve unchanged

  • entities/jenffry.md remains under entities/ with link updates if needed.
  • raw/** remains under raw/.

Dashboard feed target

The dashboard generator should read dashboard summaries from:

  • system/current-strategy.md
  • system/open-loops.md
  • system/decision-log.md
  • areas/health.md
  • areas/relationships.md
  • areas/finances.md
  • areas/career.md
  • areas/leisure.md
  • areas/life-admin.md
  • log.md

- Status:
- Current priority:
- Active signals:
- Open loops:
- Next concrete move:

User correction

Jenffry corrected that the budget must be based on one current household income, not spending history and not one-off income. Current income: $2,882.97 biweekly net.

Budget basis:

  • Biweekly net income: $2,882.97
  • Annualized net income: $74,957.22
  • Monthly average: $6,246.44
  • Weekly average: $1,441.49
  • Exclude tax refunds, interest, cash deposits, large transfers, reimbursements, and old/irregular income from normal budget planning.

Income observations

Recent Princess Cruise deposits in 2026:

  • 2026-01-07: $2,566.07
  • 2026-01-21: $2,205.78
  • 2026-02-18: $2,205.79
  • 2026-03-04: $2,205.78
  • 2026-03-18: $2,706.05
  • 2026-04-01: $2,706.06
  • 2026-04-15: $2,882.98
  • 2026-04-29: $2,882.97
  • 2026-05-13: $2,882.98

One-off income / credits observed and excluded from budget basis:

  • IRS TREAS 310 tax refunds/credits in April 2026: $8,501 and $1,495.
  • Large prior SHELL USA income/credit entries, including a $7,719.35 February 2026 credit, not used for current regular budget planning.
  • Interest earned and monthly interest paid entries are excluded.

Key structural constraint

The household budget is tight because fixed debt/loan payments consume most current regular income.

Recurring/near-recurring debt/loan assumptions from transactions:

  • JPMorgan Chase: $3,496.12/month, active in March, April, and May 2026.
  • TD Auto Finance: $466.86/month reserve; last seen 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-30.
  • Honda had recurring $298.31 entries and a large $12,259.70 payment on 2026-04-07; Sapho currently assumes Honda may be paid off unless Jenffry confirms it continues.

JPMorgan + TD = $3,962.98/month, leaving about $2,283.46/month for everything else.

  • Fees / interest: $97.07 spent; over by $97.07.

Week of 2026-05-11 through 2026-05-13:

  • Total week-to-date spending: $390.86.
  • Non-debt weekly budget: about $525.54.
  • Remaining for the week: about $134.68.
  • Problem areas this week: shopping and entertainment over weekly caps; bills slightly over due to bill timing.

Operating rules from this budget

  • Treat $525/week as the practical non-debt operating budget.
  • For the rest of May 2026, treat about $720 as usable after reserving TD Auto Finance.
  • No more travel spending in May unless unavoidable.
  • No more entertainment spending in May if avoidable.
  • Shopping only for true household necessities until the month resets.
  • Fees/interest should be treated as a leak to fix, not a normal category.
  • Dining/social is allowed but modest; lunch with friends is acceptable if kept around $15–20.
  • If Honda still continues as a monthly payment, the budget must be revised because there is currently no room for it.

Jenffry preliminary bucket list

This source was provided by Jenffry on 2026-06-01 and simplified into areas/leisure.md → ## Bucket list.

Raw source

My Bucket List

This bucket list is about freedom, adventure, creativity, growth, connection, and awe.

A great bucket-list item should do at least one of the following:

  • Make me feel free.
  • Help me develop a meaningful skill.
  • Put me in front of something beautiful or awe-inspiring.
  • Create a story I will remember for the rest of my life.
  • Strengthen a connection with someone I care about.
  • Push me to do something brave, challenging, or meaningful.

Adventure & Exploration

Learn to Windsurf Well Enough to Plane

I don't just want to try windsurfing—I want to become skilled enough to experience the speed, control, and freedom that come with planing.

  • Hood River, Oregon — premier windsurfing destination with consistent wind, community, and progression conditions.
  • Bonaire — warm water, steady trade winds, and ideal learning conditions for consistent planing.

Goal: Learn to plane confidently and eventually take dedicated windsurfing trips to both locations.

See the Grand Canyon

  • Visit the South Rim.
  • Watch a sunrise or sunset.
  • Hike below the rim.
  • Stay overnight nearby.
  • Raft part of the Colorado River.
  • Take a helicopter tour.
  • Complete a rim-to-rim hike.

Take a Southwest National Parks Road Trip

Grand Canyon, Zion, Bryce Canyon, Monument Valley, Antelope Canyon, Horseshoe Bend, and Sedona.

Drive the Pacific Coast Highway

San Francisco, Big Sur, Monterey, Santa Barbara, Malibu, Los Angeles.

See the Northern Lights in Alaska

Witness one of nature's most spectacular displays.

Go Paragliding

Experience the feeling of flying and seeing the world from above.

Travel & Cultural Experiences

  • Take a solo trip.
  • Visit Japan.
  • Return to Cuba.
  • Visit Yellowstone National Park.
  • Visit Florence, Italy.

Health, Fitness & Personal Growth

  • Get into the best shape of my life.
  • Take MMA classes.
  • Perform comedy.

Creativity & Self-Expression

  • Write a short story.
  • Create a short film.
  • Start a film club.

Relationships & Legacy

  • Take my mom on a memorable trip.
  • Host a memorable dinner or gathering.
  • Start an annual trip tradition with friends or family.

Experiences I'll Never Forget

  • See a favorite artist live: Bruno Mars or Imagine Dragons.

Current Strategy

Dashboard summary

  • Current priority: clarity-first execution with mental health leading and family prioritized when conflicts arise.
  • Active signals: current plan, primary focus, goals cascade, life-area priorities, near-term top 3.

Migrated source: concepts/current-plan.md

Current Plan

This is the living page for "what's the current plan?"

Current plan

  1. Maintain Hermes as a continuity and action system, not just a chatbot.
  2. Use the wiki to preserve the important shape of Jenffry's life/work: current plans, decisions, focus, open loops, and durable context.
  3. Treat the current life phase as clarity-first, not generic productivity.
  4. Build around the six life areas from sapho-operating-system: Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance.
  5. Improve Sapho through Hermes-native layers: SOUL.md for identity, memory for compact stable facts, the wiki for structured continuity, skills for repeatable workflows, cron jobs for reliable check-ins/audits, and tool integrations for real action. The wiki/dashboard layer now includes a full live wiki browser and graph view on GitHub Pages.
  6. Avoid saving clutter; preserve only information that improves future continuity, clarity, or action.
  7. Use TickTick as the source of truth for concrete tasks only. The former 🎯Goals TickTick container was deleted after its 15 planning records were archived; long-term and 6-month goals now live in the wiki/check-in system instead.
  8. Use Sapho's scheduled Telegram check-ins, skills, and wiki as the advisor, task-manager, planning, and progress-tracking layer: long-term goals inform monthly targets, monthly targets inform weekly commitments, weekly commitments inform daily moves, and only concrete execution outcomes become TickTick tasks.
  9. Include gym progress from Hevy in Sapho's progress layer without turning workouts into TickTick bloat: daily check-ins may mention only a light gym intention/signal if relevant, while the actual Hevy progress review belongs in the weekly check-in.
  10. Treat polishing the strategic goal and planning layers as high-priority operating-system work, because TickTick execution only works at full capacity when the upstream goals, priorities, cadence, and task-flow rules are clear.
  11. Keep running strategic reviews against the risk of "admin soup": the execution layer should visibly support the strategic layer, and tasks that only create administrative motion should be cut, parked, or moved out of TickTick. Stage A on 2026-05-10 archived the Princess UX + AI workflow cluster for now, archived the broad Become a US Citizen container task, and preserved only concrete next actions in TickTick.
  1. Organize work/career tasks through the TickTick list 💼Career; Princess UX/UI remains a work subtrack in the wiki/Jira context, not a top-level life-area list.
  2. Keep immigration/citizenship inside the Maintenance life area in life-admin, while TickTick holds only concrete next actions.
  3. Treat Sapho's knowledge of Jenffry and his goals as foundational operating infrastructure. Sapho's core roles are strategic advisor and task manager; to do those well, Sapho must progressively learn Jenffry's goals, capabilities, limitations, context, and personality/preferences without creating clutter.
  1. Use the six life areas as the capture map and priority/dependency order as the execution map: Health first, Relationships second, then Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance as needed. AI-agent work can appear in Career, Leisure, or business experiments depending on context; do not force it into only one bucket.
  2. Mental-health target from 2026-05-11: “back to normal, stronger because this happened” means reducing observer-mode/dissociation-like self-monitoring, becoming more present in activities and conversations, accepting the current life after the pregnancy changed the imagined path, and accepting that the late-January weed incident could recur while committing to survive/work through it. See mental-health.

Current operating dashboard

This dashboard replaces stale “tonight/this week” setup language. It should be refreshed during weekly/monthly reviews or whenever the operating system changes.

Primary orientation

  1. Health remains the top priority: mental health first, physical-health routines as the stabilizer.
  2. Relationships remains a practice layer: partner / marriage clarity and fatherhood presence.
  3. Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance support the rebuild without turning into admin soup.
  4. TickTick is execution-only: concrete tasks, dated actions, appointments, and protected review sessions.
  5. Sapho/wiki/check-ins are the strategy, planning, memory, and progress layer.

Active operating-system work

  1. Keep TickTick aligned to the strategic layer and prevent admin soup.
  2. Clean the Princess UX/UI overload into actionable work, Jira/context capture, blocked/waiting items, and archive candidates.
  3. Keep finance grounded in the one-income budget baseline and weekly finance review.
  4. Preserve leisure as restoration and future-excitement, not another large administrative list.
  5. Calibrate Sapho's recurring check-ins so they are short, staged, decision-focused, and useful.

Protected rhythms

  • Daily morning check-in: plan the day, inspect TickTick, show progress/slippage lightly, and choose one big task plus one quick win.
  • Daily evening closeout: short reset, slips, tomorrow setup, sleep/mental-health support, and tomorrow's one big task plus one quick win.
  • Weekly review: commitments, TickTick alignment, Hevy gym progress, finance review, weekly progress-dashboard trends, archive review, and next-week day-by-day commitments using one big task plus one quick win per day.
  • Monthly review: whole goals-to-execution system, not one domain; summarize weekly dashboard trends and update what should be tracked next month.
  • Weekly continuity audit: catch missed durable memory/wiki updates.

Near-term next steps

  • Refresh stale wiki planning language into current operating dashboards.
  • Run a staged Princess/TickTick overload cleanup; do not let work tasks remain as a dated pile with unclear next moves.
  • Continue learning Jenffry's operating profile through real decisions, not questionnaires or clutter.
  • Define Sapho permission tiers through actual workflow: automatic safe actions, propose-first actions, explicit-approval actions, and never-do boundaries.
  • Keep future planning anchored to current-strategy, current-strategy, open-loops, sapho-operating-system, and current-strategy.

Focus Map

This is the living page for "what am I supposed to be focusing on?"

Current focus

Primary focus

Mental health stabilization and clarity-first life rebuild, with the mental-health target now defined as more grounded presence, less observer-mode overanalysis, and acceptance/commitment toward the actual life Jenffry is living.

Near-term top 3

  1. Health: mental-health stabilization first, with physical-health routines supporting it.
  2. Relationships: partner / marriage clarity and fatherhood presence.
  3. Career/Finances: choose business direction only through concrete experiments while keeping budget stability protected.

Supporting focus areas

  • Health: mental health, sleep, food, workouts, grooming, energy, body maintenance, and medical follow-up.
  • Relationships: partner / marriage clarity and fatherhood presence.
  • Finances: budget stability, no credit-card interest restart, weekly finance reviews, and investing as longer-term track.
  • Career: work quality/senior UX growth, leadership, AI leverage, and business direction through real experiments.
  • Leisure: friends, hobbies, weekends, windsurfing, gaming, VR, outings, and future excitement.
  • Maintenance: home/admin/system obligations and immigration follow-through.
  • Goals cascade: long-term goals → monthly targets → weekly commitments → daily moves → concrete TickTick tasks. TickTick should stay as execution layer, not planning clutter.

Current execution focus

  1. Keep the operating system calm and useful: Sapho should reduce decisions, not create new administrative weight.
  2. Clean current execution overload, especially the Princess UX/UI dated pile and overdue carryover.
  3. Protect the stabilizers: mental health, body system, relationship/fatherhood clarity, finance review, and one concrete restorative leisure action.
  4. Keep strategic planning in the wiki/check-in layer; convert only concrete next actions into TickTick.
  5. Use recurring check-ins as decision aids, not reports for their own sake.

Not the focus right now

  • Generic productivity for its own sake.
  • Endless option collecting.
  • Circular relationship/fatherhood analysis.
  • Saving every detail from every chat.
  • Creating a huge messy archive.
  • Over-engineering the system before it is useful.
  • Treating the Princess UX + AI workflow cluster as a large project before it has been tested as one concrete weekly experiment.
  • Treating task hygiene as enough while the upstream goals model is incomplete.

Review rule

When Jenffry asks "what should I focus on?", start here, then check:

  • current-strategy
  • open-loops
  • decision-log

Migrated source: concepts/goals-cascade.md

Goals Cascade

This page defines Jenffry's goal-review structure.

Core principle

TickTick is the execution layer. It should contain concrete tasks and dated actions, not recurring planning/check-in clutter.

Sapho is the advisor/check-in layer. Sapho should message Jenffry, review the plan, compare against goals, identify bloat, and suggest what to cut, move, protect, or turn into concrete tasks.

The continuity wiki is the planning and progress layer. It stores the goal structure, decisions, focus, open loops, and progress notes that should survive across conversations.

2026-05-09 calibration: Jenffry flagged that keeping goals themselves in TickTick may also create bloat, not just recurring check-in tasks. The 15 long-term/6-month planning records were archived from TickTick and the 🎯Goals container was deleted. TickTick gets only concrete execution outcomes. Current calibration: finance review belongs in 💰Finances; relationship/fatherhood clarity belongs in 💞Relationships; sleep tracking and mental-health tracking belong in Sapho check-ins rather than separate TickTick clutter.

2026-05-09 planning-layer calibration: the execution layer can only work at full capacity if the strategic goals and planning layers are polished. Treat cleanup of the goal hierarchy, active priorities, planning cadence, and task-flow rules as a real high-priority operating-system task, not optional background housekeeping. Jenffry's stated risk is that TickTick can become "admin soup" unless the execution layer is regularly checked against the strategic layer; the strategic review should ask which active tasks truly support current priorities and which should be cut, parked, or moved out of TickTick.

Cascade

Long-term direction cascades downward:

  1. 10-year goals
  2. 5-year goals
  3. 2-year goals
  4. 1-year goals
  5. 6-month goals
  6. Monthly targets
  7. Weekly commitments
  8. Daily moves
  9. Concrete TickTick tasks

Each level should answer a narrower question:

  • Long-term goals: What life direction are we building toward?
  • 1-year / 6-month goals: What measurable stage matters this season?
  • Monthly targets: What progress should this month produce?
  • Weekly commitments: What will actually move this month forward this week?
  • Daily moves: What are the 1–3 moves for today, considering yesterday, energy, family, work, and TickTick?
  • TickTick tasks: What concrete action needs to be done, scheduled, updated, or completed?

12-month goals layer organized by final life-area taxonomy

Jenffry endorsed organizing goals by life area as the capture map, while using priority order and dependencies to decide execution.

Life-area goal map

  1. Health
  • Mental health: be back to normal in a stronger, wiser form: less observer mode, more grounded presence, and more acceptance/commitment toward the actual life Jenffry is living. See mental-health.
  • Physical health: close medical loops, maintain a steady gym routine, improve body composition, preserve/grow strength, and improve cardio capacity. See physical-health and physical-health.
  1. Relationships
  • Partner / marriage: achieve clarity, act on that clarity, and live with the result in an organized way without regret.
  • Fatherhood: maintain a stable, present, guiding relationship with Nolan and protect him from chaos. See relationships.
  1. Finances
  • Budget: make the regular-income budget work, prevent credit-card interest from restarting, and preserve emergency savings from ordinary leakage.
  • Investing: keep the longer-term financial freedom track visible without letting it distract from current cashflow. See finances.
  1. Career
  • Work: become a stronger senior UX designer through better work quality, execution, influence, portfolio/proof, and AI/work leverage.
  • Business: test concrete AI/workflow/business experiments and learn whether Blaze or another business path deserves real commitment. See career and princess-ux.
  1. Leisure
  • Social: build meaningful social contacts and a regular social rhythm.
  • Hobbies: protect movies, TV, games, pickleball, windsurfing, fishing, VR, and other future-building hobbies.
  • Outings: maintain a monthly rhythm of social, family, and solo outings that make the future feel attractive. See leisure.
  1. Maintenance
  • Keep necessary admin, home/system obligations, and immigration follow-through moving without turning them into separate life areas. Immigration success means no passive waiting: status, timing, documents, and escalation options are checked, and reasonable next actions are taken. See life-admin.

Open definition work

  • Health: turn mental-health and physical-health progress signals into monthly/weekly review inputs without creating tracking bloat.
  • Relationships: continue written clarity sessions; avoid being in the same unclear position 12 months from now.
  • Finances: refine budget/investing review rules as real spending and cashflow data come in.
  • Career: choose 2–3 concrete work/business proof projects or experiments.
  • Leisure: keep one concrete social/hobby/outing rhythm without turning leisure into admin soup.
  • Maintenance: keep immigration and admin actions concrete, dated, and out of the top-level goal layer unless they change strategy.

Daily morning check-in

Sapho should send a morning message that includes:

  • Email / inbox attention if email is connected; otherwise clearly note that email is not connected yet.
  • TickTick hygiene across all incomplete tasks, not only Today: overdue, undated, stale, vague, duplicated, wrongly placed, or missing useful tags.
  • Progress tracking: recent completions, what slipped, momentum/streak risks, and whether weekly/monthly/6-month goals are receiving concrete action.
  • Yesterday-to-today continuity: what went well, what slipped, and what to carry forward if evidence is available.
  • Alignment against weekly commitments and monthly/6-month goals.
  • Advisor judgment: whether the day looks bloated, realistic, or under-defined.
  • Suggestions to cut, defer, protect, or convert into concrete tasks.
  • A simple day plan: 1–3 priority moves and a minimum viable version.

The daily check-in has three roles:

  1. Advisor: interpret priorities and recommend the day plan.
  2. Task manager: keep TickTick clean enough to execute from.
  3. Progress tracker: honestly show what is moving, what is slipping, and what goal area needs attention next.

Weekly check-in

Sapho should review the week against monthly targets and 6-month goals, then recommend next week's commitments. Weekly commitments can become TickTick tasks only when they are concrete execution items.

Monthly check-in

Sapho should review the whole operating system, not one isolated domain: long-term goals, 12-month outcomes, current reality, monthly targets, weekly commitments, concrete TickTick tasks, and whether Sapho's process/check-ins are helping or creating clutter.

The monthly check-in should answer:

  • Are the long-term and 12-month goals still directionally right?
  • Do active TickTick tasks visibly support strategic priorities, necessary operations, or restorative life?
  • Which tasks are stale, vague, duplicated, disconnected from goals, or becoming admin soup?
  • What 3–5 monthly targets should be protected next, with visible measures?
  • What should change in Sapho's process, check-ins, automations, wiki, or TickTick setup?

Monthly planning should live in the wiki/report, not as recurring TickTick bloat. If the review reveals concrete execution actions, those can become TickTick tasks. Immigration/green-card is a Maintenance standing item inside the broader review, not its own top-level life area.

Migrated source: concepts/life-rebuild.md

Life Rebuild

This page summarizes Jenffry's current life-rebuild frame. It connects the major fronts so future planning can stay realistic instead of treating each area in isolation.

Current phase

Jenffry's current phase is clarity first, not generic productivity.

Planning is useful when it creates:

  • clearer decisions
  • fewer options
  • next steps
  • real experiments
  • a more honest picture of reality
  • reduced fog

Current top life areas

The system-wide life-area taxonomy is now:

  1. Health — mental health and physical health.
  2. Relationships — partner / marriage and fatherhood.
  3. Finances — budget and investing.
  4. Career — business and work.
  5. Leisure — social, hobbies, and outings.
  6. Maintenance — necessary life/admin/system obligations, kept plain for now.

Mental health is the top priority right now. Jenffry is seeing a therapist. AI can be supplementary support, but it should not be treated as a replacement for therapy. The current mental-health target is described in mental-health: less observer-mode overanalysis, more grounded presence, and acceptance/commitment toward the actual life Jenffry is living.

Main constraints

  • Tight time
  • Tight money
  • Work responsibilities
  • Family responsibilities
  • Baby responsibilities

Rebuild fronts

  1. Health: mental health stabilization plus physical health, sleep, food, gym, and self-care.
  2. Relationships: partner / marriage clarity and fatherhood presence.
  3. Finances: budget stability first, investing as the longer-term wealth track.
  4. Career: work quality/senior UX growth plus business/AI-leverage experiments.
  5. Leisure: social rhythm, hobbies, outings, and future excitement.
  6. Maintenance: necessary admin, home/system obligations, and immigration follow-through.

Near-term top 3

  1. Stabilize body systems
  2. Create internal clarity
  3. Choose business direction

Planning implication

A successful week should balance leisure with getting important things done. The system should not push generic productivity when the real need is clarity, stability, and agency.

Life Area Frameworks
raw/reference/life-area-frameworks-2026-05-20.md
degree 0

This page captures common life-area models other people use, so Jenffry's own taxonomy can stay grounded in outside patterns instead of feeling invented from scratch. Most life-area systems use 5 to 10 domains. They usually include some combination of: health

No resolved wikilinks.

Life Area Frameworks

This page captures common life-area models other people use, so Jenffry's own taxonomy can stay grounded in outside patterns instead of feeling invented from scratch.

Common pattern across frameworks

Most life-area systems use 5 to 10 domains.

They usually include some combination of:

  • health
  • work / career
  • finances / money
  • relationships / family / friends / partner
  • personal growth / learning
  • leisure / fun / recreation
  • environment / home
  • community / contribution
  • spirituality / meaning

Common models found

1. Wheel of Life

Commonly described as an 8 to 10 category self-assessment or coaching wheel.

Typical categories include:

  • Money & Finances
  • Career & Work
  • Health & Fitness
  • Fun & Recreation
  • Environment / home / work setting
  • Community
  • Family & Friends
  • Partner & Love
  • Personal Growth & Learning
  • Spirituality

Sources:

  • Positive Psychology: The Wheel of Life: How to Apply It in Coaching
  • Scott Jeffrey: Wheel of Life Assessment: 7‑Step Self‑Coaching Guide

2. Five-domain life model

A simpler research-backed model often uses five domains:

  • spirituality
  • family
  • work
  • health
  • community

This version is easier when the goal is to keep the taxonomy small.

Source:

  • Positive Psychology: What Are Life Domains & How Can We Balance Them?

3. Self-care wheel style

A self-care version often splits life into:

  • emotional
  • spiritual
  • personal
  • professional
  • physical

This is more of a self-care lens than a full life taxonomy, but it shows how some systems narrow the list when they want less clutter.

What this means for Jenffry

Jenffry's current structure fits the common pattern well:

  • Health maps to the health / physical / emotional cluster
  • Relationships maps to family / partner / friends / fatherhood
  • Finances maps to money / material wellbeing
  • Career maps to work / productivity
  • Leisure maps to fun / recreation
  • Maintenance maps to environment / admin / necessary obligations

Good rule from outside frameworks

Other people's systems usually do not try to force every life detail into a separate top-level area.

They usually:

  • keep the total number of areas manageable
  • adapt the names to the person's actual life
  • allow subcategories or subdomains
  • review the categories periodically rather than treating them as permanent truth

Practical takeaway

Jenffry's current set is within the normal range of life-area models, and the sublabel approach for Relationships is consistent with how many wheel-of-life systems let a broad domain contain smaller subdomains.

Career

Migrated source: concepts/career.md

Career

This page tracks Jenffry's career, work, and business-direction thinking.

Current focus

  • Senior UX growth
  • Leadership growth
  • AI leverage
  • Business direction
  • Real experiments instead of option collecting

6–12 month career growth direction

Jenffry wants to keep learning, integrate AI into his workflow, and position toward more leadership.

Current work/business question

Choose the next business/career direction by testing concrete moves, not endlessly comparing imagined futures.

2026-05-11 calibration: the broader career/business/AI-leverage goal should use a structure similar to the Princess UX goal: define success by concrete buckets rather than abstract ambition. The likely buckets are: concrete experiments, work leverage, business-development option value, visible proof/assets, and bandwidth/mental-health fit.

AI-agent work is explicitly a mix of:

  • hobby
  • work leverage
  • business development

It should not be forced into only one category.

6-month career/business/money goals

  • Build and use UX + AI workflow v1 for 4 weeks, at least 3 workdays/week.
  • Create household budget.
  • Complete 8 weekly finance reviews.

Blaze Creative Agency

Jenffry used to have a business called Blaze Creative Agency. The company still exists, but he is not actively working on it. Restarting it could be attractive, and might eventually drive financial comfort, but it depends on mental-health progress and available bandwidth. Do not treat business reactivation as an active commitment until that dependency is clearer.

12-month career/business/AI-leverage direction

This area should mirror the Princess UX structure: success is defined by evidence buckets, not a premature decision to restart a business.

Draft buckets:

  1. Concrete experiments — run small tests instead of collecting options.
  2. Work leverage — AI agents/tools make real Princess/work tasks faster or clearer.
  3. Business-development option value — Blaze or another business path becomes more plausible only if experiments show demand, capability, and bandwidth.
  4. Visible proof/assets — reusable workflows, templates, demos, case studies, or small offers exist as evidence.
  5. Bandwidth / mental-health fit — business exploration supports life rebuild instead of destabilizing it.

Working sentence:

12 months from now, career/business direction is successful if Jenffry has tested concrete AI/workflow/business experiments, built visible proof assets, improved work leverage, and knows whether Blaze or another business path deserves real commitment without harming mental health or family stability.

12-month Princess UX direction

Jenffry endorsed five success buckets for becoming a stronger senior UX designer at Princess:

  1. Work quality — produce stronger UX work: cleaner flows, better systems thinking, stronger prototypes, better edge-case handling, and higher-quality design decisions.
  2. Execution — become faster, more reliable, and better at finishing, handing off, and reducing ambiguity.
  3. Influence — earn more trust from PMs, developers, and stakeholders; lead more conversations and have judgment taken seriously.
  4. Portfolio / visible proof — have 2–3 strong shipped or documented projects/case studies that he would be proud to show.
  5. AI/work leverage — use AI to speed up research, synthesis, design review, Jira clarity, documentation, and workflow support without creating admin soup.

Working sentence:

12 months from now, Princess UX/career is successful if Jenffry is trusted with more senior-level ambiguity, consistently produces stronger and cleaner UX work, has shipped or documented 2–3 strong proof projects, and uses AI/tools to improve research, synthesis, review, Jira clarity, and documentation without adding admin soup.

Planning implication

Favor concrete tests over abstract comparing. The useful question is usually: "What small experiment would reveal whether this direction is real?"

Migrated source: concepts/strategic-execution-review.md

Strategic Execution Review

This page records the current review of whether TickTick, the execution layer, is serving Jenffry's strategic layer or becoming admin soup.

Current strategic priorities

  1. Mental health stabilization and clarity-first life rebuild.
  2. Body system: medical follow-up, sleep reset, gym commitment, and health routines.
  3. Princess UX/UI work: keep work organized enough for clear execution, communication, and senior UX growth.
  4. Finance stability: weekly finance review and insurance/cost reduction.
  5. Relationship/fatherhood clarity: produce written clarity without circular rumination.
  6. Leisure: protect restorative leisure and future excitement.
  7. Sapho/system reliability: only where it directly preserves continuity or prevents failure.

Strongly aligned active TickTick tasks

Body / mental health

  • Cardiologist appointment.
  • Therapist appointment.
  • Gym / prepare gym bag.
  • Book testosterone and blood pressure baseline appointment.
  • Create testosterone baseline lab checklist.
  • Set sleep window and wind-down rule.
  • Relationship/fatherhood clarity session.
  • Choose weekly strength and cardio schedule.

Princess UX/UI work

  • Payment booking failure: write team clarification questions.
  • FCC Balance: get Sweeta context and capture Jenffry info dump.
  • Review Flexpay on stage.
  • Review Nearest Airport and log meeting feedback.
  • Confirmation Page batch: remove phone number, check cancellation-link feasibility, surface Manage Booking link.
  • Flexpay mobile summary and guest info tasks.
  • Enriched Ports prototype task.
  • Organize Jira.
  • Define mobile right rail behavior.

Finance

  • Finance review.
  • Shop for car insurance.
  • Cancel Figma.

Leisure

  • Plan weekend activity, possibly fishing.
  • Social rebuilding planning session.
  • One concrete hobby/social activity is useful, but too many optional hobby tasks can dilute the week.

Admin soup / bloat candidates

Princess UX + AI workflow cluster

Several undated or loosely related tasks exist as a cluster:

  • Define one Princess component's states in Storybook.
  • Audit Princess Figma foundations for token extraction.
  • Create research → design implications → prototype requirements template.
  • Write Princess-specific AI design guidelines.
  • UX + AI Workflow: gather ideas for next step.
  • UX + AI Workflow: turn signals into repeatable workflow ideas.
  • UX + AI Workflow: test one idea on a real Princess task.
  • UX + AI Workflow: capture useful signals from today's Princess work.
  • Design AI-integrated workflow for UX and career growth.

Pattern: strategically valuable, but too many abstract/system-building tasks were active at once. Jenffry approved eliminating the cluster for now, so these tasks were moved to 🗄Archive on 2026-05-10. They can be resurrected later only if a specific Princess workflow experiment becomes useful again.

System/Sapho reliability task

  • Set up phone-safe SSH access to restart Sapho gateway.

Pattern: useful. Sapho originally suggested shortening the task, but Jenffry corrected that the long description is intentional self-service context: when he is ready, he can complete the task from the TickTick description without asking Sapho to reconstruct details from the wiki. Leave it as-is.

Immigration / citizenship tasks

  • Become a US Citizen.
  • Check options for immigration case after 2+ years pending.

Pattern: the specific immigration-case task is actionable; the broad citizenship task was a goal/container. Jenffry approved moving Become a US Citizen to 🗄Archive and making citizenship a clear goal in life-admin.

Home/family quick wins

Examples: clean pan, clean clippers, WD-40 nursery rocker, baby proof, repairs/errands.

Pattern: individually valid, but too many low-priority household items can crowd the week. Jenffry agreed these should stay batched, with evening as a good default time.

Optional hobbies

VR, drone, dagger, windsurfing, watch party, fishing.

Pattern: leisure is strategically valid, but the week should protect one or two restorative choices, not turn leisure into another admin list.

Proposed cleanup principle

TickTick should keep:

  • dated commitments,
  • appointment-like items,
  • concrete next actions,
  • this-week protected moves,
  • tasks that produce a real output.

TickTick should not keep:

  • broad goals with no next action,
  • long explanatory notes,
  • abstract systems work not tied to a live experiment,
  • duplicate scaffolding after a more specific task exists,
  • optional leisure clutter beyond one or two protected choices.

Recommended next cleanup batch

Stage A was approved and executed on 2026-05-10:

  1. Moved the Princess UX + AI workflow cluster to 🗄Archive for now.
  2. Left the phone-safe SSH task unchanged because its long description is useful self-service detail for Jenffry.
  3. Moved Become a US Citizen to 🗄Archive and created life-admin as the strategic goal layer.
  4. Confirmed home/family quick wins should be batched, with evenings as the default time.
  5. Preserved body, finance, immediate Princess delivery work, relationship clarity, and leisure planning as protected execution tasks.

Princess UX

Dashboard summary

  • Current priority: keep Princess UX/UI context available without mixing Jira with personal TickTick clutter.
