Status: Active fatherhood/family-duty domain. Current priority: protect Nolan's experience of Jenffry as a present, consistent, helpful father regardless of the marriage structure. Active signals: Nolan presence, care share, routines, milestones, patience, tea

Relationship Clarity SessionsRelationshipsPartner

Fatherhood

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active fatherhood/family-duty domain.
  • Current priority: protect Nolan's experience of Jenffry as a present, consistent, helpful father regardless of the marriage structure.
  • Active signals: Nolan presence, care share, routines, milestones, patience, teaching/guidance, and regrets to avoid.
  • Open loops: fatherhood logistics and future family structure must become concrete if the marriage structure changes.
  • Next concrete move: protect Nolan-related tasks and keep fatherhood duty visible during relationship clarity work.

Goals

  • Long-term direction: Be a reliably present father who protects, provides, and instructs while serving as a spiritual and emotional leader to Nolan.
  • Be reliably present for Nolan.
  • Carry a substantial and visible share of care/logistical responsibility.
  • Preserve Nolan's routines, milestones, and emotional stability.
  • Teach, guide, play, and build trust through repeated presence.
  • Avoid the major regret: missing this stage of Nolan's development.

People

  • Nolan — Jenffry's son; primary person in this relationship type.
  • Aubrey — included here when co-parenting, practical support, or family logistics affect Nolan's stability.

Durable context

  • Nolan was about 9.5 months old as of 2026-04-14.
  • Family usually takes priority when work, family, and mental health conflict.
  • Family/home tasks have included baby-proofing and assembling Nolan's bike.
  • Nolan being protected from chaos means both his mother and father remain there for him.

Good normal week signals

A good normal week with Nolan tentatively includes:

  • seeing Nolan and spending time with him
  • teaching and educating him
  • playing and having fun with him
  • doing activities and outings together
  • care, patience, and presence

Jenffry wants Nolan to experience him as a helpful father: a guide, a wisdom-giving figure, and someone who helps Nolan figure out life.

House-in-order frame

“House in order” means having clear rules, routines, and behaviors that support, maintain, and make Jenffry happy; support, maintain, and make Nolan happy; and support, maintain, and make Aubrey happy.

For the fatherhood layer, self-respect comes from giving Nolan real presence and responsibility even if the partner/marriage outcome is hard.

Fatherhood guardrails

  • Fatherhood duty is not the same thing as marriage obligation.
  • Separation or divorce cannot become disappearing.
  • Resentment about the pregnancy/trust breach cannot become permission to emotionally check out, neglect fatherhood, or shift unfair care burden onto Aubrey.
  • Any transition plan must preserve Nolan's routines, milestones, and access to a present father.

Task routing

Fatherhood tasks belong in TickTick Relationships when they are concrete: birthday logistics, care routines, school/activities, specific outings, or follow-ups. Strategy and regrets-to-avoid stay in this page and clarity-sessions.

  • relationships
  • partner

raw/reference/life-area-goal-spine-pre-reduction-2026-06-08/areas__relationships__partner.md
degree 4

Status: Active partner/marriage clarity domain. Current priority: reach and act on the honest partner/marriage truth without collapsing it into fatherhood duty. Active signals: Aubrey context, attraction/desire truth, stay/leave/transition structure, truth-tel

Relationship Clarity SessionsRelationshipsFatherhoodMental Health

Partner

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active partner/marriage clarity domain.
  • Current priority: reach and act on the honest partner/marriage truth without collapsing it into fatherhood duty.
  • Active signals: Aubrey context, attraction/desire truth, stay/leave/transition structure, truth-telling ethics, and whether another attempt would be honest or guilt-driven.
  • Open loops: the next truthful statement/conversation path remains unresolved; avoid fake commitment and avoid resentment-driven action.
  • Next concrete move: draft the honest statement to Aubrey as a truth-testing exercise, not as a message to send yet.

Goals cascade

  1. Long-term direction — what life should become: Be in a loving happy relationship with a beautiful woman whose life goals and values are aligned with mine.
  2. 12-month proof — what would prove the direction is becoming real: The relationship structure is honestly chosen and acted on.
  3. 6-month checkpoint — midpoint evidence: The decision has been executed.
  4. Monthly SMART target — June: By 2026-06-30, make the partner/marriage decision and define the immediate execution path.
  5. Weekly SMART target: This week, complete one protected decision-making session that produces written output: decision, reasons, remaining doubts, and next action.
  6. Daily next move: Choose the time and format for the decision-making session, then write the first three bullet points of the honest statement.

Supporting goals

  • Tell the truth cleanly and compassionately.
  • Avoid offering a stay-attempt Jenffry does not actually believe in.
  • Minimize unnecessary harm to Aubrey.
  • Decide whether the partner/marriage structure can honestly continue, change, or transition.
  • Build any future relationship structure on informed consent rather than private sacrifice or hidden resentment.

People

  • Aubrey — Jenffry's wife and partner/marriage counterpart. Her dignity and informed consent matter; do not make private clarity into an imposed unilateral fiction.

Durable context

  • Relationship/family context involving Aubrey is sensitive, but not off-limits to Sapho when useful.
  • Relationship interpretations should not be treated as settled unless Jenffry states them clearly.
  • Jenffry currently has issues in the marriage and has been using clarity work to determine whether the marriage structure can honestly continue.
  • 2026-05-11 mental-health context: when Aubrey became pregnant, Jenffry did not want that outcome and felt he lost the life he wanted and was working toward. A current acceptance/commitment workstream is accepting that imagined life is gone and committing to the life he can have now, which can still be and already is good.

Current clarity signals

  • Jenffry cannot define a clear/stable relationship with Aubrey as a romantic partnership unless he knows what the relationship should become.
  • Acting kindly may mean trying everything reasonable, exhausting real options, doing the right thing, and knowing he really tried for real.
  • Not abandoning himself remains a key constraint. Jenffry suspects, but is not absolutely certain, that staying married may feel like abandoning himself.
  • Aubrey feeling naturally desired/attractive and Jenffry having genuine romantic desire are both central signals, not minor preferences.
  • Current evidence from clarity-sessions has moved the live question from “Is there any ethical way to stay?” toward “Would another stay-attempt be honest, or would it be a guilt-driven delay?”

Transition / decision frame

The 12-month desired state is not a preselected outcome. Success means Jenffry has achieved clarity, acted on it, and lives with the execution in the best and most organized way possible. For the partner/marriage layer, that means a clear and stable relationship structure with Aubrey based on whatever decision has been made and acted on, without staying in indefinite limbo.

The danger state is being 12 months later in the same unclear position.

Regrets to avoid in the partner layer

  • Spending life with someone he does not want to be with.
  • Spending life doing something he does not want to do.
  • Spending life not achieving what he wants to achieve, and not even trying to achieve those things.
  • Hurting Aubrey through fake commitment, avoidance, emotional withholding, or delayed truth.

Task routing

Partner/marriage tasks belong in TickTick Relationships only when they are concrete: written draft, scheduled conversation prep, therapist exercise output, or specific follow-up. Durable evidence stays in clarity-sessions. Durable partner context stays here.

  • relationships
  • clarity-sessions
  • fatherhood
  • mental-health

Status: Active social/friendship/community rebuilding domain. Current priority: rebuild friendships, invitations, adult peer connection, and being seen as himself without making social life an admin project. Active signals: friends, D&D/tabletop leads, meetups

LeisureRelationshipsMental Health

Social

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active social/friendship/community rebuilding domain.
  • Current priority: rebuild friendships, invitations, adult peer connection, and being seen as himself without making social life an admin project.
  • Active signals: friends, D&D/tabletop leads, meetups, film/social scenes, conversation confidence, invitations, and recurring contact.
  • Open loops: choose one concrete social experiment when useful; keep the rest as a low-clutter reservoir.
  • Next concrete move: route social-life notes here and move only chosen logistics to TickTick.

Goals

  • Long-term direction: Have close friends whom I see regularly and we have fun and do regular activities that I love.
  • Create regular adult peer contact.
  • Practice being seen as himself in real life.
  • Keep social experiments low-pressure and repeatable.
  • Avoid turning friendship into a giant planning system.

People

  • Friends / potential friends — existing and future peer relationships.
  • Community leads — D&D/tabletop groups, film/social groups, meetups, and recurring peer contexts.

Scope and routing

Use this page for:

  • friendships and regular social contact
  • invitations and social rebuilding
  • D&D/tabletop, meetups, film club, and similar activities when the main value is people/contact
  • conversation confidence and being seen as himself in real life

Social plans with friends belong under Relationships. If an activity is mainly play/restoration with no meaningful people/contact layer, keep it under leisure. If it is both, put the relational thread here and the hobby/outing logistics in leisure only when useful.

Task routing

Social tasks belong in TickTick Relationships only when they become a concrete chosen action: RSVP, message someone, attend a specific event, or schedule an outing. Ideas stay in the pipeline below.

Social pipeline

Low-clutter pool for future social planning. TickTick should only get the concrete next action or chosen event.

  • TPK Miami / Total Party Kill: adult/social D&D pop-up format, including one-shots, ongoing campaigns, and new campaigns. Good fit if Jenffry wants an adult peer/social scene and a low-pressure table that allows no experience. Example found: DUNGEON & DRAGONS at The Corner Miami, 1035 N Miami Ave, Miami, Monday May 25, 2026, 6:30 PM onward, $10. Source: https://www.thecornermiami.com/events/dungeon-dragons
  • Dungeons and Dragons Miami Meetup: recurring Thursday 7 PM D&D nights in Doral at Wizards of Ecom, 8260 NW 27th St #408. Listed as free and welcoming to new players. Source: https://www.meetup.com/dungeons-dragons-miami/
  • Facebook group route: join/search South Florida D&D groups for beginner-friendly weekend one-shots or open tables. Best query/post: “new/beginner-friendly player looking for a Saturday/Sunday one-shot or casual table near Miami.”
  • Film club / repertory movie rhythm: find a low-pressure recurring movie/social scene.

Open Loops

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Active unresolved-item register.
  • Current priority: keep unresolved questions visible without turning every idea into a task.
  • Active signals: pending decisions, unfinished setup, future follow-ups.
  • Open loops: see source sections below; Sapho 2.0 upgrades are tracked in sapho-upgrade-roadmap.

Migrated source: concepts/open-loops.md

Open Loops

This is the living page for "what open loops are still unresolved?"

Open loops

Solidify Jenffry's goals layer

Status: open — first pass complete; execution/refinement remains

Question: What are Jenffry's current goals, and how should they cascade from long-term direction to 12-month outcomes, 6-month goals, monthly targets, weekly commitments, and concrete TickTick tasks?

Why it matters: Sapho's core roles are strategic advisor and task manager. Those roles require a reliable model of Jenffry's goals, capabilities, limitations, and context. The current goal information is incomplete, which weakens advice and task management.

Status update: 2026-05-10 voice intake captured the first 12-month goals layer by life area. 2026-05-11 voice intake refined the mental-health goal into mental-health, completed first-pass definitions across the earlier eight-domain draft in current-strategy, and added a Hevy-based physical-health baseline/target page. 2026-05-18 restructure collapsed the goals layer into the final six life areas: Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, and Maintenance.

Suggested next action: Treat broad goal definition as good enough for this pass. Next work is execution and calibration: monthly targets, weekly commitments, TickTick alignment, and lightweight progress signals without creating admin soup.

Create a bucket list

Status: open

Question: What experiences, trips, hobbies, milestones, family memories, social adventures, and personal achievements does Jenffry want to make room for over the next several years?

Why it matters: A bucket list can strengthen the Leisure and future-excitement layer without turning everything into productivity. It should help identify what is actually worth protecting time, money, and planning energy for.

Suggested next action: Do a first-pass capture session: collect ideas without filtering, then sort them into near-term, someday, family, solo, social, travel, hobby/skill, and achievement-style items. Only turn specific chosen items into TickTick tasks when they are ready to become concrete plans.

Scenario exercise for next relationship clarity session

Status: open

Question: How should the next relationship clarity session use the therapist-guided four-scenario exercise?

Status update: The therapist gave a concrete exercise for the next relationship clarity session. Jenffry should create four scenarios: selfish while staying in the marriage, selfless while staying in the marriage, selfish while leaving the marriage/not living in it, and selfless while leaving the marriage/not living in it.

Suggested next action: Use the scenario set in the next relationship clarity session to make tradeoffs visible instead of keeping the question abstract.

Complete Sapho personality profile

Status: partly complete — first personality profile ingested on 2026-05-10

Question: What additional persona details should shape Sapho's tone, judgment, boundaries, and working style?

Status update: Jenffry sent a Mentat personality profile. Sapho preserved the raw document, updated /opt/data/SOUL.md, created sapho-operating-system, and recorded the applied model: disciplined cognition under pressure, not emotionless-machine behavior; ethical loyalty; dynamic pattern tracking; awareness of emotional bias.

Suggested next action: Keep this loop open only for additional Jenffry-specific Sapho behavior preferences, not for more Dune lore unless it changes actual operating behavior.

Continue learning Jenffry's operating profile

Status: open — ongoing

Question: What information about Jenffry's capabilities, limitations, constraints, preferences, energy patterns, relationships, work context, and decision patterns would make Sapho a better strategic advisor and task manager?

Suggested next action: Keep a standing, low-clutter intake loop. Save durable signal when it improves future advice or execution; avoid saving noisy one-off details.

Capture January mental-health incident context

Status: partly open — sensitive/foundational

Question: What happened during the January weed-related mental-health incident, and what context does Sapho need to better support Jenffry's mental-health planning and conversations?

Status update: 2026-05-11 intake captured the current meaning for goals work: Jenffry is afraid it can happen again, is working on accepting that recurrence is possible, and wants to commit that if it happens again he can work through it because he survived it once.

Suggested next action: If/when useful, capture fuller incident context, warning signs, helpful responses, unhelpful responses, and escalation boundaries. Sapho should preserve useful context without treating itself as a therapist or medical authority.

Define concrete 12-month goal metrics

Status: open — first pass complete; calibration remains

Question: What concrete measures define the 12-month goals so they are not fuzzy?

Status update: first-pass observable 12-month markers now exist across the final six life areas in current-strategy. Health contains mental-health and physical-health markers; Relationships contains partner / marriage and fatherhood practice-layer structures; Finances contains budget and investing; Career contains work and business; Leisure contains social, hobbies, and outings; Maintenance contains concrete admin/immigration follow-through.

Suggested next action: Convert the first-pass goals into monthly/weekly progress signals without tracking bloat. Recalibrate physical-health targets after 4–6 weeks of Hevy data on the Sapho plan; refine finance comfort, Princess proof projects, career/business experiments, work-relationship target, and mental-health support boundaries during reviews.

Prepare Sapho for mental-health support role

Status: open — foundational

Question: What should Sapho know about Jenffry's mental-health issues, warning signs, helpful responses, unhelpful responses, boundaries, and escalation rules?

Status update: 2026-05-11 intake captured observer mode, acceptance/commitment toward current life, and fear/acceptance around possible recurrence of the late-January weed incident.

Suggested next action: Build a Sapho mental-health support profile that complements therapy and supports planning/clarity, without replacing professional care. Next useful details: what responses help during observer mode, what responses worsen rumination, and what escalation rules should apply if Jenffry feels unsafe.

Strategic review: execution layer versus strategic layer

Status: open

Question: Is the current TickTick execution layer genuinely supporting the strategic layer, or has it become admin soup?

Status update: Stage A executed on 2026-05-10. The Princess UX + AI workflow cluster was moved to 🗄Archive; the phone-safe SSH task was intentionally left long because Jenffry wants the self-service details in TickTick; the broad Become a US Citizen task was moved to 🗄Archive; and home/family quick wins should be batched in evening blocks.

Suggested next action: Continue protecting the execution layer against admin soup during weekly reviews. Future cleanup should focus on newly accumulated abstract/system-building tasks, broad goals without next actions, and excessive household quick wins outside evening batches.

Immigration and citizenship path

Status: open — USCIS replied; next request due in about 2 months

Question: What is actionable for Jenffry's immigration case after more than two years pending, and what is the longer citizenship path after that?

Current 12-month definition: green card if the process allows it; otherwise no passive waiting — confirm case status, normal processing time, documents/notices, escalation options, and take the reasonable next action available.

Status update: USCIS replied by email. Jenffry now needs to submit another request in about 2 months rather than waiting on the old follow-up window.

Suggested next action: Keep the broad citizenship goal in life-admin, not as an active TickTick container task. Preserve the email reply and set the next USCIS request for about 2 months out. If a non-delay issue appears — lost document, USCIS error, missed notice, approved expedite stuck, or urgent humanitarian issue — consider earlier Ombudsman/attorney/congressional escalation.

Define Sapho permissions and recurring templates

Status: partly closed on permissions; templates still open

Permission decision: Jenffry wants Sapho to have full permissions by default. Permissions are not the blocker. Sapho should act with disciplined judgment, especially around broad/destructive/sensitive actions, but should not treat lack of permission as a reason for passivity.

Template calibration rule: every recurring check-in should include a brief question asking how to improve that check-in. Use the answers to patch skills/templates and reduce noise over time.

Suggested next action: Continue calibrating output templates from real check-ins. Morning check-in is now Monday-Saturday at 5:30 AM ET; Sunday 5:30 AM ET is a combined weekly review, Sunday morning check-in, and weekend/social plan. All user-facing recurring check-ins should include a self-improvement question, tracking questions, and progress review at the right scale: light daily signal, closeout slip/move signal, weekly review, monthly strategic review. Weekly planning must cover every day of the coming week, including weekend, while using goal-oriented suggestions, pushback, and anti-overstacking guards. Monthly review must include a long-term planning session before setting monthly targets. Current weekly-review template should use clearer top-level sections; make Gym review and Financial review distinct major sections; ignore normal 9–5 Princess UX/UI work in personal weekly planning; and use one big important task plus one quick win per day as the default daily load rule. The continuity memory audit should stay background/local-only unless a critical issue needs direct attention.

Sapho operating-system improvement review

Status: active — first lean/bloat pass completed 2026-05-17

Question: Is Sapho's layer above the code — persona, memory, wiki, skills, scheduled check-ins, TickTick hygiene, and review cadence — reducing fog and increasing agency, or creating its own admin load?

Current assessment: The architecture is strong and now has a lean-governance layer. The first bloat pass found the main risk in the skills reference layer and recurring cron prompt length, not in the wiki itself. Sapho consolidated overlapping TickTick review-calibration references into ticktick-task-management/references/review-system-governance-2026-05-17.md, kept the canonical review-templates-2026-05-17.md, removed absorbed reference files, shortened user-facing review cron prompts to point at canonical templates/governance, removed the paused duplicate weekend/social cron job, and created weekly local-only lean audit job 292f4883ad21.

Suggested next action: Let the weekly lean audit run quietly. It should only compress or remove when durable context is preserved and the change is likely to improve token usage, response speed, prompt size, or recurring-output clarity. Continue staged TickTick overload cleanup separately; do not let lean governance become another user-facing admin process.

Build Sapho workspace blueprint / internal-systems dashboard

Status: active — full wiki browser added to the hosted dashboard; backup/archive mirror still undecided

Question: How should Sapho make the continuity/wiki/skills/cron/TickTick system legible as a folder/workspace-style map, so Jenffry can see the whole operating system without relying on hidden context?

Status update: 2026-05-18 comparison against the folder/workspace video found strong alignment in philosophy — markdown continuity, separated context, reusable skills, and routing AI to the right files — but a clear weakness in Sapho's legibility. Current routing is split across wiki pages, skills, cron prompts, memory, scripts, and TickTick. The missing layer is an at-a-glance workspace blueprint or dashboard showing life-area rooms, raw capture containers, review cadences, routing rules, and system health.

Status update: Initial blueprint page now exists as sapho-operating-system with room map, routing rules, and weekly review touchpoints.

Status update: A concrete implementation page now exists as dashboard to guide the first web dashboard build.

Status update: The hosted dashboard now renders the full live wiki with a graph view and page browser. The remaining question is whether to add the backup/archive mirror into the main view or keep the graph focused on the live wiki only.

Suggested next action: Decide whether to add a backup/archive toggle or keep the live wiki graph uncluttered.

Review gpt-5.4-mini quality for background continuity/system audits

Status: open — monitor after next runs

Question: Does running the weekly continuity memory audit and weekly Sapho lean-system audit on openai-codex / gpt-5.4-mini preserve enough judgment quality, or does it create missed context, shallow recommendations, bad pruning, or operating-system drift?

Status update: 2026-05-17 model-routing decision set both jobs to gpt-5.4-mini to preserve gpt-5.5 capacity while keeping important background audits inside the Codex/OpenAI family. This is a deliberate trial, not a final settled routing rule.

Suggested next action: After the next 1–2 runs, inspect the local cron outputs for quality: whether memory recommendations are conservative and useful, whether lean-system audit avoids bloat and false optimizations, and whether either job should move back to gpt-5.5 or to DeepSeek V4 Pro.

Formalize Sapho model-routing policy matrix

Status: open — return later

Question: Should Sapho create an explicit model-routing matrix before considering any custom auto-router, covering job/session type, default model, allowed fallback, forbidden downgrade, review cadence, and reason?

Status update: 2026-05-17 discussion concluded that the first step should be a visible policy-based routing matrix rather than jumping to OpenRouter auto-router or a custom live model router. Candidate rows include live mental health/relationship/family, monthly goals/full weekly review, evening closeout, memory/lean audits, mechanical status checks, and unknown/default routing.

Suggested next action: Build the matrix, audit all cron jobs against it, and only then decide whether to add a cron-only policy enforcer or a true live message router.

Configure OpenRouter fallback provider

Status: closed on 2026-05-16

Question: Should Sapho use OpenRouter as a backup provider when the primary OpenAI Codex route is unavailable/rate-limited?

Result: OpenRouter fallback and direct DeepSeek auxiliary models are configured. /opt/data/.env contains both OPENROUTER_API_KEY and DEEPSEEK_API_KEY without exposing values; /opt/data/config.yaml has fallback_providers set to OpenRouter using deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro; direct DeepSeek auxiliary compression, session-search, and title-generation use deepseek-v4-pro. Live tests returned OPENROUTER_DEEPSEEK_V4_PRO_OK for OpenRouter and DIRECT_DEEPSEEK_V4_PRO_OK for direct DeepSeek; the gateway is running after the configured state was present, so Telegram sessions should have the updated model routing available.

Pricing decision: DeepSeek direct V4 Pro and OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro show the same headline discounted prices for cache-miss input and output ($0.435/M input, $0.87/M output), so Jenffry's rule selects OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro as the fallback. Direct DeepSeek exposes an explicit cache-hit discount, so direct remains useful for auxiliary summarization/compression where already configured.

Suggested next action: Monitor the next real OpenAI Codex rate-limit event. If fallback behavior does not trigger as expected, inspect gateway logs for provider routing/fallback errors and adjust the fallback model list.

Move goal structure out of TickTick if it creates bloat

Status: closed on 2026-05-09

Result: Jenffry approved archiving the 15 planning records from the 🎯Goals TickTick list, moving Finance review to 💰Finances, moving Relationship/fatherhood clarity session to 🏋‍♂Health as the mental-health/clarity home, and deleting the empty 🎯Goals TickTick container. Long-term and 6-month goal structure now belongs in the wiki/check-ins, not TickTick.

Relationship/fatherhood clarity

Status: open — practice layer defined

Question: What observations, questions, scenarios, possible futures, boundaries, regrets to avoid, and next actions would create real clarity without circular analysis?

Status update: 2026-05-11 intake clarified that the 12-month goal cannot be fully defined in advance. Success means achieving clarity and being comfortable with it, whatever the final family/relationship structure is. The practice layer is written clarity sessions: answer prompts, play out futures, test what acting kindly/not abandoning himself means, identify regrets to avoid, protect Nolan from chaos, and choose one next observation/action.

Suggested next action: Complete the TickTick Relationship/fatherhood clarity session; use the new weekly Sapho clarity prompt to make the work recurring without turning the goal structure into TickTick bloat.

Body system

Status: open — first-pass physical-health targets defined

Question: What practical body system will make better weeks easier across sleep, food, workouts, grooming, energy, and medical follow-up?

Status update: 2026-05-11 physical-health target pass used Hevy measurements and recent-current lift anchors to create physical-health. Current baseline: 6'4", 35 years old, 215.6 lb, waist 37.0 in, abdomen 39.0 in. First-pass targets: roughly 205–215 lb with lower waist/abdomen and maintained/growing muscle, waist about 34.5–36.0 in, abdomen about 36.5–38.0 in, 20% body fat if reliably measurable, run 20 minutes at 6 mph, bench 250 lb estimated 1RM or comparable working-set proof, and progressive push/pull/leg targets.

Suggested next action: Use Hevy as source of truth. For the current week, target 5 gym sessions unless pickleball substitutes for one slot; minimum successful week remains 3 lifting sessions, with a posture/cardio day as the strong-week gap. Recalibrate after 4–6 weeks of Sapho-plan data; still add mobility and pain/ache baseline if useful.

Update workout routines, especially cardio days

Status: open

Question: How should Jenffry's workout routines change so cardio days are clearer, more usable, and better aligned with the current health/body goals?

Why it matters: The routine needs a cleaner structure so cardio days do not stay vague or get crowded out by lifting-only thinking.

Suggested next action: Review the current workout routine and decide what the cardio days should actually contain, how often they should happen, and how they fit around lifting, pickleball, and recovery.

Testosterone and lipid follow-up

Status: open

Question: What are the cardiologist/endocrinologist next steps, and what testosterone plan makes sense with medical input?

Suggested next action: Prepare questions for medical appointments; do not treat AI as medical authority.

Business/career direction

Status: open — success buckets drafted

Question: What concrete experiments will help choose the next business/career direction instead of endlessly comparing imagined futures?

Status update: 2026-05-11 goals-layer pass set this area to follow a Princess-like bucket structure: concrete experiments, work leverage, business-development option value, visible proof/assets, and bandwidth/mental-health fit. Blaze remains attractive but inactive unless experiments show demand, capability, and bandwidth.

Suggested next action: Choose 1–3 small experiments or proof assets related to AI leverage, senior UX growth, leadership, and possible business directions using career.

Princess UX/UI work organization

Status: open — success buckets defined

Question: What structure will let Sapho help Jenffry organize Princess UX/UI work without duplicating Jira or creating TickTick admin soup, while supporting senior UX growth?

Status update: 2026-05-11 goals-layer pass defined five Princess UX/career success buckets: work quality, execution, influence, portfolio/visible proof, and AI/work leverage. The next need is selecting 2–3 concrete proof projects/case-study candidates and a weekly practice/review cadence.

Status update: 2026-05-14 operating-system cleanup moved the overdue Princess UX/UI dated pile out of May 13 and spread it across Fri May 15, Mon May 18, and Tue May 19. No Princess tasks were deleted or archived; this was a reversible overload-clearing pass. Remaining issue: confirm which items are already done, blocked, should move to Jira, or should be collapsed/archived after Jenffry reviews real work status.

Suggested next action: Use princess-ux as the context layer. Keep Jira as the work source of truth, TickTick as dated execution blocks, and Sapho/wiki for synthesis. First clarify the official Jira structure and highest-stakes Princess clusters, then organize work into clarify/design/review/handoff/systemize buckets. Treat AI workflow as a bounded real-work experiment, not an abstract project.

Finance stability

Status: open — income-based budget created; verification and execution remain

Question: Can the household budget stay stable for a clean month without tax refund or one-off income distortion, without credit-card interest restarting, and with enough monthly excess/enjoyment money to stop feeling purely survival-based?

Status update: 2026-05-11 finance goal pass clarified that savings are strong: about $36k emergency fund and over $500k in 401k. The real issue is monthly budget comfort and cashflow.

Status update: 2026-05-14 transaction review rebuilt the budget using Jenffry's corrected income: $2,882.97 biweekly net, about $6,246.44/month. The new working budget excludes tax refunds/one-offs and shows the core constraint: JPMorgan + TD debt/loan assumptions consume about $3,962.98/month, leaving about $2,283.46/month or about $525.54/week for non-debt spending. See finances.

Suggested next action: Continue weekly Finance review; compare spending against the income-based caps, keep May controlled, verify whether Honda truly stopped after the April payoff-sized payment, and refine the path toward ~$400/month comfortable excess.

Social rebuild

Status: open — 12-month markers partly defined

Question: How can Jenffry protect friends, hobbies, weekends, and future excitement so leisure restores agency rather than becoming avoidance?

Status update: 2026-05-11 leisure goals set target markers: about 6 meaningful friends/regular social contacts; 2 social outings, 1 family outing, and 1 solo hobby thing per month; easier conversations, more invitations, less avoidance, and more confidence initiating; pickleball once every 1–2 weeks; windsurfing mastery means achieving planing; future excitement comes from hanging out with friends and doing new things.

Suggested next action: Use leisure and the weekly weekend/social planning prompt to create one concrete leisure activity at a time. TickTick should get only concrete outcomes, not recurring planning clutter.

Email inbox integration for morning check-ins

Status: restored on 2026-05-17 — monitor only

Result so far: Google Workspace OAuth was connected on 2026-05-08 for Jenffry's Google account with Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Contacts scopes. Gmail search and Calendar listing were verified, and the daily morning check-in job includes inbox and calendar attention.

Temporary issue: during the early 2026-05-17 morning check-in, Google Workspace auth returned TOKEN_REVOKED / invalid_grant: Token has been expired or revoked. Later the same morning, running the Google Workspace commands with the known dependency prefix (PYTHONPATH=/opt/data/google-python-deps) showed AUTHENTICATED, and Gmail unread search plus Calendar listing worked again.

Suggested next action: No re-auth needed unless the failure recurs. If a future check-in reports Google failure, first retry with the dependency prefix before assuming OAuth is revoked.

Define what belongs in the wiki

Status: open

Question: What exact kinds of personal/work information should Hermes save into the wiki versus short-term chat or built-in memory?

Suggested next action: As real conversations happen, save only things that improve continuity, clarity, or action.

Verify self-improving skill notes loop

Status: closed on 2026-05-17 — inspected

Question: Is Hermes Agent's self-improving skill loop actually capturing useful notes from repeated review/check-in runs and turning them into better skills/templates, or is Sapho only relying on memory/habit?

Finding: The loop is working in-turn. Recent check-in/review feedback produced concrete updates to governing skills, reusable templates, cron prompts, wiki decisions/open loops/logs, and concise reference files. Evidence includes same-day patches to jenffry-continuity, ticktick-task-management, personal-finance-budgeting, health-and-fitness-planning, and weekend-social-planning, plus the reusable review templates and tracking dashboard calibration.

Weakness: The loop is not yet fully autonomous or lean. It depends on Sapho recognizing reusable corrections during the turn, and the growing number of reference files/prompts could itself become bloat.

Result: Original inspection loop closed. Carry forward the governance problem into the Sapho-system bloat/lean review: keep the improvement loop, but audit whether skill/reference/template additions should be merged, shortened, or pruned.

Build the habit of updating memory

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: Replaced reliance on habit with a scheduled weekly continuity memory audit cron job.

TickTick integration

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: TickTick OAuth credentials were stored in /opt/data/.env, authorization completed with tasks:read tasks:write, project listing was verified, and write access was verified by creating and completing a harmless test task in 🛠Maintenance. Sapho can now read and write TickTick tasks through Telegram.

Closed loops

Intake Jenffry's initial info dump

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: Raw source saved and synthesized into the wiki's core continuity pages plus new topic pages.

Create the continuity operating skill

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: Created custom Hermes skill jenffry-continuity to guide when and how to use the wiki for ongoing memory.

Set up the wiki structure

Status: closed on 2026-05-08

Result: Wiki created at /opt/data/wiki with starter folders and rule files.

This file preserves the pre-cleanup physical-health page exactly as found before consolidating the canonical active page. The canonical current source is /opt/data/wiki/areas/health/physical-health.md.

---
title: Physical Health
type: wiki-page
status: active
updated: 2026-05-24
---

# Physical Health

## Dashboard summary

- Status: Active support system for mental health, capacity, body composition, activity rhythm, and strength.
- Current priority: gym consistency, body baseline, weight/measurement trend, strength-goal progress, sleep, food, medical/lab follow-up.
- Active signals: Hevy 7-day/30-day workouts and sets, pickleball/activity substitution, weight, waist, abdomen, key lift anchors, current strength baseline, 1-year strength ladder, sleep quality, energy, testosterone/lipids follow-up.
- Open loops: update weight/measurements monthly; keep the strength ladder aligned with Hevy anchors; avoid TickTick workout clutter.
- Next concrete move: keep Hevy as physical source of truth and review body/strength trends weekly/monthly against the numeric cascade below, including the current strength baseline, 3-month, 6-month, yearly, and long-term checkpoints, not as daily admin.

Physical health contains gym, sleep, food, body maintenance, grooming, medical follow-up, and physical capacity.

## Migrated source: `concepts/physical-health.md`

# Physical Health

Updated: 2026-05-17

## Role in the goals system

Physical health is Jenffry's second priority layer after mental health. The 12-month goal is not just a lower scale number; it is a stronger, leaner, more capable body with medical loops closed and a gym rhythm that supports mental health instead of becoming admin clutter.

## Current baseline from Hevy — 2026-05-11

Profile context:

- Age: 35
- Height: 6'4"
- Current weight: 97.8 kg / 215.6 lb
- Baseline weight for dashboard progress: 220 lb
- BMI: about 26.2 — useful only as a rough population marker, not a body-composition diagnosis.

Current measurements:

- Neck: 16.3 in
- Shoulders: 25.4 in
- Chest: 45.4 in
- Left/right bicep: 14.3 / 14.5 in
- Left/right forearm: 11.8 / 11.8 in
- Abdomen: 39 in
- Waist: 37 in
- Body fat estimate: 26%
- Hips: 44.1 in
- Left/right thigh: 26.5 / 27.3 in
- Left/right calf: 15.7 / 15.9 in

## Current strength baseline

| Pattern | Main indicator | Current baseline | Prior best in file | Status |
|---|---|---:|---:|---|
| Push | Bench Press, Barbell | 165 lb | 185 lb | Below your past best; first goal is to regain 185 |
| Vertical pull | Lat Pulldown | 150 lb | 155 lb | Very close to your prior best |
| Horizontal pull | Iso-Lateral Low Row / Row | 160 lb | 180 lb | Solid, but below prior best |
| Lower body | Leg Press | 450 lb | 450 lb | Current best; this is your strongest recent lift |

Recent-current strength anchors from Hevy:

- Bench press: baseline 135 x 10; current best from Hevy uses `Bench Press (Barbell)`; goal 205 x 5.
- Shoulder press dumbbell: baseline 50 x 10; current best from Hevy uses `Shoulder Press (Dumbbell)`; goal 100 x 10.
- Triceps pushdown: baseline 60 x 10; current best from Hevy uses `Triceps Pushdown`; goal 120 x 10.
- Pull up: baseline 6; current best from Hevy uses `Pull Up`, not `Assisted Pull-up`; goal 12 unassisted pull-ups across 3–5 sets.
- Bicep curl: baseline 60 x 10; current best from Hevy uses `Bicep Curl (Barbell)`; goal 95 x 10.
- Leg press: baseline 360 x 10; current best from Hevy uses `Leg Press (Machine)`; goal 550 x 8.
- Romanian deadlift: baseline 135 x 6; current best from Hevy uses `Romanian Deadlift (Barbell)`; goal 225 x 5.
- Treadmill: baseline 1.8 mph; current best from Hevy uses `Treadmill`; goal 6.0 mph. Hevy stores this as distance in meters plus duration in seconds, and the dashboard converts it to mph.

## Strength tracking exercises

These are the exercises the dashboard should use in the strength tracking section.

- Bench press — 205 x 5
- Shoulder press dumbbell — 100 x 10
- Triceps pushdown — 120 x 10
- Pull up — 12 unassisted pull-ups across 3–5 sets
- Bicep curl — 95 x 10
- Leg press — 550 x 8
- Romanian deadlift — 225 x 5
- Running / treadmill — 6.0 mph

Hevy mapping notes:

- Pull up should use `Pull Up`, not `Assisted Pull-up`.
- Romanian deadlift should use `Romanian Deadlift (Barbell)`.
- Running should use `Treadmill`; Hevy records this as `distance_meters` plus `duration_seconds`, and the dashboard should display the converted speed in mph.

## Data cautions:

- Some older Hevy entries appear unsuitable for target-setting, especially obvious outliers such as a Smith-machine bench entry around 1140 lb. Use recent-current anchors, not all-time raw PRs, for goal extrapolation.
- Body-fat percentage is not directly measured in Hevy yet. Use waist/abdomen trend, photos, strength, and weight as observable proxies unless a consistent body-fat measurement method is added.

## Physical-health goal cascade

### Good 1-year strength goals

These are based on the current baseline, previous peaks, and the body recomposition goal from before: roughly **205–212 lb body weight**, **waist around 34.5–35.5 in**, **abdomen around 35.5–36.5 in**.

| Main lift | Current 12-month good goal | Stretch goal |
|---|---:|---:|
| Bench Press, Barbell | 205 lb | 225 lb |
| Lat Pulldown | 175–180 lb | 190–200 lb |
| Row | 190–200 lb | 210–220 lb |
| Leg Press | 525–550 lb | 600 lb |

### Best 1-year target

Best realistic target:

- Body: around **208 lb**, waist **~35 in**, abdomen **~36 in**
- Strength: bench **205**, pulldown **180**, row **200**, leg press **550**

That would mean you are not just lighter — you are leaner and stronger.

### Milestones

| Time | Bench | Pulldown | Row | Leg Press |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 3 months | 180–185 | 160–165 | 170–180 | 475–500 |
| 6 months | 190–195 | 165–170 | 180–185 | 500–525 |
| 9 months | 200 | 170–175 | 190 | 525–540 |
| 12 months | 205 | 175–180 | 190–200 | 525–550 |

### What to focus on first

- Bench is the main lift to rebuild. You have hit **185 lb** many times before, but the recent May 2026 barbell bench entries are back at **165 lb**, so **185** should be the first checkpoint before chasing **205+**.
- Pulling strength looks balanced but could improve. A good target is getting both pulldown and row close to body-weight territory over time, with row especially moving back past the prior **180 lb** level.
- Leg press is already far ahead of the rest, so the goal should not just be “more weight.” Only count progress if depth, foot position, tempo, and range of motion stay consistent. A clean **550 lb** leg press is a better goal than a sloppy **650**.

### Simple rule for the year

For each main lift, aim to add about **5 lb every 4–6 weeks** on upper-body lifts and **10–20 lb every 4–6 weeks** on leg press, only when form stays clean.

### Existing body-composition goal cascade

### Long-term goal

**By 2028-05-22, I will have a lean, muscular, healthy build with lower abdominal fat and maintained or increased muscle.**

Long-term targets:

- Weight: **203 lb**
- Waist: **34.0 in**
- Abdomen: **35.0 in**
- Body fat estimate: **15%**
- Chest: **46.5 in**
- Shoulders: **26.5 in**
- Neck: **16.1 in**
- Hips: **44.0 in**
- Left bicep: **15.5 in**
- Right bicep: **15.5 in**
- Left forearm: **12.1 in**
- Right forearm: **12.1 in**
- Left thigh: **27.5 in**
- Right thigh: **28.0 in**
- Left calf: **16.4 in**
- Right calf: **16.4 in**
- Bench press: **225 x 5**
- Run: **20 minutes at 6 mph**
- Total testosterone: **500**

### 1-year goal

**By 2027-05-22, I will have completed a visible recomposition: less abdominal fat, stable or larger muscle measurements, and better training performance.**

1-year targets:

- Weight: **208 lb**
- Waist: **35.0 in**
- Abdomen: **36.0 in**
- Body fat estimate: **17%**
- Chest: **46.0 in**
- Shoulders: **26.0 in**
- Neck: **16.2 in**
- Hips: **44.0 in**
- Left bicep: **15.2 in**
- Right bicep: **15.2 in**
- Left forearm: **12.0 in**
- Right forearm: **12.0 in**
- Left thigh: **27.0 in**
- Right thigh: **27.5 in**
- Left calf: **16.2 in**
- Right calf: **16.2 in**
- Bench press: **205 x 5**
- Run: **20 minutes at 6 mph**
- Total testosterone: **400**

### 6-month goal

**By 2026-11-22, I will be clearly moving toward the 1-year recomposition target.**

6-month targets:

- Weight: **211.8 lb**
- Waist: **36.0 in**
- Abdomen: **37.5 in**
- Body fat estimate: **19%**
- Chest: **45.7 in**
- Shoulders: **25.7 in**
- Neck: **16.2 in**
- Hips: **44.1 in**
- Left bicep: **14.8 in**
- Right bicep: **14.8 in**
- Left forearm: **11.9 in**
- Right forearm: **11.9 in**
- Left thigh: **26.8 in**
- Right thigh: **27.4 in**
- Left calf: **16.0 in**
- Right calf: **16.1 in**
- Bench press: **195 x 5**
- Run: **15 minutes at 6 mph**
- Total testosterone: **350**

### 3-month goal

**By 2026-08-22, I will have started the recomposition trend and proved consistency.**

3-month targets:

- Weight: **213.7 lb**
- Waist: **36.5 in**
- Abdomen: **38.2 in**
- Body fat estimate: **20%**
- Chest: **45.5 in**
- Shoulders: **25.5 in**
- Neck: **16.3 in**
- Hips: **44.1 in**
- Left bicep: **14.5 in**
- Right bicep: **14.7 in**
- Left forearm: **11.9 in**
- Right forearm: **11.9 in**
- Left thigh: **26.6 in**
- Right thigh: **27.4 in**
- Left calf: **15.9 in**
- Right calf: **16.0 in**

## Practice layer

- Weekly: Hevy review in weekly check-in, not daily check-ins.
- Monthly: update weight/waist/abdomen/chest/arms/thighs/calves in Hevy and review trend against strength performance.
- Quarterly: reassess whether the 12-month strength targets are too aggressive, too easy, or causing pain/recovery problems.

## Weekly gym review — 2026-05-17

Hevy source-of-truth review for the first week of the Sapho 5-day routine:

- Last 7 days: **4 workouts**, **61 working sets**, average **48 minutes**.
- Routine coverage: Day 1 Push + Sauna, Day 2 Posture Cardio A, Day 3 Pull + Sauna, Day 5 Lower + Core.
- Classification: **strong week** — Push/Pull/Lower covered plus one posture/cardio day. Not yet ideal because Day 4 Posture Cardio B was missed.
- 30-day context: **9 workouts**, **112 working sets**, average **46.6 minutes**; this supports the minimum 3 sessions/week rhythm and shows the new routine is already increasing structure.
- Key anchors from the week: bench 165 x 9; incline DB press 45s x 10; shoulder press 35s x 10; chest fly 145 x 10; barbell curl 80 x 8; seated cable row 145 x 10; pull-ups 8/6/6 bodyweight if logged correctly; leg press 450 x 6; calf extension 250 x 10.
- Caution: lower-body numbers, especially leg press, should be confirmed against same machine/form before treating them as target-surpassing progress.
- Next-week target: repeat a **strong week minimum** — Push, Pull, Lower, and one posture/cardio day. Stretch target: complete all five Sapho days, especially Day 4 Posture Cardio B.

## Next refinement

- Add progress photos or a consistent body-fat estimate only if it does not create anxiety/admin clutter.
- Confirm whether all measurements are taken in a consistent way: morning, relaxed posture, same tape positions.
- Decide whether the 5-day Sapho plan is realistic after 4–6 weeks of data.

## Migrated source: `concepts/health-routines.md`

# Health Routines

This page tracks Jenffry's body-system focus: the practical health routines and medical follow-ups that make better weeks easier.

## Current body-system focus

- Sleep
- Food
- Workouts
- Grooming
- Energy
- Body maintenance
- Lower-friction weeks

## Active health goals

- Raise testosterone.
- Manage mildly elevated lipids.
- Follow up with doctor on lifestyle vs. treatment.

## Recent lab baseline from 2026-04-24

- Total cholesterol: 203
- LDL: 124
- Non-HDL: 139
- A1c: 5.3
- Total testosterone: 290, low-normal
- Glucose normal
- Kidney/liver/thyroid normal
- CBC normal
- Urinalysis normal
- HIV/RPR normal

## 6-month body goals

- Go to the gym 5 days/week for 8 total weeks.
- Average 7+ hours of sleep on 5 nights/week for 8 weeks.
- Complete cardiologist and endocrinologist next steps.
- Clarify testosterone plan with medical input.

## 12-month physical-health direction

Detailed 2026-05-11 Hevy baseline and target set now lives in [[physical-health]]. Summary:

- Close active physical-health loops: testosterone plan, cardiologist follow-up, wisdom-teeth follow-up, and other medical follow-ups.
- Build a steady gym routine with visible 12-month results: lower body fat, more muscle, better mobility, fewer aches/pains, and stronger cardio capacity.
- Current baseline: 6'4", 35 years old, 215.6 lb, waist 37.0 in, abdomen 39.0 in.
- 12-month body-composition target: roughly 205–215 lb with lower waist/abdomen and maintained/growing strength; waist about 34.5–36.0 in; abdomen about 36.5–38.0 in; body fat about 20% if reliably measurable.
- 12-month performance targets: run 20 minutes at 6 mph; bench 250 lb estimated 1RM or comparable working-set proof; progress major push/pull/leg lifts as listed in [[physical-health]].
- Hevy remains the gym source of truth; weekly check-ins review Hevy progress, not daily TickTick workout clutter.

## Current weekly workout commitment

- Week of 2026-05-10: Jenffry intends to go to the gym 5 times unless he plays pickleball, in which case pickleball can substitute for one workout slot.
- Hevy remains the gym source of truth; review progress during the weekly check-in, not the daily check-in.

## Notes for Sapho

Do not give medical instructions as if replacing a doctor. Treat this page as continuity context and help Jenffry prepare questions, track goals, and follow through with medical input.

degree 0

This wiki stores structured continuity for Jenffry and Sapho: current strategy, life-area context, decisions, open loops, dashboard-facing summaries, and raw source material. areas/ — life-area pages and subpages. This is the main personal operating map. syste

No resolved wikilinks.

Wiki Schema

Purpose

This wiki stores structured continuity for Jenffry and Sapho: current strategy, life-area context, decisions, open loops, dashboard-facing summaries, and raw source material.

Folder roles

  • areas/ — life-area pages and subpages. This is the main personal operating map.
  • system/ — Sapho/current-strategy/dashboard/open-loop/decision infrastructure.
  • inbox/ — raw-but-active capture pages such as brain dumps and perception logs.
  • entities/ — stable people/entity context.
  • raw/ — source material and reference/archive content.
  • plans/ — implementation or migration plans.

Life-area taxonomy

Keep top-level life areas small:

  • Health
  • Relationships
  • Finances
  • Career
  • Leisure
  • Life Admin

Prefer sublabels before creating new top-level areas. Keep friendship/social rebuilding under Relationships/Social; keep hobbies, restoration, outings, and fun activities under Leisure. Keep partner/fatherhood under Relationships. Use Life Admin for concrete administrative obligations, including immigration/citizenship unless that domain later earns a dedicated page.

Dashboard summary contract

Dashboard-fed pages should include:

## Dashboard summary

- Status:
- Current priority:
- Active signals:
- Open loops:
- Next concrete move:

The dashboard should be fed by sections inside real pages, not by duplicate dashboard-only pages or a dashboard-only mini-wiki.

system/dashboard.md is the home for dashboard feed rules and implementation notes, not the home for status widgets that belong to other pages. For example: progress belongs in system/progress-tracking.md, perception patterns belong in inbox/perception-log.md, and brain-dump review state belongs in inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md.

Preservation rule

No content should be deleted during restructuring. If pages are merged, preserve source content in destination pages, a backup, or a raw/reference archive before retiring the old page.

Update rule

When a meaningful wiki change is made:

  1. Update affected wikilinks.
  2. Update index.md if page structure changes.
  3. Append a concise entry to log.md.
  4. If the change affects user-facing state, update the relevant ## Dashboard summary.

Wiki Log

Chronological record of wiki actions.
Format: ## [YYYY-MM-DD] action | subject
Actions: create, ingest, update, query, lint, archive, delete

[2026-06-08] update | Life-area Goal Spine reduction pass

  • Preserved pre-reduction copies of the main life-area pages under raw/reference/life-area-goal-spine-pre-reduction-2026-06-08/.
  • Added a standard Goal spine to Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, Life Admin, My Life Story, and key subpages so long-term direction, 12-month proof, monthly target, weekly commitment, daily rule, and source-of-truth/dashboard rules are visible first.
  • Compressed the most drifted Career/Work pages by replacing migrated-source clutter with active operating rules and preserved-source links.
  • Left detailed evidence pages intact where they are useful, especially relationship clarity sessions and Princess project context.

[2026-06-08] update | Relationship practical topics map

  • Added a first-pass practical topics map to areas/relationships/clarity-sessions.md covering Nolan's routine, housing, money, timing, family communication, support/mediation, and first-conversation boundaries.
  • Framed the map as preparation, not a legal plan or finalized logistics decision.

[2026-06-08] update | Relationship conversation timing context

  • Added Nolan's 2026-06-27 birthday party, Jenffry's mother's planned Cuba visit, and her same-day birthday as timing constraints for the Aubrey conversation.
  • Captured the working implication: prepare now, avoid initiating the full separation conversation during the birthday/mother visit unless forced, and explicitly weigh before-party honesty against after-visit containment.

[2026-06-08] update | Relationship separation-oriented draft and response script

  • Saved the current shorter separation-oriented truth-testing statement in areas/relationships/clarity-sessions.md.
  • Added first-pass response-preparation scripts for likely Aubrey responses, emphasizing truth, responsibility, Nolan, non-abandonment, and non-defensiveness.

[2026-06-07] update | Night closeout progress signal

  • Added the 2026-06-07 evening closeout row to system/progress-tracking.md.
  • Captured a partial day with Finish Sapho Dashboard slipping past its 7:45 PM slot and a late-night family pickup still open.

[2026-06-07] update | Weekly Sapho system maintenance

  • Corrected stale taxonomy wording in SCHEMA.md, system/sapho-operating-system.md, and the jenffry-continuity skill: friendship/social rebuilding belongs under Relationships/Social, while Leisure owns hobbies, outings, restoration, and fun activities.
  • Corrected stale dashboard-tab wording in jenffry-continuity so the default action cockpit is Life Admin, not the retired Goals & Tasks label.
  • Noted disk pressure during maintenance: /opt/data is at 90% usage; recommend a scoped cache/old-backup cleanup instead of deleting context automatically.

[2026-06-07] update | Life-area long-term directions

  • Captured Jenffry's long-term directions for Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, and Life Admin.
  • Updated Relationships source pages so Partner, Fatherhood, and Social each carry the long-term direction inside the relationship type model.
  • Updated the dashboard generator goal summaries and corrected the Partner long-term relationship card wording.

[2026-06-07] update | Weekly progress-tracking rollover

  • Rolled system/progress-tracking.md to week 2026-06-08 through 2026-06-14.
  • Preserved the 2026-06-01 through 2026-06-07 trend summary with corrected Hevy and finance figures.

[2026-06-07] update | Weekly review active goal

  • Updated system/current-strategy.md → Active weekly goal for 2026-06-08 through 2026-06-14.
  • New focus: health-first execution week — complete two medical appointments, finish Sapho Dashboard carryover, keep finance inside the weekly guardrail, and choose one intentional leisure/social anchor.
  • Sunday TickTick cleanup left only Mow the lawn plus Finish Nolan's one year birthday party invite active for the day; Finish Sapho Dashboard was protected for Wednesday after a Monday overload check.

[2026-06-05] update | Dashboard Life Admin tab consolidation

  • Renamed the default Goals & Tasks dashboard tab to Life Admin.
  • Removed the former standalone Life Admin tab from the navigation and visible tab panels.
  • Kept the old #tab-life-admin hash as a compatibility alias to the default command-center tab.

[2026-06-05] update | Life Story nested timeline scaffold

  • Replaced the My Life Story dashboard tab with a blank nested structured timeline.
  • The tab now uses chapter-level storytelling structure: opening image, want/need, pressure/conflict, choice, turning point, cost, meaning, and bridge.
  • Each chapter and scene slot follows the same story arc as the whole life story, so missing information can be added later without turning the tab into a journal dump.

[2026-06-05] update | Relationships card goal cadence

  • Updated each Relationships dashboard card to show long-term goal, month goal, and this-week goal.
  • Kept the simplified visible tab limited to the three cards: Partner, Fatherhood, and Social.

[2026-06-05] update | Relationships card chrome simplified

  • Removed the Relationships tab kicker and per-card Relationship type label from the visible dashboard.
  • Changed the relationship card chip label to Wiki page and made page-anchor clicks open the Wiki tab to the linked source page.

[2026-06-05] update | Relationships type card names simplified

  • Renamed the visible Relationships dashboard type cards to Partner, Fatherhood, and Social.
  • Kept the simplified Relationships tab limited to the three type cards only.

[2026-06-05] update | Relationships tab reduced to type cards

  • Simplified the Relationships dashboard tab to show only the three relationship type cards: Partner, Fatherhood, and Social.
  • Removed the separate summary, clarity snapshot, guardrails, Phase 3 signals, all-tasks, social reservoir, and improvement-plan sections from the visible dashboard tab.
  • Kept the type-card structure: relationship type → goals → people contained within that kind → related active TickTick tasks.

[2026-06-05] update | Relationships Phase 3 signals

  • Added live Phase 3 Relationships signal cards for clarity stage, fatherhood presence, social experiment status, and task concreteness.
  • Signals are derived from existing source pages and active TickTick Relationships tasks; they should stay lightweight and not become new tracking obligations.
  • Updated areas/relationships.md and system/dashboard.md, then republished and verified the live dashboard.

[2026-06-02] update | Relationship clarity next move accepted

[2026-06-02] update | Relationship clarity six-month attempt reaction

  • Added Jenffry's reaction to the hypothetical six-month honest committed staying attempt in areas/relationships/clarity-sessions.md.
  • Captured that the offer would evoke dread, obligation, sadness, and resistance — not relief or hope — which shifts the live question toward truth-telling and separation/co-parenting ethics rather than another stay-attempt.

[2026-06-01] update | Relationship clarity session staying-option check

  • Added Jenffry's answer to the selfless-staying options check in areas/relationships/clarity-sessions.md.
  • Captured that he could theoretically offer honest committed staying for a defined period, but current time apart and mental-health progress have increased clarity toward ending the marriage, making another attempt feel potentially unwanted or repetitive rather than fully honest.

[2026-06-01] update | Relationship clarity session fourth quadrant

  • Added alternative selfless-staying models to areas/relationships/clarity-sessions.md.
  • Captured Jenffry's selfless-leaving quadrant: changing the marriage structure while remaining consistently present for Nolan, helpful to Aubrey, financially/logistically responsible, and available for milestones and care needs.

[2026-06-01] update | Relationship clarity session selfish-leaving quadrant

  • Added Sapho's selfless-staying analysis to areas/relationships/clarity-sessions.md, including the desire-vs-action distinction and the need for alternate selfless-staying models.
  • Captured Jenffry's selfish-leaving answer: disappearing, chasing fantasy, leaving Aubrey with the mess, underestimating Nolan's damage, and becoming a terrible father.

[2026-06-01] update | Relationship clarity session selfless-staying quadrant

  • Added Sapho's selfish-staying analysis to areas/relationships/clarity-sessions.md because Jenffry flagged it as useful to preserve.
  • Captured Jenffry's selfless-staying answer: he has tried to put away the desire for a different life and desired romantic attraction, but suppression has produced resentment/guilt/anger and has not worked for him or Aubrey.

[2026-06-01] create | Relationship clarity session notes

[2026-06-01] update | June credit-card cycles

  • Added Jenffry-provided card cycle data for Savor, Quicksilver 0736, and Amazon; inferred missing June planning dates for Quicksilver 1208 and Chase from observed payments.
  • Updated the estimated June card due total to $3,169.46 and lowered the checking-only EveryDollar cushion to $1,278.07.

[2026-06-01] update | Leisure pipeline cleanup

  • Removed Fishing setup/rhythm from the Leisure Pipeline wiki/dashboard source and added an indoor rock climbing image rule for dashboard cards.

[2026-06-01] update | Leisure pipeline

  • Added indoor rock climbing to areas/leisure.md Pipeline as a weather-safe physical leisure / skill-track option.

[2026-06-01] update | June finance budget draft

  • Added a June cash-flow draft to areas/finances.md based on the current biweekly paycheck cadence, current checking/savings, and the live credit-card balances.
  • Set tighter June discretionary caps for shopping, leisure, and dining/social, and recommended paying the cards in full if the month stays on track.
  • Added an essentials-only survival version that zeros discretionary categories and shows the remaining shortfall against June income.
  • Corrected the June card line to use estimated statement-payment amounts from the latest payment pattern instead of the full running balances.
  • Tightened the bills/utilities line by removing irregular items seen only once in history (Pestie and MDWS) from the monthly reserve.
  • Replaced the survival budget with a June EveryDollar zero-based budget that assigns starting cash plus June income down to $0 unassigned.
  • Re-reviewed credit-card dues and corrected the June card estimate from last-payment amounts to an inferred due portion capped by current balances; updated the cash cushion.
  • Rechecked MDWS against all available finance history back to 2026-01-02; it appears once, so it is possible quarterly/irregular rather than confirmed monthly.
  • Removed savings from June available cash; EveryDollar now assigns checking-only starting cash plus June income.
  • Refined June credit-card due estimates using observed payment dates plus estimated statement closing dates; noted that exact closing/due fields require statements or portal data.

[2026-06-01] update | Leisure dashboard pipeline audit and image fixes

  • Removed the stale comic-book-store lead from Leisure and Relationships/Social so it does not reappear in the dashboard pipeline.
  • Consolidated the Leisure wiki back to one canonical Pipeline section for the dashboard to render.
  • Updated Leisure dashboard image matching so the six movie leads use title-specific poster/source images and the VR headset item uses a VR image instead of Elden Ring art.
  • Saved those poster/VR images under dashboard assets so the live page does not depend on fragile hotlink loading.

[2026-05-31] update | Hedonic Calculator copy tightened and description field added

  • Updated /opt/data/scripts/publish_sapho_dashboard.py so the embedded Hedonic Calculator in the Leisure tab now has an activity description field alongside the activity name and same 10-metric leisure rubric.
  • Tightened system/dashboard.md so the wiki copy matches the current Leisure-tab layout exactly and no longer describes the Hedonic Calculator as a separate tab.

[2026-05-29] update | Leisure scoring rubric expanded

  • Updated /opt/data/scripts/publish_sapho_dashboard.py so Leisure activity scoring now uses a 10-metric weighted rubric: Enjoyment, Restoration, Excitement, Social, Money, Risk, Novelty, Logistics, Meaning, and Physical health.
  • Kept Money, Risk, and Logistics as friction dimensions and added alias handling so older saved leisure-score rows using pleasure, future, or regret still map to the new inputs.
  • Updated areas/leisure.md and system/dashboard.md to record the new weighted rubric and dashboard wording.

[2026-05-29] update | Dashboard task score backend scaffolded

  • Added dashboard-only 1–5 score controls to the Goals & Tasks TickTick task cards in /opt/data/scripts/publish_sapho_dashboard.py; TickTick is not written to.
  • Added local SQLite-backed score API /opt/data/scripts/sapho_task_score_api.py, database path /opt/data/task_scores/task_scores.sqlite3, and watchdog cron Sapho task score API watchdog (a92bd543e90d) for the local API.
  • Published the visible score controls to GitHub Pages and recorded the remaining open loop: choose a reachable public HTTPS API URL before cross-device saving is fully live from phone/computer.

[2026-05-31] update | Cloudflare persistence path chosen for dashboard state

  • Chose Cloudflare Workers + D1 as the preferred free cross-device backend for dashboard task/leisure score state and the Hedonic Calculator draft state.
  • Kept the local SQLite API as a fallback/watchdog path and left SAPHO_TASK_SCORE_API_URL as the switch that points the dashboard at the deployed HTTPS endpoint.
  • Updated the dashboard generator copy so the live page reflects the Cloudflare-backed persistence direction.

[2026-05-25] update | Mental-health goals aligned to final dashboard trackers

  • Updated areas/health/mental-health.md so the dashboard summary, current weekly goal, daily move, and tracking method align with the final 15 TickTick mental-health habit trackers.
  • Reframed the weekly layer around the dashboard's score averages and completion-rate signals while preserving weekly review as the interpretation layer to avoid daily rumination.
