[2026-05-24] update | Weekly Sapho system maintenance repaired cron/wiki drift

  • Patched recurring review cron prompts so they reference current canonical wiki homes: system/progress-tracking.md, system/current-strategy.md, system/open-loops.md, system/decision-log.md, areas/leisure.md, and relationship area pages instead of retired concepts/ paths.
  • Updated system/sapho-operating-system.md to reflect that the dashboard/map layer now exists and that the weekly maintenance job is the current full Sapho system improvement loop, not the older continuity-only audit.
  • Verified cron/jobs.json remains valid JSON and hermes cron list --all reports no last errors or delivery errors.
  • Flagged remaining non-urgent lean issue: cron output history is large, mostly from the now-paused standalone dashboard publisher, and should be handled with retention/archival rather than ad hoc deletion.

[2026-05-25] update | Final TickTick mental-health habit tracker list

  • Finalized 15 TickTick habit trackers: Stop checking, Therapy, Physical activity, Healthy diet, Walk, no phone, Social outing, Mood, Sleep, Anxiety, Energy, Relax, Leisure activity, Life story, Self reflect, and Meditate.
  • Stop checking, Healthy diet, Mood, Sleep, Anxiety, and Energy are daily 1–5 + note trackers.
  • Walk, no phone and Self reflect are daily note trackers.
  • Therapy, Physical activity, Social outing, Leisure activity, and Life story are at least once/week + note trackers.
  • Relax is at least 3/week + note; Meditate is at least 5/week + note.
  • Habit Log notes are now intentionally included in the public dashboard via /opt/data/wiki/private/health/mood-tracking-notes.md.

[2026-05-25] update | TickTick mental-health habits consolidated

  • Consolidated mental-health TickTick tracking to six active habits: Sleep, Mood, Anxiety, Energy, Relax, and Meditate.
  • Archived Outward-focus action and the 25 Boolean choice-score habits (Sleep 1–Sleep 5, Mood 1–Mood 5, Anxiety 1–Anxiety 5, Energy 1–Energy 5, Body calm 1–Body calm 5) to reduce admin soup.
  • Renamed the body-calm signal to Relax.
  • Set Anxiety to Option B: 1 = poor anxiety control, 5 = strong anxiety control.
  • Dashboard originally read Habit Log emoji scores from the six-habit setup and wrote raw Habit Log notes to /opt/data/wiki/private/health/mood-tracking-notes.md; this was later superseded by the final 15-habit tracker and public note inclusion.

[2026-05-25] update | TickTick Habit Log emoji scores wired

  • Confirmed TickTick private Habit Records returns content and emoji for Habit Log entries.
  • Verified TickTick web mood enum: cry=10, sad=20, general=30, smile=40, smiley=50; dashboard maps these to 1–5.
  • Updated the dashboard to use Habit Log emoji scores when a local TickTick web cookie is present, while keeping Boolean choice habits as the stable fallback.
  • Initial safety rule was not to publish raw Habit Log notes by default; Jenffry later explicitly changed this so the mood-tracking notes path is included in the public dashboard.

[2026-05-24] update | Mental-health TickTick dashboard changed to choice-score habits

  • Corrected the TickTick mental-health design after Jenffry clarified that mobile habits were only showing complete/not-complete, not direct numeric entry.
  • Deactivated the five unusable numeric score habits and created Boolean score-choice habits: Sleep 1–Sleep 5, Mood 1–Mood 5, Anxiety 1–Anxiety 5, Energy 1–Energy 5, and Body calm 1–Body calm 5.
  • Renamed active tracking habits to remove the Mental health — prefix; current habit names include Meditate, Outward-focus action, and the score-choice habit sets.
  • Updated the dashboard publisher to infer each daily 1–5 score from whichever choice habit is marked, using the latest choice if multiple scores are marked for the same metric/day.
  • Republished and verified the live Health tab shows 27 configured TickTick choice habits and the instruction to mark exactly one score choice per family.

[2026-05-24] update | Mental-health TickTick dashboard source wired

  • Created five TickTick numeric habits for mental-health scores: mood, anxiety, energy, body calm, and sleep.
  • Updated /opt/data/scripts/ticktick_habits.py to support numeric habit creation and safer habit summaries.
  • Updated /opt/data/scripts/publish_sapho_dashboard.py so the Health tab reads TickTick mental-health habit check-ins and numeric values directly, with wiki fallback only where TickTick has no value yet.
  • Republished the dashboard and verified the generated HTML shows TickTick mental-health scores, seven configured habits, and the stable-source caveat that notes/emojis are not API inputs.
  • Updated areas/health/mental-health.md, system/progress-tracking.md, and system/open-loops.md to make TickTick the source of truth for mental-health dashboard tracking while keeping ignore+redirect out of habit tracking.

[2026-05-24] update | Mental-health SMART goals written

  • Recorded Jenffry's decision that the physical-health goals, dashboard, and Hevy-backed pipeline are complete enough to move into normal review/calibration mode.
  • Updated areas/health/mental-health.md with a SMART mental-health cascade built around fear as the core mechanism and the strategy: ignore, redirect, accept, and embrace.
  • Added concrete monthly/weekly behavior targets: 20-minute acceptance/embrace practice 5 days/week with 3/week as the minimum acceptable floor, daytime ignore+redirect, and non-specific outward-focus actions that will connect to Leisure and Relationships goals.
  • Added an observer-mode reset script and configured lightweight TickTick habits for acceptance/embrace practice plus outward-focus action; ignore+redirect remains in the script, not as a habit.
  • Shifted system/current-strategy.md, system/open-loops.md, system/progress-tracking.md, areas/health.md, areas/health/physical-health.md, and system/decision-log.md toward the new mental-health practice pipeline.

[2026-05-24] update | Compression-summary 429 routing fixed

  • Diagnosed the recurring compression-summary failure as the auxiliary compression task being pinned to openai-codex / gpt-5.4-mini, while the ChatGPT team plan returned usage_limit_reached 429s for that route.
  • Changed auxiliary.compression.provider to auto and cleared the pinned compression model so compression follows the active main route (gpt-5.5 now) instead of the exhausted mini route.
  • Preserved a config backup at /opt/data/config.yaml.bak-compression-20260524-180743 and verified a direct compression auxiliary call returned POST_RESTORE_COMPRESSION_OK.

[2026-05-24] ingest | Life-story and perception dumps captured

  • Captured a new raw brain dump about writing Jenffry's life story and fixing the recurring compression-summary 429 error.
  • Added four perception entries about the checking-vs-processing tension, global “my life is over” language, life-story reframing, and the energy/future-excitement link.
  • Updated areas/health/mental-health.md with compact, non-diagnostic synthesis for the durable recurring mechanisms.

[2026-05-24] update | Physical-health dashboard pipeline cleanup

  • Consolidated areas/health/physical-health.md into the canonical current physical-health source page and preserved the prior migrated material under raw/reference/physical-health-migrated-material-2026-05-24.md.
  • Updated the dashboard architecture docs so the direct no-agent source refresh owns Hevy/SimpleFIN source refresh plus publish/verification, and paused the redundant standalone dashboard publish cron.
  • Clarified review governance: Friday mental-health work is currently the local/background perception-pattern review, not a separate user-facing check-in.

[2026-05-22] update | Dashboard Health panel made more compact and visual

  • Changed the Health tab's strength ladder from tracker rows into four compact visual strength cards for bench, lat pulldown, row, and leg press.
  • Republished the dashboard generator output so the local dashboard HTML reflects the new compact strength layout.

[2026-05-22] update | Dashboard propagated physical-health strength ladder

  • Updated system/dashboard.md and areas/health/physical-health.md so the Health tab explicitly surfaces the current strength baseline and 1-year strength ladder.
  • Republished the live dashboard and verified the published HTML now contains the new strength-baseline and 1-year ladder text.

[2026-05-22] update | Physical-health strength ladder added

  • Expanded areas/health/physical-health.md with a current strength baseline table, 1-year good/stretch strength goals, milestone targets, first-focus guidance, and a simple progression rule.
  • Kept the body-composition goals intact while making the bench/pulldown/row/leg-press ladder explicit and checkable.

[2026-05-22] update | Dashboard tightened around physical-health goal ladder

  • Updated system/dashboard.md so the Health tab surfaces the full physical-health ladder: 3-month, 6-month, yearly, long-term, monthly, weekly, and daily targets.
  • Updated the physical-health page and health skill notes so the dashboard stays aligned with the cascade rather than collapsing it to one final target.

[2026-05-22] update | Dashboard surfaces all Hevy body measurements

  • Expanded the dashboard Health tab to show a full body-measurement matrix with progress bars for weight, waist, abdomen, neck, shoulders, chest, hips, arms, forearms, thighs, and calves.
  • Tightened the dashboard copy so the physical-health section is clearly a live progress surface rather than a single snapshot.

[2026-05-22] update | Physical-health goals cascade written

  • Wrote the full numeric physical-health goal cascade into areas/health/physical-health.md with long-term, yearly, 6-month, 3-month, monthly, weekly, and daily targets.
  • Updated the Physical Health dashboard summary, the body-system open loop, and the current-strategy blocker note so the new cascade is the canonical source.

[2026-05-22] update | Dashboard propagated physical-health cascade

  • Updated system/dashboard.md so the Health tab and dashboard summary explicitly surface the numeric physical-health cascade from areas/health/physical-health.md.

[2026-05-22] create | Goals cascade template

  • Added system/goals-cascade-template.md as the reusable cascade shape for long-term, yearly, 6-month, monthly, weekly, and daily goals.
  • Linked the template from index.md so it is easy to find when filling out each life area.

[2026-05-21] update | Dashboard Today default restored

  • Fixed the tabbed dashboard load behavior so it no longer auto-selects the first wiki node and scrolls to the root page.
  • Today remains the default landing tab; Wiki still opens intentionally when using a wiki page hash or clicking a graph node.

[2026-05-21] update | Dashboard organized into five tabs

  • Implemented the approved dashboard tab structure: Today, Goals & Tasks, Health, Finance, and Wiki.
  • Today is the default command center; Health, Finance, and Wiki are separated into their own focused tabs.
  • Verified the published GitHub Pages dashboard contains the tab navigation and keeps Finance cockpit, Health cockpit, Wiki graph, and page cards available.

[2026-05-21] update | Dashboard life-area summary block removed

  • Removed the extra life-area summary block from the dashboard.
  • Kept area context available through Goals, Finance cockpit, Health cockpit, personal signals, wiki graph, and page cards.

[2026-05-21] update | Dashboard personal-signal clutter removed

  • Removed the two extra personal-signal cards that duplicated gym/activity status and daily-prompt detail.
  • Kept gym/activity information inside the split Health cockpit where it belongs.

[2026-05-21] update | Health cockpit split into mental and physical panels

  • Updated the dashboard Health cockpit so it shows separate Mental health and Physical health sections.
  • Mental health now shows lightweight sleep/capacity/rumination/presence signals plus future data placeholders.
  • Physical health now shows Hevy 7-day/30-day workout rhythm, pickleball/activity signal, weight/measurement trackers, and lifting-goal trackers.

[2026-05-21] update | Dashboard title changed to Sapho's Dashboard

  • Updated the published dashboard browser title and visible page heading from Dashboard to Sapho's Dashboard.

[2026-05-21] update | Daily dashboard source refresh cron added

  • Created cron job Daily dashboard source refresh (b7a5b1d24d5c) to run daily at 9:00 AM America/New_York with local-only delivery.
  • Job refreshes dashboard-facing source summaries from the wiki and TickTick, republishes the Dashboard, and verifies the live page markers without interrupting Jenffry unless a broken-state appears.

[2026-05-21] update | Dashboard reoriented around goals, tasks, and live areas

  • Changed the published dashboard from a generic “Sapho Workspace Dashboard” landing page to a plain “Dashboard” with no hero description.
  • Reworked the first screen to show goals and main tasks for today, this week, and this month from current strategy plus live TickTick tasks.
  • Added provisional goal progress bars by life area, and added finance/health cockpit panels using source-owned budget, progress-tracking, and Hevy signals.
  • Removed the old top-screen “Current bearing,” “Recent continuity changes,” and “Tracking cockpit” framing in favor of Jenffry-facing information.

[2026-05-18] update | Full life-area taxonomy implemented across wiki and TickTick

  • Renamed/relinked main wiki life-area pages to match the final taxonomy: health, relationships, finances, career, leisure, and maintenance, with supporting health subpages mental-health, physical-health, and health-routines.
  • Created concepts/health.md as the Health hub and updated index.md, life-area-map.md, goals-cascade.md, current-plan.md, focus-map.md, and life-rebuild.md around the six-area structure.
  • Simplified Relationships sublabels to partner / marriage and fatherhood; social activity now routes through Leisure.
  • Reorganized TickTick projects to match the taxonomy: 🏋‍♂Health, 💞Relationships, 💰Finances, 💼Career, 🎲Leisure, 🛠Maintenance, plus 🗄Archive.
  • Moved active tasks from the former Social, Hobbies, Achievement, Princess UX/UI, Home / Family Ops, and System & Maintenance projects into the matching taxonomy projects and deleted the emptied old projects.
  • Saved a pre-restructure wiki markdown backup at /opt/data/wiki/backups/life-area-taxonomy-restructure-2026-05-18.

[2026-05-18] update | Life area taxonomy finalized

  • Finalized the life-area structure in concepts/life-area-map.md as: Health (mental health, physical health), Relationships (partner / marriage, fatherhood), Finances (budget, investing), Career (business, work), Leisure (social, hobbies, outings), and Maintenance (no subcategories for now).
  • Clarified that social belongs under Leisure, not Relationships, and that Maintenance stays plain for now.
  • Updated concepts/decision-log.md to record the final taxonomy decision.

[2026-05-18] create | Life area map

  • Created concepts/life-area-map.md to separate real life areas from operating-system pages and to recommend a cleaner canonical set: mental health, body/physical health, family/relationship/fatherhood, finance, career/work/business direction, leisure, and immigration/citizenship.
  • Clarified the merge hierarchy: keep body-system + physical-health together as one area, and keep career-business-direction + princess-ux-work together as one area.
  • Updated index.md to include the new taxonomy page.
  • Verified backup jobs still exist and are enabled: Princess Storybook GitHub backup (90d5cf918b5e) last ran ok at 2026-05-18 18:15 UTC, and Sapho system backup (ff09fc1ab416) last ran ok at 2026-05-18 08:01 UTC.
  • Verified backup repos jenffry90-arch/jenffry-sapho-system-backup and jenffry90-arch/princess-storybook remain private and unauthenticated checks return 404.
  • Confirmed free private GitHub Pages is not available on the current plan, then restored the public sanitized dashboard link because Jenffry said a public link is acceptable if private is not possible.
  • Resumed dashboard auto-publish cron job a8af467ed943; verified the public URL returns 200, contains Dynamic, privacy-sanitized, and does not match checked forbidden patterns.

[2026-05-18] update | Relationship clarity exercise saved

  • Added the therapist-guided four-scenario exercise to concepts/relationships.md: selfish vs selfless while staying in the marriage, and selfish vs selfless while leaving the marriage/not living in it.
  • Added a matching open loop so the next relationship clarity session can use the scenario set instead of keeping the question abstract.

[2026-05-18] create | Life area frameworks

  • Created concepts/life-area-frameworks.md to capture common outside life-area models.
  • Found the usual pattern: 5 to 10 domains, often including health, work/career, finances, relationships/family/friends/partner, personal growth, leisure/fun, environment/home, community/contribution, and spirituality/meaning.
  • Noted that Jenffry's current taxonomy fits the normal range and that a sublabel approach for Relationships matches common Wheel-of-Life practice.

[2026-05-18] update | Relationship sublabels added under Relationships

  • Later simplified Relationships sublabels to partner/marriage and fatherhood only.
  • Linked those sublabels from concepts/life-area-map.md so the Relationships area has clearer internal structure.

[2026-05-18] update | Life-area taxonomy settled on maintenance bucket

  • Jenffry chose Maintenance as the catch-all bucket name.
  • Immigration was moved under Maintenance as a subcategory instead of staying top-level.
  • Updated the life-area map and decision log to reflect the shorter naming set: Health, Relationships, Finances, Career, Leisure, Maintenance.

[2026-05-19] update | Sapho dashboard expanded to full live wiki browser

  • Updated concepts/sapho-workspace-dashboard-v0-plan.md, concepts/open-loops.md, concepts/current-plan.md, and concepts/decision-log.md to reflect the new full-wiki dashboard state and the remaining archive-toggle question.

[2026-05-18] update | Sapho dashboard public link removed from active use

  • Made jenffry90-arch/sapho-workspace-dashboard private and verified unauthenticated GitHub API/web checks return 404.
  • Overwrote the old GitHub Pages URL with a tombstone/unavailable page before disabling Pages, so if GitHub CDN still serves the path it no longer shows the dashboard.
  • Updated concepts/sapho-workspace-dashboard-v0-plan.md, concepts/open-loops.md, and concepts/decision-log.md to reflect that future dashboard work needs private/access-controlled hosting.

[2026-05-18] update | Sapho workspace blueprint open loop

[2026-05-18] update | Brain dump spillover reverted after user correction

[2026-05-18] update | Perception log is a containment layer like brain dumps

  • Updated concepts/perception-log.md so perception entries are captured raw first and not spilled into other pages during capture.
  • Updated concepts/decision-log.md and jenffry-continuity so the perception log works like the brain dump container: capture now, process later only if Jenffry asks.
  • Kept the weekly perception review structure in place for later pattern work.

[2026-05-18] update | Perception log broadened beyond emotions

  • Replaced concepts/feelings-log.md with concepts/perception-log.md to reflect Jenffry's correction that the page should track broad body/mind perceptions, not just emotions.
  • Updated cron job 1052949b0556 to Weekly perception pattern review, reviewing body sensations, emotions, energy, stress, mood, pain/tension, clarity/fog, urges, sensory impressions, and random/mixed perceptions.
  • Updated index.md, concepts/decision-log.md, persistent memory, and jenffry-continuity with the broadened scope.

[2026-05-18] create | Initial feelings log and weekly pattern review

  • Created the initial feelings-log concept and cron job; later superseded the same day by concepts/perception-log.md after Jenffry clarified the scope should include body sensations and broad/random perceptions, not only emotions.

[2026-05-18] update | Processed brain dumps removed from inbox

  • Updated concepts/brain-dump-sessions.md: processed brain dump sessions should be deleted from that page after durable outputs are saved/logged, so the page remains a temporary inbox instead of a noisy archive.
  • Updated cron job 7445bd2463da and jenffry-continuity so weekly brain dump reviews remove fully processed session blocks instead of retaining processed content.
  • Updated concepts/decision-log.md with the correction.

[2026-05-18] update | Brain dump processing status added

  • Updated concepts/brain-dump-sessions.md with explicit processing fields: Status, Reviewed on, Processed on, and Action taken / destination links.
  • Updated weekly brain dump review cron job 7445bd2463da so it can distinguish raw/unprocessed, reviewed/proposed, partially processed, and processed sessions.
  • Patched jenffry-continuity so future captures mark new dumps as raw/unprocessed.

[2026-05-18] update | Weekly brain dump review cron created

  • Created cron job Weekly brain dump review (7445bd2463da) for Sunday 6:00 PM America/New_York during EDT (0 22 * * 0 UTC), delivered to Telegram.
  • Purpose: review concepts/brain-dump-sessions.md, preserve raw dumps, add later-organization notes, extract only clear durable wiki items, and propose TickTick tasks instead of creating them unless explicitly requested.
  • Updated concepts/decision-log.md with the operating-system decision.

[2026-05-18] create | Brain dump sessions page

  • Created concepts/brain-dump-sessions.md as the raw capture page for Jenffry's freeform brain dump sessions.
  • Rule: when Jenffry says "this is a brain dump session" or clearly identifies a message as a brain dump, Sapho captures the stream there first and organizes it later on request.
  • Updated index.md to include the page.

[2026-05-18] update | Daily tracking signal logged

  • Logged Monday morning signals in concepts/weekly-progress-dashboard.md: sleep 4/5, capacity 4/5, observer-mode 2/5.
  • Kept the note compact: solid operating state; protect a narrow plan despite work noise.

[2026-05-17] update | Model-routing policy matrix open loop

  • Added an open loop to formalize a visible Sapho model-routing matrix before considering a custom auto-router.
  • Scope: job/session type, default model, allowed fallback, forbidden downgrade, review cadence, and reason; then audit cron jobs against the matrix.

[2026-05-17] update | Weekend/social idea added: Disturbed concert

  • Added Disturbed at Hard Rock Live, Hollywood — Saturday July 25, 2026, 8:00 PM — to concepts/leisure.md as a possible future weekend live-music outing.
  • Kept it in the wiki backlog only; no TickTick task created unless Jenffry decides to buy tickets, invite someone, or plan logistics.

[2026-05-17] update | Silent backup output pruning

  • Pruned 118 silent cron output files from 90d5cf918b5e (Princess Storybook GitHub backup), keeping the most recent 24 outputs for short-term auditability while removing redundant no-op history.
  • Reduced total cron output files from 168 to 50 and trimmed stored cron output size without touching secrets, runtime DBs, or source code.

[2026-05-17] update | Background audit model-routing trial

  • Set weekly continuity memory audit (a42c23ac4083) and weekly Sapho lean-system audit (292f4883ad21) to openai-codex / gpt-5.4-mini.
  • Added an open loop to review whether running these important background audits on 5.4-mini creates quality issues, shallow recommendations, bad pruning, or operating-system drift after the next 1–2 runs.

[2026-05-17] update | Self-improving skill loop inspected

  • Inspected whether Sapho's recurring-output feedback loop is becoming durable skill/template improvements rather than relying only on memory.
  • Finding: the loop works in-turn; recent corrections produced patches to governing skills, reusable templates, cron prompts, and wiki pages. Weakness: it is not yet fully autonomous and can create reference-file/template bloat.
  • Closed the original open loop in concepts/open-loops.md and carried the lean-governance question into the planned Sapho-system bloat review. Added jenffry-continuity/references/self-improving-skill-loop-audit-2026-05-17.md.

[2026-05-17] update | Dedicated gym review refreshed

  • Ran a Hevy source-of-truth gym review for the first week of the Sapho 5-day routine: 4 workouts, 61 working sets, average 48 minutes; classified as a strong week because Push/Pull/Lower plus one posture/cardio day were covered.
  • Updated concepts/physical-health.md with the weekly review, key lift anchors, routine coverage, and next-week target.

[2026-05-17] update | Independent finance review refreshed

  • Refreshed SimpleFIN data for the independent finance review: 15 accounts and 556 transactions; transaction data currently runs through 2026-05-14.
  • Updated concepts/finances.md with May month-to-date and 2026-05-08 through 2026-05-14 weekly budget status, remaining monthly usable amount after TD reserve, and next-week pacing guardrail.

[2026-05-17] update | Progress tracking dashboard and 1–5 review scale

  • Created concepts/progress-tracking-system.md to define lightweight daily/weekly/monthly tracking, using a 1–5 scale by default instead of green/yellow/red.
  • Created concepts/weekly-progress-dashboard.md as the living weekly page where daily signals can be logged and summarized into trends for weekly and monthly reviews.
  • Updated review templates, governing skill, cron prompts, current plan, decision log, and index so daily questions feed weekly/monthly reviews and both daily/weekly plans enforce one big task plus one quick win per day.

[2026-05-17] update | Daily templates aligned to cascade

  • Updated the daily morning template to mirror the monthly/weekly structure at daily altitude: yesterday review and lessons, weekly/monthly goal alignment, plan today, capacity/mental-health/gym/finance signals, system/hygiene, tracking/calibration.
  • Updated the daily night closeout template to review today, check weekly/monthly alignment, then prepare tomorrow with protected moves and a reset/sleep guardrail.
  • Updated the daily morning and night cron prompts to follow this cascade.

[2026-05-17] update | Weekly weekend/social reservoir requirement

  • Updated the weekly review weekend/social section so Sapho brings three realistic weekend/fun suggestions plus the current ideas reservoir from concepts/leisure.md.
  • Updated weekend-social-planning so previously shared ideas are preserved in the wiki reservoir and resurfaced at the right weekly planning moment.
  • Updated the Sunday weekly review cron prompt to use the reservoir, optionally search for fresh local/event ideas, recommend one option, and let Jenffry pick one to plan.

[2026-05-17] update | Monthly and weekly gym/finance template calibration

  • Updated the monthly template so past-month review explicitly includes numeric gym and finance reviews: Hevy attendance/performance/progression/stalls and SimpleFIN/budget actuals against monthly budget and weekly pacing.
  • Updated monthly planning so the month-by-week plan includes finance and gym goals: monthly budget target, weekly budget/pacing number, gym attendance target, minimum successful week/month, and progression focus.
  • Updated the weekly template to mimic the monthly review at weekly execution altitude: past-week review/lessons with gym and finance actuals, monthly-goal alignment, then concrete day-by-day planning with goal links.
  • Updated the Sunday weekly review cron prompt and monthly cron prompt to follow this structure.

[2026-05-17] update | Monthly review template reordered

  • Updated the monthly strategic review template from Jenffry's feedback: start with past-month review and lessons, then long-term bearing, then plan the month by week.
  • Combined the old progress/drift and domain review sections into the first section so the month starts with what happened, what went right/wrong, what improved/worsened, and lessons for next month.
  • Monthly planning now explicitly creates weekly goals that stem from long-term goals and inform future weekly reviews.
  • Updated the monthly cron prompt and check-in cadence reference to match this order.

[2026-05-17] update | Friday mental-health check-in and audio watch item

  • Created recurring Friday 5:30 PM ET dedicated mental-health check-in cron job 7cb679d0225b with TTS/audio attachment enabled.
  • Added the Friday mental-health template to the recurring review templates reference.
  • Kept the daily morning/night mental-health signals in place.
  • Noted one missed audio attachment as a watch item only; no active TTS investigation unless the issue recurs.

[2026-05-17] update | Review templates, audio parity, mental-health check-in, and SimpleFIN fix

  • Created reusable review templates at ticktick-task-management/references/review-templates-2026-05-17.md for monthly strategic review, weekly review, daily morning check-in, and daily night closeout/mental-health reset.
  • Updated the check-in cadence calibration reference so recurring user-facing reviews use the templates, attach audio notes matching written text exactly, and include a light mental-health capacity/reset check.
  • Added an open loop to verify whether Hermes Agent's self-improving skills loop is capturing useful notes and turning review feedback into skill/template patches.
  • Fixed /opt/data/scripts/simplefin_import.py to request SimpleFIN /accounts with version=2, pending=1, and a start-date window. Verified the default import now returns 15 accounts and 556 transactions; SimpleFIN returns a non-fatal 90-day cap warning.

[2026-05-17] update | Google Workspace check-in signal restored

  • During the Sunday weekly review job, reran Google Workspace auth with the known dependency prefix and verified AUTHENTICATED.
  • Verified Gmail unread search and Calendar listing for the coming week work again.
  • Updated concepts/open-loops.md: no Google re-auth is needed unless the failure recurs; future failures should first retry with PYTHONPATH=/opt/data/google-python-deps before assuming OAuth revocation.

[2026-05-17] update | Check-in cadence recalibrated

  • Updated Sapho's soft check-in cron cadence from Jenffry's voice feedback.
  • Daily morning check-in remains 5:30 AM ET but now runs Monday-Saturday only; Sunday 5:30 AM ET is a combined weekly review, Sunday morning check-in, and weekend/social plan.
  • Paused the standalone Tuesday weekend/social planning job to avoid duplicate planning messages.
  • Moved the relationship/fatherhood clarity prompt to Saturday 10:00 AM ET as a default weekend reflection window.
  • Rewrote the weekly and monthly review cron prompts to be more goal-oriented and planning-smart rather than generic audits.
  • Added weekly and monthly gym progress review using Hevy as source of truth.
  • Added weekly and monthly finance check-in/planning using SimpleFIN/budget signals where available.
  • Changed continuity memory audit to background/local delivery only, so it updates continuity without interrupting Jenffry.
  • Added self-improvement questions, tracking questions, and right-sized past progress review to user-facing check-ins/reviews.
  • Added Jenffry's follow-up calibration: monthly reviews must include a long-term planning session; weekly reviews must plan every day of the week including the weekend, use goal-oriented suggestions and pushback, and avoid overstacking days/weeks with tasks.
  • Added second weekly-review calibration: use clearer top-level sections; make Gym review and Financial review distinct major sections; ignore normal 9–5 Princess UX/UI work in personal weekly planning; use one big important task plus one quick win per day as the default load rule; schedule chosen weekend/social activity as a concrete TickTick item.
  • Updated concepts/decision-log.md, concepts/open-loops.md, and the relevant check-in/weekend/health/finance planning skills.

[2026-05-17] update | Google Workspace morning-check-in signal reopened

  • During the 5:30 AM morning check-in, Google Workspace auth returned TOKEN_REVOKED / invalid_grant, so Gmail and Calendar could not be inspected.
  • Reopened the email/calendar morning-check-in open loop in concepts/open-loops.md with the next action to re-run OAuth setup and verify Gmail search plus Calendar listing.

[2026-05-17] backup | Sapho system backup regularized

  • Checked cron and confirmed Sapho previously had only one-off local/GitHub-safe backups from 2026-05-14, while Princess Storybook had a recurring 30-minute backup.
  • Added /opt/data/scripts/sapho_system_backup.sh.
  • Manually verified it creates a local full restore archive plus a GitHub-safe sanitized backup, then pushes the safe backup to private repo jenffry90-arch/jenffry-sapho-system-backup.
  • Scheduled no-agent daily cron job ff09fc1ab416 at 0 8 * * * so backups do not consume model credits.
  • Local full restore archives stay server-only because they may contain secrets/tokens.

[2026-05-17] fix | Gateway restart/compaction continuity guard

  • Investigated Jenffry's report that context is often lost after compaction, gateway restart, or model/session switch, especially the last user message.
  • Confirmed compaction code already anchors the latest user message into the protected tail (_ensure_last_user_message_in_tail) and existing compression-boundary tests pass.
  • Found a separate restart-resume weakness: during an in-flight gateway restart, the synthetic auto-resume event had empty text, and the user's just-received message could be missing from the JSONL transcript because normal transcript persistence happened only after the agent turn completed.
  • Patched /opt/hermes/gateway/run.py to checkpoint non-internal inbound user text to JSONL before the agent starts, de-duplicate that checkpoint during normal post-turn persistence, and change empty synthetic auto-resume wording to continue the latest unanswered user request from history.
  • Added regression coverage in tests/gateway/test_restart_resume_pending.py; verified 80 passed across restart-resume and compression persistence/boundary tests.

[2026-05-17] verify | DeepSeek V4 Pro configured for fallback and auxiliary tasks

  • Jenffry clarified the intended setup: keep direct DeepSeek API for compression/session compacting, compare direct DeepSeek V4 Pro pricing against OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro, and if headline prices match use OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro as primary fallback.
  • Verified current pricing sources: direct DeepSeek V4 Pro shows $0.435/M cache-miss input and $0.87/M output during the 75% discount window; OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro shows the same headline $0.435/M input and $0.87/M output.
  • Verified /opt/data/config.yaml: primary remains openai-codex + gpt-5.5; fallback_providers is openrouter + deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro; auxiliary compression, session-search, and title-generation are direct deepseek + deepseek-v4-pro.
  • Verified live calls: OpenRouter DeepSeek V4 Pro returned OPENROUTER_DEEPSEEK_V4_PRO_OK; direct DeepSeek V4 Pro returned DIRECT_DEEPSEEK_V4_PRO_OK.
  • Verified gateway is running and started after the configured state was present.

[2026-05-16] verify | OpenRouter fallback configured and live-tested

  • Jenffry asked whether OpenRouter fallback was done after sending the API key.
  • Verified OPENROUTER_API_KEY is present in /opt/data/.env without exposing the value.
  • Verified /opt/data/config.yaml has fallback_providers configured for OpenRouter with openai/gpt-4o-mini.
  • Ran a tiny live OpenRouter test through Hermes CLI; response returned exactly OPENROUTER_TEST_OK.
  • Gateway is running after the key/config were present, so Telegram sessions should have the fallback available. Closed the OpenRouter fallback open loop.

[2026-05-16] update | Sapho operating-system checkup refreshed

  • Jenffry asked for a full system checkup from the strategic layer above code: how Sapho helps, where the support system is strong, and what should improve.
  • Reviewed continuity wiki pages, scheduled jobs, and TickTick Today/Overdue signals.
  • Updated concepts/open-loops.md with the refreshed assessment: architecture is strong but not fully self-correcting; main gaps are dated TickTick overload, stale dashboard language relative to active Storybook work, check-in calibration, OpenRouter fallback blocked on API key, and the need for periodic Sapho usefulness audits.

[2026-05-16] update | Princess Storybook tokenization rule saved as decision

  • Jenffry emphasized that future components must keep the tokenized layer alive.
  • Updated plans/princess-storybook-scratch-build.md with a standing rule: new components must define/extend semantic tokens, component CSS must consume those tokens, exact source values belong in semantic tokens, Storybook display surfaces continue using --pc-viz-* / --pc-card-*, and tokenization is part of done.
  • Added a decision-log entry: 2026-05-16 — Tokenization is a standing rule for Princess Storybook components.

[2026-05-16] update | Semantic Storybook/component tokens added and OpenRouter fallback scoped

  • Jenffry requested full tokenization so token changes propagate across the design system, plus OpenRouter fallbacks.
  • Added src/styles/semantic-tokens.css and imported it after generated foundation tokens. It now centralizes Storybook visualization, card, Button, Form field, Alert, and Evidence/source-confidence semantic tokens.
  • Refactored Button, FormField, Alert, and Evidence CSS to consume semantic tokens instead of scattering source values/hardcoded colors through component rules. Exact source-backed values still exist, but now live as semantic token values.
  • Verified npm run build and npm run build-storybook; committed and pushed 7a86baa (Add semantic component tokens).
  • Inspected Hermes/OpenRouter setup without exposing secrets. No OPENROUTER_API_KEY or OpenRouter credential exists yet, so OpenRouter fallback activation is blocked on Jenffry providing a key; recorded this in open loops.

[2026-05-16] update | Storybook visualization now uses Princess spacing tokens

  • Jenffry identified a broader visual-system issue: many Storybook display boxes felt like 1px wrappers, and the Storybook visualization should use the Princess design system itself to display foundations/components.
  • Added a dedicated Storybook visualization layer in src/index.css with token-based --pc-viz-* variables for page padding, card padding, preview padding, gaps, and mild 8px display radius.
  • Strengthened shared display cards/panels across Foundations and Components so cards, previews, text/detail areas, and list containers use Princess spacing tokens instead of cramped ad-hoc spacing.
  • Verified npm run build and npm run build-storybook; committed and pushed 8dd07bf (Polish Storybook visualization spacing).

[2026-05-16] update | Components overview and layout polish added

  • Jenffry requested a Components / Overview page and identified visual polish issues: insufficient inner padding, bullets escaping containers, and overly large rounded corners outside the Rounded corner foundation examples.
  • Added src/components/Overview.stories.tsx and placed Components / Overview first in the Components storySort order.
  • Added global Storybook layout polish in src/index.css: safer list padding, container/card padding, bullets inside container bounds, and broad 8px radius normalization for cards/frames/placeholders while preserving explicit source/component shapes where needed.
  • Verified npm run build and npm run build-storybook; verified static Storybook index has Components / Overview plus existing Buttons & links Documentation/content stories. Backup cron committed/pushed this as 893b6d0 (Backup Princess Storybook work: 2026-05-16 17:08:06 UTC).

[2026-05-16] update | Local Princess font files installed

  • Safely inspected the ZIP: 2 entries, 0 unsafe paths, both valid font collections.
  • Split the .ttc collections into local .ttf files under public/fonts/, then removed the original collections from the repo working tree to keep browser mapping simpler.

[2026-05-16] fix | External font URLs removed

  • Jenffry preferred actual local font files and did not want Storybook relying on Princess.com or Typekit font URLs.
  • Removed the runtime https://use.typekit.net/mvj6ajr.css import and the Princess.com DomaineDisplay font URL references from src/index.css.
  • Kept the corrected CSS family names in generated tokens (DomaineDisplay and proxima-nova) so local font files can map cleanly when supplied.
  • Added public/fonts/README.md documenting the expected local font files/weights and the rule not to commit unlicensed commercial font files without confirmation.
  • Verified npm run build and npm run build-storybook; confirmed no Princess.com/Typekit font URL references remain in source or static Storybook output; committed and pushed 0893481 (Remove external font URLs).

[2026-05-16] fix | Typography web font rendering corrected

  • Jenffry flagged that Storybook was not using the right typographies.
  • Root cause: the token export names the fonts Domaine Display and Proxima Nova, but the Princess web CSS exposes the real browser font-family names as DomaineDisplay and proxima-nova. Storybook was falling back instead of rendering the source fonts.
  • Updated scripts/generate-tokens.mjs so generated typography variables use the real Princess web family names while preserving Figma-name fallbacks.
  • Added the Princess Typekit CSS import for proxima-nova and @font-face definitions for DomaineDisplay regular and bold from the public Princess font assets.
  • Verified the Typekit CSS and Domaine font URLs return HTTP 200; reran npm run tokens:build, npm run build, and npm run build-storybook; verified the Typography story exists in the local Storybook index; committed and pushed 520292c (Correct Princess typography font loading).

[2026-05-15] fix | Princess Storybook backup path and interruption check

  • Investigated Jenffry's credit-limit interruption concern and failed backup alert.
  • Verified /opt/data/projects/princess-storybook is intact, Git status is clean, local main matches GitHub origin/main at d8880418ab1f, and both npm run build and npm run build-storybook succeed.
  • Root cause of failed cron backup: script was created at /opt/data/home/scripts/princess_storybook_git_backup.sh, but cron resolves relative script paths under /opt/data/scripts/.
  • Fixed by copying the script to /opt/data/scripts/princess_storybook_git_backup.sh, verifying the script target, manually running it, and updating cron job 90d5cf918b5e.

[2026-05-15] update | Princess Storybook scratch v0 created

  • Removed old Storybook Design System clone locally and removed its obsolete backup script/job.
  • Created fresh React + Vite + TypeScript + Storybook 10 project at /opt/data/projects/princess-storybook.
  • Added v0 foundations, placeholder token layer, Button, Form Field, Alert, and Payment/booking failure pattern.
  • Verified npm run build-storybook and npm run build both succeed.
  • Created private GitHub repo jenffry90-arch/princess-storybook, pushed initial commits to main, and created new script-only backup job 90d5cf918b5e every 30 minutes.
  • Created temporary Cloudflare preview tunnel: https://generators-drilling-sand-publish.trycloudflare.com.

[2026-05-15] decision | Princess Storybook build-from-scratch

  • Jenffry decided to build a custom Princess Storybook from scratch rather than clone-and-prune Storybook Design System.
  • Rationale: Storybook Design System felt too small; React USWDS and Carbon are valuable references but too externally opinionated to own directly.
  • Direction: use Storybook Design System, React USWDS, and Carbon as references/north stars; build Princess-specific foundations/components/patterns intentionally.
  • Cleanup: stopped the old clone's Storybook server and temporary Cloudflare tunnel; paused old clone backup job f1ea41a336d3.

[2026-05-14] update | Princess Storybook clone-and-prune started

  • Jenffry chose Storybook Design System as the base for a clone-and-prune strategy.
  • Cloned storybookjs/design-system into /opt/data/projects/princess-storybook-design-system and created branch princess-clone-and-prune-spike.
  • Installed dependencies, verified static Storybook build, started dev server at http://localhost:6006/, and added PRINCESS_CLONE_AND_PRUNE.md as the local operating note.
  • Recorded the setup details, warnings, and next prune step in concepts/princess-ux-work.md.

[2026-05-14] update | Princess Storybook scaffold comparison

  • Compared three possible Storybook scaffold paths for the Princess design-system pilot: Storybook Design System, React USWDS, and a minimal Storybook + tokens starter.
  • Working recommendation: use a clean minimal React + Vite + Storybook project as the actual base, while borrowing structure/testing ideas from Storybook Design System and accessibility/documentation rigor from React USWDS.
  • Recorded the rationale in concepts/princess-ux-work.md: this maximizes control, avoids false completeness, and fits incomplete Princess design foundations.

[2026-05-14] update | Princess Storybook design-system project research

  • Updated concepts/princess-ux-work.md with Storybook design-system project progress.
  • Captured the research direction: use Storybook as a coded/design-system reference for Princess, starting with a small pilot rather than a full system.
  • Recorded the feasibility assessment that an existing open-source Storybook/design system can be valuable as scaffolding, but should not be blindly reskinned into a false-complete Princess system.

[2026-05-14] update | Sapho permissions and check-in calibration

  • Recorded Jenffry's decision that Sapho has full permissions by default; permissions are not the issue, though Sapho should still use judgment for broad/destructive/sensitive actions.
  • Recorded the recurring-output rule that every check-in should include a brief question asking how to improve that check-in.
  • Updated persistent memory and concepts/open-loops.md.

  • Updated concepts/mental-health.md with the practice layer: therapy, meditation 4x/week tied to gym, weekly future-excitement activities, Tuesday weekend/social planning support, Sapho planning sessions, and therapist-recommended future-oriented exercises.
  • Updated concepts/goals-cascade.md, concepts/open-loops.md, and concepts/focus-map.md to mark the mental-health practice layer as partly defined and to avoid tracking bloat.

[2026-05-11] update | Physical-health target pass

  • Updated concepts/health-routines.md and concepts/goals-cascade.md with initial 12-month physical-health targets: 20% body fat, run 20 minutes at 6 mph, and bench press 250 lb.
  • Left remaining body-system definition open until Jenffry adds current body measurements to Hevy.
  • Updated concepts/open-loops.md to make current Hevy measurements the next dependency for defining the rest of the physical-health goals.

[2026-05-11] ingest | Family relationship fatherhood clarity framework

  • Saved raw voice-transcript source to raw/transcripts/family-relationship-clarity-intake-2026-05-11.md.
  • Updated concepts/relationships.md with the framework that the 12-month answer cannot be fully known in advance; written clarity sessions are the practice layer.
  • Updated concepts/goals-cascade.md, concepts/open-loops.md, and concepts/planning-style.md with fatherhood signals, regrets to avoid, house-in-order definition, and the need to avoid being in the same unclear position 12 months from now.
  • Created recurring cron job Weekly relationship/fatherhood clarity prompt and updated the existing TickTick Relationship/fatherhood clarity session task content.

[2026-05-11] ingest | Finance stability target refinement

  • Copied uploaded transaction CSV to raw/finance/transactions-2026-05-03.csv as source material.
  • Updated concepts/finances.md with Jenffry's 12-month finance target: strong reserves already exist; the main goal is monthly comfort, about $400/month excess after normal expenses, no credit-card interest restart, and some enjoyment money.
  • Updated concepts/goals-cascade.md and concepts/open-loops.md to mark finance thresholds as partly defined while preserving weekly finance review as the execution/practice layer.

[2026-05-11] update | Princess UX career success buckets

  • Updated concepts/career.md and concepts/princess-ux-work.md with Jenffry's endorsed 12-month Princess UX success buckets: work quality, execution, influence, portfolio/visible proof, and AI/work leverage.
  • Updated concepts/goals-cascade.md and concepts/open-loops.md to mark Princess UX/career success measures as partly defined and to set the next refinement: choose 2–3 proof projects and define a weekly practice/review cadence.

[2026-05-11] update | Career business AI leverage buckets

  • Updated concepts/career.md with a Princess-like bucket structure for broader career/business/AI leverage: concrete experiments, work leverage, business-development option value, visible proof/assets, and bandwidth/mental-health fit.
  • Updated concepts/goals-cascade.md and concepts/open-loops.md so Blaze remains attractive but inactive unless experiments show demand, capability, and bandwidth.
  • Noted that relationship/fatherhood clarity has advanced to a defined practice layer earlier in the same goals-layer session.

[2026-05-11] update | Social fun 12-month markers

  • Updated concepts/leisure.md with 12-month leisure targets: 6 meaningful contacts, 2 social outings/month, 1 family outing/month, 1 solo hobby thing/month, pickleball every 1–2 weeks, windsurfing planing, and future excitement through friends/new things.
  • Updated concepts/goals-cascade.md and concepts/open-loops.md to mark leisure goals as partly defined and to keep weekly planning as Sapho/check-in layer rather than TickTick recurring clutter.

[2026-05-17] update | Sapho lean-system cleanup

  • Consolidated overlapping TickTick review-calibration references into ticktick-task-management/references/review-system-governance-2026-05-17.md while keeping review-templates-2026-05-17.md as the canonical reusable template file.
  • Removed absorbed TickTick reference files to reduce skill-reference bloat without losing durable rules.
  • Shortened daily, weekly, monthly, night, and Friday mental-health review cron prompts so they point to canonical templates/governance instead of carrying long duplicated instructions.
  • Removed the paused duplicate Tuesday weekend/social cron job because Sunday weekly review now absorbs that function.
  • Added /opt/data/scripts/sapho_lean_audit_snapshot.py and weekly local-only cron job 292f4883ad21 to monitor bloat, token usage, prompt drift, duplicate jobs, and reference growth without interrupting Telegram.
  • Made successful Princess Storybook and Sapho system backup runs silent so no-agent backup jobs do not create routine Telegram noise; failures still emit output and trigger alerts.
  • Updated concepts/open-loops.md with the new lean-governance state.

[2026-05-17] update | Weekly review Today/Overdue cleanup correction

  • Corrected the Sunday weekly-review governance: weekly review must actively inspect and clean the current Sunday Today/Overdue view before delivering the week plan, rather than leaving dated spillover untouched.
  • Cleared the 2026-05-17 Today/Overdue TickTick view by rescheduling stale home/hobby tasks into realistic future windows and verified Today and Overdue are empty.
  • Updated the Sunday weekly review cron prompt so future weekly reviews perform and verify this cleanup step.

[2026-05-18] update | Bucket-list open loop

  • Added an open loop to concepts/open-loops.md to create a bucket list.
  • Framed it as a future-excitement / Leisure support item: capture ideas first, then sort them into concrete plans only when chosen.

[2026-05-18] update | Workout routine cardio-day open loop

  • Added an open loop to concepts/open-loops.md to update workout routines, especially the cardio days.
  • Framed the next step as clarifying what cardio days contain, how often they happen, and how they fit around lifting, pickleball, and recovery.

  • Moved the hosted dashboard contract into system/dashboard.md and updated dashboard generator paths.

2026-05-20 — Continuity cron updated for wiki cleanup

  • Updated cron job a42c23ac4083 from Weekly continuity memory audit to Weekly continuity memory audit and wiki cleanup.
  • The job now explicitly audits recent sessions and cleans/upgrades the current life-area wiki structure: stale links, dashboard summaries, index alignment, and durable continuity routing.
  • Kept delivery local-only so it does not interrupt Telegram unless a later review finds a critical broken state.

2026-05-20 — Sapho upgrade roadmap created

  • Preserved the uploaded Sapho System Improvement Plan as raw reference: raw/reference/sapho-system-improvement-plan-2026-05-20.md.
  • Created system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md as the home of record for future Sapho upgrades and staged Sapho 2.0 work.
  • Added roadmap links to index.md and connected Sapho upgrade loops to the roadmap instead of scattering them across open loops.

2026-05-20 — Open loops restructured into control surface

  • Archived the pre-cleanup open loops page to raw/reference/open-loops-pre-cleanup-2026-05-20.md.
  • Reorganized system/open-loops.md into Active Open Loops, Needs Decision, Needs Information, Parked / Later, System Open Loops, Monitor Only, and Retired / Closed.
  • Kept Active Open Loops to 10 and routed Sapho 2.0 upgrade work to system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md.

[2026-05-20] update | Current strategy compressed into control tower

  • Preserved the full pre-compression system/current-strategy.md source at raw/reference/current-strategy-pre-compression-2026-05-20.md.
  • Rewrote system/current-strategy.md as a scannable strategy control surface: Now, Next, Waiting / Blocked, this week's operating rule, TickTick action firewall, review cadence, not-now items, and deeper page links.
  • Kept the key strategy intact: clarity-first rebuild, Health first, Relationships second, TickTick execution-only, Sapho/wiki as planning/progress layer, and one big important task plus one quick win per day.

[2026-05-20] update | TickTick action firewall strengthened

  • Patched ticktick-task-management with an explicit TickTick action firewall: executable action/date/event/review/errand/follow-up stays in TickTick; ideas, strategy, unresolved questions, Sapho upgrades, and raw dumps route to the appropriate wiki surfaces.
  • Added ticktick-task-management/references/ticktick-action-firewall-2026-05-20.md and updated review-system governance so daily/weekly/monthly hygiene can classify questionable tasks as keep, clarify, reschedule, move to wiki/open loop/roadmap, archive, or complete/close.
  • Updated system/current-strategy.md to mark open-loop cleanup, strategy compression, and action-firewall strengthening complete; next cleanup stage is cron/review overlap audit.

[2026-05-20] update | Perception pattern review made quiet and dashboard-fed

  • Updated cron job Weekly perception pattern review (1052949b0556) to local delivery and a dashboard/wiki-output prompt instead of routine Telegram delivery.
  • Updated inbox/perception-log.md so weekly perception review runs quietly, preserves raw containment, and writes compact patterns/experiments back to the page.
  • Updated system/dashboard.md to include [[perception-log]] as a dashboard-fed page and added a perception-pattern dashboard section for the latest quiet review output.

[2026-05-20] update | Brain dump review made quiet and dashboard-fed

  • Updated cron job Weekly brain dump review (7445bd2463da) to local delivery and a dashboard/wiki-output prompt instead of routine Telegram delivery.
  • Updated inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md so weekly review runs quietly, preserves raw containment, updates status/review summaries in place, and waits for explicit processing requests before moving content elsewhere.
  • Updated system/dashboard.md to include [[brain-dump-sessions]] as a dashboard-fed page and added a brain-dump review dashboard section for the latest quiet review output.

[2026-05-20] update | Dashboard widgets moved to owning pages

  • Moved weekly progress tracking out of system/dashboard.md into new system/progress-tracking.md with its own ## Dashboard summary.
  • Added dashboard summaries to inbox/perception-log.md and inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md so perception patterns and brain-dump review state are fed by their owning pages.
  • Rewrote system/dashboard.md as a dashboard governance/feed-contract page and strengthened SCHEMA.md to prohibit dashboard-only mini-wiki widgets.

[2026-05-20] update | Canonical source-of-truth ownership rules added

  • Expanded system/source-of-truth-registry.md from broad information routing into canonical object ownership rules for major Sapho surfaces: SOUL, memory, core system pages, area pages, inbox/raw/backups, skills, cron jobs, scripts, TickTick, dashboard, and future run ledger.
  • Added the ownership rule: skills own method, wiki owns truth, cron owns recurrence, TickTick owns concrete execution, dashboard displays, raw preserves evidence, and backups preserve recovery history.
  • Updated system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md with Stage 1.5 so page metadata normalization can proceed only where it prevents real routing confusion instead of creating bureaucracy.

[2026-05-20] create | Communication taxonomy added

  • Created system/communication-taxonomy.md as the home of record for Sapho command-language interpretation and constraint inheritance.
  • Defined intent categories for analysis/opinion, inspect/audit, save/document, implement, implement-no-commit, commit/push, schedule, send, destructive action, and correction/calibration.
  • Updated index.md, system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, system/source-of-truth-registry.md, and system/sapho-operating-system.md so future Sapho work uses the taxonomy when interpreting commit/no-commit and superseding instructions.

[2026-05-20] create | Communication-intent eval examples started

  • Created /opt/data/evals/sapho/communication-intent-v1.jsonl with seed scenarios for ambiguous command handling, commit/no-commit supersession, correction/calibration, external sends, scheduling, and destructive actions.
  • Added /opt/data/evals/sapho/README.md to define the eval format and current suite.
  • Updated system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, system/communication-taxonomy.md, and system/source-of-truth-registry.md so the eval examples have a canonical home without pretending the full eval runner exists yet.

[2026-05-20] create | Run ledger schema designed

  • Created system/run-ledger.md as the human-readable run ledger / flight recorder policy page.
  • Created /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/README.md, /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/schema-v1.json, and /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/entries/ as the runtime home for future meaningful-run records.
  • Updated index.md, system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, and system/source-of-truth-registry.md so run ledger policy and runtime traces have clear canonical homes.
  • Marked Stage 5 schema design complete while leaving the lightweight writer, cron failure notes, health metrics, and dashboard system-health decisions open.

[2026-05-20] create | Run ledger writer added

  • Created /opt/data/scripts/sapho_run_ledger.py, a dependency-free writer/validator for local run ledger entries.
  • Updated system/run-ledger.md, /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/README.md, system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, and system/source-of-truth-registry.md so the writer is documented and Stage 5 writer setup is marked complete.
  • Verified the script can produce a valid dry-run entry and that runtime entries remain local under /opt/data/logs/agent-runs/entries/.

[2026-05-20] create | Restore helper and backup scope updated

  • Created /opt/data/scripts/sapho_restore.py to list backups, inspect archive contents, stage selected restore paths, restore selected paths with pre-restore safety copies, and dry-run Git safe-repo restores.
  • Updated /opt/data/scripts/sapho_system_backup.sh so future daily Sapho system backups include evals and local logs/agent-runs alongside wiki, memories, skills, scripts, cron, and core config/state.
  • Updated system/source-of-truth-registry.md with the restore helper and backup-capture rule.
  • Saved Jenffry's fast-action preference into persistent user memory by replacing the permissions memory entry.

[2026-05-20] update | Commit vs push taxonomy clarified

  • Updated system/communication-taxonomy.md with a plain-English distinction between commit, push, and the Sapho backup script.
  • Added the operating rule that if Jenffry's practical intent is to protect/back up current Sapho state, Sapho should prefer the established backup script over manual file syncing unless a scoped local-only commit is specifically requested.

[2026-05-20] create | Context compiler Phase 1 added

  • Created system/context-compiler.md as the home of record for task-specific context loading.
  • Defined lean context bundles for Sapho system work, TickTick/execution, current strategy, finance, health, relationships, career, leisure, life admin, raw capture, and external research.
  • Updated index.md, system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, system/source-of-truth-registry.md, and system/sapho-operating-system.md so context loading has a canonical home and roadmap status.

[2026-05-20] create | Autonomy policy and Level 4 boundaries added

  • Created system/autonomy-policy.md as the home of record for Sapho action-permission levels.
  • Defined Levels 0–4 with Level 4 requiring explicit approval for destructive, external, money, credential/privacy, legal/medical/immigration, broad automation, and high-blast-radius changes.
  • Updated index.md, system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, system/source-of-truth-registry.md, system/sapho-operating-system.md, and system/communication-taxonomy.md to wire the policy into Sapho's operating layers.

[2026-05-20] create | Autonomy Level 4 eval examples added

  • Created /opt/data/evals/sapho/autonomy-levels-v1.jsonl with 16 seed scenarios covering Levels 0–4, especially Level 4 boundaries around destructive data actions, live restore, external sends, public publishing, money, credentials/privacy, legal/medical/immigration, broad automation, and high-blast-radius model/tool changes.
  • Updated /opt/data/evals/sapho/README.md, system/autonomy-policy.md, system/sapho-upgrade-roadmap.md, and system/source-of-truth-registry.md.

2026-05-24 — Weekly goal clarified by Jenffry

2026-05-25 — Dashboard Goals section polish

  • Updated Goals rows to use full-width Health/Finance-style progress bars with spacing between stacked bars.
  • Replaced vague goal subtitles with short one-sentence goal summaries for each life area.

2026-05-25 — Dashboard life-area tabs added

  • Added dashboard tabs for Relationships, Career, Leisure, Life Admin, and Sapho System.
  • Kept Goals & Tasks, Health, Finance, and Wiki as existing surfaces.
  • New life-area tabs use lean wiki-backed summaries and source cards so richer dashboards can be added later without recreating the generic removed Life Map block.

  • Updated system/dashboard.md so dashboard documentation matches the live tab structure.

2026-05-25 — Mental-health aggregate dashboard bars

  • Added two aggregate groups directly under the Mental health title in the Health tab: Overall mental stability and Mental health stabilizers.
  • Overall mental stability uses 7-day and 30-day averages from Stop checking, Mood, Anxiety control, and Energy score habits.
  • Mental health stabilizers uses 7-day and 30-day averaged progress from the remaining mental-health supports: score habits become score percent, completion habits become expected-completion percent, and the group averages those percents.
  • Kept detailed individual mental-health rows below the two aggregate groups.

[2026-05-25] decision | Social moved to Relationships

  • Moved Social from Leisure to Relationships in the continuity wiki: areas/relationships/social.md is now the home for friendships, invitations, social rebuilding, D&D/tabletop leads when socially framed, meetups, and conversation confidence.
  • Updated Leisure to focus on hobbies, outings, play, restoration, and future excitement rather than social rebuilding.
  • Updated dashboard generation so the Relationships tab includes Social and the Leisure goal wording no longer treats social as a leisure signal.
  • Moved obvious social TickTick items from Leisure to Relationships: Explore beginner-friendly D&D options near Miami and Delve back into pick-up.
  • Patched relevant Sapho skills so future reviews and weekend planning route social/friendship ideas to Relationships/Social and hobby/fun ideas to Leisure.

[2026-06-05] update | Night closeout progress tracking

  • Added tonight's progress-tracking row to record a partial day with Gym still open and two overdue tasks carrying into tomorrow.
  • Kept tomorrow's make-it-count signal centered on finishing the Sapho Dashboard and preserving the one-big-task plus one-quick-win rule.

Status: Compressed into a scannable control tower on 2026-05-20; detailed pre-compression source is preserved in raw/reference/current-strategy-pre-compression-2026-05-20.md. Current priority: clarity-first execution with mental health leading and family prior

Current Strategy

Dashboard summary

  • Status: Compressed into a scannable control tower on 2026-05-20; detailed pre-compression source is preserved in raw/reference/current-strategy-pre-compression-2026-05-20.md.
  • Current priority: clarity-first execution with mental health leading and family prioritized when work, family, and mental health conflict.
  • Active signals: one big important task plus one quick win per day; TickTick as execution-only; wiki/Sapho as strategy, planning, progress, and memory layer.
  • Open loops: keep this page synchronized with open-loops, life-area summaries, and weekly/monthly review outputs.
  • Next concrete move: use this page as the first answer source for “what now?”, “what should I focus on?”, and “what is the plan?”

This page is the current strategy control tower. It should stay short enough to scan quickly. Detailed life-area context belongs in the area pages; unresolved items belong in open-loops; decisions belong in decision-log.

Now

Current phase

Jenffry is in a clarity-first rebuild, not a generic productivity push.

The system should create:

  • clearer decisions
  • fewer live options
  • realistic next steps
  • stabilizing routines
  • useful experiments
  • reduced fog

Priority order

  1. Health — mental health first; physical-health routines as stabilizer.
  2. Relationships — partner / marriage clarity and fatherhood presence.
  3. Finances — one-income budget stability and no credit-card-interest restart.
  4. Career — strong Princess UX execution, senior UX growth, AI/work leverage, and business experiments only when concrete.
  5. Leisure — restoration, hobbies, outings, and future excitement.
  6. Life Admin — immigration/admin/home obligations kept concrete and contained.

Current operating truth

  • TickTick is the execution layer only: concrete tasks, dates, appointments, and protected review sessions.
  • Sapho/check-ins/wiki are the strategy, planning, memory, progress, and advisor layer.
  • Hevy is the gym source of truth; weekly reviews interpret it.
  • The wiki/dashboard should make the operating system legible without becoming another task system.

Next

Current priority

  • Mental-health support profile and practice pipeline upgrade: make mental-health goals/dashboard/planning as clear as the completed physical-health pipeline without feeding overanalysis.

Near-term top 3

  1. Mental-health system: support profile, observer-mode reset script, bounded processing practice, and future-excitement artifact.
  2. Relationships/fatherhood clarity: continue written clarity sessions without circular analysis; use the therapist-guided scenario exercise when ready.
  3. Execution cleanup: keep Princess UX/UI, TickTick, and Sapho system work from turning into admin soup.

Current concrete moves

  • Complete the overdue health baseline actions: book the testosterone/blood-pressure appointment and prepare the lab checklist.
  • Keep the current workday anchored to the payment-page Princess UX task.
  • Use weekly/monthly reviews to turn broad goals into a few weekly commitments, not a larger TickTick inventory.
  • Continue cleaning the continuity control surfaces in this order: open-loops → this page → TickTick action firewall → cron/review overlap audit. The first three are now complete; next is the cron/review overlap audit.

Waiting / Blocked

  • Relationship/fatherhood clarity: waiting on the next written clarity session and/or scenario exercise output.
  • Mental-health support profile: active upgrade now; first-pass cascade lives in mental-health, next missing pieces are helpful/unhelpful responses, warning signs, escalation boundaries, and the first artifact choice.
  • Physical-health targets: system/pipeline marked complete on 2026-05-24; keep under normal weekly/monthly review with medical input/labs and 4–6 weeks of Hevy data before over-calibrating or changing targets.
  • Finance stability: waiting on clean month-to-month budget data and weekly finance review signals.
  • Princess UX organization: waiting on real work-status classification: done, blocked, Jira-only, collapse, archive, or next action.
  • Immigration: USCIS replied; next request is due in about two months unless a non-delay issue appears.
  • Sapho upgrades: roadmap items wait behind open-loop cleanup, strategy compression, and action-firewall work.

Active weekly goal

  • Goal: By Saturday night, 2026-06-13, complete a health-and-family-first week: 3 weightlifting workouts, testosterone test, 2 pickleball sessions, tinnitus-doctor search, meditation audio, honest statement to Aubrey, one Frost Science/activity outing with Nolan, and one RC racing or drone-flying leisure/social anchor.
  • Progress: 0%
  • Source: Jenffry's weekly goals stated on 2026-06-07.
  • Next concrete move: schedule the health anchors first, then protect one 30–45 minute Aubrey honest-statement writing block and choose the Nolan/leisure outing window.

Weekly commitments — 2026-06-07 to 2026-06-13

  1. Health: complete 3 weightlifting workouts, get the testosterone test, play 2 pickleball sessions, find a doctor for tinnitus, and create meditation audio.
  2. Relationships / Partner: produce the honest statement to Aubrey about marriage clarity.
  3. Fatherhood: go to Frost Science Museum or do another intentional activity with Nolan.
  4. Leisure / Social: do RC racing or go to the park and fly the drone with Dex.

Active monthly goal

  • Goal: By 2026-06-30, keep the goals-to-execution system legible and stable: dashboard-facing weekly/monthly goals stay current, the health baseline loop stays on track, the one-income budget remains inside guardrails, and the next relationship/fatherhood clarity session happens without circular analysis.
  • Progress: 0%
  • Source: June strategy reset before the monthly review.
  • Next concrete move: use the 2026-06-01 monthly review to confirm the June goal, then keep daily load to one big task plus one quick win.

SMART goal rule

Active goals should be written as one sentence each with:

  • a specific outcome
  • a measurable proof point or count
  • a clear deadline
  • one next concrete move

Keep the goal short enough to scan quickly. If a goal cannot be made SMART yet, move the missing piece to open-loops instead of padding the goal.

This week's operating rule

One big important task plus one quick win per day.

  • The big task must support a goal, key life domain, or necessary operation.
  • Quick wins are allowed, but they should not crowd out the big task.
  • Normal 9–5 Princess UX/UI work is not personal-planning clutter unless it creates after-hours spillover or strategic risk.
  • If health, family, and work conflict, protect mental health and family first, then choose the minimum viable work/admin move.

TickTick action firewall

No TickTick task should exist just because an idea is interesting.

A task belongs in TickTick only when it has at least one of these:

  • a visible next action
  • a real date/deadline
  • an appointment or event
  • a protected review session that creates output
  • a concrete errand/admin action

Otherwise route it to:

  • open-loops for unresolved questions
  • the relevant area page for durable context
  • sapho-upgrade-roadmap for Sapho/Hermes system upgrades
  • inbox/brain-dump-sessions.md or inbox/perception-log.md for raw capture
  • current chat only if it is not durable

Review cadence

  • Daily morning check-in: choose today’s one big task and one quick win; inspect TickTick enough to avoid overload.
  • Daily evening closeout: land the day, capture slips, set tomorrow’s one big task and one quick win.
  • Weekly review: review commitments, TickTick alignment, Hevy, finance, weekly progress signals, archive candidates, and plan every day of the next week.
  • Monthly review: review the whole goals-to-execution system: long-term bearing, 12-month outcomes, monthly targets, weekly commitments, TickTick alignment, and Sapho process quality.
  • Weekly continuity audit: quietly catch missed durable memory/wiki updates without interrupting unless something is critically broken.

Not the focus right now

  • Generic productivity for its own sake.
  • Endless option collecting.
  • Circular relationship/fatherhood analysis.
  • Saving every detail from every chat.
  • Turning the wiki, dashboard, or TickTick into a giant archive.
  • Building second-order Sapho upgrades before the decision surfaces are clean.
  • Treating task hygiene as sufficient when upstream goals and strategy are unclear.

Deeper pages

  • open-loops — current unresolved loops and pending decisions.
  • decision-log — actual decisions and their implications.
  • sapho-operating-system — how Sapho, wiki, skills, cron, dashboard, and task layers fit together.
  • dashboard — dashboard feed contract and progress-tracking surface.
  • health, mental-health, physical-health — health strategy and body system.
  • relationships — partner/marriage, fatherhood, family, friendships, and social connection.
  • finances — budget and investing context.
  • career, princess-ux — work, senior UX growth, AI leverage, and business direction.
  • leisure — hobbies, outings, restoration, and future excitement.
  • life-admin — immigration, admin, and home obligations.

Preserved source

The previous merged strategy page contained detailed migrated material from current-plan, focus-map, goals-cascade, and life-rebuild. It was preserved before compression at:

  • raw/reference/current-strategy-pre-compression-2026-05-20.md

Future cleanup can progressively distribute that preserved detail into the relevant area pages if useful. Do not re-expand this page unless the top control tower becomes insufficient for real planning.
